Genesis1:1 In the beginning
Genesis1:2 The earth was without form and void
Genesis1:3 And God said
Genesis1:4 And God saw that the light was good. And God separated the light from the darkness.
Genesis1:5 God called the light Day
Genesis1:6 And God said
Genesis1:7 And God made the expanse and separated the waters that were under the expanse from the waters that were above the expanse. And it was so.
Genesis1:8 And God called the expanse Heaven. And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:9 And God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry land Earth
Genesis1:11 And God said
Genesis1:12 The earth brought forth vegetation
Genesis1:13 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:14 And God said
Genesis1:15 and let them be lights in the expanse of the heavens to give light upon the earth. And it was so.
Genesis1:16 And God made the two great lights- the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night- and the stars.
Genesis1:17 And God set them in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth
Genesis1:18 to rule over the day and over the night
Genesis1:19 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:20 And God said
Genesis1:21 So God created the great sea creatures and every living creature that moves
Genesis1:22 And God blessed them
Genesis1:23 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:24 And God said
Genesis1:25 And God made the beasts of the earth according to their kinds and the livestock according to their kinds
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 So God created man in his own image
Genesis1:28 And God blessed them. And God said to them
Genesis1:29 And God said
Genesis1:30 And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the heavens and to everything that creeps on the earth
Genesis1:31 And God saw everything that he had made
Genesis2:1 Thus the heavens and the earth were finished
Genesis2:2 And on the seventh day God finished his work that he had done
Genesis2:3 So God blessed the seventh day and made it holy
Genesis2:4 These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created
Genesis2:5 When no bush of the field was yet in the land and no small plant of the field had yet sprung up- for the LORD God had not caused it to rain on the land
Genesis2:6 and a mist was going up from the land and was watering the whole face of the ground-
Genesis2:7 then the LORD God formed the man of dust from the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life
Genesis2:8 And the LORD God planted a garden in Eden
Genesis2:9 And out of the ground the LORD God made to spring up every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food. The tree of life was in the midst of the garden
Genesis2:10 A river flowed out of Eden to water the garden
Genesis2:11 The name of the first is the Pishon. It is the one that flowed around the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 And the gold of that land is good; bdellium and onyx stone are there.
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is the Gihon. It is the one that flowed around the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 And the name of the third river is the Tigris
Genesis2:15 The LORD God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to work it and keep it.
Genesis2:16 And the LORD God commanded the man
Genesis2:17 but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat
Genesis2:18 Then the LORD God said
Genesis2:19 So out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the heavens and brought them to the man to see what he would call them. And whatever the man called every living creature
Genesis2:20 The man gave names to all livestock and to the birds of the heavens and to every beast of the field. But for Adam there was not found a helper fit for him.
Genesis2:21 So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man
Genesis2:22 And the rib that the LORD God had taken from the man he made into a woman and brought her to the man.
Genesis2:23 Then the man said
Genesis2:24 Therefore a man shall leave his father and his mother and hold fast to his wife
Genesis2:25 And the man and his wife were both naked and were not ashamed.

Genesis5:23 Thus all the days of Enoch were 365 years.
Genesis5:24 Enoch walked with God
Genesis5:25 When Methuselah had lived 187 years

Exodus12:9 Do not eat any of it raw or boiled in water
Exodus12:10 And you shall let none of it remain until the morning; anything that remains until the morning you shall burn.

Exodus12:18 In the first month
Exodus12:19 For seven days no leaven is to be found in your houses. If anyone eats what is leavened
Exodus12:20 You shall eat nothing leavened; in all your dwelling places you shall eat unleavened bread.

Exodus20:13 You shall not murder.

Exodus21:1 Now these are the rules that you shall set before them.
Exodus21:2 When you buy a Hebrew slave
Exodus21:3 If he comes in single
Exodus21:4 If his master gives him a wife and she bears him sons or daughters
Exodus21:5 But if the slave plainly says
Exodus21:6 then his master shall bring him to God
Exodus21:7 When a man sells his daughter as a slave
Exodus21:8 If she does not please her master
Exodus21:9 If he designates her for his son
Exodus21:10 If he takes another wife to himself
Exodus21:11 And if he does not do these three things for her
Exodus21:12 Whoever strikes a man so that he dies shall be put to death.
Exodus21:13 But if he did not lie in wait for him
Exodus21:14 But if a man willfully attacks another to kill him by cunning
Exodus21:15 Whoever strikes his father or his mother shall be put to death.
Exodus21:16 Whoever steals a man and sells him
Exodus21:17 Whoever curses his father or his mother shall be put to death.
Exodus21:18 When men quarrel and one strikes the other with a stone or with his fist and the man does not die but takes to his bed
Exodus21:19 then if the man rises again and walks outdoors with his staff
Exodus21:20 When a man strikes his slave
Exodus21:21 But if the slave survives a day or two
Exodus21:22 When men strive together and hit a pregnant woman
Exodus21:23 But if there is harm
Exodus21:24 eye for eye
Exodus21:25 burn for burn
Exodus21:26 When a man strikes the eye of his slave
Exodus21:27 If he knocks out the tooth of his slave
Exodus21:28 When an ox gores a man or a woman to death
Exodus21:29 But if the ox has been accustomed to gore in the past
Exodus21:30 If a ransom is imposed on him
Exodus21:31 If it gores a man's son or daughter
Exodus21:32 If the ox gores a slave
Exodus21:33 When a man opens a pit
Exodus21:34 the owner of the pit shall make restoration. He shall give money to its owner
Exodus21:35 When one man's ox butts another's
Exodus21:36 Or if it is known that the ox has been accustomed to gore in the past
Exodus22:1 If a man steals an ox or a sheep
Exodus22:2 If a thief is found breaking in and is struck so that he dies
Exodus22:3 but if the sun has risen on him
Exodus22:4 If the stolen beast is found alive in his possession
Exodus22:5 If a man causes a field or vineyard to be grazed over
Exodus22:6 If fire breaks out and catches in thorns so that the stacked grain or the standing grain or the field is consumed
Exodus22:7 If a man gives to his neighbor money or goods to keep safe
Exodus22:8 If the thief is not found
Exodus22:9 For every breach of trust
Exodus22:10 If a man gives to his neighbor a donkey or an ox or a sheep or any beast to keep safe
Exodus22:11 an oath by the LORD shall be between them both to see whether or not he has put his hand to his neighbor's property. The owner shall accept the oath
Exodus22:12 But if it is stolen from him
Exodus22:13 If it is torn by beasts
Exodus22:14 If a man borrows anything of his neighbor
Exodus22:15 If the owner was with it
Exodus22:16 If a man seduces a virgin who is not engaged to be married and lies with her
Exodus22:17 If her father utterly refuses to give her to him
Exodus22:18 You shall not permit a sorceress to live.
Exodus22:19 Whoever lies with an animal shall be put to death.
Exodus22:20 Whoever sacrifices to any god
Exodus22:21 You shall not wrong a sojourner or oppress him
Exodus22:22 You shall not mistreat any widow or fatherless child.
Exodus22:23 If you do mistreat them
Exodus22:24 and my wrath will burn
Exodus22:25 If you lend money to any of my people with you who is poor
Exodus22:26 If ever you take your neighbor's cloak in pledge
Exodus22:27 for that is his only covering
Exodus22:28 You shall not revile God
Exodus22:29 You shall not delay to offer from the fullness of your harvest and from the outflow of your presses. The firstborn of your sons you shall give to me.
Exodus22:30 You shall do the same with your oxen and with your sheep: seven days it shall be with its mother; on the eighth day you shall give it to me.
Exodus22:31 You shall be consecrated to me. Therefore you shall not eat any flesh that is torn by beasts in the field; you shall throw it to the dogs.
Exodus23:1 You shall not spread a false report. You shall not join hands with a wicked man to be a malicious witness.
Exodus23:2 You shall not fall in with the many to do evil
Exodus23:3 nor shall you be partial to a poor man in his lawsuit.
Exodus23:4 If you meet your enemy's ox or his donkey going astray
Exodus23:5 If you see the donkey of one who hates you lying down under its burden
Exodus23:6 You shall not pervert the justice due to your poor in his lawsuit.
Exodus23:7 Keep far from a false charge
Exodus23:8 And you shall take no bribe
Exodus23:9 You shall not oppress a sojourner. You know the heart of a sojourner

Exodus23:11 but the seventh year you shall let it rest and lie fallow

Exodus23:16 You shall keep the Feast of Harvest

Exodus23:18 You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with anything leavened
Exodus23:19 The best of the firstfruits of your ground you shall bring into the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
Exodus23:20 Behold
Exodus23:21 Pay careful attention to him and obey his voice; do not rebel against him
Exodus23:22 But if you carefully obey his voice and do all that I say

Exodus23:24 you shall not bow down to their gods nor serve them
Exodus23:25 You shall serve the LORD your God
Exodus23:26 None shall miscarry or be barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
Exodus23:27 I will send my terror before you and will throw into confusion all the people against whom you shall come
Exodus23:28 And I will send hornets before you
Exodus23:29 I will not drive them out from before you in one year
Exodus23:30 Little by little I will drive them out from before you
Exodus23:31 And I will set your border from the Red Sea to the Sea of the Philistines
Exodus23:32 You shall make no covenant with them and their gods.
Exodus23:33 They shall not dwell in your land

Exodus26:35 And you shall set the table outside the veil
Exodus26:36 You shall make a screen for the entrance of the tent
Exodus26:37 And you shall make for the screen five pillars of acacia
Exodus27:1 You shall make the altar of acacia wood
Exodus27:2 And you shall make horns for it on its four corners; its horns shall be of one piece with it
Exodus27:3 You shall make pots for it to receive its ashes
Exodus27:4 You shall also make for it a grating
Exodus27:5 And you shall set it under the ledge of the altar so that the net extends halfway down the altar.
Exodus27:6 And you shall make poles for the altar
Exodus27:7 And the poles shall be put through the rings
Exodus27:8 You shall make it hollow
Exodus27:9 You shall make the court of the tabernacle. On the south side the court shall have hangings of fine twined linen a hundred cubits long for one side.
Exodus27:10 Its twenty pillars and their twenty bases shall be of bronze
Exodus27:11 And likewise for its length on the north side there shall be hangings a hundred cubits long
Exodus27:12 And for the breadth of the court on the west side there shall be hangings for fifty cubits
Exodus27:13 The breadth of the court on the front to the east shall be fifty cubits.
Exodus27:14 The hangings for the one side of the gate shall be fifteen cubits
Exodus27:15 On the other side the hangings shall be fifteen cubits
Exodus27:16 For the gate of the court there shall be a screen twenty cubits long
Exodus27:17 All the pillars around the court shall be filleted with silver. Their hooks shall be of silver
Exodus27:18 The length of the court shall be a hundred cubits
Exodus27:19 All the utensils of the tabernacle for every use
Exodus27:20 You shall command the people of Israel that they bring to you pure beaten olive oil for the light
Exodus27:21 In the tent of meeting
Exodus28:1 Then bring near to you Aaron your brother
Exodus28:2 And you shall make holy garments for Aaron your brother
Exodus28:3 You shall speak to all the skillful
Exodus28:4 These are the garments that they shall make: a breastpiece
Exodus28:5 They shall receive gold
Exodus28:6 And they shall make the ephod of gold
Exodus28:7 It shall have two shoulder pieces attached to its two edges
Exodus28:8 And the skillfully woven band on it shall be made like it and be of one piece with it
Exodus28:9 You shall take two onyx stones
Exodus28:10 six of their names on the one stone
Exodus28:11 As a jeweler engraves signets
Exodus28:12 And you shall set the two stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus28:13 You shall make settings of gold filigree
Exodus28:14 and two chains of pure gold
Exodus28:15 You shall make a breastpiece of judgment
Exodus28:16 It shall be square and doubled
Exodus28:17 You shall set in it four rows of stones. A row of sardius
Exodus28:18 and the second row an emerald
Exodus28:19 and the third row a jacinth
Exodus28:20 and the fourth row a beryl
Exodus28:21 There shall be twelve stones with their names according to the names of the sons of Israel. They shall be like signets
Exodus28:22 You shall make for the breastpiece twisted chains like cords
Exodus28:23 And you shall make for the breastpiece two rings of gold
Exodus28:24 And you shall put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece.
Exodus28:25 The two ends of the two cords you shall attach to the two settings of filigree
Exodus28:26 You shall make two rings of gold
Exodus28:27 And you shall make two rings of gold
Exodus28:28 And they shall bind the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue
Exodus28:29 So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastpiece of judgment on his heart
Exodus28:30 And in the breastpiece of judgment you shall put the Urim and the Thummim
Exodus28:31 You shall make the robe of the ephod all of blue.
Exodus28:32 It shall have an opening for the head in the middle of it
Exodus28:33 On its hem you shall make pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet yarns
Exodus28:34 a golden bell and a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 And it shall be on Aaron when he ministers
Exodus28:36 You shall make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it
Exodus28:37 And you shall fasten it on the turban by a cord of blue. It shall be on the front of the turban.
Exodus28:38 It shall be on Aaron's forehead
Exodus28:39 You shall weave the coat in checker work of fine linen
Exodus28:40 For Aaron's sons you shall make coats and sashes and caps. You shall make them for glory and beauty.
Exodus28:41 And you shall put them on Aaron your brother
Exodus28:42 You shall make for them linen undergarments to cover their naked flesh. They shall reach from the hips to the thighs;
Exodus28:43 and they shall be on Aaron and on his sons when they go into the tent of meeting or when they come near the altar to minister in the Holy Place
Exodus29:1 Now this is what you shall do to them to consecrate them
Exodus29:2 and unleavened bread
Exodus29:3 You shall put them in one basket and bring them in the basket
Exodus29:4 You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus29:5 Then you shall take the garments
Exodus29:6 And you shall set the turban on his head and put the holy crown on the turban.
Exodus29:7 You shall take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and anoint him.
Exodus29:8 Then you shall bring his sons and put coats on them
Exodus29:9 and you shall gird Aaron and his sons with sashes and bind caps on them. And the priesthood shall be theirs by a statute forever. Thus you shall ordain Aaron and his sons.
Exodus29:10 Then you shall bring the bull before the tent of meeting. Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands on the head of the bull.
Exodus29:11 Then you shall kill the bull before the LORD at the entrance of the tent of meeting
Exodus29:12 and shall take part of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger
Exodus29:13 And you shall take all the fat that covers the entrails
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the bull and its skin and its dung you shall burn with fire outside the camp; it is a sin offering.
Exodus29:15 Then you shall take one of the rams
Exodus29:16 and you shall kill the ram and shall take its blood and throw it against the sides of the altar.
Exodus29:17 Then you shall cut the ram into pieces
Exodus29:18 and burn the whole ram on the altar. It is a burnt offering to the LORD. It is a pleasing aroma
Exodus29:19 You shall take the other ram
Exodus29:20 and you shall kill the ram and take part of its blood and put it on the tip of the right ear of Aaron and on the tips of the right ears of his sons
Exodus29:21 Then you shall take part of the blood that is on the altar
Exodus29:22 You shall also take the fat from the ram and the fat tail and the fat that covers the entrails
Exodus29:23 and one loaf of bread and one cake of bread made with oil
Exodus29:24 You shall put all these on the palms of Aaron and on the palms of his sons
Exodus29:25 Then you shall take them from their hands and burn them on the altar on top of the burnt offering
Exodus29:26 You shall take the breast of the ram of Aaron's ordination and wave it for a wave offering before the LORD
Exodus29:27 And you shall consecrate the breast of the wave offering that is waved and the thigh of the priests' portion that is contributed from the ram of ordination
Exodus29:28 It shall be for Aaron and his sons as a perpetual due from the people of Israel
Exodus29:29 The holy garments of Aaron shall be for his sons after him; they shall be anointed in them and ordained in them.
Exodus29:30 The son who succeeds him as priest
Exodus29:31 You shall take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread that is in the basket in the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Exodus29:33 They shall eat those things with which atonement was made at their ordination and consecration
Exodus29:34 And if any of the flesh for the ordination or of the bread remain until the morning
Exodus29:35 Thus you shall do to Aaron and to his sons
Exodus29:36 and every day you shall offer a bull as a sin offering for atonement. Also you shall purify the altar
Exodus29:37 Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and consecrate it
Exodus29:38 Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old day by day regularly.
Exodus29:39 One lamb you shall offer in the morning
Exodus29:40 And with the first lamb a tenth seah of fine flour mingled with a fourth of a hin of beaten oil
Exodus29:41 The other lamb you shall offer at twilight
Exodus29:42 It shall be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the LORD
Exodus29:43 There I will meet with the people of Israel
Exodus29:44 I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar. Aaron also and his sons I will consecrate to serve me as priests.
Exodus29:45 I will dwell among the people of Israel and will be their God.
Exodus29:46 And they shall know that I am the LORD their God
Exodus30:1 You shall make an altar on which to burn incense; you shall make it of acacia wood.
Exodus30:2 A cubit shall be its length
Exodus30:3 You shall overlay it with pure gold
Exodus30:4 And you shall make two golden rings for it. Under its molding on two opposite sides of it you shall make them
Exodus30:5 You shall make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Exodus30:6 And you shall put it in front of the veil that is above the ark of the testimony
Exodus30:7 And Aaron shall burn fragrant incense on it. Every morning when he dresses the lamps he shall burn it
Exodus30:8 and when Aaron sets up the lamps at twilight
Exodus30:9 You shall not offer unauthorized incense on it
Exodus30:10 Aaron shall make atonement on its horns once a year. With the blood of the sin offering of atonement he shall make atonement for it once in the year throughout your generations. It is most holy to the LORD.
Exodus30:11 The LORD said to Moses
Exodus30:12 When you take the census of the people of Israel

Exodus30:15 The rich shall not give more

Exodus30:24 and 500 of cassia

Exodus30:31 And you shall say to the people of Israel

Exodus30:33 Whoever compounds any like it or whoever puts any of it on an outsider shall be cut off from his people.'

Exodus30:35 and make an incense blended as by the perfumer
Exodus30:36 You shall beat some of it very small
Exodus30:37 And the incense that you shall make according to its composition
Exodus30:38 Whoever makes any like it to use as perfume shall be cut off from his people.

Exodus34:1 The LORD said to Moses

Exodus34:5 The LORD descended in the cloud and stood with him there
Exodus34:6 The LORD passed before him and proclaimed
Exodus34:7 keeping steadfast love for thousands
Exodus34:8 And Moses quickly bowed his head toward the earth and worshiped.

Exodus34:18 You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. Seven days you shall eat unleavened bread
Exodus34:19 All that open the womb are mine
Exodus34:20 The firstborn of a donkey you shall redeem with a lamb
Exodus34:21 Six days you shall work
Exodus34:22 You shall observe the Feast of Weeks
Exodus34:23 Three times in the year shall all your males appear before the LORD God
Exodus34:24 For I will cast out nations before you and enlarge your borders; no one shall covet your land
Exodus34:25 You shall not offer the blood of my sacrifice with anything leavened
Exodus34:26 The best of the firstfruits of your ground you shall bring to the house of the LORD your God. You shall not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
Exodus34:27 And the LORD said to Moses

Exodus36:1 Bezalel and Oholiab and every craftsman in whom the LORD has put skill and intelligence to know how to do any work in the construction of the sanctuary shall work in accordance with all that the LORD has commanded.
Exodus36:2 And Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab and every craftsman in whose mind the LORD had put skill
Exodus36:3 And they received from Moses all the contribution that the people of Israel had brought for doing the work on the sanctuary. They still kept bringing him freewill offerings every morning
Exodus36:4 so that all the craftsmen who were doing every sort of task on the sanctuary came
Exodus36:5 and said to Moses
Exodus36:6 So Moses gave command
Exodus36:7 for the material they had was sufficient to do all the work
Exodus36:8 And all the craftsmen among the workmen made the tabernacle with ten curtains. They were made of fine twined linen and blue and purple and scarlet yarns
Exodus36:9 The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits
Exodus36:10 He coupled five curtains to one another
Exodus36:11 He made loops of blue on the edge of the outermost curtain of the first set. Likewise he made them on the edge of the outermost curtain of the second set.
Exodus36:12 He made fifty loops on the one curtain
Exodus36:13 And he made fifty clasps of gold
Exodus36:14 He also made curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle. He made eleven curtains.
Exodus36:15 The length of each curtain was thirty cubits
Exodus36:16 He coupled five curtains by themselves
Exodus36:17 And he made fifty loops on the edge of the outermost curtain of the one set
Exodus36:18 And he made fifty clasps of bronze to couple the tent together that it might be a single whole.
Exodus36:19 And he made for the tent a covering of tanned rams' skins and goatskins.
Exodus36:20 Then he made the upright frames for the tabernacle of acacia wood.
Exodus36:21 Ten cubits was the length of a frame
Exodus36:22 Each frame had two tenons for fitting together. He did this for all the frames of the tabernacle.
Exodus36:23 The frames for the tabernacle he made thus: twenty frames for the south side.
Exodus36:24 And he made forty bases of silver under the twenty frames
Exodus36:25 For the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:26 and their forty bases of silver
Exodus36:27 For the rear of the tabernacle westward he made six frames.
Exodus36:28 He made two frames for corners of the tabernacle in the rear.
Exodus36:29 And they were separate beneath but joined at the top
Exodus36:30 There were eight frames with their bases of silver: sixteen bases
Exodus36:31 He made bars of acacia wood
Exodus36:32 and five bars for the frames of the other side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:33 And he made the middle bar to run from end to end halfway up the frames.
Exodus36:34 And he overlaid the frames with gold

Exodus39:7 And he set them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod to be stones of remembrance for the sons of Israel
Exodus39:8 He made the breastpiece
Exodus39:9 It was square. They made the breastpiece doubled
Exodus39:10 And they set in it four rows of stones. A row of sardius
Exodus39:11 and the second row
Exodus39:12 and the third row
Exodus39:13 and the fourth row
Exodus39:14 There were twelve stones with their names according to the names of the sons of Israel. They were like signets
Exodus39:15 And they made on the breastpiece twisted chains like cords
Exodus39:16 And they made two settings of gold filigree and two gold rings
Exodus39:17 And they put the two cords of gold in the two rings at the edges of the breastpiece.
Exodus39:18 They attached the two ends of the two cords to the two settings of filigree. Thus they attached it in front to the shoulder pieces of the ephod.
Exodus39:19 Then they made two rings of gold
Exodus39:20 And they made two rings of gold
Exodus39:21 And they bound the breastpiece by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue
Exodus39:22 He also made the robe of the ephod woven all of blue
Exodus39:23 and the opening of the robe in it was like the opening in a garment
Exodus39:24 On the hem of the robe they made pomegranates of blue and purple and scarlet yarns and fine twined linen.
Exodus39:25 They also made bells of pure gold
Exodus39:26 a bell and a pomegranate
Exodus39:27 They also made the coats
Exodus39:28 and the turban of fine linen
Exodus39:29 and the sash of fine twined linen and of blue and purple and scarlet yarns
Exodus39:30 They made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold
Exodus39:31 And they tied to it a cord of blue to fasten it on the turban above
Exodus39:32 Thus all the work of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting was finished
Exodus39:33 Then they brought the tabernacle to Moses
Exodus39:34 the covering of tanned rams' skins and goatskins
Exodus39:35 the ark of the testimony with its poles and the mercy seat;
Exodus39:36 the table with all its utensils
Exodus39:37 the lampstand of pure gold and its lamps with the lamps set and all its utensils
Exodus39:38 the golden altar
Exodus39:39 the bronze altar
Exodus39:40 the hangings of the court
Exodus39:41 the finely worked garments for ministering in the Holy Place
Exodus39:42 According to all that the LORD had commanded Moses
Exodus39:43 And Moses saw all the work
Exodus40:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Exodus40:2 On the first day of the first month you shall erect the tabernacle of the tent of meeting.
Exodus40:3 And you shall put in it the ark of the testimony
Exodus40:4 And you shall bring in the table and arrange it
Exodus40:5 And you shall put the golden altar for incense before the ark of the testimony
Exodus40:6 You shall set the altar of burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:7 and place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:8 And you shall set up the court all around
Exodus40:9 Then you shall take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it
Exodus40:10 You shall also anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils
Exodus40:11 You shall also anoint the basin and its stand
Exodus40:12 Then you shall bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the tent of meeting and shall wash them with water
Exodus40:13 and put on Aaron the holy garments. And you shall anoint him and consecrate him
Exodus40:14 You shall bring his sons also and put coats on them
Exodus40:15 and anoint them
Exodus40:16 This Moses did; according to all that the LORD commanded him
Exodus40:17 In the first month in the second year
Exodus40:18 Moses erected the tabernacle. He laid its bases
Exodus40:19 And he spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent over it
Exodus40:20 He took the testimony and put it into the ark
Exodus40:21 And he brought the ark into the tabernacle and set up the veil of the screen
Exodus40:22 He put the table in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:23 and arranged the bread on it before the LORD
Exodus40:24 He put the lampstand in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:25 and set up the lamps before the LORD
Exodus40:26 He put the golden altar in the tent of meeting before the veil
Exodus40:27 and burned fragrant incense on it
Exodus40:28 He put in place the screen for the door of the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 And he set the altar of burnt offering at the entrance of the tabernacle of the tent of meeting
Exodus40:30 He set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:31 with which Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet.
Exodus40:32 When they went into the tent of meeting
Exodus40:33 And he erected the court around the tabernacle and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:35 And Moses was not able to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud settled on it
Exodus40:36 Throughout all their journeys
Exodus40:37 But if the cloud was not taken up
Exodus40:38 For the cloud of the LORD was on the tabernacle by day
Leviticus1:1 The LORD called Moses and spoke to him from the tent of meeting
Leviticus1:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them
Leviticus1:3 If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd
Leviticus1:4 He shall lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering
Leviticus1:5 Then he shall kill the bull before the LORD
Leviticus1:6 Then he shall flay the burnt offering and cut it into pieces
Leviticus1:7 and the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on the altar and arrange wood on the fire.
Leviticus1:8 And Aaron's sons the priests shall arrange the pieces
Leviticus1:9 but its entrails and its legs he shall wash with water. And the priest shall burn all of it on the altar
Leviticus1:10 If his gift for a burnt offering is from the flock
Leviticus1:11 and he shall kill it on the north side of the altar before the LORD
Leviticus1:12 And he shall cut it into pieces
Leviticus1:13 but the entrails and the legs he shall wash with water. And the priest shall offer all of it and burn it on the altar; it is a burnt offering
Leviticus1:14 If his offering to the LORD is a burnt offering of birds
Leviticus1:15 And the priest shall bring it to the altar and wring off its head and burn it on the altar. Its blood shall be drained out on the side of the altar.
Leviticus1:16 He shall remove its crop with its contents and cast it beside the altar on the east side
Leviticus1:17 He shall tear it open by its wings
Leviticus2:1 When anyone brings a grain offering as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus2:2 and bring it to Aaron's sons the priests. And he shall take from it a handful of the fine flour and oil
Leviticus2:3 But the rest of the grain offering shall be for Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy part of the LORD's food offerings.
Leviticus2:4 When you bring a grain offering baked in the oven as an offering
Leviticus2:5 And if your offering is a grain offering baked on a griddle
Leviticus2:6 You shall break it in pieces and pour oil on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 And if your offering is a grain offering cooked in a pan
Leviticus2:8 And you shall bring the grain offering that is made of these things to the LORD
Leviticus2:9 And the priest shall take from the grain offering its memorial portion and burn this on the altar
Leviticus2:10 But the rest of the grain offering shall be for Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy part of the LORD's food offerings.
Leviticus2:11 No grain offering that you bring to the LORD shall be made with leaven
Leviticus2:12 As an offering of firstfruits you may bring them to the LORD
Leviticus2:13 You shall season all your grain offerings with salt. You shall not let the salt of the covenant with your God be missing from your grain offering; with all your offerings you shall offer salt.
Leviticus2:14 If you offer a grain offering of firstfruits to the LORD
Leviticus2:15 And you shall put oil on it and lay frankincense on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:16 And the priest shall burn as its memorial portion some of the crushed grain and some of the oil with all of its frankincense; it is a food offering to the LORD.
Leviticus3:1 If his offering is a sacrifice of peace offering
Leviticus3:2 And he shall lay his hand on the head of his offering and kill it at the entrance of the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:3 And from the sacrifice of the peace offering
Leviticus3:4 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus3:5 Then Aaron's sons shall burn it on the altar on top of the burnt offering
Leviticus3:6 If his offering for a sacrifice of peace offering to the LORD is an animal from the flock
Leviticus3:7 If he offers a lamb for his offering
Leviticus3:8 lay his hand on the head of his offering
Leviticus3:9 Then from the sacrifice of the peace offering he shall offer as a food offering to the LORD its fat; he shall remove the whole fat tail
Leviticus3:10 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins and the long lobe of the liver that he shall remove with the kidneys.
Leviticus3:11 And the priest shall burn it on the altar as a food offering to the LORD.
Leviticus3:12 If his offering is a goat
Leviticus3:13 and lay his hand on its head and kill it in front of the tent of meeting
Leviticus3:14 Then he shall offer from it
Leviticus3:15 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins and the long lobe of the liver that he shall remove with the kidneys.
Leviticus3:16 And the priest shall burn them on the altar as a food offering with a pleasing aroma. All fat is the LORD's.
Leviticus3:17 It shall be a statute forever throughout your generations
Leviticus4:1 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus4:2 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus4:3 if it is the anointed priest who sins
Leviticus4:4 He shall bring the bull to the entrance of the tent of meeting before the LORD and lay his hand on the head of the bull and kill the bull before the LORD.
Leviticus4:5 And the anointed priest shall take some of the blood of the bull and bring it into the tent of meeting
Leviticus4:6 and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle part of the blood seven times before the LORD in front of the veil of the sanctuary.
Leviticus4:7 And the priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense before the LORD that is in the tent of meeting
Leviticus4:8 And all the fat of the bull of the sin offering he shall remove from it
Leviticus4:9 and the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins and the long lobe of the liver that he shall remove with the kidneys
Leviticus4:10 (just as these are taken from the ox of the sacrifice of the peace offerings); and the priest shall burn them on the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:11 But the skin of the bull and all its flesh
Leviticus4:12 all the rest of the bull- he shall carry outside the camp to a clean place
Leviticus4:13 If the whole congregation of Israel sins unintentionally and the thing is hidden from the eyes of the assembly
Leviticus4:14 when the sin which they have committed becomes known
Leviticus4:15 And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before the LORD
Leviticus4:16 Then the anointed priest shall bring some of the blood of the bull into the tent of meeting
Leviticus4:17 and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD in front of the veil.
Leviticus4:18 And he shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar that is in the tent of meeting before the LORD
Leviticus4:19 And all its fat he shall take from it and burn on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 Thus shall he do with the bull. As he did with the bull of the sin offering
Leviticus4:21 And he shall carry the bull outside the camp and burn it up as he burned the first bull; it is the sin offering for the assembly.
Leviticus4:22 When a leader sins
Leviticus4:23 or the sin which he has committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:24 and shall lay his hand on the head of the goat and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the LORD; it is a sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering and pour out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:26 And all its fat he shall burn on the altar
Leviticus4:27 If anyone of the common people sins unintentionally in doing any one of the things that by the LORD's commandments ought not to be done
Leviticus4:28 or the sin which he has committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:29 And he shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and kill the sin offering in the place of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:30 And the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering and pour out all the rest of its blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:31 And all its fat he shall remove
Leviticus4:32 If he brings a lamb as his offering for a sin offering
Leviticus4:33 and lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and kill it for a sin offering in the place where they kill the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:34 Then the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering and pour out all the rest of its blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:35 And all its fat he shall remove as the fat of the lamb is removed from the sacrifice of peace offerings
Leviticus5:1 If anyone sins in that he hears a public adjuration to testify
Leviticus5:2 or if anyone touches an unclean thing
Leviticus5:3 or if he touches human uncleanness
Leviticus5:4 or if anyone utters with his lips a rash oath to do evil or to do good
Leviticus5:5 when he realizes his guilt in any of these and confesses the sin he has committed
Leviticus5:6 he shall bring to the LORD as his compensation for the sin that he has committed
Leviticus5:7 But if he cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus5:8 He shall bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 and he shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 Then he shall offer the second for a burnt offering according to the rule. And the priest shall make atonement for him for the sin that he has committed
Leviticus5:11 But if he cannot afford two turtledoves or two pigeons
Leviticus5:12 And he shall bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 Thus the priest shall make atonement for him for the sin which he has committed in any one of these things
Leviticus5:14 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus5:15 If anyone commits a breach of faith and sins unintentionally in any of the holy things of the LORD
Leviticus5:16 He shall also make restitution for what he has done amiss in the holy thing and shall add a fifth to it and give it to the priest. And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering
Leviticus5:17 If anyone sins
Leviticus5:18 He shall bring to the priest a ram without blemish out of the flock
Leviticus5:19 It is a guilt offering; he has indeed incurred guilt before the LORD.
Leviticus6:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:2 If anyone sins and commits a breach of faith against the LORD by deceiving his neighbor in a matter of deposit or security
Leviticus6:3 or has found something lost and lied about it
Leviticus6:4 if he has sinned and has realized his guilt and will restore what he took by robbery or what he got by oppression or the deposit that was committed to him or the lost thing that he found
Leviticus6:5 or anything about which he has sworn falsely
Leviticus6:6 And he shall bring to the priest as his compensation to the LORD a ram without blemish out of the flock
Leviticus6:7 And the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD
Leviticus6:8 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:9 Command Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:10 And the priest shall put on his linen garment and put his linen undergarment on his body
Leviticus6:11 Then he shall take off his garments and put on other garments and carry the ashes outside the camp to a clean place.
Leviticus6:12 The fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it; it shall not go out. The priest shall burn wood on it every morning
Leviticus6:13 Fire shall be kept burning on the altar continually; it shall not go out.
Leviticus6:14 And this is the law of the grain offering. The sons of Aaron shall offer it before the LORD in front of the altar.
Leviticus6:15 And one shall take from it a handful of the fine flour of the grain offering and its oil and all the frankincense that is on the grain offering and burn this as its memorial portion on the altar
Leviticus6:16 And the rest of it Aaron and his sons shall eat. It shall be eaten unleavened in a holy place. In the court of the tent of meeting they shall eat it.
Leviticus6:17 It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their portion of my food offerings. It is a thing most holy
Leviticus6:18 Every male among the children of Aaron may eat of it
Leviticus6:19 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:20 This is the offering that Aaron and his sons shall offer to the LORD on the day when he is anointed: a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a regular grain offering
Leviticus6:21 It shall be made with oil on a griddle. You shall bring it well mixed
Leviticus6:22 The priest from among Aaron's sons
Leviticus6:23 Every grain offering of a priest shall be wholly burned. It shall not be eaten.
Leviticus6:24 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus6:25 Speak to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers it for sin shall eat it. In a holy place it shall be eaten
Leviticus6:27 Whatever touches its flesh shall be holy
Leviticus6:28 And the earthenware vessel in which it is boiled shall be broken. But if it is boiled in a bronze vessel
Leviticus6:29 Every male among the priests may eat of it; it is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 But no sin offering shall be eaten from which any blood is brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the Holy Place; it shall be burned up with fire.
Leviticus7:1 This is the law of the guilt offering. It is most holy.
Leviticus7:2 In the place where they kill the burnt offering they shall kill the guilt offering
Leviticus7:3 And all its fat shall be offered
Leviticus7:4 the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus7:5 The priest shall burn them on the altar as a food offering to the LORD; it is a guilt offering.
Leviticus7:6 Every male among the priests may eat of it. It shall be eaten in a holy place. It is most holy.
Leviticus7:7 The guilt offering is just like the sin offering; there is one law for them. The priest who makes atonement with it shall have it.
Leviticus7:8 And the priest who offers any man's burnt offering shall have for himself the skin of the burnt offering that he has offered.
Leviticus7:9 And every grain offering baked in the oven and all that is prepared on a pan or a griddle shall belong to the priest who offers it.
Leviticus7:10 And every grain offering
Leviticus7:11 And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings that one may offer to the LORD.
Leviticus7:12 If he offers it for a thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 With the sacrifice of his peace offerings for thanksgiving he shall bring his offering with loaves of leavened bread.
Leviticus7:14 And from it he shall offer one loaf from each offering
Leviticus7:15 And the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings for thanksgiving shall be eaten on the day of his offering. He shall not leave any of it until the morning.
Leviticus7:16 But if the sacrifice of his offering is a vow offering or a freewill offering
Leviticus7:17 But what remains of the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day shall be burned up with fire.
Leviticus7:18 If any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offering is eaten on the third day
Leviticus7:19 Flesh that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten. It shall be burned up with fire. All who are clean may eat flesh
Leviticus7:20 but the person who eats of the flesh of the sacrifice of the LORD's peace offerings while an uncleanness is on him
Leviticus7:21 And if anyone touches an unclean thing
Leviticus7:22 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:23 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus7:24 The fat of an animal that dies of itself and the fat of one that is torn by beasts may be put to any other use
Leviticus7:25 For every person who eats of the fat of an animal of which a food offering may be made to the LORD shall be cut off from his people.
Leviticus7:26 Moreover
Leviticus7:27 Whoever eats any blood
Leviticus7:28 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus7:29 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus7:30 His own hands shall bring the LORD's food offerings. He shall bring the fat with the breast
Leviticus7:31 The priest shall burn the fat on the altar
Leviticus7:32 And the right thigh you shall give to the priest as a contribution from the sacrifice of your peace offerings.
Leviticus7:33 Whoever among the sons of Aaron offers the blood of the peace offerings and the fat shall have the right thigh for a portion.
Leviticus7:34 For the breast that is waved and the thigh that is contributed I have taken from the people of Israel
Leviticus7:35 This is the portion of Aaron and of his sons from the LORD's food offerings
Leviticus7:36 The LORD commanded this to be given them by the people of Israel
Leviticus7:37 This is the law of the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 which the LORD commanded Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus8:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus8:2 Take Aaron and his sons with him
Leviticus8:3 And assemble all the congregation at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus8:4 And Moses did as the LORD commanded him
Leviticus8:5 And Moses said to the congregation
Leviticus8:6 And Moses brought Aaron and his sons and washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 And he put the coat on him and tied the sash around his waist and clothed him with the robe and put the ephod on him and tied the skillfully woven band of the ephod around him
Leviticus8:8 And he placed the breastpiece on him
Leviticus8:9 And he set the turban on his head
Leviticus8:10 Then Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and all that was in it
Leviticus8:11 And he sprinkled some of it on the altar seven times
Leviticus8:12 And he poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron's head and anointed him to consecrate him.
Leviticus8:13 And Moses brought Aaron's sons and clothed them with coats and tied sashes around their waists and bound caps on them
Leviticus8:14 Then he brought the bull of the sin offering
Leviticus8:15 And he killed it
Leviticus8:16 And he took all the fat that was on the entrails and the long lobe of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat
Leviticus8:17 But the bull and its skin and its flesh and its dung he burned up with fire outside the camp
Leviticus8:18 Then he presented the ram of the burnt offering
Leviticus8:19 And he killed it
Leviticus8:20 He cut the ram into pieces
Leviticus8:21 He washed the entrails and the legs with water
Leviticus8:22 Then he presented the other ram
Leviticus8:23 And he killed it
Leviticus8:24 Then he presented Aaron's sons
Leviticus8:25 Then he took the fat and the fat tail and all the fat that was on the entrails and the long lobe of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat and the right thigh
Leviticus8:26 and out of the basket of unleavened bread that was before the LORD he took one unleavened loaf and one loaf of bread with oil and one wafer and placed them on the pieces of fat and on the right thigh.
Leviticus8:27 And he put all these in the hands of Aaron and in the hands of his sons and waved them as a wave offering before the LORD.
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took them from their hands and burned them on the altar with the burnt offering. This was an ordination offering with a pleasing aroma
Leviticus8:29 And Moses took the breast and waved it for a wave offering before the LORD. It was Moses' portion of the ram of ordination
Leviticus8:30 Then Moses took some of the anointing oil and of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled it on Aaron and his garments
Leviticus8:31 And Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 And what remains of the flesh and the bread you shall burn up with fire.
Leviticus8:33 And you shall not go outside the entrance of the tent of meeting for seven days
Leviticus8:34 As has been done today
Leviticus8:35 At the entrance of the tent of meeting you shall remain day and night for seven days
Leviticus8:36 And Aaron and his sons did all the things that the LORD commanded by Moses.
Leviticus9:1 On the eighth day Moses called Aaron and his sons and the elders of Israel
Leviticus9:2 and he said to Aaron
Leviticus9:3 And say to the people of Israel
Leviticus9:4 and an ox and a ram for peace offerings
Leviticus9:5 And they brought what Moses commanded in front of the tent of meeting
Leviticus9:6 And Moses said
Leviticus9:7 Then Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus9:8 So Aaron drew near to the altar and killed the calf of the sin offering
Leviticus9:9 And the sons of Aaron presented the blood to him
Leviticus9:10 But the fat and the kidneys and the long lobe of the liver from the sin offering he burned on the altar

Leviticus9:12 Then he killed the burnt offering
Leviticus9:13 And they handed the burnt offering to him

Leviticus9:15 Then he presented the people's offering and took the goat of the sin offering that was for the people and killed it and offered it as a sin offering
Leviticus9:16 And he presented the burnt offering and offered it according to the rule.
Leviticus9:17 And he presented the grain offering

Leviticus9:23 And Moses and Aaron went into the tent of meeting

Leviticus10:1 Now Nadab and Abihu
Leviticus10:2 And fire came out from before the LORD and consumed them
Leviticus10:3 Then Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus10:4 And Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan
Leviticus10:5 So they came near and carried them in their coats out of the camp
Leviticus10:6 And Moses said to Aaron and to Eleazar and Ithamar his sons
Leviticus10:7 And do not go outside the entrance of the tent of meeting
Leviticus10:8 And the LORD spoke to Aaron
Leviticus10:9 Drink no wine or strong drink
Leviticus10:10 You are to distinguish between the holy and the common
Leviticus10:11 and you are to teach the people of Israel all the statutes that the LORD has spoken to them by Moses.
Leviticus10:12 Moses spoke to Aaron and to Eleazar and Ithamar
Leviticus10:13 You shall eat it in a holy place
Leviticus10:14 But the breast that is waved and the thigh that is contributed you shall eat in a clean place
Leviticus10:15 The thigh that is contributed and the breast that is waved they shall bring with the food offerings of the fat pieces to wave for a wave offering before the LORD
Leviticus10:16 Now Moses diligently inquired about the goat of the sin offering
Leviticus10:17 Why have you not eaten the sin offering in the place of the sanctuary
Leviticus10:18 Behold
Leviticus10:19 And Aaron said to Moses
Leviticus10:20 And when Moses heard that
Leviticus11:1 And the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus11:3 Whatever parts the hoof and is cloven-footed and chews the cud
Leviticus11:4 Nevertheless
Leviticus11:5 And the rock badger
Leviticus11:6 And the hare
Leviticus11:7 And the pig
Leviticus11:8 You shall not eat any of their flesh
Leviticus11:9 These you may eat
Leviticus11:10 But anything in the seas or the rivers that has not fins and scales
Leviticus11:11 You shall regard them as detestable; you shall not eat any of their flesh
Leviticus11:12 Everything in the waters that has not fins and scales is detestable to you.
Leviticus11:13 And these you shall detest among the birds; they shall not be eaten; they are detestable: the eagle
Leviticus11:14 the kite
Leviticus11:15 every raven of any kind
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich
Leviticus11:17 the little owl
Leviticus11:18 the barn owl
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 All winged insects that go on all fours are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:21 Yet among the winged insects that go on all fours you may eat those that have jointed legs above their feet
Leviticus11:22 Of them you may eat: the locust of any kind
Leviticus11:23 But all other winged insects that have four feet are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:24 And by these you shall become unclean. Whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening
Leviticus11:25 and whoever carries any part of their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:26 Every animal that parts the hoof but is not cloven-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean to you. Everyone who touches them shall be unclean.
Leviticus11:27 And all that walk on their paws
Leviticus11:28 and he who carries their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean to you.
Leviticus11:29 And these are unclean to you among the swarming things that swarm on the ground: the mole rat
Leviticus11:30 the gecko
Leviticus11:31 These are unclean to you among all that swarm. Whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:32 And anything on which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean
Leviticus11:33 And if any of them falls into any earthenware vessel
Leviticus11:34 Any food in it that could be eaten
Leviticus11:35 And everything on which any part of their carcass falls shall be unclean. Whether oven or stove
Leviticus11:36 Nevertheless
Leviticus11:37 And if any part of their carcass falls upon any seed grain that is to be sown
Leviticus11:38 but if water is put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it
Leviticus11:39 And if any animal which you may eat dies
Leviticus11:40 and whoever eats of its carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening. And whoever carries the carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:41 Every swarming thing that swarms on the ground is detestable; it shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Whatever goes on its belly
Leviticus11:43 You shall not make yourselves detestable with any swarming thing that swarms
Leviticus11:44 For I am the LORD your God. Consecrate yourselves therefore
Leviticus11:45 For I am the LORD who brought you up out of the land of Egypt to be your God. You shall therefore be holy
Leviticus11:46 This is the law about beast and bird and every living creature that moves through the waters and every creature that swarms on the ground
Leviticus11:47 to make a distinction between the unclean and the clean and between the living creature that may be eaten and the living creature that may not be eaten.

Leviticus15:32 This is the law for him who has a discharge and for him who has an emission of semen

Leviticus16:28 And he who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water

Leviticus16:33 He shall make atonement for the holy sanctuary

Leviticus18:22 You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; it is an abomination.

Leviticus18:30 So keep my charge never to practice any of these abominable customs that were practiced before you

Leviticus19:2 Speak to all the congregation of the people of Israel and say to them
Leviticus19:3 Every one of you shall revere his mother and his father
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves any gods of cast metal: I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:5 When you offer a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD
Leviticus19:6 It shall be eaten the same day you offer it or on the day after
Leviticus19:7 If it is eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus19:8 and everyone who eats it shall bear his iniquity
Leviticus19:9 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 And you shall not strip your vineyard bare
Leviticus19:11 You shall not steal; you shall not deal falsely; you shall not lie to one another.
Leviticus19:12 You shall not swear by my name falsely
Leviticus19:13 You shall not oppress your neighbor or rob him. The wages of a hired servant shall not remain with you all night until the morning.
Leviticus19:14 You shall not curse the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind
Leviticus19:15 You shall do no injustice in court. You shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great
Leviticus19:16 You shall not go around as a slanderer among your people
Leviticus19:17 You shall not hate your brother in your heart
Leviticus19:18 You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the sons of your own people
Leviticus19:19 You shall keep my statutes. You shall not let your cattle breed with a different kind. You shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed
Leviticus19:20 If a man lies sexually with a woman who is a slave
Leviticus19:21 but he shall bring his compensation to the LORD
Leviticus19:22 And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt offering before the LORD for his sin that he has committed
Leviticus19:23 When you come into the land and plant any kind of tree for food
Leviticus19:24 And in the fourth year all its fruit shall be holy
Leviticus19:25 But in the fifth year you may eat of its fruit
Leviticus19:26 You shall not eat any flesh with the blood in it. You shall not interpret omens or tell fortunes.
Leviticus19:27 You shall not round off the hair on your temples or mar the edges of your beard.
Leviticus19:28 You shall not make any cuts on your body for the dead or tattoo yourselves: I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:29 Do not profane your daughter by making her a prostitute
Leviticus19:30 You shall keep my Sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:31 Do not turn to mediums or wizards; do not seek them out
Leviticus19:32 You shall stand up before the gray head and honor the face of an old man
Leviticus19:33 When a stranger sojourns with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 You shall treat the stranger who sojourns with you as the native among you
Leviticus19:35 You shall do no wrong in judgment
Leviticus19:36 You shall have just balances
Leviticus19:37 And you shall observe all my statutes and all my rules
