Leviticus21:17 Speak to Aaron
Leviticus21:18 For no one who has a blemish shall draw near
Leviticus21:19 or a man who has an injured foot or an injured hand
Leviticus21:20 or a hunchback or a dwarf or a man with a defect in his sight or an itching disease or scabs or crushed testicles.
Leviticus21:21 No man of the offspring of Aaron the priest who has a blemish shall come near to offer the LORD's food offerings; since he has a blemish
Leviticus21:22 He may eat the bread of his God
Leviticus21:23 but he shall not go through the veil or approach the altar
Leviticus21:24 So Moses spoke to Aaron and to his sons and to all the people of Israel.
Leviticus22:1 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:2 Speak to Aaron and his sons so that they abstain from the holy things of the people of Israel
Leviticus22:3 Say to them
Leviticus22:4 None of the offspring of Aaron who has a leprous disease or a discharge may eat of the holy things until he is clean. Whoever touches anything that is unclean through contact with the dead or a man who has had an emission of semen
Leviticus22:5 and whoever touches a swarming thing by which he may be made unclean or a person from whom he may take uncleanness
Leviticus22:6 the person who touches such a thing shall be unclean until the evening and shall not eat of the holy things unless he has bathed his body in water.
Leviticus22:7 When the sun goes down he shall be clean
Leviticus22:8 He shall not eat what dies of itself or is torn by beasts
Leviticus22:9 They shall therefore keep my charge
Leviticus22:10 A lay person shall not eat of a holy thing; no foreign guest of the priest or hired servant shall eat of a holy thing
Leviticus22:11 but if a priest buys a slave as his property for money
Leviticus22:12 If a priest's daughter marries a layman
Leviticus22:13 But if a priest's daughter is widowed or divorced and has no child and returns to her father's house
Leviticus22:14 And if anyone eats of a holy thing unintentionally
Leviticus22:15 They shall not profane the holy things of the people of Israel
Leviticus22:16 and so cause them to bear iniquity and guilt
Leviticus22:17 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:18 Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the people of Israel and say to them
Leviticus22:19 if it is to be accepted for you it shall be a male without blemish
Leviticus22:20 You shall not offer anything that has a blemish
Leviticus22:21 And when anyone offers a sacrifice of peace offerings to the LORD to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering from the herd or from the flock
Leviticus22:22 Animals blind or disabled or mutilated or having a discharge or an itch or scabs you shall not offer to the LORD or give them to the LORD as a food offering on the altar.
Leviticus22:23 You may present a bull or a lamb that has a part too long or too short for a freewill offering
Leviticus22:24 Any animal that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut you shall not offer to the LORD; you shall not do it within your land
Leviticus22:25 neither shall you offer as the bread of your God any such animals gotten from a foreigner. Since there is a blemish in them
Leviticus22:26 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus22:27 When an ox or sheep or goat is born
Leviticus22:28 But you shall not kill an ox or a sheep and her young in one day.
Leviticus22:29 And when you sacrifice a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the LORD
Leviticus22:30 It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall leave none of it until morning: I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:31 So you shall keep my commandments and do them: I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:32 And you shall not profane my holy name
Leviticus22:33 who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the LORD.
Leviticus23:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them
Leviticus23:3 Six days shall work be done
Leviticus23:4 These are the appointed feasts of the LORD
Leviticus23:5 In the first month
Leviticus23:6 And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the Feast of Unleavened bread to the LORD; for seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not do any ordinary work.
Leviticus23:8 But you shall present a food offering to the LORD for seven days. On the seventh day is a holy convocation; you shall not do any ordinary work.
Leviticus23:9 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:10 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them
Leviticus23:11 and he shall wave the sheaf before the LORD
Leviticus23:12 And on the day when you wave the sheaf
Leviticus23:13 And the grain offering with it shall be two tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil
Leviticus23:14 And you shall eat neither bread nor grain parched or fresh until this same day
Leviticus23:15 You shall count seven full weeks from the day after the Sabbath
Leviticus23:16 You shall count fifty days to the day after the seventh Sabbath. Then you shall present a grain offering of new grain to the LORD.
Leviticus23:17 You shall bring from your dwelling places two loaves of bread to be waved
Leviticus23:18 And you shall present with the bread seven lambs a year old without blemish
Leviticus23:19 And you shall offer one male goat for a sin offering
Leviticus23:20 And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits as a wave offering before the LORD
Leviticus23:21 And you shall make proclamation on the same day. You shall hold a holy convocation. You shall not do any ordinary work. It is a statute forever in all your dwelling places throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 And when you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:24 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus23:25 You shall not do any ordinary work
Leviticus23:26 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:27 Now on the tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. It shall be for you a time of holy convocation
Leviticus23:28 And you shall not do any work on that very day
Leviticus23:29 For whoever is not afflicted on that very day shall be cut off from his people.
Leviticus23:30 And whoever does any work on that very day
Leviticus23:31 You shall not do any work. It is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your dwelling places.
Leviticus23:32 It shall be to you a Sabbath of solemn rest
Leviticus23:33 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus23:34 Speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus23:35 On the first day shall be a holy convocation; you shall not do any ordinary work.
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you shall present food offerings to the LORD. On the eighth day you shall hold a holy convocation and present a food offering to the LORD. It is a solemn assembly; you shall not do any ordinary work.
Leviticus23:37 These are the appointed feasts of the LORD
Leviticus23:38 besides the LORD's Sabbaths and besides your gifts and besides all your vow offerings and besides all your freewill offerings
Leviticus23:39 On the fifteenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 And you shall take on the first day the fruit of splendid trees
Leviticus23:41 You shall celebrate it as a feast to the LORD for seven days in the year. It is a statute forever throughout your generations; you shall celebrate it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You shall dwell in booths for seven days. All native Israelites shall dwell in booths
Leviticus23:43 that your generations may know that I made the people of Israel dwell in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus23:44 Thus Moses declared to the people of Israel the appointed feasts of the LORD.
Leviticus24:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:2 Command the people of Israel to bring you pure oil from beaten olives for the lamp
Leviticus24:3 Outside the veil of the testimony
Leviticus24:4 He shall arrange the lamps on the lampstand of pure gold before the LORD regularly.
Leviticus24:5 You shall take fine flour and bake twelve loaves from it; two tenths of an ephah shall be in each loaf.
Leviticus24:6 And you shall set them in two piles
Leviticus24:7 And you shall put pure frankincense on each pile
Leviticus24:8 Every Sabbath day Aaron shall arrange it before the LORD regularly; it is from the people of Israel as a covenant forever.
Leviticus24:9 And it shall be for Aaron and his sons
Leviticus24:10 Now an Israelite woman's son
Leviticus24:11 and the Israelite woman's son blasphemed the Name
Leviticus24:12 And they put him in custody
Leviticus24:13 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus24:14 Bring out of the camp the one who cursed
Leviticus24:15 And speak to the people of Israel
Leviticus24:16 Whoever blasphemes the name of the LORD shall surely be put to death. All the congregation shall stone him. The sojourner as well as the native
Leviticus24:17 Whoever takes a human life shall surely be put to death.
Leviticus24:18 Whoever takes an animal's life shall make it good
Leviticus24:19 If anyone injures his neighbor
Leviticus24:20 fracture for fracture
Leviticus24:21 Whoever kills an animal shall make it good
Leviticus24:22 You shall have the same rule for the sojourner and for the native
Leviticus24:23 So Moses spoke to the people of Israel
Leviticus25:1 The LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them
Leviticus25:3 For six years you shall sow your field
Leviticus25:4 but in the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of solemn rest for the land
Leviticus25:5 You shall not reap what grows of itself in your harvest
Leviticus25:6 The Sabbath of the land shall provide food for you
Leviticus25:7 and for your cattle and for the wild animals that are in your land: all its yield shall be for food.
Leviticus25:8 You shall count seven weeks of years
Leviticus25:9 Then you shall sound the loud trumpet on the tenth day of the seventh month. On the Day of Atonement you shall sound the trumpet throughout all your land.
Leviticus25:10 And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year
Leviticus25:11 That fiftieth year shall be a jubilee for you; in it you shall neither sow nor reap what grows of itself nor gather the grapes from the undressed vines.
Leviticus25:12 For it is a jubilee. It shall be holy to you. You may eat the produce of the field.
Leviticus25:13 In this year of jubilee each of you shall return to his property.
Leviticus25:14 And if you make a sale to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor
Leviticus25:15 You shall pay your neighbor according to the number of years after the jubilee
Leviticus25:16 If the years are many
Leviticus25:17 You shall not wrong one another
Leviticus25:18 Therefore you shall do my statutes and keep my rules and perform them
Leviticus25:19 The land will yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 And if you say
Leviticus25:21 I will command my blessing on you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 When you sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 The land shall not be sold in perpetuity
Leviticus25:24 And in all the country you possess
Leviticus25:25 If your brother becomes poor and sells part of his property
Leviticus25:26 If a man has no one to redeem it and then himself becomes prosperous and finds sufficient means to redeem it
Leviticus25:27 let him calculate the years since he sold it and pay back the balance to the man to whom he sold it
Leviticus25:28 But if he has not sufficient means to recover it
Leviticus25:29 If a man sells a dwelling house in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 If it is not redeemed within a full year
Leviticus25:31 But the houses of the villages that have no wall around them shall be classified with the fields of the land. They may be redeemed
Leviticus25:32 As for the cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 And if one of the Levites exercises his right of redemption
Leviticus25:34 But the fields of pastureland belonging to their cities may not be sold
Leviticus25:35 If your brother becomes poor and cannot maintain himself with you
Leviticus25:36 Take no interest from him or profit
Leviticus25:37 You shall not lend him your money at interest
Leviticus25:38 I am the LORD your God
Leviticus25:39 If your brother becomes poor beside you and sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 he shall be with you as a hired servant and as a sojourner. He shall serve with you until the year of the jubilee.
Leviticus25:41 Then he shall go out from you
Leviticus25:42 For they are my servants
Leviticus25:43 You shall not rule over him ruthlessly but shall fear your God.
Leviticus25:44 As for your male and female slaves whom you may have: you may buy male and female slaves from among the nations that are around you.
Leviticus25:45 You may also buy from among the strangers who sojourn with you and their clans that are with you
Leviticus25:46 You may bequeath them to your sons after you to inherit as a possession forever. You may make slaves of them
Leviticus25:47 If a stranger or sojourner with you becomes rich
Leviticus25:48 then after he is sold he may be redeemed. One of his brothers may redeem him
Leviticus25:49 or his uncle or his cousin may redeem him
Leviticus25:50 He shall calculate with his buyer from the year when he sold himself to him until the year of jubilee
Leviticus25:51 If there are still many years left
Leviticus25:52 If there remain but a few years until the year of jubilee
Leviticus25:53 He shall treat him as a servant hired year by year. He shall not rule ruthlessly over him in your sight.
Leviticus25:54 And if he is not redeemed by these means
Leviticus25:55 For it is to me that the people of Israel are servants. They are my servants whom I brought out of the land of Egypt: I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus26:1 You shall not make idols for yourselves or erect an image or pillar
Leviticus26:2 You shall keep my Sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the LORD.
Leviticus26:3 If you walk in my statutes and observe my commandments and do them
Leviticus26:4 then I will give you your rains in their season
Leviticus26:5 Your threshing shall last to the time of the grape harvest
Leviticus26:6 I will give peace in the land
Leviticus26:7 You shall chase your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you shall chase a hundred
Leviticus26:9 I will turn to you and make you fruitful and multiply you and will confirm my covenant with you.
Leviticus26:10 You shall eat old store long kept
Leviticus26:11 I will make my dwelling among you
Leviticus26:12 And I will walk among you and will be your God
Leviticus26:13 I am the LORD your God
Leviticus26:14 But if you will not listen to me and will not do all these commandments
Leviticus26:15 if you spurn my statutes
Leviticus26:16 then I will do this to you: I will visit you with panic
Leviticus26:17 I will set my face against you
Leviticus26:18 And if in spite of this you will not listen to me
Leviticus26:19 and I will break the pride of your power
Leviticus26:20 And your strength shall be spent in vain
Leviticus26:21 Then if you walk contrary to me and will not listen to me
Leviticus26:22 And I will let loose the wild beasts against you
Leviticus26:23 And if by this discipline you are not turned to me but walk contrary to me
Leviticus26:24 then I also will walk contrary to you
Leviticus26:25 And I will bring a sword upon you
Leviticus26:26 When I break your supply of bread
Leviticus26:27 But if in spite of this you will not listen to me
Leviticus26:28 then I will walk contrary to you in fury
Leviticus26:29 You shall eat the flesh of your sons
Leviticus26:30 And I will destroy your high places and cut down your incense altars and cast your dead bodies upon the dead bodies of your idols
Leviticus26:31 And I will lay your cities waste and will make your sanctuaries desolate
Leviticus26:32 And I myself will devastate the land
Leviticus26:33 And I will scatter you among the nations
Leviticus26:34 Then the land shall enjoy its Sabbaths as long as it lies desolate
Leviticus26:35 As long as it lies desolate it shall have rest
Leviticus26:36 And as for those of you who are left
Leviticus26:37 They shall stumble over one another
Leviticus26:38 And you shall perish among the nations
Leviticus26:39 And those of you who are left shall rot away in your enemies' lands because of their iniquity
Leviticus26:40 But if they confess their iniquity and the iniquity of their fathers in their treachery that they committed against me
Leviticus26:41 so that I walked contrary to them and brought them into the land of their enemies- if then their uncircumcised heart is humbled and they make amends for their iniquity
Leviticus26:42 then I will remember my covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 But the land shall be abandoned by them and enjoy its Sabbaths while it lies desolate without them
Leviticus26:44 Yet for all that
Leviticus26:45 But I will for their sake remember the covenant with their forefathers
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes and rules and laws that the LORD made between him and the people of Israel through Moses on Mount Sinai.
Leviticus27:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Leviticus27:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them
Leviticus27:3 then the valuation of a male from twenty years old up to sixty years old shall be fifty shekels of silver
Leviticus27:4 If the person is a female
Leviticus27:5 If the person is from five years old up to twenty years old
Leviticus27:6 If the person is from a month old up to five years old
Leviticus27:7 And if the person is sixty years old or over
Leviticus27:8 And if someone is too poor to pay the valuation
Leviticus27:9 If the vow is an animal that may be offered as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus27:10 He shall not exchange it or make a substitute for it
Leviticus27:11 And if it is any unclean animal that may not be offered as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus27:12 and the priest shall value it as either good or bad; as the priest values it
Leviticus27:13 But if he wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:14 When a man dedicates his house as a holy gift to the LORD
Leviticus27:15 And if the donor wishes to redeem his house
Leviticus27:16 If a man dedicates to the LORD part of the land that is his possession
Leviticus27:17 If he dedicates his field from the year of jubilee
Leviticus27:18 but if he dedicates his field after the jubilee
Leviticus27:19 And if he who dedicates the field wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:20 But if he does not wish to redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 But the field
Leviticus27:22 If he dedicates to the LORD a field that he has bought
Leviticus27:23 then the priest shall calculate the amount of the valuation for it up to the year of jubilee
Leviticus27:24 In the year of jubilee the field shall return to him from whom it was bought
Leviticus27:25 Every valuation shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs shall make a shekel.
Leviticus27:26 But a firstborn of animals
Leviticus27:27 And if it is an unclean animal
Leviticus27:28 But no devoted thing that a man devotes to the LORD
Leviticus27:29 No one devoted
Leviticus27:30 Every tithe of the land
Leviticus27:31 If a man wishes to redeem some of his tithe
Leviticus27:32 And every tithe of herds and flocks
Leviticus27:33 One shall not differentiate between good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments that the LORD commanded Moses for the people of Israel on Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 The LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers1:2 Take a census of all the congregation of the people of Israel
Numbers1:3 From twenty years old and upward
Numbers1:4 And there shall be with you a man from each tribe
Numbers1:5 And these are the names of the men who shall assist you. From Reuben
Numbers1:6 from Simeon
Numbers1:7 from Judah
Numbers1:8 from Issachar
Numbers1:9 from Zebulun
Numbers1:10 from the sons of Joseph
Numbers1:11 from Benjamin
Numbers1:12 from Dan
Numbers1:13 from Asher
Numbers1:14 from Gad
Numbers1:15 from Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These were the ones chosen from the congregation
Numbers1:17 Moses and Aaron took these men who had been named
Numbers1:18 and on the first day of the second month
Numbers1:19 as the LORD commanded Moses. So he listed them in the wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers1:20 The people of Reuben
Numbers1:21 those listed of the tribe of Reuben were 46
Numbers1:22 Of the people of Simeon
Numbers1:23 those listed of the tribe of Simeon were 59
Numbers1:24 Of the people of Gad
Numbers1:25 those listed of the tribe of Gad were 45
Numbers1:26 Of the people of Judah
Numbers1:27 those listed of the tribe of Judah were 74
Numbers1:28 Of the people of Issachar
Numbers1:29 those listed of the tribe of Issachar were 54
Numbers1:30 Of the people of Zebulun
Numbers1:31 those listed of the tribe of Zebulun were 57
Numbers1:32 Of the people of Joseph
Numbers1:33 those listed of the tribe of Ephraim were 40
Numbers1:34 Of the people of Manasseh
Numbers1:35 those listed of the tribe of Manasseh were 32
Numbers1:36 Of the people of Benjamin
Numbers1:37 those listed of the tribe of Benjamin were 35
Numbers1:38 Of the people of Dan
Numbers1:39 those listed of the tribe of Dan were 62
Numbers1:40 Of the people of Asher
Numbers1:41 those listed of the tribe of Asher were 41
Numbers1:42 Of the people of Naphtali
Numbers1:43 those listed of the tribe of Naphtali were 53
Numbers1:44 These are those who were listed
Numbers1:45 So all those listed of the people of Israel
Numbers1:46 all those listed were 603
Numbers1:47 But the Levites were not listed along with them by their ancestral tribe.
Numbers1:48 For the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers1:49 Only the tribe of Levi you shall not list
Numbers1:50 But appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:51 When the tabernacle is to set out
Numbers1:52 The people of Israel shall pitch their tents by their companies
Numbers1:53 But the Levites shall camp around the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:54 Thus did the people of Israel; they did according to all that the LORD commanded Moses.
Numbers2:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers2:2 The people of Israel shall camp each by his own standard
Numbers2:3 Those to camp on the east side toward the sunrise shall be of the standard of the camp of Judah by their companies
Numbers2:4 his company as listed being 74
Numbers2:5 Those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Issachar
Numbers2:6 his company as listed being 54
Numbers2:7 Then the tribe of Zebulun
Numbers2:8 his company as listed being 57
Numbers2:9 All those listed of the camp of Judah
Numbers2:10 On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Reuben by their companies
Numbers2:11 his company as listed being 46
Numbers2:12 And those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Simeon
Numbers2:13 his company as listed being 59
Numbers2:14 Then the tribe of Gad
Numbers2:15 his company as listed being 45
Numbers2:16 All those listed of the camp of Reuben
Numbers2:17 Then the tent of meeting shall set out
Numbers2:18 On the west side shall be the standard of the camp of Ephraim by their companies
Numbers2:19 his company as listed being 40
Numbers2:20 And next to him shall be the tribe of Manasseh
Numbers2:21 his company as listed being 32
Numbers2:22 Then the tribe of Benjamin
Numbers2:23 his company as listed being 35
Numbers2:24 All those listed of the camp of Ephraim
Numbers2:25 On the north side shall be the standard of the camp of Dan by their companies
Numbers2:26 his company as listed being 62
Numbers2:27 And those to camp next to him shall be the tribe of Asher
Numbers2:28 his company as listed being 41
Numbers2:29 Then the tribe of Naphtali
Numbers2:30 his company as listed being 53
Numbers2:31 All those listed of the camp of Dan were 157
Numbers2:32 These are the people of Israel as listed by their fathers' houses. All those listed in the camps by their companies were 603
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not listed among the people of Israel
Numbers2:34 Thus did the people of Israel. According to all that the LORD commanded Moses
Numbers3:1 These are the generations of Aaron and Moses at the time when the LORD spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn
Numbers3:3 These are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu died before the LORD when they offered unauthorized fire before the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:5 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers3:6 Bring the tribe of Levi near
Numbers3:7 They shall keep guard over him and over the whole congregation before the tent of meeting
Numbers3:8 They shall guard all the furnishings of the tent of meeting
Numbers3:9 And you shall give the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they are wholly given to him from among the people of Israel.
Numbers3:10 And you shall appoint Aaron and his sons
Numbers3:11 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers3:12 Behold
Numbers3:13 for all the firstborn are mine. On the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 And the LORD spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 List the sons of Levi
Numbers3:16 So Moses listed them according to the word of the LORD
Numbers3:17 And these were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon and Kohath and Merari.
Numbers3:18 And these are the names of the sons of Gershon by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 And the sons of Kohath by their clans: Amram
Numbers3:20 And the sons of Merari by their clans: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites
Numbers3:21 To Gershon belonged the clan of the Libnites and the clan of the Shimeites; these were the clans of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 Their listing according to the number of all the males from a month old and upward was 7
Numbers3:23 The clans of the Gershonites were to camp behind the tabernacle on the west
Numbers3:24 with Eliasaph
Numbers3:25 And the guard duty of the sons of Gershon in the tent of meeting involved the tabernacle
Numbers3:26 the hangings of the court
Numbers3:27 To Kohath belonged the clan of the Amramites and the clan of the Izharites and the clan of the Hebronites and the clan of the Uzzielites; these are the clans of the Kohathites.
Numbers3:28 According to the number of all the males
Numbers3:29 The clans of the sons of Kohath were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle
Numbers3:30 with Elizaphan the son of Uzziel as chief of the fathers' house of the clans of the Kohathites.
Numbers3:31 And their guard duty involved the ark
Numbers3:32 And Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest was to be chief over the chiefs of the Levites
Numbers3:33 To Merari belonged the clan of the Mahlites and the clan of the Mushites: these are the clans of Merari.
Numbers3:34 Their listing according to the number of all the males from a month old and upward was 6
Numbers3:35 And the chief of the fathers' house of the clans of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail. They were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 And the appointed guard duty of the sons of Merari involved the frames of the tabernacle
Numbers3:37 also the pillars around the court
Numbers3:38 Those who were to camp before the tabernacle on the east
Numbers3:39 All those listed among the Levites
Numbers3:40 And the LORD said to Moses
Numbers3:41 And you shall take the Levites for me- I am the LORD- instead of all the firstborn among the people of Israel
Numbers3:42 So Moses listed all the firstborn among the people of Israel
Numbers3:43 And all the firstborn males
Numbers3:44 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers3:45 Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the people of Israel
Numbers3:46 And as the redemption price for the 273 of the firstborn of the people of Israel
Numbers3:47 you shall take five shekels per head; you shall take them according to the shekel of the sanctuary (the shekel of twenty gerahs)
Numbers3:48 and give the money to Aaron and his sons as the redemption price for those who are over.
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the redemption money from those who were over and above those redeemed by the Levites.
Numbers3:50 From the firstborn of the people of Israel he took the money
Numbers3:51 And Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:2 Take a census of the sons of Kohath from among the sons of Levi
Numbers4:3 from thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:4 This is the service of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting: the most holy things.
Numbers4:5 When the camp is to set out
Numbers4:6 Then they shall put on it a covering of goatskin and spread on top of that a cloth all of blue
Numbers4:7 And over the table of the bread of the Presence they shall spread a cloth of blue and put on it the plates
Numbers4:8 Then they shall spread over them a cloth of scarlet and cover the same with a covering of goatskin
Numbers4:9 And they shall take a cloth of blue and cover the lampstand for the light
Numbers4:10 And they shall put it with all its utensils in a covering of goatskin and put it on the carrying frame.
Numbers4:11 And over the golden altar they shall spread a cloth of blue and cover it with a covering of goatskin
Numbers4:12 And they shall take all the vessels of the service that are used in the sanctuary and put them in a cloth of blue and cover them with a covering of goatskin and put them on the carrying frame.
Numbers4:13 And they shall take away the ashes from the altar and spread a purple cloth over it.

Numbers5:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers5:2 Command the people of Israel that they put out of the camp everyone who is leprous or has a discharge and everyone who is unclean through contact with the dead.
Numbers5:3 You shall put out both male and female
Numbers5:4 And the people of Israel did so
Numbers5:5 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers5:6 Speak to the people of Israel
Numbers5:7 he shall confess his sin that he has committed. And he shall make full restitution for his wrong
Numbers5:8 But if the man has no next of kin to whom restitution may be made for the wrong
Numbers5:9 And every contribution
Numbers5:10 Each one shall keep his holy donations: whatever anyone gives to the priest shall be his.
Numbers5:11 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers5:12 Speak to the people of Israel
Numbers5:13 if a man lies with her sexually
Numbers5:14 and if the spirit of jealousy comes over him and he is jealous of his wife who has defiled herself
Numbers5:15 then the man shall bring his wife to the priest and bring the offering required of her
Numbers5:16 And the priest shall bring her near and set her before the LORD.
Numbers5:17 And the priest shall take holy water in an earthenware vessel and take some of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle and put it into the water.
Numbers5:18 And the priest shall set the woman before the LORD and unbind the hair of the woman's head and place in her hands the grain offering of remembrance
Numbers5:19 Then the priest shall make her take an oath
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray
Numbers5:21 then' (let the priest make the woman take the oath of the curse
Numbers5:22 May this water that brings the curse pass into your bowels and make your womb swell and your thigh fall away.' And the woman shall say
Numbers5:23 Then the priest shall write these curses in a book and wash them off into the water of bitterness.
Numbers5:24 And he shall make the woman drink the water of bitterness that brings the curse
Numbers5:25 And the priest shall take the grain offering of jealousy out of the woman's hand and shall wave the grain offering before the LORD and bring it to the altar.
Numbers5:26 And the priest shall take a handful of the grain offering
Numbers5:27 And when he has made her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean
Numbers5:29 This is the law in cases of jealousy
Numbers5:30 or when the spirit of jealousy comes over a man and he is jealous of his wife. Then he shall set the woman before the LORD

Numbers6:1 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers6:2 Speak to the people of Israel and say to them
Numbers6:3 he shall separate himself from wine and strong drink. He shall drink no vinegar made from wine or strong drink and shall not drink any juice of grapes or eat grapes
Numbers6:4 All the days of his separation he shall eat nothing that is produced by the grapevine
Numbers6:5 All the days of his vow of separation
Numbers6:6 All the days that he separates himself to the LORD he shall not go near a dead body.
Numbers6:7 Not even for his father or for his mother
Numbers6:8 All the days of his separation he is holy to the LORD.
Numbers6:9 And if any man dies very suddenly beside him and he defiles his consecrated head
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two pigeons to the priest to the entrance of the tent of meeting
Numbers6:11 and the priest shall offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering
Numbers6:12 and separate himself to the LORD for the days of his separation and bring a male lamb a year old for a guilt offering. But the previous period shall be void

Numbers6:14 and he shall bring his gift to the LORD
Numbers6:15 and a basket of unleavened bread
Numbers6:16 And the priest shall bring them before the LORD and offer his sin offering and his burnt offering
Numbers6:17 and he shall offer the ram as a sacrifice of peace offering to the LORD

Numbers6:19 And the priest shall take the shoulder of the ram
Numbers6:20 and the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the LORD. They are a holy portion for the priest

Numbers15:10 And you shall offer for the drink offering half a hin of wine

Numbers16:3 They assembled themselves together against Moses and against Aaron and said to them
Numbers16:4 When Moses heard it
Numbers16:5 and he said to Korah and all his company
Numbers16:6 Do this: take censers
Numbers16:7 put fire in them and put incense on them before the LORD tomorrow
Numbers16:8 And Moses said to Korah
Numbers16:9 is it too small a thing for you that the God of Israel has separated you from the congregation of Israel
Numbers16:10 and that he has brought you near him
Numbers16:11 Therefore it is against the LORD that you and all your company have gathered together. What is Aaron that you grumble against him?
Numbers16:12 And Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiram the sons of Eliab
Numbers16:13 Is it a small thing that you have brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey
Numbers16:14 Moreover
Numbers16:15 And Moses was very angry and said to the LORD
Numbers16:16 And Moses said to Korah
Numbers16:17 And let every one of you take his censer and put incense on it
Numbers16:18 So every man took his censer and put fire in them and laid incense on them and stood at the entrance of the tent of meeting with Moses and Aaron.
Numbers16:19 Then Korah assembled all the congregation against them at the entrance of the tent of meeting. And the glory of the LORD appeared to all the congregation.
Numbers16:20 And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers16:21 Separate yourselves from among this congregation
Numbers16:22 And they fell on their faces and said
Numbers16:23 And the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers16:24 Say to the congregation
Numbers16:25 Then Moses rose and went to Dathan and Abiram
Numbers16:26 And he spoke to the congregation
Numbers16:27 So they got away from the dwelling of Korah
Numbers16:28 And Moses said
Numbers16:29 If these men die as all men die
Numbers16:30 But if the LORD creates something new
Numbers16:31 And as soon as he had finished speaking all these words
Numbers16:32 And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up
Numbers16:33 So they and all that belonged to them went down alive into Sheol
Numbers16:34 And all Israel who were around them fled at their cry
Numbers16:35 And fire came out from the LORD and consumed the 250 men offering the incense.
Numbers16:36 Then the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers16:37 Tell Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest to take up the censers out of the blaze. Then scatter the fire far and wide
Numbers16:38 As for the censers of these men who have sinned at the cost of their lives
Numbers16:39 So Eleazar the priest took the bronze censers
Numbers16:40 to be a reminder to the people of Israel
Numbers16:41 But on the next day all the congregation of the people of Israel grumbled against Moses and against Aaron
Numbers16:42 And when the congregation had assembled against Moses and against Aaron
Numbers16:43 And Moses and Aaron came to the front of the tent of meeting
Numbers16:44 and the LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers16:45 Get away from the midst of this congregation
Numbers16:46 And Moses said to Aaron
Numbers16:47 So Aaron took it as Moses said and ran into the midst of the assembly. And behold
Numbers16:48 And he stood between the dead and the living
Numbers16:49 Now those who died in the plague were 14

Numbers18:12 All the best of the oil and all the best of the wine and of the grain

Numbers18:31 And you may eat it in any place

Numbers19:1 Now the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron
Numbers19:2 This is the statute of the law that the LORD has commanded: Tell the people of Israel to bring you a red heifer without defect
Numbers19:3 And you shall give it to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 And Eleazar the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger
Numbers19:5 And the heifer shall be burned in his sight. Its skin
Numbers19:6 And the priest shall take cedarwood and hyssop and scarlet yarn
Numbers19:7 Then the priest shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:8 The one who burns the heifer shall wash his clothes in water and bathe his body in water and shall be unclean until evening.
Numbers19:9 And a man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer and deposit them outside the camp in a clean place. And they shall be kept for the water for impurity for the congregation of the people of Israel; it is a sin offering.
Numbers19:10 And the one who gathers the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes and be unclean until evening. And this shall be a perpetual statute for the people of Israel
Numbers19:11 Whoever touches the dead body of any person shall be unclean seven days.
Numbers19:12 He shall cleanse himself with the water on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:13 Whoever touches a dead person
Numbers19:14 This is the law when someone dies in a tent: everyone who comes into the tent and everyone who is in the tent shall be unclean seven days.
Numbers19:15 And every open vessel that has no cover fastened on it is unclean.
Numbers19:16 Whoever in the open field touches someone who was killed with a sword or who died naturally
Numbers19:17 For the unclean they shall take some ashes of the burnt sin offering
Numbers19:18 Then a clean person shall take hyssop and dip it in the water and sprinkle it on the tent and on all the furnishings and on the persons who were there and on whoever touched the bone
Numbers19:19 And the clean person shall sprinkle it on the unclean on the third day and on the seventh day. Thus on the seventh day he shall cleanse him
Numbers19:20 If the man who is unclean does not cleanse himself
Numbers19:21 And it shall be a statute forever for them. The one who sprinkles the water for impurity shall wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 And whatever the unclean person touches shall be unclean

Numbers28:2 Command the people of Israel and say to them

Numbers28:5 also a tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a grain offering
Numbers28:6 It is a regular burnt offering
Numbers28:7 Its drink offering shall be a quarter of a hin for each lamb. In the Holy Place you shall pour out a drink offering of strong drink to the LORD.
Numbers28:8 The other lamb you shall offer at twilight. Like the grain offering of the morning
Numbers28:9 On the Sabbath day
Numbers28:10 this is the burnt offering of every Sabbath
Numbers28:11 At the beginnings of your months
Numbers28:12 also three tenths of an ephah of fine flour for a grain offering
Numbers28:13 and a tenth of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for every lamb; for a burnt offering with a pleasing aroma

Numbers28:15 Also one male goat for a sin offering to the LORD; it shall be offered besides the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.
Numbers28:16 On the fourteenth day of the first month is the LORD's Passover
Numbers28:17 and on the fifteenth day of this month is a feast. Seven days shall unleavened bread be eaten.

Numbers28:19 but offer a food offering

Numbers28:23 You shall offer these besides the burnt offering of the morning
Numbers28:24 In the same way you shall offer daily
Numbers28:25 And on the seventh day you shall have a holy convocation. You shall not do any ordinary work.

Numbers28:27 but offer a burnt offering
Numbers28:28 also their grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers28:29 a tenth for each of the seven lambs;
Numbers28:30 with one male goat
Numbers28:31 Besides the regular burnt offering and its grain offering

2Samuel22:1 And David spoke to the LORD the words of this song on the day when the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies
2Samuel22:2 He said
2Samuel22:3 my God
2Samuel22:4 I call upon the LORD
2Samuel22:5 For the waves of death encompassed me
2Samuel22:6 the cords of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called upon the LORD; to my God I called. From his temple he heard my voice
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth reeled and rocked; the foundations of the heavens trembled and quaked
2Samuel22:9 Smoke went up from his nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He bowed the heavens and came down; thick darkness was under his feet.
2Samuel22:11 He rode on a cherub and flew; he was seen on the wings of the wind.
2Samuel22:12 He made darkness around him his canopy
2Samuel22:13 Out of the brightness before him coals of fire flamed forth.
2Samuel22:14 The LORD thundered from heaven
2Samuel22:15 And he sent out arrows and scattered them; lightning
2Samuel22:16 Then the channels of the sea were seen; the foundations of the world were laid bare
2Samuel22:17 He sent from on high
2Samuel22:18 He rescued me from my strong enemy
2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in the day of my calamity
2Samuel22:20 He brought me out into a broad place; he rescued me
2Samuel22:21 The LORD dealt with me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he rewarded me.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not wickedly departed from my God.
2Samuel22:23 For all his rules were before me
2Samuel22:24 I was blameless before him
2Samuel22:25 And the LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 With the merciful you show yourself merciful; with the blameless man you show yourself blameless;
2Samuel22:27 with the purified you deal purely
2Samuel22:28 You save a humble people
2Samuel22:29 For you are my lamp
2Samuel22:30 For by you I can run against a troop
2Samuel22:31 This God- his way is perfect; the word of the LORD proves true; he is a shield for all those who take refuge in him.
2Samuel22:32 For who is God
2Samuel22:33 This God is my strong refuge and has made my way blameless.
2Samuel22:34 He made my feet like the feet of a deer and set me secure on the heights.
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for war
2Samuel22:36 You have given me the shield of your salvation
2Samuel22:37 You gave a wide place for my steps under me
2Samuel22:38 I pursued my enemies and destroyed them
2Samuel22:39 I consumed them; I thrust them through
2Samuel22:40 For you equipped me with strength for the battle; you made those who rise against me sink under me.
2Samuel22:41 You made my enemies turn their backs to me
2Samuel22:42 They looked
2Samuel22:43 I beat them fine as the dust of the earth; I crushed them and stamped them down like the mire of the streets.
2Samuel22:44 You delivered me from strife with my people; you kept me as the head of the nations; people whom I had not known served me.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners came cringing to me; as soon as they heard of me
2Samuel22:46 Foreigners lost heart and came trembling out of their fortresses.
2Samuel22:47 The LORD lives
2Samuel22:48 the God who gave me vengeance and brought down peoples under me
2Samuel22:49 who brought me out from my enemies; you exalted me above those who rose against me; you delivered me from men of violence.
2Samuel22:50 For this I will praise you
2Samuel22:51 Great salvation he brings to his king
2Samuel23:1 Now these are the last words of David: The oracle of David
2Samuel23:2 The Spirit of the LORD speaks by me; his word is on my tongue.
2Samuel23:3 The God of Israel has spoken; the Rock of Israel has said to me: When one rules justly over men
2Samuel23:4 he dawns on them like the morning light
2Samuel23:5 For does not my house stand so with God? For he has made with me an everlasting covenant
2Samuel23:6 But worthless men are all like thorns that are thrown away
2Samuel23:7 but the man who touches them arms himself with iron and the shaft of a spear
2Samuel23:8 These are the names of the mighty men whom David had: Josheb-basshebeth a Tahchemonite; he was chief of the three. He wielded his spear against eight hundred whom he killed at one time.
2Samuel23:9 And next to him among the three mighty men was Eleazar the son of Dodo
2Samuel23:10 He rose and struck down the Philistines until his hand was weary
2Samuel23:11 And next to him was Shammah
2Samuel23:12 But he took his stand in the midst of the plot and defended it and struck down the Philistines
2Samuel23:13 And three of the thirty chief men went down and came about harvest time to David at the cave of Adullam
2Samuel23:14 David was then in the stronghold
2Samuel23:15 And David said longingly
2Samuel23:16 Then the three mighty men broke through the camp of the Philistines and drew water out of the well of Bethlehem that was by the gate and carried and brought it to David. But he would not drink of it. He poured it out to the LORD
2Samuel23:17 and said
2Samuel23:18 Now Abishai
2Samuel23:19 He was the most renowned of the thirty and became their commander
2Samuel23:20 And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was a valiant man of Kabzeel
2Samuel23:21 And he struck down an Egyptian
2Samuel23:22 These things did Benaiah the son of Jehoiada
2Samuel23:23 He was renowned among the thirty
2Samuel23:24 Asahel the brother of Joab was one of the thirty; Elhanan the son of Dodo of Bethlehem
2Samuel23:25 Shammah of Harod
2Samuel23:26 Helez the Paltite

2Samuel23:33 Shammah the Hararite
2Samuel23:34 Eliphelet the son of Ahasbai of Maacah
2Samuel23:35 Hezro of Carmel
2Samuel23:36 Igal the son of Nathan of Zobah
2Samuel23:37 Zelek the Ammonite
2Samuel23:38 Ira the Ithrite
2Samuel23:39 Uriah the Hittite: thirty-seven in all.

1Kings5:2 And Solomon sent word to Hiram
1Kings5:3 You know that David my father could not build a house for the name of the LORD his God because of the warfare with which his enemies surrounded him
1Kings5:4 But now the LORD my God has given me rest on every side. There is neither adversary nor misfortune.
1Kings5:5 And so I intend to build a house for the name of the LORD my God
1Kings5:6 Now therefore command that cedars of Lebanon be cut for me. And my servants will join your servants

1Kings6:1 In the four hundred and eightieth year after the people of Israel came out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 The house that King Solomon built for the LORD was sixty cubits long
1Kings6:3 The vestibule in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:4 And he made for the house windows with recessed frames.
1Kings6:5 He also built a structure against the wall of the house
1Kings6:6 The lowest story was five cubits broad
1Kings6:7 When the house was built
1Kings6:8 The entrance for the lowest story was on the south side of the house
1Kings6:9 So he built the house and finished it
1Kings6:10 He built the structure against the whole house
1Kings6:11 Now the word of the LORD came to Solomon
1Kings6:12 Concerning this house that you are building
1Kings6:13 And I will dwell among the children of Israel and will not forsake my people Israel.
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the house and finished it.
1Kings6:15 He lined the walls of the house on the inside with boards of cedar. From the floor of the house to the walls of the ceiling
1Kings6:16 He built twenty cubits of the rear of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the walls
1Kings6:17 The house
1Kings6:18 The cedar within the house was carved in the form of gourds and open flowers. All was cedar; no stone was seen.
1Kings6:19 The inner sanctuary he prepared in the innermost part of the house
1Kings6:20 The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:21 And Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold
1Kings6:22 And he overlaid the whole house with gold
1Kings6:23 In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olivewood
1Kings6:24 Five cubits was the length of one wing of the cherub
1Kings6:25 The other cherub also measured ten cubits; both cherubim had the same measure and the same form.
1Kings6:26 The height of one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 He put the cherubim in the innermost part of the house. And the wings of the cherubim were spread out so that a wing of one touched the one wall
1Kings6:28 And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 Around all the walls of the house he carved engraved figures of cherubim and palm trees and open flowers
1Kings6:30 The floor of the house he overlaid with gold in the inner and outer rooms.
1Kings6:31 For the entrance to the inner sanctuary he made doors of olivewood; the lintel and the doorposts were five-sided.
1Kings6:32 He covered the two doors of olivewood with carvings of cherubim
1Kings6:33 So also he made for the entrance to the nave doorposts of olivewood
1Kings6:34 and two doors of cypress wood. The two leaves of the one door were folding
1Kings6:35 On them he carved cherubim and palm trees and open flowers
1Kings6:36 He built the inner court with three courses of cut stone and one course of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the LORD was laid
1Kings6:38 And in the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 Solomon was building his own house thirteen years
1Kings7:2 He built the House of the Forest of Lebanon. Its length was a hundred cubits and its breadth fifty cubits and its height thirty cubits
1Kings7:3 And it was covered with cedar above the chambers that were on the forty-five pillars
1Kings7:4 There were window frames in three rows
1Kings7:5 All the doorways and windows had square frames
1Kings7:6 And he made the Hall of Pillars; its length was fifty cubits
1Kings7:7 And he made the Hall of the Throne where he was to pronounce judgment
1Kings7:8 His own house where he was to dwell
1Kings7:9 All these were made of costly stones
1Kings7:10 The foundation was of costly stones
1Kings7:11 And above were costly stones
1Kings7:12 The great court had three courses of cut stone all around
1Kings7:13 And King Solomon sent and brought Hiram from Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 He cast two pillars of bronze. Eighteen cubits was the height of one pillar
1Kings7:16 He also made two capitals of cast bronze to set on the tops of the pillars. The height of the one capital was five cubits
1Kings7:17 There were lattices of checker work with wreaths of chain work for the capitals on the tops of the pillars
1Kings7:18 Likewise he made pomegranates in two rows around the one latticework to cover the capital that was on the top of the pillar
1Kings7:19 Now the capitals that were on the tops of the pillars in the vestibule were of lily-work
1Kings7:20 The capitals were on the two pillars and also above the rounded projection which was beside the latticework. There were two hundred pomegranates in two rows all around
1Kings7:21 He set up the pillars at the vestibule of the temple. He set up the pillar on the south and called its name Jachin
1Kings7:22 And on the tops of the pillars was lily-work. Thus the work of the pillars was finished.
1Kings7:23 Then he made the sea of cast metal. It was round
1Kings7:24 Under its brim were gourds
1Kings7:25 It stood on twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 Its thickness was a handbreadth
1Kings7:27 He also made the ten stands of bronze. Each stand was four cubits long
1Kings7:28 This was the construction of the stands: they had panels
1Kings7:29 and on the panels that were set in the frames were lions
1Kings7:30 Moreover
1Kings7:31 Its opening was within a crown that projected upward one cubit. Its opening was round
1Kings7:32 And the four wheels were underneath the panels. The axles of the wheels were of one piece with the stands
1Kings7:33 The wheels were made like a chariot wheel; their axles
1Kings7:34 There were four supports at the four corners of each stand. The supports were of one piece with the stands.
1Kings7:35 And on the top of the stand there was a round band half a cubit high; and on the top of the stand its stays and its panels were of one piece with it.
1Kings7:36 And on the surfaces of its stays and on its panels
1Kings7:37 After this manner he made the ten stands. All of them were cast alike
1Kings7:38 And he made ten basins of bronze. Each basin held forty baths
1Kings7:39 And he set the stands
1Kings7:40 Hiram also made the pots
1Kings7:41 the two pillars
1Kings7:42 and the four hundred pomegranates for the two latticeworks
1Kings7:43 the ten stands
1Kings7:44 and the one sea
1Kings7:45 Now the pots
1Kings7:46 In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them
1Kings7:47 And Solomon left all the vessels unweighed
1Kings7:48 So Solomon made all the vessels that were in the house of the LORD: the golden altar
1Kings7:49 the lampstands of pure gold
1Kings7:50 the cups
1Kings7:51 Thus all the work that King Solomon did on the house of the LORD was finished. And Solomon brought in the things that David his father had dedicated

1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread out their wings over the place of the ark
1Kings8:8 And the poles were so long that the ends of the poles were seen from the Holy Place before the inner sanctuary; but they could not be seen from outside. And they are there to this day.
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets of stone that Moses put there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 And when the priests came out of the Holy Place
1Kings8:11 so that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud

1Kings8:15 And he said
1Kings8:16 'Since the day that I brought my people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 Now it was in the heart of David my father to build a house for the name of the LORD
1Kings8:18 But the LORD said to David my father
1Kings8:19 Nevertheless
1Kings8:20 Now the LORD has fulfilled his promise that he made. For I have risen in the place of David my father
1Kings8:21 And there I have provided a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in the presence of all the assembly of Israel and spread out his hands toward heaven
1Kings8:23 and said
1Kings8:24 who have kept with your servant David my father what you declared to him. You spoke with your mouth
1Kings8:25 Now therefore
1Kings8:26 Now therefore
1Kings8:27 But will God indeed dwell on the earth? Behold
1Kings8:28 Yet have regard to the prayer of your servant and to his plea
1Kings8:29 that your eyes may be open night and day toward this house
1Kings8:30 And listen to the plea of your servant and of your people Israel
1Kings8:31 If a man sins against his neighbor and is made to take an oath and comes and swears his oath before your altar in this house
1Kings8:32 then hear in heaven and act and judge your servants
1Kings8:33 When your people Israel are defeated before the enemy because they have sinned against you
1Kings8:34 then hear in heaven and forgive the sin of your people Israel and bring them again to the land that you gave to their fathers.
1Kings8:35 When heaven is shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against you
1Kings8:36 then hear in heaven and forgive the sin of your servants
1Kings8:37 If there is famine in the land
1Kings8:38 whatever prayer
1Kings8:39 then hear in heaven your dwelling place and forgive and act and render to each whose heart you know
1Kings8:40 that they may fear you all the days that they live in the land that you gave to our fathers.
1Kings8:41 Likewise
1Kings8:42 (for they shall hear of your great name and your mighty hand
1Kings8:43 hear in heaven your dwelling place and do according to all for which the foreigner calls to you
1Kings8:44 If your people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 then hear in heaven their prayer and their plea
1Kings8:46 If they sin against you- for there is no one who does not sin- and you are angry with them and give them to an enemy
1Kings8:47 yet if they turn their heart in the land to which they have been carried captive
1Kings8:48 if they repent with all their mind and with all their heart in the land of their enemies
1Kings8:49 then hear in heaven your dwelling place their prayer and their plea
1Kings8:50 and forgive your people who have sinned against you
1Kings8:51 (for they are your people
1Kings8:52 Let your eyes be open to the plea of your servant and to the plea of your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For you separated them from among all the peoples of the earth to be your heritage
1Kings8:54 Now as Solomon finished offering all this prayer and plea to the LORD
1Kings8:55 And he stood and blessed all the assembly of Israel with a loud voice
1Kings8:56 Blessed be the LORD who has given rest to his people Israel
1Kings8:57 The LORD our God be with us
1Kings8:58 that he may incline our hearts to him
1Kings8:59 Let these words of mine
1Kings8:60 that all the peoples of the earth may know that the LORD is God; there is no other.
1Kings8:61 Let your heart therefore be wholly true to the LORD our God
1Kings8:62 Then the king
1Kings8:63 Solomon offered as peace offerings to the LORD 22
1Kings8:64 The same day the king consecrated the middle of the court that was before the house of the LORD
1Kings8:65 So Solomon held the feast at that time
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away

1Kings10:16 King Solomon made 200 large shields of beaten gold; 600 shekels of gold went into each shield.
1Kings10:17 And he made 300 shields of beaten gold; three minas of gold went into each shield. And the king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 The king also made a great ivory throne and overlaid it with the finest gold.

1Kings10:20 while twelve lions stood there

2Kings23:22 For no such Passover had been kept since the days of the judges who judged Israel
2Kings23:23 But in the eighteenth year of King Josiah this Passover was kept to the LORD in Jerusalem.
2Kings23:24 Moreover
2Kings23:25 Before him there was no king like him
2Kings23:26 Still the LORD did not turn from the burning of his great wrath
2Kings23:27 And the LORD said
2Kings23:28 Now the rest of the acts of Josiah and all that he did
2Kings23:29 In his days Pharaoh Neco king of Egypt went up to the king of Assyria to the river Euphrates. King Josiah went to meet him
2Kings23:30 And his servants carried him dead in a chariot from Megiddo and brought him to Jerusalem and buried him in his own tomb. And the people of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah
2Kings23:31 Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he began to reign
2Kings23:32 And he did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings23:33 And Pharaoh Neco put him in bonds at Riblah in the land of Hamath
2Kings23:34 And Pharaoh Neco made Eliakim the son of Josiah king in the place of Josiah his father
2Kings23:35 And Jehoiakim gave the silver and the gold to Pharaoh
2Kings23:36 Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he began to reign
2Kings23:37 And he did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings24:1 In his days
2Kings24:2 And the LORD sent against him bands of the Chaldeans and bands of the Syrians and bands of the Moabites and bands of the Ammonites
2Kings24:3 Surely this came upon Judah at the command of the LORD
2Kings24:4 and also for the innocent blood that he had shed. For he filled Jerusalem with innocent blood
2Kings24:5 Now the rest of the deeds of Jehoiakim and all that he did
2Kings24:6 So Jehoiakim slept with his fathers
2Kings24:7 And the king of Egypt did not come again out of his land
2Kings24:8 Jehoiachin was eighteen years old when he became king
2Kings24:9 And he did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings24:10 At that time the servants of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up to Jerusalem
2Kings24:11 And Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came to the city while his servants were besieging it
2Kings24:12 and Jehoiachin the king of Judah gave himself up to the king of Babylon
2Kings24:13 and carried off all the treasures of the house of the LORD and the treasures of the king's house
2Kings24:14 He carried away all Jerusalem and all the officials and all the mighty men of valor
2Kings24:15 And he carried away Jehoiachin to Babylon. The king's mother
2Kings24:16 And the king of Babylon brought captive to Babylon all the men of valor
2Kings24:17 And the king of Babylon made Mattaniah
2Kings24:18 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
2Kings24:19 And he did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
2Kings24:20 For because of the anger of the LORD it came to the point in Jerusalem and Judah that he cast them out from his presence. And Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
2Kings25:1 And in the ninth year of his reign
2Kings25:2 So the city was besieged till the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
2Kings25:3 On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine was so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.
2Kings25:4 Then a breach was made in the city
2Kings25:5 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king and overtook him in the plains of Jericho
2Kings25:6 Then they captured the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah
2Kings25:7 They slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
2Kings25:8 In the fifth month
2Kings25:9 And he burned the house of the LORD and the king's house and all the houses of Jerusalem; every great house he burned down.
2Kings25:10 And all the army of the Chaldeans
2Kings25:11 And the rest of the people who were left in the city and the deserters who had deserted to the king of Babylon
2Kings25:12 But the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and plowmen.
2Kings25:13 And the pillars of bronze that were in the house of the LORD
2Kings25:14 And they took away the pots and the shovels and the snuffers and the dishes for incense and all the vessels of bronze used in the temple service
2Kings25:15 the fire pans also and the bowls. What was of gold the captain of the guard took away as gold
2Kings25:16 As for the two pillars
2Kings25:17 The height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits
2Kings25:18 And the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest and Zephaniah the second priest and the three keepers of the threshold
2Kings25:19 and from the city he took an officer who had been in command of the men of war
2Kings25:20 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
2Kings25:21 And the king of Babylon struck them down and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. So Judah was taken into exile out of its land.
2Kings25:22 And over the people who remained in the land of Judah
2Kings25:23 Now when all the captains and their men heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah governor
2Kings25:24 And Gedaliah swore to them and their men
2Kings25:25 But in the seventh month
2Kings25:26 Then all the people
2Kings25:27 And in the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Jehoiachin king of Judah
2Kings25:28 And he spoke kindly to him and gave him a seat above the seats of the kings who were with him in Babylon.
2Kings25:29 So Jehoiachin put off his prison garments. And every day of his life he dined regularly at the king's table
2Kings25:30 and for his allowance

Ezra4:1 Now when the adversaries of Judah and Benjamin heard that the returned exiles were building a temple to the LORD
Ezra4:2 they approached Zerubbabel and the heads of fathers' houses and said to them
Ezra4:3 But Zerubbabel
Ezra4:4 Then the people of the land discouraged the people of Judah and made them afraid to build
Ezra4:5 and bribed counselors against them to frustrate their purpose
Ezra4:6 And in the reign of Ahasuerus
Ezra4:7 In the days of Artaxerxes
Ezra4:8 Rehum the commander and Shimshai the scribe wrote a letter against Jerusalem to Artaxerxes the king as follows:
Ezra4:9 Rehum the commander
Ezra4:10 and the rest of the nations whom the great and noble Osnappar deported and settled in the cities of Samaria and in the rest of the province Beyond the River.
Ezra4:11 (This is a copy of the letter that they sent.) To Artaxerxes the king: Your servants
Ezra4:12 be it known to the king that the Jews who came up from you to us have gone to Jerusalem. They are rebuilding that rebellious and wicked city. They are finishing the walls and repairing the foundations.
Ezra4:13 Now be it known to the king that if this city is rebuilt and the walls finished
Ezra4:14 Now because we eat the salt of the palace and it is not fitting for us to witness the king's dishonor
Ezra4:15 in order that search may be made in the book of the records of your fathers. You will find in the book of the records and learn that this city is a rebellious city
Ezra4:16 We make known to the king that if this city is rebuilt and its walls finished
Ezra4:17 The king sent an answer: To Rehum the commander and Shimshai the scribe and the rest of their associates who live in Samaria and in the rest of the province Beyond the River
Ezra4:18 the letter that you sent to us has been plainly read before me.
Ezra4:19 And I made a decree
Ezra4:20 And mighty kings have been over Jerusalem
Ezra4:21 Therefore make a decree that these men be made to cease
Ezra4:22 And take care not to be slack in this matter. Why should damage grow to the hurt of the king?
Ezra4:23 Then
Ezra4:24 Then the work on the house of God that is in Jerusalem stopped

Nehemiah4:16 From that day on
Nehemiah4:17 who were building on the wall. Those who carried burdens were loaded in such a way that each labored on the work with one hand and held his weapon with the other.

Nehemiah8:12 And all the people went their way to eat and drink and to send portions and to make great rejoicing
Nehemiah8:13 On the second day the heads of fathers' houses of all the people
Nehemiah8:14 And they found it written in the Law that the LORD had commanded by Moses that the people of Israel should dwell in booths during the feast of the seventh month
Nehemiah8:15 and that they should proclaim it and publish it in all their towns and in Jerusalem
Nehemiah8:16 So the people went out and brought them and made booths for themselves
Nehemiah8:17 And all the assembly of those who had returned from the captivity made booths and lived in the booths

Nehemiah9:1 Now on the twenty-fourth day of this month the people of Israel were assembled with fasting and in sackcloth
Nehemiah9:2 And the Israelites separated themselves from all foreigners and stood and confessed their sins and the iniquities of their fathers.
Nehemiah9:3 And they stood up in their place and read from the Book of the Law of the LORD their God for a quarter of the day; for another quarter of it they made confession and worshiped the LORD their God.
Nehemiah9:4 On the stairs of the Levites stood Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 Then the Levites
Nehemiah9:6 You are the LORD
Nehemiah9:7 You are the LORD
Nehemiah9:8 You found his heart faithful before you
Nehemiah9:9 And you saw the affliction of our fathers in Egypt and heard their cry at the Red Sea
Nehemiah9:10 and performed signs and wonders against Pharaoh and all his servants and all the people of his land
Nehemiah9:11 And you divided the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 By a pillar of cloud you led them in the day
Nehemiah9:13 You came down on Mount Sinai and spoke with them from heaven and gave them right rules and true laws
Nehemiah9:14 and you made known to them your holy Sabbath and commanded them commandments and statutes and a law by Moses your servant.
Nehemiah9:15 You gave them bread from heaven for their hunger and brought water for them out of the rock for their thirst
Nehemiah9:16 But they and our fathers acted presumptuously and stiffened their neck and did not obey your commandments.
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to obey and were not mindful of the wonders that you performed among them
Nehemiah9:18 Even when they had made for themselves a golden calf and said
Nehemiah9:19 you in your great mercies did not forsake them in the wilderness. The pillar of cloud to lead them in the way did not depart from them by day
Nehemiah9:20 You gave your good Spirit to instruct them and did not withhold your manna from their mouth and gave them water for their thirst.
Nehemiah9:21 Forty years you sustained them in the wilderness
Nehemiah9:22 And you gave them kingdoms and peoples and allotted to them every corner. So they took possession of the land of Sihon king of Heshbon and the land of Og king of Bashan.
Nehemiah9:23 You multiplied their children as the stars of heaven
Nehemiah9:24 So the descendants went in and possessed the land
Nehemiah9:25 And they captured fortified cities and a rich land
Nehemiah9:26 Nevertheless
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore you gave them into the hand of their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 But after they had rest they did evil again before you
Nehemiah9:29 And you warned them in order to turn them back to your law. Yet they acted presumptuously and did not obey your commandments
Nehemiah9:30 Many years you bore with them and warned them by your Spirit through your prophets. Yet they would not give ear. Therefore you gave them into the hand of the peoples of the lands.
Nehemiah9:31 Nevertheless
Nehemiah9:32 Now
Nehemiah9:33 Yet you have been righteous in all that has come upon us
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 Even in their own kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 Behold
Nehemiah9:37 And its rich yield goes to the kings whom you have set over us because of our sins. They rule over our bodies and over our livestock as they please
Nehemiah9:38 Because of all this we make a firm covenant in writing; on the sealed document are the names of our princes

Esther1:1 Now in the days of Ahasuerus
Esther1:2 in those days when King Ahasuerus sat on his royal throne in Susa
Esther1:3 in the third year of his reign he gave a feast for all his officials and servants. The army of Persia and Media and the nobles and governors of the provinces were before him
Esther1:4 while he showed the riches of his royal glory and the splendor and pomp of his greatness for many days
Esther1:5 And when these days were completed
Esther1:6 There were white cotton curtains and violet hangings fastened with cords of fine linen and purple to silver rods and marble pillars
Esther1:7 Drinks were served in golden vessels
Esther1:8 And drinking was according to this edict: There is no compulsion. For the king had given orders to all the staff of his palace to do as each man desired.
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti also gave a feast for the women in the palace that belonged to King Ahasuerus.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti before the king with her royal crown
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king's command delivered by the eunuchs. At this the king became enraged
Esther1:13 Then the king said to the wise men who knew the times (for this was the king's procedure toward all who were versed in law and judgment
Esther1:14 the men next to him being Carshena
Esther1:15 According to the law
Esther1:16 Then Memucan said in the presence of the king and the officials
Esther1:17 For the queen's behavior will be made known to all women
Esther1:18 This very day the noble women of Persia and Media who have heard of the queen's behavior will say the same to all the king's officials
Esther1:19 If it please the king
Esther1:20 So when the decree made by the king is proclaimed throughout all his kingdom
Esther1:21 This advice pleased the king and the princes
Esther1:22 He sent letters to all the royal provinces

Esther2:4 And let the young woman who pleases the king be queen instead of Vashti. This pleased the king
Esther2:5 Now there was a Jew in Susa the citadel whose name was Mordecai
Esther2:6 who had been carried away from Jerusalem among the captives carried away with Jeconiah king of Judah
Esther2:7 He was bringing up Hadassah
