Job1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job
Job1:2 There were born to him seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 He possessed 7
Job1:4 His sons used to go and hold a feast in the house of each one on his day
Job1:5 And when the days of the feast had run their course
Job1:6 Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD
Job1:7 The LORD said to Satan
Job1:8 And the LORD said to Satan
Job1:9 Then Satan answered the LORD and said
Job1:10 Have you not put a hedge around him and his house and all that he has
Job1:11 But stretch out your hand and touch all that he has
Job1:12 And the LORD said to Satan
Job1:13 Now there was a day when his sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother's house
Job1:14 and there came a messenger to Job and said
Job1:15 and the Sabeans fell upon them and took them and struck down the servants with the edge of the sword
Job1:16 While he was yet speaking
Job1:17 While he was yet speaking
Job1:18 While he was yet speaking
Job1:19 and behold
Job1:20 Then Job arose and tore his robe and shaved his head and fell on the ground and worshiped.
Job1:21 And he said
Job1:22 In all this Job did not sin or charge God with wrong.
Job2:1 Again there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD
Job2:2 And the LORD said to Satan
Job2:3 And the LORD said to Satan
Job2:4 Then Satan answered the LORD and said
Job2:5 But stretch out your hand and touch his bone and his flesh
Job2:6 And the LORD said to Satan
Job2:7 So Satan went out from the presence of the LORD and struck Job with loathsome sores from the sole of his foot to the crown of his head.
Job2:8 And he took a piece of broken pottery with which to scrape himself while he sat in the ashes.
Job2:9 Then his wife said to him
Job2:10 But he said to her
Job2:11 Now when Job's three friends heard of all this evil that had come upon him
Job2:12 And when they saw him from a distance
Job2:13 And they sat with him on the ground seven days and seven nights
Job3:1 After this Job opened his mouth and cursed the day of his birth.
Job3:2 And Job said:
Job3:3 Let the day perish on which I was born
Job3:4 Let that day be darkness! May God above not seek it
Job3:5 Let gloom and deep darkness claim it. Let clouds dwell upon it; let the blackness of the day terrify it.
Job3:6 That night- let thick darkness seize it! Let it not rejoice among the days of the year; let it not come into the number of the months.
Job3:7 Behold
Job3:8 Let those curse it who curse the day
Job3:9 Let the stars of its dawn be dark; let it hope for light
Job3:10 because it did not shut the doors of my mother's womb
Job3:11 Why did I not die at birth
Job3:12 Why did the knees receive me? Or why the breasts
Job3:13 For then I would have lain down and been quiet; I would have slept; then I would have been at rest
Job3:14 with kings and counselors of the earth who rebuilt ruins for themselves
Job3:15 or with princes who had gold
Job3:16 Or why was I not as a hidden stillborn child
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from troubling
Job3:18 There the prisoners are at ease together; they hear not the voice of the taskmaster.
Job3:19 The small and the great are there
Job3:20 Why is light given to him who is in misery
Job3:21 who long for death
Job3:22 who rejoice exceedingly and are glad when they find the grave?
Job3:23 Why is light given to a man whose way is hidden
Job3:24 For my sighing comes instead of my bread
Job3:25 For the thing that I fear comes upon me
Job3:26 I am not at ease
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
Job4:2 If one ventures a word with you
Job4:3 Behold
Job4:4 Your words have upheld him who was stumbling
Job4:5 But now it has come to you
Job4:6 Is not your fear of God your confidence
Job4:7 Remember: who that was innocent ever perished? Or where were the upright cut off?
Job4:8 As I have seen
Job4:9 By the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 The roar of the lion
Job4:11 The strong lion perishes for lack of prey
Job4:12 Now a word was brought to me stealthily; my ear received the whisper of it.
Job4:13 Amid thoughts from visions of the night
Job4:14 dread came upon me
Job4:15 A spirit glided past my face; the hair of my flesh stood up.
Job4:16 It stood still
Job4:17 'Can mortal man be in the right before God? Can a man be pure before his Maker?
Job4:18 Even in his servants he puts no trust
Job4:19 how much more those who dwell in houses of clay
Job4:20 Between morning and evening they are beaten to pieces; they perish forever without anyone regarding it.
Job4:21 Is not their tent-cord plucked up within them
Job5:1 Call now; is there anyone who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
Job5:2 Surely vexation kills the fool
Job5:3 I have seen the fool taking root
Job5:4 His children are far from safety; they are crushed in the gate
Job5:5 The hungry eat his harvest
Job5:6 For affliction does not come from the dust
Job5:7 but man is born to trouble as the sparks fly upward.
Job5:8 As for me
Job5:9 who does great things and unsearchable
Job5:10 he gives rain on the earth and sends waters on the fields;
Job5:11 he sets on high those who are lowly
Job5:12 He frustrates the devices of the crafty
Job5:13 He catches the wise in their own craftiness
Job5:14 They meet with darkness in the daytime and grope at noonday as in the night.
Job5:15 But he saves the needy from the sword of their mouth and from the hand of the mighty.
Job5:16 So the poor have hope
Job5:17 Behold
Job5:18 For he wounds
Job5:19 He will deliver you from six troubles; in seven no evil shall touch you.
Job5:20 In famine he will redeem you from death
Job5:21 You shall be hidden from the lash of the tongue
Job5:22 At destruction and famine you shall laugh
Job5:23 For you shall be in league with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You shall know that your tent is at peace
Job5:25 You shall know also that your offspring shall be many
Job5:26 You shall come to your grave in ripe old age
Job5:27 Behold
Job6:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job6:2 Oh that my vexation were weighed
Job6:3 For then it would be heavier than the sand of the sea; therefore my words have been rash.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are in me; my spirit drinks their poison; the terrors of God are arrayed against me.
Job6:5 Does the wild donkey bray when he has grass
Job6:6 Can that which is tasteless be eaten without salt
Job6:7 My appetite refuses to touch them; they are as food that is loathsome to me.
Job6:8 Oh that I might have my request
Job6:9 that it would please God to crush me
Job6:10 This would be my comfort; I would even exult in pain unsparing
Job6:11 What is my strength
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones
Job6:13 Have I any help in me
Job6:14 He who withholds kindness from a friend forsakes the fear of the Almighty.
Job6:15 My brothers are treacherous as a torrent-bed
Job6:16 which are dark with ice
Job6:17 When they melt
Job6:18 The caravans turn aside from their course; they go up into the waste and perish.
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema look
Job6:20 They are ashamed because they were confident; they come there and are disappointed.
Job6:21 For you have now become nothing; you see my calamity and are afraid.
Job6:22 Have I said
Job6:23 Or
Job6:24 Teach me
Job6:25 How forceful are upright words! But what does reproof from you reprove?
Job6:26 Do you think that you can reprove words
Job6:27 You would even cast lots over the fatherless
Job6:28 But now
Job6:29 Please turn; let no injustice be done. Turn now; my vindication is at stake.
Job6:30 Is there any injustice on my tongue? Cannot my palate discern the cause of calamity?
Job7:1 Has not man a hard service on earth
Job7:2 Like a slave who longs for the shadow
Job7:3 so I am allotted months of emptiness
Job7:4 When I lie down I say
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms and dirt; my skin hardens
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle and come to their end without hope.
Job7:7 Remember that my life is a breath; my eye will never again see good.
Job7:8 The eye of him who sees me will behold me no more; while your eyes are on me
Job7:9 As the cloud fades and vanishes
Job7:10 he returns no more to his house
Job7:11 Therefore I will not restrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
Job7:12 Am I the sea
Job7:13 When I say
Job7:14 then you scare me with dreams and terrify me with visions
Job7:15 so that I would choose strangling and death rather than my bones.
Job7:16 I loathe my life; I would not live forever. Leave me alone
Job7:17 What is man
Job7:18 visit him every morning and test him every moment?
Job7:19 How long will you not look away from me
Job7:20 If I sin
Job7:21 Why do you not pardon my transgression and take away my iniquity? For now I shall lie in the earth; you will seek me
Job8:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
Job8:2 How long will you say these things
Job8:3 Does God pervert justice? Or does the Almighty pervert the right?
Job8:4 If your children have sinned against him
Job8:5 If you will seek God and plead with the Almighty for mercy
Job8:6 if you are pure and upright
Job8:7 And though your beginning was small
Job8:8 For inquire
Job8:9 For we are but of yesterday and know nothing
Job8:10 Will they not teach you and tell you and utter words out of their understanding?
Job8:11 Can papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Can reeds flourish where there is no water?
Job8:12 While yet in flower and not cut down
Job8:13 Such are the paths of all who forget God; the hope of the godless shall perish.
Job8:14 His confidence is severed
Job8:15 He leans against his house
Job8:16 He is a lush plant before the sun
Job8:17 His roots entwine the stone heap; he looks upon a house of stones.
Job8:18 If he is destroyed from his place
Job8:19 Behold
Job8:20 Behold
Job8:21 He will yet fill your mouth with laughter
Job8:22 Those who hate you will be clothed with shame
Job9:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job9:2 Truly I know that it is so: But how can a man be in the right before God?
Job9:3 If one wished to contend with him
Job9:4 He is wise in heart and mighty in strength- who has hardened himself against him
Job9:5 he who removes mountains
Job9:6 who shakes the earth out of its place
Job9:7 who commands the sun
Job9:8 who alone stretched out the heavens and trampled the waves of the sea;
Job9:9 who made the Bear and Orion
Job9:10 who does great things beyond searching out
Job9:11 Behold
Job9:12 Behold
Job9:13 God will not turn back his anger; beneath him bowed the helpers of Rahab.
Job9:14 How then can I answer him
Job9:15 Though I am in the right
Job9:16 If I summoned him and he answered me
Job9:17 For he crushes me with a tempest and multiplies my wounds without cause;
Job9:18 he will not let me get my breath
Job9:19 If it is a contest of strength
Job9:20 Though I am in the right
Job9:21 I am blameless; I regard not myself; I loathe my life.
Job9:22 It is all one; therefore I say
Job9:23 When disaster brings sudden death
Job9:24 The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; he covers the faces of its judges- if it is not he
Job9:25 My days are swifter than a runner; they flee away; they see no good.
Job9:26 They go by like skiffs of reed
Job9:27 If I say
Job9:28 I become afraid of all my suffering
Job9:29 I shall be condemned; why then do I labor in vain?
Job9:30 If I wash myself with snow and cleanse my hands with lye
Job9:31 yet you will plunge me into a pit
Job9:32 For he is not a man
Job9:33 There is no arbiter between us
Job9:34 Let him take his rod away from me
Job9:35 Then I would speak without fear of him
Job10:1 I loathe my life; I will give free utterance to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say to God
Job10:3 Does it seem good to you to oppress
Job10:4 Have you eyes of flesh? Do you see as man sees?
Job10:5 Are your days as the days of man
Job10:6 that you seek out my iniquity and search for my sin
Job10:7 although you know that I am not guilty
Job10:8 Your hands fashioned and made me
Job10:9 Remember that you have made me like clay; and will you return me to the dust?
Job10:10 Did you not pour me out like milk and curdle me like cheese?
Job10:11 You clothed me with skin and flesh
Job10:12 You have granted me life and steadfast love
Job10:13 Yet these things you hid in your heart; I know that this was your purpose.
Job10:14 If I sin
Job10:15 If I am guilty
Job10:16 And were my head lifted up
Job10:17 You renew your witnesses against me and increase your vexation toward me; you bring fresh troops against me.
Job10:18 Why did you bring me out from the womb? Would that I had died before any eye had seen me
Job10:19 and were as though I had not been
Job10:20 Are not my days few? Then cease
Job10:21 before I go- and I shall not return- to the land of darkness and deep shadow
Job10:22 the land of gloom like thick darkness
Job11:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
Job11:2 Should a multitude of words go unanswered
Job11:3 Should your babble silence men
Job11:4 For you say
Job11:5 But oh
Job11:6 and that he would tell you the secrets of wisdom! For he is manifold in understanding. Know then that God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves.
Job11:7 Can you find out the deep things of God? Can you find out the limit of the Almighty?
Job11:8 It is higher than heaven- what can you do? Deeper than Sheol- what can you know?
Job11:9 Its measure is longer than the earth and broader than the sea.
Job11:10 If he passes through and imprisons and summons the court
Job11:11 For he knows worthless men; when he sees iniquity
Job11:12 But a stupid man will get understanding when a wild donkey's colt is born a man!
Job11:13 If you prepare your heart
Job11:14 If iniquity is in your hand
Job11:15 Surely then you will lift up your face without blemish; you will be secure and will not fear.
Job11:16 You will forget your misery; you will remember it as waters that have passed away.
Job11:17 And your life will be brighter than the noonday; its darkness will be like the morning.
Job11:18 And you will feel secure
Job11:19 You will lie down
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked will fail; all way of escape will be lost to them
Job12:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job12:2 No doubt you are the people
Job12:3 But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?
Job12:4 I am a laughingstock to my friends; I
Job12:5 In the thought of one who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune; it is ready for those whose feet slip.
Job12:6 The tents of robbers are at peace
Job12:7 But ask the beasts
Job12:8 or the bushes of the earth
Job12:9 Who among all these does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?
Job12:10 In his hand is the life of every living thing and the breath of all mankind.
Job12:11 Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
Job12:12 Wisdom is with the aged
Job12:13 With God are wisdom and might; he has counsel and understanding.
Job12:14 If he tears down
Job12:15 If he withholds the waters
Job12:16 With him are strength and sound wisdom; the deceived and the deceiver are his.
Job12:17 He leads counselors away stripped
Job12:18 He looses the bonds of kings and binds a waistcloth on their hips.
Job12:19 He leads priests away stripped and overthrows the mighty.
Job12:20 He deprives of speech those who are trusted and takes away the discernment of the elders.
Job12:21 He pours contempt on princes and loosens the belt of the strong.
Job12:22 He uncovers the deeps out of darkness and brings deep darkness to light.
Job12:23 He makes nations great
Job12:24 He takes away understanding from the chiefs of the people of the earth and makes them wander in a pathless waste.
Job12:25 They grope in the dark without light
Job13:1 Behold
Job13:2 What you know
Job13:3 But I would speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 As for you
Job13:5 Oh that you would keep silent
Job13:6 Hear now my argument and listen to the pleadings of my lips.
Job13:7 Will you speak falsely for God and speak deceitfully for him?
Job13:8 Will you show partiality toward him? Will you plead the case for God?
Job13:9 Will it be well with you when he searches you out? Or can you deceive him
Job13:10 He will surely rebuke you if in secret you show partiality.
Job13:11 Will not his majesty terrify you
Job13:12 Your maxims are proverbs of ashes; your defenses are defenses of clay.
Job13:13 Let me have silence
Job13:14 Why should I take my flesh in my teeth and put my life in my hand?
Job13:15 Though he slay me
Job13:16 This will be my salvation
Job13:17 Keep listening to my words
Job13:18 Behold
Job13:19 Who is there who will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
Job13:20 Only grant me two things
Job13:21 withdraw your hand far from me
Job13:22 Then call
Job13:23 How many are my iniquities and my sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.
Job13:24 Why do you hide your face and count me as your enemy?
Job13:25 Will you frighten a driven leaf and pursue dry chaff?
Job13:26 For you write bitter things against me and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
Job13:27 You put my feet in the stocks and watch all my paths; you set a limit for the soles of my feet.
Job13:28 Man wastes away like a rotten thing
Job14:1 Man who is born of a woman is few of days and full of trouble.
Job14:2 He comes out like a flower and withers; he flees like a shadow and continues not.
Job14:3 And do you open your eyes on such a one and bring me into judgment with you?
Job14:4 Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? There is not one.
Job14:5 Since his days are determined
Job14:6 look away from him and leave him alone
Job14:7 For there is hope for a tree
Job14:8 Though its root grow old in the earth
Job14:9 yet at the scent of water it will bud and put out branches like a young plant.
Job14:10 But a man dies and is laid low; man breathes his last
Job14:11 As waters fail from a lake and a river wastes away and dries up
Job14:12 so a man lies down and rises not again; till the heavens are no more he will not awake or be roused out of his sleep.
Job14:13 Oh that you would hide me in Sheol
Job14:14 If a man dies
Job14:15 You would call
Job14:16 For then you would number my steps; you would not keep watch over my sin;
Job14:17 my transgression would be sealed up in a bag
Job14:18 But the mountain falls and crumbles away
Job14:19 the waters wear away the stones; the torrents wash away the soil of the earth; so you destroy the hope of man.
Job14:20 You prevail forever against him
Job14:21 His sons come to honor
Job14:22 He feels only the pain of his own body
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
Job15:2 Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge
Job15:3 Should he argue in unprofitable talk
Job15:4 But you are doing away with the fear of God and hindering meditation before God.
Job15:5 For your iniquity teaches your mouth
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 Are you the first man who was born? Or were you brought forth before the hills?
Job15:8 Have you listened in the council of God? And do you limit wisdom to yourself?
Job15:9 What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that is not clear to us?
Job15:10 Both the gray-haired and the aged are among us
Job15:11 Are the comforts of God too small for you
Job15:12 Why does your heart carry you away
Job15:13 that you turn your spirit against God and bring such words out of your mouth?
Job15:14 What is man
Job15:15 Behold
Job15:16 how much less one who is abominable and corrupt
Job15:17 I will show you; hear me
Job15:18 (what wise men have told
Job15:19 to whom alone the land was given
Job15:20 The wicked man writhes in pain all his days
Job15:21 Dreadful sounds are in his ears; in prosperity the destroyer will come upon him.
Job15:22 He does not believe that he will return out of darkness
Job15:23 He wanders abroad for bread
Job15:24 distress and anguish terrify him; they prevail against him
Job15:25 Because he has stretched out his hand against God and defies the Almighty
Job15:26 running stubbornly against him with a thickly bossed shield;
Job15:27 because he has covered his face with his fat and gathered fat upon his waist
Job15:28 and has lived in desolate cities
Job15:29 he will not be rich
Job15:30 he will not depart from darkness; the flame will dry up his shoots
Job15:31 Let him not trust in emptiness
Job15:32 It will be paid in full before his time
Job15:33 He will shake off his unripe grape like the vine
Job15:34 For the company of the godless is barren
Job15:35 They conceive trouble and give birth to evil
Job16:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job16:2 I have heard many such things; miserable comforters are you all.
Job16:3 Shall windy words have an end? Or what provokes you that you answer?
Job16:4 I also could speak as you do
Job16:5 I could strengthen you with my mouth
Job16:6 If I speak
Job16:7 Surely now God has worn me out; he has made desolate all my company.
Job16:8 And he has shriveled me up
Job16:9 He has torn me in his wrath and hated me; he has gnashed his teeth at me; my adversary sharpens his eyes against me.
Job16:10 Men have gaped at me with their mouth; they have struck me insolently on the cheek; they mass themselves together against me.
Job16:11 God gives me up to the ungodly and casts me into the hands of the wicked.
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 his archers surround me. He slashes open my kidneys and does not spare; he pours out my gall on the ground.
Job16:14 He breaks me with breach upon breach; he runs upon me like a warrior.
Job16:15 I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin and have laid my strength in the dust.
Job16:16 My face is red with weeping
Job16:17 although there is no violence in my hands
Job16:18 O earth
Job16:19 Even now
Job16:20 My friends scorn me; my eye pours out tears to God
Job16:21 that he would argue the case of a man with God
Job16:22 For when a few years have come I shall go the way from which I shall not return.

Job17:2 Surely there are mockers about me
Job17:3 Lay down a pledge for me with yourself; who is there who will put up security for me?
Job17:4 Since you have closed their hearts to understanding
Job17:5 He who informs against his friends to get a share of their property- the eyes of his children will fail.
Job17:6 He has made me a byword of the peoples
Job17:7 My eye has grown dim from vexation
Job17:8 The upright are appalled at this
Job17:9 Yet the righteous holds to his way
Job17:10 But you

Job17:16 Will it go down to the bars of Sheol? Shall we descend together into the dust?

Job18:2 How long will you hunt for words? Consider
Job18:3 Why are we counted as cattle? Why are we stupid in your sight?
Job18:4 You who tear yourself in your anger

Job18:6 The light is dark in his tent
Job18:7 His strong steps are shortened

Job18:12 His strength is famished
Job18:13 It consumes the parts of his skin; the firstborn of death consumes his limbs.

Job19:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job19:2 How long will you torment me and break me in pieces with words?
Job19:3 These ten times you have cast reproach upon me; are you not ashamed to wrong me?
Job19:4 And even if it be true that I have erred
Job19:5 If indeed you magnify yourselves against me and make my disgrace an argument against me
Job19:6 know then that God has put me in the wrong and closed his net about me.
Job19:7 Behold
Job19:8 He has walled up my way
Job19:9 He has stripped from me my glory and taken the crown from my head.
Job19:10 He breaks me down on every side
Job19:11 He has kindled his wrath against me and counts me as his adversary.
Job19:12 His troops come on together; they have cast up their siege ramp against me and encamp around my tent.
Job19:13 He has put my brothers far from me
Job19:14 My relatives have failed me
Job19:15 The guests in my house and my maidservants count me as a stranger; I have become a foreigner in their eyes.

Job19:17 My breath is strange to my wife
Job19:18 Even young children despise me; when I rise they talk against me.
Job19:19 All my intimate friends abhor me
Job19:20 My bones stick to my skin and to my flesh
Job19:21 Have mercy on me

Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
Job20:2 Therefore my thoughts answer me
Job20:3 I hear censure that insults me
Job20:4 Do you not know this from of old
Job20:5 that the exulting of the wicked is short
Job20:6 Though his height mount up to the heavens
Job20:7 he will perish forever like his own dung; those who have seen him will say
Job20:8 He will fly away like a dream and not be found; he will be chased away like a vision of the night.
Job20:9 The eye that saw him will see him no more
Job20:10 His children will seek the favor of the poor
Job20:11 His bones are full of his youthful vigor
Job20:12 Though evil is sweet in his mouth
Job20:13 though he is loath to let it go and holds it in his mouth
Job20:14 yet his food is turned in his stomach; it is the venom of cobras within him.
Job20:15 He swallows down riches and vomits them up again; God casts them out of his belly.
Job20:16 He will suck the poison of cobras; the tongue of a viper will kill him.
Job20:17 He will not look upon the rivers
Job20:18 He will give back the fruit of his toil and will not swallow it down; from the profit of his trading he will get no enjoyment.
Job20:19 For he has crushed and abandoned the poor; he has seized a house that he did not build.
Job20:20 Because he knew no contentment in his belly
Job20:21 There was nothing left after he had eaten; therefore his prosperity will not endure.
Job20:22 In the fullness of his sufficiency he will be in distress; the hand of everyone in misery will come against him.
Job20:23 To fill his belly to the full God will send his burning anger against him and rain it upon him into his body.
Job20:24 He will flee from an iron weapon; a bronze arrow will strike him through.
Job20:25 It is drawn forth and comes out of his body; the glittering point comes out of his gallbladder; terrors come upon him.
Job20:26 Utter darkness is laid up for his treasures; a fire not fanned will devour him; what is left in his tent will be consumed.
Job20:27 The heavens will reveal his iniquity
Job20:28 The possessions of his house will be carried away
Job20:29 This is the wicked man's portion from God
Job21:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job21:2 Keep listening to my words
Job21:3 Bear with me
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Look at me and be appalled
Job21:6 When I remember I am dismayed
Job21:7 Why do the wicked live
Job21:8 Their offspring are established in their presence
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from fear
Job21:10 Their bull breeds without fail; their cow calves and does not miscarry.
Job21:11 They send out their little boys like a flock
Job21:12 They sing to the tambourine and the lyre and rejoice to the sound of the pipe.
Job21:13 They spend their days in prosperity
Job21:14 They say to God
Job21:15 What is the Almighty
Job21:16 Behold
Job21:17 How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out? That their calamity comes upon them? That God distributes pains in his anger?
Job21:18 That they are like straw before the wind
Job21:19 You say
Job21:20 Let their own eyes see their destruction
Job21:21 For what do they care for their houses after them
Job21:22 Will any teach God knowledge
Job21:23 One dies in his full vigor
Job21:24 his pails full of milk and the marrow of his bones moist.
Job21:25 Another dies in bitterness of soul
Job21:26 They lie down alike in the dust
Job21:27 Behold
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you not asked those who travel the roads
Job21:30 that the evil man is spared in the day of calamity
Job21:31 Who declares his way to his face
Job21:32 When he is carried to the grave
Job21:33 The clods of the valley are sweet to him; all mankind follows after him
Job21:34 How then will you comfort me with empty nothings? There is nothing left of your answers but falsehood.
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
Job22:2 Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.
Job22:3 Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if you are in the right
Job22:4 Is it for your fear of him that he reproves you and enters into judgment with you?
Job22:5 Is not your evil abundant? There is no end to your iniquities.
Job22:6 For you have exacted pledges of your brothers for nothing and stripped the naked of their clothing.
Job22:7 You have given no water to the weary to drink
Job22:8 The man with power possessed the land
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty
Job22:10 Therefore snares are all around you
Job22:11 or darkness
Job22:12 Is not God high in the heavens? See the highest stars
Job22:13 But you say
Job22:14 Thick clouds veil him
Job22:15 Will you keep to the old way that wicked men have trod?
Job22:16 They were snatched away before their time; their foundation was washed away.
Job22:17 They said to God
Job22:18 Yet he filled their houses with good things- but the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
Job22:19 The righteous see it and are glad; the innocent one mocks at them
Job22:20 saying
Job22:21 Agree with God
Job22:22 Receive instruction from his mouth
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty you will be built up; if you remove injustice far from your tents
Job22:24 if you lay gold in the dust
Job22:25 then the Almighty will be your gold and your precious silver.
Job22:26 For then you will delight yourself in the Almighty and lift up your face to God.
Job22:27 You will make your prayer to him
Job22:28 You will decide on a matter
Job22:29 For when they are humbled you say
Job22:30 He delivers even the one who is not innocent
Job23:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job23:2 Today also my complaint is bitter; my hand is heavy on account of my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh
Job23:4 I would lay my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
Job23:5 I would know what he would answer me and understand what he would say to me.
Job23:6 Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No; he would pay attention to me.
Job23:7 There an upright man could argue with him
Job23:8 Behold
Job23:9 on the left hand when he is working
Job23:10 But he knows the way that I take; when he has tried me
Job23:11 My foot has held fast to his steps; I have kept his way and have not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not departed from the commandment of his lips; I have treasured the words of his mouth more than my portion of food.
Job23:13 But he is unchangeable
Job23:14 For he will complete what he appoints for me
Job23:15 Therefore I am terrified at his presence; when I consider
Job23:16 God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me;
Job23:17 yet I am not silenced because of the darkness
Job24:1 Why are not times of judgment kept by the Almighty
Job24:2 Some move landmarks; they seize flocks and pasture them.
Job24:3 They drive away the donkey of the fatherless; they take the widow's ox for a pledge.
Job24:4 They thrust the poor off the road; the poor of the earth all hide themselves.
Job24:5 Behold
Job24:6 They gather their fodder in the field
Job24:7 They lie all night naked
Job24:8 They are wet with the rain of the mountains and cling to the rock for lack of shelter.
Job24:9 (There are those who snatch the fatherless child from the breast
Job24:10 They go about naked
Job24:11 among the olive rows of the wicked they make oil; they tread the winepresses
Job24:12 From out of the city the dying groan
Job24:13 There are those who rebel against the light
Job24:14 The murderer rises before it is light
Job24:15 The eye of the adulterer also waits for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves up; they do not know the light.
Job24:17 For deep darkness is morning to all of them; for they are friends with the terrors of deep darkness.
Job24:18 You say
Job24:19 Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters; so does Sheol those who have sinned.
Job24:20 The womb forgets them; the worm finds them sweet; they are no longer remembered
Job24:21 They wrong the barren childless woman
Job24:22 Yet God prolongs the life of the mighty by his power; they rise up when they despair of life.
Job24:23 He gives them security
Job24:24 They are exalted a little while
Job24:25 If it is not so
Job25:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
Job25:2 Dominion and fear are with God; he makes peace in his high heaven.
Job25:3 Is there any number to his armies? Upon whom does his light not arise?
Job25:4 How then can man be in the right before God? How can he who is born of woman be pure?
Job25:5 Behold
Job25:6 how much less man
Job26:1 Then Job answered and said:
Job26:2 How you have helped him who has no power! How you have saved the arm that has no strength!
Job26:3 How you have counseled him who has no wisdom
Job26:4 With whose help have you uttered words
Job26:5 The dead tremble under the waters and their inhabitants.
Job26:6 Sheol is naked before God
Job26:7 He stretches out the north over the void and hangs the earth on nothing.
Job26:8 He binds up the waters in his thick clouds
Job26:9 He covers the face of the full moon and spreads over it his cloud.
Job26:10 He has inscribed a circle on the face of the waters at the boundary between light and darkness.
Job26:11 The pillars of heaven tremble and are astounded at his rebuke.
Job26:12 By his power he stilled the sea; by his understanding he shattered Rahab.
Job26:13 By his wind the heavens were made fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
Job26:14 Behold
Job27:1 And Job again took up his discourse
Job27:2 As God lives
Job27:3 as long as my breath is in me
Job27:4 my lips will not speak falsehood
Job27:5 Far be it from me to say that you are right; till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
Job27:6 I hold fast my righteousness and will not let it go; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days.
Job27:7 Let my enemy be as the wicked
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the godless when God cuts him off
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry when distress comes upon him?
Job27:10 Will he take delight in the Almighty? Will he call upon God at all times?
Job27:11 I will teach you concerning the hand of God; what is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 This is the portion of a wicked man with God
Job27:14 If his children are multiplied
Job27:15 Those who survive him the pestilence buries
Job27:16 Though he heap up silver like dust
Job27:17 he may pile it up
Job27:18 He builds his house like a moth's
Job27:19 He goes to bed rich
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like a flood; in the night a whirlwind carries him off.
Job27:21 The east wind lifts him up and he is gone; it sweeps him out of his place.
Job27:22 It hurls at him without pity; he flees from its power in headlong flight.
Job27:23 It claps its hands at him and hisses at him from its place.
Job28:1 Surely there is a mine for silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken out of the earth
Job28:3 Man puts an end to darkness and searches out to the farthest limit the ore in gloom and deep darkness.
Job28:4 He opens shafts in a valley away from where anyone lives; they are forgotten by travelers; they hang in the air
Job28:5 As for the earth
Job28:6 Its stones are the place of sapphires
Job28:7 That path no bird of prey knows
Job28:8 The proud beasts have not trodden it; the lion has not passed over it.
Job28:9 Man puts his hand to the flinty rock and overturns mountains by the roots.
Job28:10 He cuts out channels in the rocks
Job28:11 He dams up the streams so that they do not trickle
Job28:12 But where shall wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Man does not know its worth
Job28:14 The deep says
Job28:15 It cannot be bought for gold
Job28:16 It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold and glass cannot equal it
Job28:18 No mention shall be made of coral or of crystal; the price of wisdom is above
Job28:19 The topaz of Ethiopia cannot equal it
Job28:20 From where
Job28:21 It is hidden from the eyes of all living and concealed from the birds of the air.
Job28:22 Abaddon and Death say
Job28:23 God understands the way to it
Job28:24 For he looks to the ends of the earth and sees everything under the heavens.
Job28:25 When he gave to the wind its weight and apportioned the waters by measure
Job28:26 when he made a decree for the rain and a way for the lightning of the thunder
Job28:27 then he saw it and declared it; he established it
Job28:28 And he said to man
Job29:1 And Job again took up his discourse
Job29:2 Oh
Job29:3 when his lamp shone upon my head
Job29:4 as I was in my prime
Job29:5 when the Almighty was yet with me
Job29:6 when my steps were washed with butter
Job29:7 When I went out to the gate of the city
Job29:8 the young men saw me and withdrew
Job29:9 the princes refrained from talking and laid their hand on their mouth;
Job29:10 the voice of the nobles was hushed
Job29:11 When the ear heard
Job29:12 because I delivered the poor who cried for help
Job29:13 The blessing of him who was about to perish came upon me
Job29:14 I put on righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind and feet to the lame.
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy
Job29:17 I broke the fangs of the unrighteous and made him drop his prey from his teeth.
Job29:18 Then I thought
Job29:19 my roots spread out to the waters
Job29:20 my glory fresh with me
Job29:21 Men listened to me and waited and kept silence for my counsel.
Job29:22 After I spoke they did not speak again
Job29:23 They waited for me as for the rain
Job29:24 I smiled on them when they had no confidence
Job29:25 I chose their way and sat as chief
Job30:1 But now they laugh at me
Job30:2 What could I gain from the strength of their hands
Job30:3 Through want and hard hunger they gnaw the dry ground by night in waste and desolation;
Job30:4 they pick saltwort and the leaves of bushes
Job30:5 They are driven out from human company; they shout after them as after a thief.
Job30:6 In the gullies of the torrents they must dwell
Job30:7 Among the bushes they bray; under the nettles they huddle together.
Job30:8 A senseless
Job30:9 And now I have become their song; I am a byword to them.
Job30:10 They abhor me; they keep aloof from me; they do not hesitate to spit at the sight of me.
Job30:11 Because God has loosed my cord and humbled me
Job30:12 On my right hand the rabble rise; they push away my feet; they cast up against me their ways of destruction.
Job30:13 They break up my path; they promote my calamity; they need no one to help them.
Job30:14 As through a wide breach they come; amid the crash they roll on.
Job30:15 Terrors are turned upon me; my honor is pursued as by the wind
Job30:16 And now my soul is poured out within me; days of affliction have taken hold of me.
Job30:17 The night racks my bones
Job30:18 With great force my garment is disfigured; it binds me about like the collar of my tunic.
Job30:19 God has cast me into the mire
Job30:20 I cry to you for help and you do not answer me; I stand
Job30:21 You have turned cruel to me; with the might of your hand you persecute me.
Job30:22 You lift me up on the wind; you make me ride on it
Job30:23 For I know that you will bring me to death and to the house appointed for all living.
Job30:24 Yet does not one in a heap of ruins stretch out his hand
Job30:25 Did not I weep for him whose day was hard? Was not my soul grieved for the needy?
Job30:26 But when I hoped for good
Job30:27 My inward parts are in turmoil and never still; days of affliction come to meet me.
Job30:28 I go about darkened
Job30:29 I am a brother of jackals and a companion of ostriches.
Job30:30 My skin turns black and falls from me
Job30:31 My lyre is turned to mourning
Job31:1 I have made a covenant with my eyes; how then could I gaze at a virgin?
Job31:2 What would be my portion from God above and my heritage from the Almighty on high?
Job31:3 Is not calamity for the unrighteous
Job31:4 Does not he see my ways and number all my steps?
Job31:5 If I have walked with falsehood and my foot has hastened to deceit;
Job31:6 (Let me be weighed in a just balance
Job31:7 if my step has turned aside from the way and my heart has gone after my eyes
Job31:8 then let me sow
Job31:9 If my heart has been enticed toward a woman
Job31:10 then let my wife grind for another
Job31:11 For that would be a heinous crime; that would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges;
Job31:12 for that would be a fire that consumes as far as Abaddon
Job31:13 If I have rejected the cause of my manservant or my maidservant
Job31:14 what then shall I do when God rises up? When he makes inquiry
Job31:15 Did not he who made me in the womb make him? And did not one fashion us in the womb?
Job31:16 If I have withheld anything that the poor desired
Job31:17 or have eaten my morsel alone
Job31:18 (for from my youth the fatherless grew up with me as with a father
Job31:19 if I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing
Job31:20 if his body has not blessed me
Job31:21 if I have raised my hand against the fatherless
Job31:22 then let my shoulder blade fall from my shoulder
Job31:23 For I was in terror of calamity from God
Job31:24 If I have made gold my trust or called fine gold my confidence
Job31:25 if I have rejoiced because my wealth was abundant or because my hand had found much
Job31:26 if I have looked at the sun when it shone
Job31:27 and my heart has been secretly enticed
Job31:28 this also would be an iniquity to be punished by the judges
Job31:29 If I have rejoiced at the ruin of him who hated me
Job31:30 (I have not let my mouth sin by asking for his life with a curse)
Job31:31 if the men of my tent have not said
Job31:32 (the sojourner has not lodged in the street; I have opened my doors to the traveler)
Job31:33 if I have concealed my transgressions as others do by hiding my iniquity in my bosom
Job31:34 because I stood in great fear of the multitude
Job31:35 Oh
Job31:36 Surely I would carry it on my shoulder; I would bind it on me as a crown;
Job31:37 I would give him an account of all my steps; like a prince I would approach him.
Job31:38 If my land has cried out against me and its furrows have wept together
Job31:39 if I have eaten its yield without payment and made its owners breathe their last
Job31:40 let thorns grow instead of wheat

Job34:1 Then Elihu answered and said:
Job34:2 Hear my words
Job34:3 for the ear tests words as the palate tastes food.
Job34:4 Let us choose what is right; let us know among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 For Job has said
Job34:6 in spite of my right I am counted a liar; my wound is incurable
Job34:7 What man is like Job
Job34:8 who travels in company with evildoers and walks with wicked men?
Job34:9 For he has said
Job34:10 Therefore
Job34:11 For according to the work of a man he will repay him
Job34:12 Of a truth
Job34:13 Who gave him charge over the earth
Job34:14 If he should set his heart to it and gather to himself his spirit and his breath
Job34:15 all flesh would perish together
Job34:16 If you have understanding
Job34:17 Shall one who hates justice govern? Will you condemn him who is righteous and mighty
Job34:18 who says to a king
Job34:19 who shows no partiality to princes
Job34:20 In a moment they die; at midnight the people are shaken and pass away
Job34:21 For his eyes are on the ways of a man
Job34:22 There is no gloom or deep darkness where evildoers may hide themselves.
Job34:23 For God has no need to consider a man further
Job34:24 He shatters the mighty without investigation and sets others in their place.
Job34:25 Thus
Job34:26 He strikes them for their wickedness in a place for all to see
Job34:27 because they turned aside from following him and had no regard for any of his ways
Job34:28 so that they caused the cry of the poor to come to him
Job34:29 When he is quiet
Job34:30 that a godless man should not reign
Job34:31 For has anyone said to God

Job34:33 Will he then make repayment to suit you
Job34:34 Men of understanding will say to me
Job34:35 'Job speaks without knowledge; his words are without insight.'
Job34:36 Would that Job were tried to the end
Job34:37 For he adds rebellion to his sin; he claps his hands among us and multiplies his words against God.

Job35:7 If you are righteous

Job35:10 But none says

Job35:13 Surely God does not hear an empty cry
Job35:14 How much less when you say that you do not see him
Job35:15 And now

Job36:1 And Elihu continued
Job36:2 Bear with me a little
Job36:3 I will get my knowledge from afar and ascribe righteousness to my Maker.
Job36:4 For truly my words are not false; one who is perfect in knowledge is with you.
Job36:5 Behold
Job36:6 He does not keep the wicked alive
Job36:7 He does not withdraw his eyes from the righteous
Job36:8 And if they are bound in chains and caught in the cords of affliction
Job36:9 then he declares to them their work and their transgressions
Job36:10 He opens their ears to instruction and commands that they return from iniquity.
Job36:11 If they listen and serve him
Job36:12 But if they do not listen
Job36:13 The godless in heart cherish anger; they do not cry for help when he binds them.
Job36:14 They die in youth
Job36:15 He delivers the afflicted by their affliction and opens their ear by adversity.
Job36:16 He also allured you out of distress into a broad place where there was no cramping
Job36:17 But you are full of the judgment on the wicked; judgment and justice seize you.
Job36:18 Beware lest wrath entice you into scoffing
Job36:19 Will your cry for help avail to keep you from distress
Job36:20 Do not long for the night
Job36:21 Take care; do not turn to iniquity
Job36:22 Behold
Job36:23 Who has prescribed for him his way
Job36:24 Remember to extol his work
Job36:25 All mankind has looked on it; man beholds it from afar.
Job36:26 Behold
Job36:27 For he draws up the drops of water; they distill his mist in rain
Job36:28 which the skies pour down and drop on mankind abundantly.
Job36:29 Can anyone understand the spreading of the clouds
Job36:30 Behold
Job36:31 For by these he judges peoples; he gives food in abundance.
Job36:32 He covers his hands with the lightning and commands it to strike the mark.
Job36:33 Its crashing declares his presence; the cattle also declare that he rises.
Job37:1 At this also my heart trembles and leaps out of its place.
Job37:2 Keep listening to the thunder of his voice and the rumbling that comes from his mouth.
Job37:3 Under the whole heaven he lets it go
Job37:4 After it his voice roars; he thunders with his majestic voice
Job37:5 God thunders wondrously with his voice; he does great things that we cannot comprehend.
Job37:6 For to the snow he says
Job37:7 He seals up the hand of every man
Job37:8 Then the beasts go into their lairs
Job37:9 From its chamber comes the whirlwind
Job37:10 By the breath of God ice is given
Job37:11 He loads the thick cloud with moisture; the clouds scatter his lightning.
Job37:12 They turn around and around by his guidance
Job37:13 Whether for correction or for his land or for love
Job37:14 Hear this
Job37:15 Do you know how God lays his command upon them and causes the lightning of his cloud to shine?
Job37:16 Do you know the balancings of the clouds
Job37:17 you whose garments are hot when the earth is still because of the south wind?
Job37:18 Can you
Job37:19 Teach us what we shall say to him; we cannot draw up our case because of darkness.
Job37:20 Shall it be told him that I would speak? Did a man ever wish that he would be swallowed up?
Job37:21 And now no one looks on the light when it is bright in the skies
Job37:22 Out of the north comes golden splendor; God is clothed with awesome majesty.
Job37:23 The Almighty- we cannot find him; he is great in power; justice and abundant righteousness he will not violate.
Job37:24 Therefore men fear him; he does not regard any who are wise in their own conceit.
Job38:1 Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind and said:
Job38:2 Who is this that darkens counsel by words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Dress for action like a man; I will question you
Job38:4 Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Tell me
Job38:5 Who determined its measurements- surely you know! Or who stretched the line upon it?
Job38:6 On what were its bases sunk
Job38:7 when the morning stars sang together and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
Job38:8 Or who shut in the sea with doors when it burst out from the womb
Job38:9 when I made clouds its garment and thick darkness its swaddling band
Job38:10 and prescribed limits for it and set bars and doors
Job38:11 and said
Job38:12 Have you commanded the morning since your days began
Job38:13 that it might take hold of the skirts of the earth
Job38:14 It is changed like clay under the seal
Job38:15 From the wicked their light is withheld
Job38:16 Have you entered into the springs of the sea
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you
Job38:18 Have you comprehended the expanse of the earth? Declare
Job38:19 Where is the way to the dwelling of light
Job38:20 that you may take it to its territory and that you may discern the paths to its home?
Job38:21 You know
Job38:22 Have you entered the storehouses of the snow
Job38:23 which I have reserved for the time of trouble
Job38:24 What is the way to the place where the light is distributed
Job38:25 Who has cleft a channel for the torrents of rain and a way for the thunderbolt
Job38:26 to bring rain on a land where no man is
Job38:27 to satisfy the waste and desolate land
Job38:28 Has the rain a father
Job38:29 From whose womb did the ice come forth
Job38:30 The waters become hard like stone
Job38:31 Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades or loose the cords of Orion?
Job38:32 Can you lead forth the Mazzaroth1 in their season
Job38:33 Do you know the ordinances of the heavens? Can you establish their rule on the earth?
Job38:34 Can you lift up your voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Can you send forth lightnings
Job38:36 Who has put wisdom in the inward parts or given understanding to the mind?
Job38:37 Who can number the clouds by wisdom? Or who can tilt the waterskins of the heavens
Job38:38 when the dust runs into a mass and the clods stick fast together?
Job38:39 Can you hunt the prey for the lion
Job38:40 when they crouch in their dens or lie in wait in their thicket?
Job38:41 Who provides for the raven its prey
Job39:1 Do you know when the mountain goats give birth? Do you observe the calving of the does?
Job39:2 Can you number the months that they fulfill
Job39:3 when they crouch
Job39:4 Their young ones become strong; they grow up in the open; they go out and do not return to them.
Job39:5 Who has let the wild donkey go free? Who has loosed the bonds of the swift donkey
Job39:6 to whom I have given the arid plain for his home and the salt land for his dwelling place?
Job39:7 He scorns the tumult of the city; he hears not the shouts of the driver.
Job39:8 He ranges the mountains as his pasture
Job39:9 Is the wild ox willing to serve you? Will he spend the night at your manger?
Job39:10 Can you bind him in the furrow with ropes
Job39:11 Will you depend on him because his strength is great
Job39:12 Do you have faith in him that he will return your grain and gather it to your threshing floor?
Job39:13 The wings of the ostrich wave proudly
Job39:14 For she leaves her eggs to the earth and lets them be warmed on the ground
Job39:15 forgetting that a foot may crush them and that the wild beast may trample them.
Job39:16 She deals cruelly with her young
Job39:17 because God has made her forget wisdom and given her no share in understanding.
Job39:18 When she rouses herself to flee
Job39:19 Do you give the horse his might? Do you clothe his neck with a mane?
Job39:20 Do you make him leap like the locust? His majestic snorting is terrifying.
Job39:21 He paws in the valley and exults in his strength; he goes out to meet the weapons.
Job39:22 He laughs at fear and is not dismayed; he does not turn back from the sword.
Job39:23 Upon him rattle the quiver
Job39:24 With fierceness and rage he swallows the ground; he cannot stand still at the sound of the trumpet.
Job39:25 When the trumpet sounds
Job39:26 Is it by your understanding that the hawk soars and spreads his wings toward the south?
Job39:27 Is it at your command that the eagle mounts up and makes his nest on high?
Job39:28 On the rock he dwells and makes his home
Job39:29 From there he spies out the prey; his eyes behold it afar off.
Job39:30 His young ones suck up blood
Job40:1 And the LORD said to Job:
Job40:2 Shall a faultfinder contend with the Almighty? He who argues with God
Job40:3 Then Job answered the LORD and said:
Job40:4 Behold
Job40:5 I have spoken once
Job40:6 Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind and said:
Job40:7 Dress for action like a man; I will question you
Job40:8 Will you even put me in the wrong? Will you condemn me that you may be in the right?
Job40:9 Have you an arm like God
Job40:10 Adorn yourself with majesty and dignity; clothe yourself with glory and splendor.
Job40:11 Pour out the overflowings of your anger
Job40:12 Look on everyone who is proud and bring him low and tread down the wicked where they stand.
Job40:13 Hide them all in the dust together; bind their faces in the world below.
Job40:14 Then will I also acknowledge to you that your own right hand can save you.
Job40:15 Behold
Job40:16 Behold
Job40:17 He makes his tail stiff like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are knit together.
Job40:18 His bones are tubes of bronze
Job40:19 He is the first of the works of God; let him who made him bring near his sword!
Job40:20 For the mountains yield food for him where all the wild beasts play.
Job40:21 Under the lotus plants he lies
Job40:22 For his shade the lotus trees cover him; the willows of the brook surround him.
Job40:23 Behold
Job40:24 Can one take him by his eyes
Job41:1 Can you draw out Leviathan with a fishhook or press down his tongue with a cord?
Job41:2 Can you put a rope in his nose or pierce his jaw with a hook?
Job41:3 Will he make many pleas to you? Will he speak to you soft words?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with you to take him for your servant forever?
Job41:5 Will you play with him as with a bird
Job41:6 Will traders bargain over him? Will they divide him up among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill his skin with harpoons or his head with fishing spears?
Job41:8 Lay your hands on him; remember the battle- you will not do it again!
Job41:9 Behold
Job41:10 No one is so fierce that he dares to stir him up. Who then is he who can stand before me?
Job41:11 Who has first given to me
Job41:12 I will not keep silence concerning his limbs
Job41:13 Who can strip off his outer garment? Who would come near him with a bridle?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of his face? Around his teeth is terror.
Job41:15 His back is made of rows of shields
Job41:16 One is so near to another that no air can come between them.
Job41:17 They are joined one to another; they clasp each other and cannot be separated.
Job41:18 His sneezings flash forth light
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go flaming torches; sparks of fire leap forth.
Job41:20 Out of his nostrils comes forth smoke
Job41:21 His breath kindles coals
Job41:22 In his neck abides strength
Job41:23 The folds of his flesh stick together
Job41:24 His heart is hard as a stone
Job41:25 When he raises himself up the mighty are afraid; at the crashing they are beside themselves.
Job41:26 Though the sword reaches him
Job41:27 He counts iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make him flee; for him sling stones are turned to stubble.
Job41:29 Clubs are counted as stubble; he laughs at the rattle of javelins.
Job41:30 His underparts are like sharp potsherds; he spreads himself like a threshing sledge on the mire.
Job41:31 He makes the deep boil like a pot; he makes the sea like a pot of ointment.
Job41:32 Behind him he leaves a shining wake; one would think the deep to be white-haired.
Job41:33 On earth there is not his like
Job41:34 He sees everything that is high; he is king over all the sons of pride.
Job42:1 Then Job answered the LORD and said:
Job42:2 I know that you can do all things
Job42:3 'Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?' Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand
Job42:4 'Hear
Job42:5 I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear
Job42:6 therefore I despise myself
Job42:7 After the LORD had spoken these words to Job
Job42:8 Now therefore take seven bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves. And my servant Job shall pray for you
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did what the LORD had told them
Job42:10 And the LORD restored the fortunes of Job
Job42:11 Then came to him all his brothers and sisters and all who had known him before
Job42:12 And the LORD blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning. And he had 14
Job42:13 He had also seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 And he called the name of the first daughter Jemimah
Job42:15 And in all the land there were no women so beautiful as Job's daughters. And their father gave them an inheritance among their brothers.
Job42:16 And after this Job lived 140 years
Job42:17 And Job died

Psalms18:1 TO THE CHOIRMASTER. A PSALM OF DAVID
Psalms18:2 The LORD is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer
Psalms18:3 I call upon the LORD
Psalms18:4 The cords of death encompassed me; the torrents of destruction assailed me;
Psalms18:5 the cords of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.
Psalms18:6 In my distress I called upon the LORD; to my God I cried for help. From his temple he heard my voice
Psalms18:7 Then the earth reeled and rocked; the foundations also of the mountains trembled and quaked
Psalms18:8 Smoke went up from his nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens and came down; thick darkness was under his feet.
Psalms18:10 He rode on a cherub and flew; he came swiftly on the wings of the wind.
Psalms18:11 He made darkness his covering
Psalms18:12 Out of the brightness before him hailstones and coals of fire broke through his clouds.
Psalms18:13 The LORD also thundered in the heavens
Psalms18:14 And he sent out his arrows and scattered them; he flashed forth lightnings and routed them.
Psalms18:15 Then the channels of the sea were seen
Psalms18:16 He sent from on high
Psalms18:17 He rescued me from my strong enemy and from those who hated me
Psalms18:18 They confronted me in the day of my calamity
Psalms18:19 He brought me out into a broad place; he rescued me
Psalms18:20 The LORD dealt with me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he rewarded me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the LORD
Psalms18:22 For all his rules were before me
Psalms18:23 I was blameless before him
Psalms18:24 So the LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the merciful you show yourself merciful; with the blameless man you show yourself blameless;
Psalms18:26 with the purified you show yourself pure; and with the crooked you make yourself seem tortuous.
Psalms18:27 For you save a humble people
Psalms18:28 For it is you who light my lamp; the LORD my God lightens my darkness.
Psalms18:29 For by you I can run against a troop
Psalms18:30 This God- his way is perfect; the word of the LORD proves true; he is a shield for all those who take refuge in him.
Psalms18:31 For who is God
Psalms18:32 the God who equipped me with strength and made my way blameless.
Psalms18:33 He made my feet like the feet of a deer and set me secure on the heights.
Psalms18:34 He trains my hands for war
Psalms18:35 You have given me the shield of your salvation
Psalms18:36 You gave a wide place for my steps under me
Psalms18:37 I pursued my enemies and overtook them
Psalms18:38 I thrust them through
Psalms18:39 For you equipped me with strength for the battle; you made those who rise against me sink under me.
Psalms18:40 You made my enemies turn their backs to me
Psalms18:41 They cried for help
Psalms18:42 I beat them fine as dust before the wind; I cast them out like the mire of the streets.
Psalms18:43 You delivered me from strife with the people; you made me the head of the nations; people whom I had not known served me.
Psalms18:44 As soon as they heard of me they obeyed me; foreigners came cringing to me.
Psalms18:45 Foreigners lost heart and came trembling out of their fortresses.
Psalms18:46 The LORD lives
Psalms18:47 the God who gave me vengeance and subdued peoples under me
Psalms18:48 who delivered me from my enemies; yes
Psalms18:49 For this I will praise you
Psalms18:50 Great salvation he brings to his king

Psalms27:13 I believe that I shall look upon the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living!

Psalms35:1 OF DAVID.Contend
Psalms35:2 Take hold of shield and buckler and rise for my help!
Psalms35:3 Draw the spear and javelin against my pursuers! Say to my soul
Psalms35:4 Let them be put to shame and dishonor who seek after my life! Let them be turned back and disappointed who devise evil against me!
Psalms35:5 Let them be like chaff before the wind
Psalms35:6 Let their way be dark and slippery
Psalms35:7 For without cause they hid their net for me; without cause they dug a pit for my life.
Psalms35:8 Let destruction come upon him when he does not know it! And let the net that he hid ensnare him; let him fall into it- to his destruction!
Psalms35:9 Then my soul will rejoice in the LORD
Psalms35:10 All my bones shall say
Psalms35:11 Malicious witnesses rise up; they ask me of things that I do not know.
Psalms35:12 They repay me evil for good; my soul is bereft.
Psalms35:13 But I
Psalms35:14 I went about as though I grieved for my friend or my brother; as one who laments his mother
Psalms35:15 But at my stumbling they rejoiced and gathered; they gathered together against me; wretches whom I did not know tore at me without ceasing;
Psalms35:16 like profane mockers at a feast
Psalms35:17 How long
Psalms35:18 I will thank you in the great congregation; in the mighty throng I will praise you.
Psalms35:19 Let not those rejoice over me who are wrongfully my foes
Psalms35:20 For they do not speak peace
Psalms35:21 They open wide their mouths against me; they say
Psalms35:22 You have seen
Psalms35:23 Awake and rouse yourself for my vindication
Psalms35:24 Vindicate me
Psalms35:25 Let them not say in their hearts
Psalms35:26 Let them be put to shame and disappointed altogether who rejoice at my calamity! Let them be clothed with shame and dishonor who magnify themselves against me!
Psalms35:27 Let those who delight in my righteousness shout for joy and be glad and say evermore
Psalms35:28 Then my tongue shall tell of your righteousness and of your praise all the day long.

Psalms37:1 OF DAVID. Fret not yourself because of evildoers; be not envious of wrongdoers!
Psalms37:2 For they will soon fade like the grass and wither like the green herb.
Psalms37:3 Trust in the LORD
Psalms37:4 Delight yourself in the LORD
Psalms37:5 Commit your way to the LORD; trust in him
Psalms37:6 He will bring forth your righteousness as the light
Psalms37:7 Be still before the LORD and wait patiently for him; fret not yourself over the one who prospers in his way
Psalms37:8 Refrain from anger
Psalms37:9 For the evildoers shall be cut off
Psalms37:10 In just a little while
Psalms37:11 But the meek shall inherit the land and delight themselves in abundant peace.
Psalms37:12 The wicked plots against the righteous and gnashes his teeth at him
Psalms37:13 but the Lord laughs at the wicked
Psalms37:14 The wicked draw the sword and bend their bows to bring down the poor and needy
Psalms37:15 their sword shall enter their own heart
Psalms37:16 Better is the little that the righteous has than the abundance of many wicked.
Psalms37:17 For the arms of the wicked shall be broken
Psalms37:18 The LORD knows the days of the blameless
Psalms37:19 they are not put to shame in evil times; in the days of famine they have abundance.
Psalms37:20 But the wicked will perish; the enemies of the LORD are like the glory of the pastures; they vanish- like smoke they vanish away.
Psalms37:21 The wicked borrows but does not pay back
Psalms37:22 for those blessed by the LORD shall inherit the land
Psalms37:23 The steps of a man are established by the LORD
Psalms37:24 though he fall
Psalms37:25 I have been young
Psalms37:26 He is ever lending generously
Psalms37:27 Turn away from evil and do good; so shall you dwell forever.
Psalms37:28 For the LORD loves justice; he will not forsake his saints. They are preserved forever
Psalms37:29 The righteous shall inherit the land and dwell upon it forever.
Psalms37:30 The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom
Psalms37:31 The law of his God is in his heart; his steps do not slip.
Psalms37:32 The wicked watches for the righteous and seeks to put him to death.
Psalms37:33 The LORD will not abandon him to his power or let him be condemned when he is brought to trial.
Psalms37:34 Wait for the LORD and keep his way
Psalms37:35 I have seen a wicked
Psalms37:36 But he passed away
Psalms37:37 Mark the blameless and behold the upright
Psalms37:38 But transgressors shall be altogether destroyed; the future of the wicked shall be cut off.
Psalms37:39 The salvation of the righteous is from the LORD; he is their stronghold in the time of trouble.
Psalms37:40 The LORD helps them and delivers them; he delivers them from the wicked and saves them

Psalms44:1 TO THE CHOIRMASTER. A MASKIL OF THE SONS OF KORAH. O God
Psalms44:2 you with your own hand drove out the nations
Psalms44:3 for not by their own sword did they win the land
Psalms44:4 You are my King
Psalms44:5 Through you we push down our foes; through your name we tread down those who rise up against us.
Psalms44:6 For not in my bow do I trust
Psalms44:7 But you have saved us from our foes and have put to shame those who hate us.
Psalms44:8 In God we have boasted continually
Psalms44:9 But you have rejected us and disgraced us and have not gone out with our armies.
Psalms44:10 You have made us turn back from the foe
Psalms44:11 You have made us like sheep for slaughter and have scattered us among the nations.
Psalms44:12 You have sold your people for a trifle
Psalms44:13 You have made us the taunt of our neighbors
Psalms44:14 You have made us a byword among the nations
Psalms44:15 All day long my disgrace is before me
Psalms44:16 at the sound of the taunter and reviler
Psalms44:17 All this has come upon us
Psalms44:18 Our heart has not turned back
Psalms44:19 yet you have broken us in the place of jackals and covered us with the shadow of death.
Psalms44:20 If we had forgotten the name of our God or spread out our hands to a foreign god
Psalms44:21 would not God discover this? For he knows the secrets of the heart.
Psalms44:22 Yet for your sake we are killed all the day long; we are regarded as sheep to be slaughtered.
Psalms44:23 Awake! Why are you sleeping
Psalms44:24 Why do you hide your face? Why do you forget our affliction and oppression?
Psalms44:25 For our soul is bowed down to the dust; our belly clings to the ground.
Psalms44:26 Rise up; come to our help! Redeem us for the sake of your steadfast love!

Psalms45:17 I will cause your name to be remembered in all generations; therefore nations will praise you forever and ever.

Psalms49:6 those who trust in their wealth and boast of the abundance of their riches?
Psalms49:7 Truly no man can ransom another
Psalms49:8 for the ransom of their life is costly and can never suffice
Psalms49:9 that he should live on forever and never see the pit.
Psalms49:10 For he sees that even the wise die; the fool and the stupid alike must perish and leave their wealth to others.
Psalms49:11 Their graves are their homes forever
Psalms49:12 Man in his pomp will not remain; he is like the beasts that perish.

Psalms50:1 A PSALM OF ASAPH. The Mighty One
Psalms50:2 Out of Zion
Psalms50:3 Our God comes; he does not keep silence; before him is a devouring fire
Psalms50:4 He calls to the heavens above and to the earth
Psalms50:5 Gather to me my faithful ones
Psalms50:6 The heavens declare his righteousness
Psalms50:7 Hear
Psalms50:8 Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you; your burnt offerings are continually before me.
Psalms50:9 I will not accept a bull from your house or goats from your folds.
Psalms50:10 For every beast of the forest is mine
Psalms50:11 I know all the birds of the hills
Psalms50:12 If I were hungry
Psalms50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls or drink the blood of goats?
Psalms50:14 Offer to God a sacrifice of thanksgiving
Psalms50:15 and call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver you
Psalms50:16 But to the wicked God says: What right have you to recite my statutes or take my covenant on your lips?
Psalms50:17 For you hate discipline
Psalms50:18 If you see a thief
Psalms50:19 You give your mouth free rein for evil
Psalms50:20 You sit and speak against your brother; you slander your own mother's son.
Psalms50:21 These things you have done
Psalms50:22 Mark this
Psalms50:23 The one who offers thanksgiving as his sacrifice glorifies me; to one who orders his way rightly I will show the salvation of God!

Psalms51:6 Behold

Psalms69:1 TO THE CHOIRMASTER: ACCORDING TO LILIES. OF DAVID. Save me
Psalms69:2 I sink in deep mire
Psalms69:3 I am weary with my crying out; my throat is parched. My eyes grow dim with waiting for my God.

Psalms69:6 Let not those who hope in you be put to shame through me
Psalms69:7 For it is for your sake that I have borne reproach
Psalms69:8 I have become a stranger to my brothers
Psalms69:9 For zeal for your house has consumed me
Psalms69:10 When I wept and humbled my soul with fasting
Psalms69:11 When I made sackcloth my clothing
Psalms69:12 I am the talk of those who sit in the gate
Psalms69:13 But as for me
Psalms69:14 Deliver me from sinking in the mire; let me be delivered from my enemies and from the deep waters.
Psalms69:15 Let not the flood sweep over me

Psalms69:17 Hide not your face from your servant; for I am in distress; make haste to answer me.

Psalms69:19 You know my reproach
Psalms69:20 Reproaches have broken my heart
Psalms69:21 They gave me poison for food
Psalms69:22 Let their own table before them become a snare; and when they are at peace
Psalms69:23 Let their eyes be darkened
Psalms69:24 Pour out your indignation upon them
Psalms69:25 May their camp be a desolation; let no one dwell in their tents.
Psalms69:26 For they persecute him whom you have struck down
Psalms69:27 Add to them punishment upon punishment; may they have no acquittal from you.
Psalms69:28 Let them be blotted out of the book of the living; let them not be enrolled among the righteous.
Psalms69:29 But I am afflicted and in pain; let your salvation

Psalms69:31 This will please the LORD more than an ox or a bull with horns and hoofs.
Psalms69:32 When the humble see it they will be glad; you who seek God

Psalms69:35 For God will save Zion and build up the cities of Judah
Psalms69:36 the offspring of his servants shall inherit it

Psalms73:1 A PSALM OF ASAPH.Truly God is good to Israel
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I was envious of the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
Psalms73:4 For they have no pangs until death; their bodies are fat and sleek.
Psalms73:5 They are not in trouble as others are; they are not stricken like the rest of mankind.
Psalms73:6 Therefore pride is their necklace; violence covers them as a garment.
Psalms73:7 Their eyes swell out through fatness; their hearts overflow with follies.
Psalms73:8 They scoff and speak with malice; loftily they threaten oppression.
Psalms73:9 They set their mouths against the heavens
Psalms73:10 Therefore his people turn back to them
Psalms73:11 And they say
Psalms73:12 Behold
Psalms73:13 All in vain have I kept my heart clean and washed my hands in innocence.
Psalms73:14 For all the day long I have been stricken and rebuked every morning.
Psalms73:15 If I had said
Psalms73:16 But when I thought how to understand this
Psalms73:17 until I went into the sanctuary of God; then I discerned their end.
Psalms73:18 Truly you set them in slippery places; you make them fall to ruin.
Psalms73:19 How they are destroyed in a moment
Psalms73:20 Like a dream when one awakes
Psalms73:21 When my soul was embittered
Psalms73:22 I was brutish and ignorant; I was like a beast toward you.
Psalms73:23 Nevertheless
Psalms73:24 You guide me with your counsel
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but you? And there is nothing on earth that I desire besides you.
Psalms73:26 My flesh and my heart may fail
Psalms73:27 For behold
Psalms73:28 But for me it is good to be near God; I have made the Lord GOD my refuge

Psalms74:3 Direct your steps to the perpetual ruins; the enemy has destroyed everything in the sanctuary!
Psalms74:4 Your foes have roared in the midst of your meeting place; they set up their own signs for signs.
Psalms74:5 They were like those who swing axes in a forest of trees.
Psalms74:6 And all its carved wood they broke down with hatchets and hammers.
Psalms74:7 They set your sanctuary on fire; they profaned the dwelling place of your name
Psalms74:8 They said to themselves
Psalms74:9 We do not see our signs; there is no longer any prophet
Psalms74:10 How long
Psalms74:11 Why do you hold back your hand
Psalms74:12 Yet God my King is from of old
Psalms74:13 You divided the sea by your might; you broke the heads of the sea monsters on the waters.
Psalms74:14 You crushed the heads of Leviathan; you gave him as food for the creatures of the wilderness.
Psalms74:15 You split open springs and brooks; you dried up ever-flowing streams.
Psalms74:16 Yours is the day
Psalms74:17 You have fixed all the boundaries of the earth; you have made summer and winter.
Psalms74:18 Remember this
Psalms74:19 Do not deliver the soul of your dove to the wild beasts; do not forget the life of your poor forever.
Psalms74:20 Have regard for the covenant
Psalms74:21 Let not the downtrodden turn back in shame; let the poor and needy praise your name.
Psalms74:22 Arise
Psalms74:23 Do not forget the clamor of your foes
