Psalms76:7 But you
Psalms76:8 From the heavens you uttered judgment; the earth feared and was still
Psalms76:9 when God arose to establish judgment
Psalms76:10 Surely the wrath of man shall praise you; the remnant of wrath you will put on like a belt.
Psalms76:11 Make your vows to the LORD your God and perform them; let all around him bring gifts to him who is to be feared
Psalms76:12 who cuts off the spirit of princes
Psalms77:1 TO THE CHOIRMASTER: ACCORDING TO JEDUTHUN. A PSALM OF ASAPH. I cry aloud to God
Psalms77:2 In the day of my trouble I seek the Lord; in the night my hand is stretched out without wearying; my soul refuses to be comforted.
Psalms77:3 When I remember God
Psalms77:4 You hold my eyelids open; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
Psalms77:5 I consider the days of old
Psalms77:6 I said
Psalms77:7 Will the Lord spurn forever
Psalms77:8 Has his steadfast love forever ceased? Are his promises at an end for all time?
Psalms77:9 Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has he in anger shut up his compassion? Selah
Psalms77:10 Then I said
Psalms77:11 I will remember the deeds of the LORD; yes
Psalms77:12 I will ponder all your work
Psalms77:13 Your way
Psalms77:14 You are the God who works wonders; you have made known your might among the peoples.
Psalms77:15 You with your arm redeemed your people
Psalms77:16 When the waters saw you
Psalms77:17 The clouds poured out water; the skies gave forth thunder; your arrows flashed on every side.
Psalms77:18 The crash of your thunder was in the whirlwind; your lightnings lighted up the world; the earth trembled and shook.
Psalms77:19 Your way was through the sea
Psalms77:20 You led your people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
Psalms78:1 A MASKIL OF ASAPH. Give ear
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter dark sayings from of old
Psalms78:3 things that we have heard and known
Psalms78:4 We will not hide them from their children
Psalms78:5 He established a testimony in Jacob and appointed a law in Israel
Psalms78:6 that the next generation might know them
Psalms78:7 so that they should set their hope in God and not forget the works of God
Psalms78:8 and that they should not be like their fathers
Psalms78:9 The Ephraimites
Psalms78:10 They did not keep God's covenant
Psalms78:11 They forgot his works and the wonders that he had shown them.
Psalms78:12 In the sight of their fathers he performed wonders in the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He divided the sea and let them pass through it
Psalms78:14 In the daytime he led them with a cloud
Psalms78:15 He split rocks in the wilderness and gave them drink abundantly as from the deep.
Psalms78:16 He made streams come out of the rock and caused waters to flow down like rivers.
Psalms78:17 Yet they sinned still more against him
Psalms78:18 They tested God in their heart by demanding the food they craved.
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God
Psalms78:20 He struck the rock so that water gushed out and streams overflowed. Can he also give bread or provide meat for his people?
Psalms78:21 Therefore
Psalms78:22 because they did not believe in God and did not trust his saving power.
Psalms78:23 Yet he commanded the skies above and opened the doors of heaven
Psalms78:24 and he rained down on them manna to eat and gave them the grain of heaven.
Psalms78:25 Man ate of the bread of the angels; he sent them food in abundance.
Psalms78:26 He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens
Psalms78:27 he rained meat on them like dust
Psalms78:28 he let them fall in the midst of their camp
Psalms78:29 And they ate and were well filled
Psalms78:30 But before they had satisfied their craving
Psalms78:31 the anger of God rose against them
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this
Psalms78:33 So he made their days vanish like a breath
Psalms78:34 When he killed them
Psalms78:35 They remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they flattered him with their mouths; they lied to him with their tongues.
Psalms78:37 Their heart was not steadfast toward him; they were not faithful to his covenant.
Psalms78:38 Yet he
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were but flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness and grieved him in the desert!
Psalms78:41 They tested God again and again and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
Psalms78:42 They did not remember his power or the day when he redeemed them from the foe
Psalms78:43 when he performed his signs in Egypt and his marvels in the fields of Zoan.
Psalms78:44 He turned their rivers to blood
Psalms78:45 He sent among them swarms of flies
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to the destroying locust and the fruit of their labor to the locust.
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hail and their sycamores with frost.
Psalms78:48 He gave over their cattle to the hail and their flocks to thunderbolts.
Psalms78:49 He let loose on them his burning anger
Psalms78:50 He made a path for his anger; he did not spare them from death
Psalms78:51 He struck down every firstborn in Egypt
Psalms78:52 Then he led out his people like sheep and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.
Psalms78:53 He led them in safety
Psalms78:54 And he brought them to his holy land
Psalms78:55 He drove out nations before them; he apportioned them for a possession and settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
Psalms78:56 Yet they tested and rebelled against the Most High God and did not keep his testimonies
Psalms78:57 but turned away and acted treacherously like their fathers; they twisted like a deceitful bow.
Psalms78:58 For they provoked him to anger with their high places; they moved him to jealousy with their idols.
Psalms78:59 When God heard
Psalms78:60 He forsook his dwelling at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 and delivered his power to captivity
Psalms78:62 He gave his people over to the sword and vented his wrath on his heritage.
Psalms78:63 Fire devoured their young men
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as from sleep
Psalms78:66 And he put his adversaries to rout; he put them to everlasting shame.
Psalms78:67 He rejected the tent of Joseph; he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim
Psalms78:68 but he chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He built his sanctuary like the high heavens
Psalms78:70 He chose David his servant and took him from the sheepfolds;
Psalms78:71 from following the nursing ewes he brought him to shepherd Jacob his people
Psalms78:72 With upright heart he shepherded them and guided them with his skillful hand.

Psalms80:4 O LORD God of hosts

Psalms80:7 Restore us

Psalms80:10 The mountains were covered with its shade

Psalms80:18 Then we shall not turn back from you; give us life
Psalms80:19 Restore us

Psalms89:8 O LORD God of hosts
Psalms89:9 You rule the raging of the sea; when its waves rise
Psalms89:10 You crushed Rahab like a carcass; you scattered your enemies with your mighty arm.
Psalms89:11 The heavens are yours; the earth also is yours; the world and all that is in it
Psalms89:12 The north and the south
Psalms89:13 You have a mighty arm; strong is your hand
Psalms89:14 Righteousness and justice are the foundation of your throne; steadfast love and faithfulness go before you.
Psalms89:15 Blessed are the people who know the festal shout
Psalms89:16 who exult in your name all the day and in your righteousness are exalted.
Psalms89:17 For you are the glory of their strength; by your favor our horn is exalted.
Psalms89:18 For our shield belongs to the LORD
Psalms89:19 Of old you spoke in a vision to your godly one
Psalms89:20 I have found David

Psalms89:22 The enemy shall not outwit him; the wicked shall not humble him.
Psalms89:23 I will crush his foes before him and strike down those who hate him.
Psalms89:24 My faithfulness and my steadfast love shall be with him
Psalms89:25 I will set his hand on the sea and his right hand on the rivers.
Psalms89:26 He shall cry to me
Psalms89:27 And I will make him the firstborn
Psalms89:28 My steadfast love I will keep for him forever
Psalms89:29 I will establish his offspring forever and his throne as the days of the heavens.
Psalms89:30 If his children forsake my law and do not walk according to my rules
Psalms89:31 if they violate my statutes and do not keep my commandments
Psalms89:32 then I will punish their transgression with the rod and their iniquity with stripes
Psalms89:33 but I will not remove from him my steadfast love or be false to my faithfulness.
Psalms89:34 I will not violate my covenant or alter the word that went forth from my lips.
Psalms89:35 Once for all I have sworn by my holiness; I will not lie to David.
Psalms89:36 His offspring shall endure forever
Psalms89:37 Like the moon it shall be established forever
Psalms89:38 But now you have cast off and rejected; you are full of wrath against your anointed.
Psalms89:39 You have renounced the covenant with your servant; you have defiled his crown in the dust.
Psalms89:40 You have breached all his walls; you have laid his strongholds in ruins.
Psalms89:41 All who pass by plunder him; he has become the scorn of his neighbors.
Psalms89:42 You have exalted the right hand of his foes; you have made all his enemies rejoice.
Psalms89:43 You have also turned back the edge of his sword
Psalms89:44 You have made his splendor to cease and cast his throne to the ground.
Psalms89:45 You have cut short the days of his youth; you have covered him with shame. Selah
Psalms89:46 How long
Psalms89:47 Remember how short my time is! For what vanity you have created all the children of man!
Psalms89:48 What man can live and never see death? Who can deliver his soul from the power of Sheol? Selah

Psalms101:1 A PSALM OF DAVID.I will sing of steadfast love and justice; to you
Psalms101:2 I will ponder the way that is blameless. Oh when will you come to me? I will walk with integrity of heart within my house;
Psalms101:3 I will not set before my eyes anything that is worthless. I hate the work of those who fall away; it shall not cling to me.
Psalms101:4 A perverse heart shall be far from me; I will know nothing of evil.
Psalms101:5 Whoever slanders his neighbor secretly I will destroy. Whoever has a haughty look and an arrogant heart I will not endure.
Psalms101:6 I will look with favor on the faithful in the land
Psalms101:7 No one who practices deceit shall dwell in my house; no one who utters lies shall continue before my eyes.
Psalms101:8 Morning by morning I will destroy all the wicked in the land
Psalms102:1 A PRAYER OF ONE AFFLICTED
Psalms102:2 Do not hide your face from me in the day of my distress! Incline your ear to me; answer me speedily in the day when I call!
Psalms102:3 For my days pass away like smoke
Psalms102:4 My heart is struck down like grass and has withered; I forget to eat my bread.
Psalms102:5 Because of my loud groaning my bones cling to my flesh.
Psalms102:6 I am like a desert owl of the wilderness
Psalms102:7 I lie awake; I am like a lonely sparrow on the housetop.
Psalms102:8 All the day my enemies taunt me; those who deride me use my name for a curse.
Psalms102:9 For I eat ashes like bread and mingle tears with my drink
Psalms102:10 because of your indignation and anger; for you have taken me up and thrown me down.
Psalms102:11 My days are like an evening shadow; I wither away like grass.
Psalms102:12 But you
Psalms102:13 You will arise and have pity on Zion; it is the time to favor her; the appointed time has come.
Psalms102:14 For your servants hold her stones dear and have pity on her dust.
Psalms102:15 Nations will fear the name of the LORD
Psalms102:16 For the LORD builds up Zion; he appears in his glory;
Psalms102:17 he regards the prayer of the destitute and does not despise their prayer.
Psalms102:18 Let this be recorded for a generation to come
Psalms102:19 that he looked down from his holy height; from heaven the LORD looked at the earth
Psalms102:20 to hear the groans of the prisoners
Psalms102:21 that they may declare in Zion the name of the LORD
Psalms102:22 when peoples gather together
Psalms102:23 He has broken my strength in midcourse; he has shortened my days.
Psalms102:24 O my God
Psalms102:25 Of old you laid the foundation of the earth
Psalms102:26 They will perish
Psalms102:27 but you are the same
Psalms102:28 The children of your servants shall dwell secure; their offspring shall be established before you.

Psalms104:8 The mountains rose
Psalms104:9 You set a boundary that they may not pass
Psalms104:10 You make springs gush forth in the valleys; they flow between the hills;
Psalms104:11 they give drink to every beast of the field; the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
Psalms104:12 Beside them the birds of the heavens dwell; they sing among the branches.
Psalms104:13 From your lofty abode you water the mountains; the earth is satisfied with the fruit of your work.
Psalms104:14 You cause the grass to grow for the livestock and plants for man to cultivate
Psalms104:15 and wine to gladden the heart of man
Psalms104:16 The trees of the LORD are watered abundantly
Psalms104:17 In them the birds build their nests; the stork has her home in the fir trees.
Psalms104:18 The high mountains are for the wild goats; the rocks are a refuge for the rock badgers.
Psalms104:19 He made the moon to mark the seasons; the sun knows its time for setting.
Psalms104:20 You make darkness

Psalms106:46 He caused them to be pitied by all those who held them captive.

Psalms109:1 TO THE CHOIRMASTER. A PSALM OF DAVID. Be not silent
Psalms109:2 For wicked and deceitful mouths are opened against me
Psalms109:3 They encircle me with words of hate
Psalms109:4 In return for my love they accuse me
Psalms109:5 So they reward me evil for good
Psalms109:6 Appoint a wicked man against him; let an accuser stand at his right hand.
Psalms109:7 When he is tried
Psalms109:8 May his days be few; may another take his office!
Psalms109:9 May his children be fatherless and his wife a widow!
Psalms109:10 May his children wander about and beg
Psalms109:11 May the creditor seize all that he has; may strangers plunder the fruits of his toil!
Psalms109:12 Let there be none to extend kindness to him
Psalms109:13 May his posterity be cut off; may his name be blotted out in the second generation!
Psalms109:14 May the iniquity of his fathers be remembered before the LORD
Psalms109:15 Let them be before the LORD continually
Psalms109:16 For he did not remember to show kindness
Psalms109:17 He loved to curse; let curses come upon him! He did not delight in blessing; may it be far from him!
Psalms109:18 He clothed himself with cursing as his coat; may it soak into his body like water
Psalms109:19 May it be like a garment that he wraps around him
Psalms109:20 May this be the reward of my accusers from the LORD
Psalms109:21 But you
Psalms109:22 For I am poor and needy
Psalms109:23 I am gone like a shadow at evening; I am shaken off like a locust.
Psalms109:24 My knees are weak through fasting; my body has become gaunt
Psalms109:25 I am an object of scorn to my accusers; when they see me
Psalms109:26 Help me
Psalms109:27 Let them know that this is your hand; you
Psalms109:28 Let them curse
Psalms109:29 May my accusers be clothed with dishonor; may they be wrapped in their own shame as in a cloak!
Psalms109:30 With my mouth I will give great thanks to the LORD; I will praise him in the midst of the throng.
Psalms109:31 For he stands at the right hand of the needy

Psalms119:35 Lead me in the path of your commandments
Psalms119:36 Incline my heart to your testimonies
Psalms119:37 Turn my eyes from looking at worthless things; and give me life in your ways.
Psalms119:38 Confirm to your servant your promise
Psalms119:39 Turn away the reproach that I dread
Psalms119:40 Behold
Psalms119:41 Let your steadfast love come to me
Psalms119:42 then shall I have an answer for him who taunts me
Psalms119:43 And take not the word of truth utterly out of my mouth
Psalms119:44 I will keep your law continually
Psalms119:45 and I shall walk in a wide place
Psalms119:46 I will also speak of your testimonies before kings and shall not be put to shame
Psalms119:47 for I find my delight in your commandments
Psalms119:48 I will lift up my hands toward your commandments
Psalms119:49 Remember your word to your servant
Psalms119:50 This is my comfort in my affliction
Psalms119:51 The insolent utterly deride me
Psalms119:52 When I think of your rules from of old
Psalms119:53 Hot indignation seizes me because of the wicked
Psalms119:54 Your statutes have been my songs in the house of my sojourning.
Psalms119:55 I remember your name in the night
Psalms119:56 This blessing has fallen to me
Psalms119:57 The LORD is my portion; I promise to keep your words.
Psalms119:58 I entreat your favor with all my heart; be gracious to me according to your promise.
Psalms119:59 When I think on my ways
Psalms119:60 I hasten and do not delay to keep your commandments.
Psalms119:61 Though the cords of the wicked ensnare me
Psalms119:62 At midnight I rise to praise you
Psalms119:63 I am a companion of all who fear you
Psalms119:64 The earth
Psalms119:65 You have dealt well with your servant
Psalms119:66 Teach me good judgment and knowledge
Psalms119:67 Before I was afflicted I went astray
Psalms119:68 You are good and do good; teach me your statutes.
Psalms119:69 The insolent smear me with lies
Psalms119:70 their heart is unfeeling like fat
Psalms119:71 It is good for me that I was afflicted
Psalms119:72 The law of your mouth is better to me than thousands of gold and silver pieces.
Psalms119:73 Your hands have made and fashioned me; give me understanding that I may learn your commandments.
Psalms119:74 Those who fear you shall see me and rejoice
Psalms119:75 I know
Psalms119:76 Let your steadfast love comfort me according to your promise to your servant.
Psalms119:77 Let your mercy come to me
Psalms119:78 Let the insolent be put to shame
Psalms119:79 Let those who fear you turn to me
Psalms119:80 May my heart be blameless in your statutes
Psalms119:81 My soul longs for your salvation; I hope in your word.
Psalms119:82 My eyes long for your promise; I ask
Psalms119:83 For I have become like a wineskin in the smoke
Psalms119:84 How long must your servant endure? When will you judge those who persecute me?
Psalms119:85 The insolent have dug pitfalls for me; they do not live according to your law.
Psalms119:86 All your commandments are sure; they persecute me with falsehood; help me!
Psalms119:87 They have almost made an end of me on earth
Psalms119:88 In your steadfast love give me life
Psalms119:89 Forever
Psalms119:90 Your faithfulness endures to all generations; you have established the earth
Psalms119:91 By your appointment they stand this day
Psalms119:92 If your law had not been my delight
Psalms119:93 I will never forget your precepts
Psalms119:94 I am yours; save me
Psalms119:95 The wicked lie in wait to destroy me
Psalms119:96 I have seen a limit to all perfection
Psalms119:97 Oh how I love your law! It is my meditation all the day.
Psalms119:98 Your commandment makes me wiser than my enemies
Psalms119:99 I have more understanding than all my teachers
Psalms119:100 I understand more than the aged
Psalms119:101 I hold back my feet from every evil way
Psalms119:102 I do not turn aside from your rules
Psalms119:103 How sweet are your words to my taste
Psalms119:104 Through your precepts I get understanding; therefore I hate every false way.
Psalms119:105 Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path.
Psalms119:106 I have sworn an oath and confirmed it
Psalms119:107 I am severely afflicted; give me life
Psalms119:108 Accept my freewill offerings of praise
Psalms119:109 I hold my life in my hand continually
Psalms119:110 The wicked have laid a snare for me
Psalms119:111 Your testimonies are my heritage forever
Psalms119:112 I incline my heart to perform your statutes forever
Psalms119:113 I hate the double-minded
Psalms119:114 You are my hiding place and my shield; I hope in your word.
Psalms119:115 Depart from me
Psalms119:116 Uphold me according to your promise
Psalms119:117 Hold me up
Psalms119:118 You spurn all who go astray from your statutes
Psalms119:119 All the wicked of the earth you discard like dross
Psalms119:120 My flesh trembles for fear of you
Psalms119:121 I have done what is just and right; do not leave me to my oppressors.
Psalms119:122 Give your servant a pledge of good; let not the insolent oppress me.
Psalms119:123 My eyes long for your salvation and for the fulfillment of your righteous promise.
Psalms119:124 Deal with your servant according to your steadfast love
Psalms119:125 I am your servant; give me understanding
Psalms119:126 It is time for the LORD to act
Psalms119:127 Therefore I love your commandments above gold
Psalms119:128 Therefore I consider all your precepts to be right; I hate every false way.
Psalms119:129 Your testimonies are wonderful; therefore my soul keeps them.
Psalms119:130 The unfolding of your words gives light; it imparts understanding to the simple.
Psalms119:131 I open my mouth and pant
Psalms119:132 Turn to me and be gracious to me
Psalms119:133 Keep steady my steps according to your promise
Psalms119:134 Redeem me from man's oppression
Psalms119:135 Make your face shine upon your servant
Psalms119:136 My eyes shed streams of tears
Psalms119:137 Righteous are you
Psalms119:138 You have appointed your testimonies in righteousness and in all faithfulness.
Psalms119:139 My zeal consumes me
Psalms119:140 Your promise is well tried
Psalms119:141 I am small and despised
Psalms119:142 Your righteousness is righteous forever
Psalms119:143 Trouble and anguish have found me out
Psalms119:144 Your testimonies are righteous forever; give me understanding that I may live.
Psalms119:145 With my whole heart I cry; answer me
Psalms119:146 I call to you; save me
Psalms119:147 I rise before dawn and cry for help; I hope in your words.
Psalms119:148 My eyes are awake before the watches of the night
Psalms119:149 Hear my voice according to your steadfast love; O LORD
Psalms119:150 They draw near who persecute me with evil purpose; they are far from your law.
Psalms119:151 But you are near
Psalms119:152 Long have I known from your testimonies that you have founded them forever.
Psalms119:153 Look on my affliction and deliver me
Psalms119:154 Plead my cause and redeem me; give me life according to your promise!
Psalms119:155 Salvation is far from the wicked
Psalms119:156 Great is your mercy
Psalms119:157 Many are my persecutors and my adversaries
Psalms119:158 I look at the faithless with disgust
Psalms119:159 Consider how I love your precepts! Give me life according to your steadfast love.
Psalms119:160 The sum of your word is truth
Psalms119:161 Princes persecute me without cause
Psalms119:162 I rejoice at your word like one who finds great spoil.
Psalms119:163 I hate and abhor falsehood
Psalms119:164 Seven times a day I praise you for your righteous rules.
Psalms119:165 Great peace have those who love your law; nothing can make them stumble.
Psalms119:166 I hope for your salvation
Psalms119:167 My soul keeps your testimonies; I love them exceedingly.
Psalms119:168 I keep your precepts and testimonies
Psalms119:169 Let my cry come before you

Psalms119:172 My tongue will sing of your word
Psalms119:173 Let your hand be ready to help me
Psalms119:174 I long for your salvation
Psalms119:175 Let my soul live and praise you

Psalms147:16 He gives snow like wool; he scatters hoarfrost like ashes.

Psalms148:4 Praise him
Psalms148:5 Let them praise the name of the LORD! For he commanded and they were created.
Psalms148:6 And he established them forever and ever; he gave a decree
Psalms148:7 Praise the LORD from the earth
Psalms148:8 fire and hail
Psalms148:9 Mountains and all hills
Psalms148:10 Beasts and all livestock
Psalms148:11 Kings of the earth and all peoples
Psalms148:12 Young men and maidens together
Psalms148:13 Let them praise the name of the LORD
Psalms148:14 He has raised up a horn for his people
Psalms149:1 Praise the LORD!Sing to the LORD a new song
Psalms149:2 Let Israel be glad in his Maker; let the children of Zion rejoice in their King!
Psalms149:3 Let them praise his name with dancing
Psalms149:4 For the LORD takes pleasure in his people; he adorns the humble with salvation.
Psalms149:5 Let the godly exult in glory; let them sing for joy on their beds.
Psalms149:6 Let the high praises of God be in their throats and two-edged swords in their hands
Psalms149:7 to execute vengeance on the nations and punishments on the peoples
Psalms149:8 to bind their kings with chains and their nobles with fetters of iron
Psalms149:9 to execute on them the judgment written! This is honor for all his godly ones. Praise the LORD!
Psalms150:1 Praise the LORD!Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in his mighty heavens!
Psalms150:2 Praise him for his mighty deeds; praise him according to his excellent greatness!
Psalms150:3 Praise him with trumpet sound; praise him with lute and harp!
Psalms150:4 Praise him with tambourine and dance; praise him with strings and pipe!
Psalms150:5 Praise him with sounding cymbals; praise him with loud clashing cymbals!
Psalms150:6 Let everything that has breath praise the LORD! Praise the LORD!
Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon
Proverbs1:2 To know wisdom and instruction
Proverbs1:3 to receive instruction in wise dealing
Proverbs1:4 to give prudence to the simple
Proverbs1:5 Let the wise hear and increase in learning
Proverbs1:6 to understand a proverb and a saying
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction.
Proverbs1:8 Hear
Proverbs1:9 for they are a graceful garland for your head and pendants for your neck.
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 like Sheol let us swallow them alive
Proverbs1:13 we shall find all precious goods
Proverbs1:14 throw in your lot among us; we will all have one purse-
Proverbs1:15 my son
Proverbs1:16 for their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 For in vain is a net spread in the sight of any bird
Proverbs1:18 but these men lie in wait for their own blood; they set an ambush for their own lives.
Proverbs1:19 Such are the ways of everyone who is greedy for unjust gain; it takes away the life of its possessors.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom cries aloud in the street
Proverbs1:21 at the head of the noisy streets she cries out; at the entrance of the city gates she speaks:
Proverbs1:22 How long
Proverbs1:23 If you turn at my reproof
Proverbs1:24 Because I have called and you refused to listen
Proverbs1:25 because you have ignored all my counsel and would have none of my reproof
Proverbs1:26 I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when terror strikes you
Proverbs1:27 when terror strikes you like a storm and your calamity comes like a whirlwind
Proverbs1:28 Then they will call upon me
Proverbs1:29 Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the LORD
Proverbs1:30 would have none of my counsel and despised all my reproof
Proverbs1:31 therefore they shall eat the fruit of their way
Proverbs1:32 For the simple are killed by their turning away
Proverbs1:33 but whoever listens to me will dwell secure and will be at ease
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 making your ear attentive to wisdom and inclining your heart to understanding;
Proverbs2:3 yes
Proverbs2:4 if you seek it like silver and search for it as for hidden treasures
Proverbs2:5 then you will understand the fear of the LORD and find the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 For the LORD gives wisdom; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding;
Proverbs2:7 he stores up sound wisdom for the upright; he is a shield to those who walk in integrity
Proverbs2:8 guarding the paths of justice and watching over the way of his saints.
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand righteousness and justice and equity
Proverbs2:10 for wisdom will come into your heart
Proverbs2:11 discretion will watch over you
Proverbs2:12 delivering you from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 who forsake the paths of uprightness to walk in the ways of darkness
Proverbs2:14 who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perverseness of evil
Proverbs2:15 men whose paths are crooked
Proverbs2:16 So you will be delivered from the forbidden woman
Proverbs2:17 who forsakes the companion of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God;
Proverbs2:18 for her house sinks down to death
Proverbs2:19 none who go to her come back
Proverbs2:20 So you will walk in the way of the good and keep to the paths of the righteous.
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will inhabit the land
Proverbs2:22 but the wicked will be cut off from the land
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 for length of days and years of life and peace they will add to you.
Proverbs3:3 Let not steadfast love and faithfulness forsake you; bind them around your neck; write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs3:4 So you will find favor and good success in the sight of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the LORD with all your heart
Proverbs3:6 In all your ways acknowledge him
Proverbs3:7 Be not wise in your own eyes; fear the LORD
Proverbs3:8 It will be healing to your flesh and refreshment to your bones.
Proverbs3:9 Honor the LORD with your wealth and with the firstfruits of all your produce;
Proverbs3:10 then your barns will be filled with plenty
Proverbs3:11 My son
Proverbs3:12 for the LORD reproves him whom he loves
Proverbs3:13 Blessed is the one who finds wisdom
Proverbs3:14 for the gain from her is better than gain from silver and her profit better than gold.
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than jewels
Proverbs3:16 Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are ways of pleasantness
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who lay hold of her; those who hold her fast are called blessed.
Proverbs3:19 The LORD by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding he established the heavens;
Proverbs3:20 by his knowledge the deeps broke open
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 and they will be life for your soul and adornment for your neck.
Proverbs3:23 Then you will walk on your way securely
Proverbs3:24 If you lie down
Proverbs3:25 Do not be afraid of sudden terror or of the ruin of the wicked
Proverbs3:26 for the LORD will be your confidence and will keep your foot from being caught.
Proverbs3:27 Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due
Proverbs3:28 Do not say to your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Do not plan evil against your neighbor
Proverbs3:30 Do not contend with a man for no reason
Proverbs3:31 Do not envy a man of violence and do not choose any of his ways
Proverbs3:32 for the devious person is an abomination to the LORD
Proverbs3:33 The LORD's curse is on the house of the wicked
Proverbs3:34 Toward the scorners he is scornful
Proverbs3:35 The wise will inherit honor
Proverbs4:1 Hear
Proverbs4:2 for I give you good precepts; do not forsake my teaching.
Proverbs4:3 When I was a son with my father
Proverbs4:4 he taught me and said to me
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom; get insight; do not forget
Proverbs4:6 Do not forsake her
Proverbs4:7 The beginning of wisdom is this: Get wisdom
Proverbs4:8 Prize her highly
Proverbs4:9 She will place on your head a graceful garland; she will bestow on you a beautiful crown.
Proverbs4:10 Hear
Proverbs4:11 I have taught you the way of wisdom; I have led you in the paths of uprightness.
Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Keep hold of instruction; do not let go; guard her
Proverbs4:14 Do not enter the path of the wicked
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it; do not go on it; turn away from it and pass on.
Proverbs4:16 For they cannot sleep unless they have done wrong; they are robbed of sleep unless they have made someone stumble.
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know over what they stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Let them not escape from your sight; keep them within your heart.
Proverbs4:22 For they are life to those who find them
Proverbs4:23 Keep your heart with all vigilance
Proverbs4:24 Put away from you crooked speech
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look directly forward
Proverbs4:26 Ponder the path of your feet; then all your ways will be sure.
Proverbs4:27 Do not swerve to the right or to the left; turn your foot away from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 that you may keep discretion
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of a forbidden woman drip honey
Proverbs5:4 but in the end she is bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death; her steps follow the path to Sheol;
Proverbs5:6 she does not ponder the path of life; her ways wander
Proverbs5:7 And now
Proverbs5:8 Keep your way far from her
Proverbs5:9 lest you give your honor to others and your years to the merciless
Proverbs5:10 lest strangers take their fill of your strength
Proverbs5:11 and at the end of your life you groan
Proverbs5:12 and you say
Proverbs5:13 I did not listen to the voice of my teachers or incline my ear to my instructors.
Proverbs5:14 I am at the brink of utter ruin in the assembled congregation.
Proverbs5:15 Drink water from your own cistern
Proverbs5:16 Should your springs be scattered abroad
Proverbs5:17 Let them be for yourself alone
Proverbs5:18 Let your fountain be blessed
Proverbs5:19 a lovely deer
Proverbs5:20 Why should you be intoxicated
Proverbs5:21 For a man's ways are before the eyes of the LORD
Proverbs5:22 The iniquities of the wicked ensnare him
Proverbs5:23 He dies for lack of discipline
Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 if you are snared in the words of your mouth
Proverbs6:3 then do this
Proverbs6:4 Give your eyes no sleep and your eyelids no slumber;
Proverbs6:5 save yourself like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter
Proverbs6:6 Go to the ant
Proverbs6:7 Without having any chief
Proverbs6:8 she prepares her bread in summer and gathers her food in harvest.
Proverbs6:9 How long will you lie there
Proverbs6:10 A little sleep
Proverbs6:11 and poverty will come upon you like a robber
Proverbs6:12 A worthless person
Proverbs6:13 winks with his eyes
Proverbs6:14 with perverted heart devises evil
Proverbs6:15 therefore calamity will come upon him suddenly; in a moment he will be broken beyond healing.
Proverbs6:16 There are six things that the LORD hates
Proverbs6:17 haughty eyes
Proverbs6:18 a heart that devises wicked plans
Proverbs6:19 a false witness who breathes out lies
Proverbs6:20 My son
Proverbs6:21 Bind them on your heart always; tie them around your neck.
Proverbs6:22 When you walk
Proverbs6:23 For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching a light
Proverbs6:24 to preserve you from the evil woman
Proverbs6:25 Do not desire her beauty in your heart
Proverbs6:26 for the price of a prostitute is only a loaf of bread
Proverbs6:27 Can a man carry fire next to his chest and his clothes not be burned?
Proverbs6:28 Or can one walk on hot coals and his feet not be scorched?
Proverbs6:29 So is he who goes in to his neighbor's wife; none who touches her will go unpunished.
Proverbs6:30 People do not despise a thief if he steals to satisfy his appetite when he is hungry
Proverbs6:31 but if he is caught
Proverbs6:32 He who commits adultery lacks sense; he who does it destroys himself.
Proverbs6:33 Wounds and dishonor will he get
Proverbs6:34 For jealousy makes a man furious
Proverbs6:35 He will accept no compensation; he will refuse though you multiply gifts.
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 keep my commandments and live; keep my teaching as the apple of your eye;
Proverbs7:3 bind them on your fingers; write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 to keep you from the forbidden woman
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house I have looked out through my lattice
Proverbs7:7 and I have seen among the simple
Proverbs7:8 passing along the street near her corner
Proverbs7:9 in the twilight
Proverbs7:10 And behold
Proverbs7:11 She is loud and wayward; her feet do not stay at home;
Proverbs7:12 now in the street
Proverbs7:13 She seizes him and kisses him
Proverbs7:14 I had to offer sacrifices
Proverbs7:15 so now I have come out to meet you
Proverbs7:16 I have spread my couch with coverings
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my bed with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey;
Proverbs7:20 he took a bag of money with him; at full moon he will come home.
Proverbs7:21 With much seductive speech she persuades him; with her smooth talk she compels him.
Proverbs7:22 All at once he follows her
Proverbs7:23 till an arrow pierces its liver; as a bird rushes into a snare; he does not know that it will cost him his life.
Proverbs7:24 And now
Proverbs7:25 Let not your heart turn aside to her ways; do not stray into her paths
Proverbs7:26 for many a victim has she laid low
Proverbs7:27 Her house is the way to Sheol
Proverbs8:1 Does not wisdom call?Does not understanding raise her voice?
Proverbs8:2 On the heights beside the way
Proverbs8:3 beside the gates in front of the town
Proverbs8:4 To you
Proverbs8:5 O simple ones
Proverbs8:6 Hear
Proverbs8:7 for my mouth will utter truth; wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are righteous; there is nothing twisted or crooked in them.
Proverbs8:9 They are all straight to him who understands
Proverbs8:10 Take my instruction instead of silver
Proverbs8:11 for wisdom is better than jewels
Proverbs8:12 I
Proverbs8:13 The fear of the LORD is hatred of evil. Pride and arrogance and the way of evil and perverted speech I hate.
Proverbs8:14 I have counsel and sound wisdom; I have insight; I have strength.
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 by me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me
Proverbs8:18 Riches and honor are with me
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the way of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 granting an inheritance to those who love me
Proverbs8:22 The LORD possessed me at the beginning of his work
Proverbs8:23 Ages ago I was set up
Proverbs8:24 When there were no depths I was brought forth
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains had been shaped
Proverbs8:26 before he had made the earth with its fields
Proverbs8:27 When he established the heavens
Proverbs8:28 when he made firm the skies above
Proverbs8:29 when he assigned to the sea its limit
Proverbs8:30 then I was beside him
Proverbs8:31 rejoicing in his inhabited world and delighting in the children of man.
Proverbs8:32 And now
Proverbs8:33 Hear instruction and be wise
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the one who listens to me
Proverbs8:35 For whoever finds me finds life and obtains favor from the LORD
Proverbs8:36 but he who fails to find me injures himself; all who hate me love death.

Proverbs29:24 The partner of a thief hates his own life; he hears the curse
Proverbs29:25 The fear of man lays a snare
Proverbs29:26 Many seek the face of a ruler
Proverbs29:27 An unjust man is an abomination to the righteous
Proverbs30:1 The words of Agur son of Jakeh. The oracle. The man declares
Proverbs30:2 Surely I am too stupid to be a man. I have not the understanding of a man.
Proverbs30:3 I have not learned wisdom
Proverbs30:4 Who has ascended to heaven and come down? Who has gathered the wind in his fists? Who has wrapped up the waters in a garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is his name
Proverbs30:5 Every word of God proves true; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.
Proverbs30:6 Do not add to his words
Proverbs30:7 Two things I ask of you; deny them not to me before I die:
Proverbs30:8 Remove far from me falsehood and lying; give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with the food that is needful for me
Proverbs30:9 lest I be full and deny you and say
Proverbs30:10 Do not slander a servant to his master
Proverbs30:11 There are those who curse their fathers and do not bless their mothers.
Proverbs30:12 There are those who are clean in their own eyes but are not washed of their filth.
Proverbs30:13 There are those- how lofty are their eyes
Proverbs30:14 There are those whose teeth are swords
Proverbs30:15 The leech has two daughters; Give and Give
Proverbs30:16 Sheol
Proverbs30:17 The eye that mocks a father and scorns to obey a mother will be picked out by the ravens of the valley and eaten by the vultures.
Proverbs30:18 Three things are too wonderful for me; four I do not understand:
Proverbs30:19 the way of an eagle in the sky
Proverbs30:20 This is the way of an adulteress: she eats and wipes her mouth and says
Proverbs30:21 Under three things the earth trembles; under four it cannot bear up:
Proverbs30:22 a slave when he becomes king
Proverbs30:23 an unloved woman when she gets a husband
Proverbs30:24 Four things on earth are small
Proverbs30:25 the ants are a people not strong
Proverbs30:26 the rock badgers are a people not mighty
Proverbs30:27 the locusts have no king
Proverbs30:28 the lizard you can take in your hands
Proverbs30:29 Three things are stately in their tread; four are stately in their stride:
Proverbs30:30 the lion
Proverbs30:31 the strutting rooster
Proverbs30:32 If you have been foolish
Proverbs30:33 For pressing milk produces curds
Proverbs31:1 The words of King Lemuel. An oracle that his mother taught him:
Proverbs31:2 What are you doing
Proverbs31:3 Do not give your strength to women
Proverbs31:4 It is not for kings
Proverbs31:5 lest they drink and forget what has been decreed and pervert the rights of all the afflicted.
Proverbs31:6 Give strong drink to the one who is perishing
Proverbs31:7 let them drink and forget their poverty and remember their misery no more.
Proverbs31:8 Open your mouth for the mute
Proverbs31:9 Open your mouth
Proverbs31:10 An excellent wife who can find? She is far more precious than jewels.
Proverbs31:11 The heart of her husband trusts in her
Proverbs31:12 She does him good
Proverbs31:13 She seeks wool and flax
Proverbs31:14 She is like the ships of the merchant; she brings her food from afar.
Proverbs31:15 She rises while it is yet night and provides food for her household and portions for her maidens.
Proverbs31:16 She considers a field and buys it; with the fruit of her hands she plants a vineyard.
Proverbs31:17 She dresses herself with strength and makes her arms strong.
Proverbs31:18 She perceives that her merchandise is profitable. Her lamp does not go out at night.
Proverbs31:19 She puts her hands to the distaff
Proverbs31:20 She opens her hand to the poor and reaches out her hands to the needy.
Proverbs31:21 She is not afraid of snow for her household
Proverbs31:22 She makes bed coverings for herself; her clothing is fine linen and purple.
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is known in the gates when he sits among the elders of the land.
Proverbs31:24 She makes linen garments and sells them; she delivers sashes to the merchant.
Proverbs31:25 Strength and dignity are her clothing
Proverbs31:26 She opens her mouth with wisdom
Proverbs31:27 She looks well to the ways of her household and does not eat the bread of idleness.
Proverbs31:28 Her children rise up and call her blessed; her husband also
Proverbs31:29 Many women have done excellently
Proverbs31:30 Charm is deceitful
Proverbs31:31 Give her of the fruit of her hands
Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What does man gain by all the toil at which he toils under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 A generation goes
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind blows to the south and goes around to the north; around and around goes the wind
Ecclesiastes1:7 All streams run to the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are full of weariness; a man cannot utter it; the eye is not satisfied with seeing
Ecclesiastes1:9 What has been is what will be
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there a thing of which it is said
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of former things
Ecclesiastes1:12 I the Preacher have been king over Israel in Jerusalem.
Ecclesiastes1:13 And I applied my heart to seek and to search out by wisdom all that is done under heaven. It is an unhappy business that God has given to the children of man to be busy with.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen everything that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is crooked cannot be made straight
Ecclesiastes1:16 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I applied my heart to know wisdom and to know madness and folly. I perceived that this also is but a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes1:18 For in much wisdom is much vexation
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I searched with my heart how to cheer my body with wine- my heart still guiding me with wisdom- and how to lay hold on folly
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made great works. I built houses and planted vineyards for myself.
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made myself gardens and parks
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made myself pools from which to water the forest of growing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought male and female slaves
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also gathered for myself silver and gold and the treasure of kings and provinces. I got singers
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I became great and surpassed all who were before me in Jerusalem. Also my wisdom remained with me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 And whatever my eyes desired I did not keep from them. I kept my heart from no pleasure
Ecclesiastes2:11 Then I considered all that my hands had done and the toil I had expended in doing it
Ecclesiastes2:12 So I turned to consider wisdom and madness and folly. For what can the man do who comes after the king? Only what has already been done.
Ecclesiastes2:13 Then I saw that there is more gain in wisdom than in folly
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise person has his eyes in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:16 For of the wise as of the fool there is no enduring remembrance
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 I hated all my toil in which I toil under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 and who knows whether he will be wise or a fool? Yet he will be master of all for which I toiled and used my wisdom under the sun. This also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I turned about and gave my heart up to despair over all the toil of my labors under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:21 because sometimes a person who has toiled with wisdom and knowledge and skill must leave everything to be enjoyed by someone who did not toil for it. This also is vanity and a great evil.
Ecclesiastes2:22 What has a man from all the toil and striving of heart with which he toils beneath the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 For all his days are full of sorrow
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for a person than that he should eat and drink and find enjoyment in his toil. This also
Ecclesiastes2:25 for apart from him who can eat or who can have enjoyment?
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to the one who pleases him God has given wisdom and knowledge and joy
Ecclesiastes3:1 For everything there is a season
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to weep
Ecclesiastes3:5 a time to cast away stones
Ecclesiastes3:6 a time to seek
Ecclesiastes3:7 a time to tear
Ecclesiastes3:8 a time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What gain has the worker from his toil?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the business that God has given to the children of man to be busy with.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything beautiful in its time. Also
Ecclesiastes3:12 I perceived that there is nothing better for them than to be joyful and to do good as long as they live;
Ecclesiastes3:13 also that everyone should eat and drink and take pleasure in all his toil- this is God's gift to man.
Ecclesiastes3:14 I perceived that whatever God does endures forever; nothing can be added to it
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is
Ecclesiastes3:16 Moreover
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said in my heart with regard to the children of man that God is testing them that they may see that they themselves are but beasts.
Ecclesiastes3:19 For what happens to the children of man and what happens to the beasts is the same; as one dies
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to one place. All are from the dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows whether the spirit of man goes upward and the spirit of the beast goes down into the earth?
Ecclesiastes3:22 So I saw that there is nothing better than that a man should rejoice in his work
Ecclesiastes4:1 Again I saw all the oppressions that are done under the sun. And behold
Ecclesiastes4:2 And I thought the dead who are already dead more fortunate than the living who are still alive.
Ecclesiastes4:3 But better than both is he who has not yet been and has not seen the evil deeds that are done under the sun.
Ecclesiastes4:4 Then I saw that all toil and all skill in work come from a man's envy of his neighbor. This also is vanity and a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his hands and eats his own flesh.
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better is a handful of quietness than two hands full of toil and a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes4:7 Again
Ecclesiastes4:8 one person who has no other
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if they fall
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 And though a man might prevail against one who is alone
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better was a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who no longer knew how to take advice.
Ecclesiastes4:14 For he went from prison to the throne
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw all the living who move about under the sun
Ecclesiastes4:16 There was no end of all the people
Ecclesiastes5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God. To draw near to listen is better than to offer the sacrifice of fools
Ecclesiastes5:2 Be not rash with your mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 For a dream comes with much business
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you vow a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay.
Ecclesiastes5:6 Let not your mouth lead you into sin
Ecclesiastes5:7 For when dreams increase and words grow many
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see in a province the oppression of the poor and the violation of justice and righteousness
Ecclesiastes5:9 But this is gain for a land in every way: a king committed to cultivated fields.
Ecclesiastes5:10 He who loves money will not be satisfied with money
Ecclesiastes5:11 When goods increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 Sweet is the sleep of a laborer
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a grievous evil that I have seen under the sun: riches were kept by their owner to his hurt
Ecclesiastes5:14 and those riches were lost in a bad venture. And he is father of a son
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he came from his mother's womb he shall go again
Ecclesiastes5:16 This also is a grievous evil: just as he came
Ecclesiastes5:17 Moreover
Ecclesiastes5:18 Behold
Ecclesiastes5:19 Everyone also to whom God has given wealth and possessions and power to enjoy them
Ecclesiastes5:20 For he will not much remember the days of his life because God keeps him occupied with joy in his heart.
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is an evil that I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 a man to whom God gives wealth
Ecclesiastes6:3 If a man fathers a hundred children and lives many years
Ecclesiastes6:4 For it comes in vanity and goes in darkness
Ecclesiastes6:5 Moreover
Ecclesiastes6:6 Even though he should live a thousand years twice over
Ecclesiastes6:7 All the toil of man is for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what advantage has the wise man over the fool? And what does the poor man have who knows how to conduct himself before the living?
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of the appetite: this also is vanity and a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever has come to be has already been named
Ecclesiastes6:11 The more words
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for man while he lives the few days of his vain life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than precious ointment
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to the house of mourning than to go to the house of feasting
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better for a man to hear the rebuke of the wise than to hear the song of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:6 For as the crackling of thorns under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Surely oppression drives the wise into madness
Ecclesiastes7:8 Better is the end of a thing than its beginning
Ecclesiastes7:9 Be not quick in your spirit to become angry
Ecclesiastes7:10 Say not
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is good with an inheritance
Ecclesiastes7:12 For the protection of wisdom is like the protection of money
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God: who can make straight what he has made crooked?
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity be joyful
Ecclesiastes7:15 In my vain life I have seen everything. There is a righteous man who perishes in his righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Be not overly righteous
Ecclesiastes7:17 Be not overly wicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that you should take hold of this
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom gives strength to the wise man more than ten rulers who are in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 Surely there is not a righteous man on earth who does good and never sins.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Do not take to heart all the things that people say
Ecclesiastes7:22 Your heart knows that many times you have yourself cursed others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 All this I have tested by wisdom. I said
Ecclesiastes7:24 That which has been is far off
Ecclesiastes7:25 I turned my heart to know and to search out and to seek wisdom and the scheme of things
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I find something more bitter than death: the woman whose heart is snares and nets
Ecclesiastes7:27 Behold
Ecclesiastes7:28 which my soul has sought repeatedly
Ecclesiastes7:29 See
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like the wise?And who knows the interpretation of a thing? A man's wisdom makes his face shine
Ecclesiastes8:2 I say: Keep the king's command
Ecclesiastes8:3 Be not hasty to go from his presence. Do not take your stand in an evil cause
Ecclesiastes8:4 For the word of the king is supreme
Ecclesiastes8:5 Whoever keeps a command will know no evil thing
Ecclesiastes8:6 For there is a time and a way for everything
Ecclesiastes8:7 For he does not know what is to be
Ecclesiastes8:8 No man has power to retain the spirit
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this I observed while applying my heart to all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes8:10 Then I saw the wicked buried. They used to go in and out of the holy place and were praised in the city where they had done such things. This also is vanity.
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because the sentence against an evil deed is not executed speedily
Ecclesiastes8:12 Though a sinner does evil a hundred times and prolongs his life
Ecclesiastes8:13 But it will not be well with the wicked
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is a vanity that takes place on earth
Ecclesiastes8:15 And I commend joy
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my heart to know wisdom
Ecclesiastes8:17 then I saw all the work of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 But all this I laid to heart
Ecclesiastes9:2 It is the same for all
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil in all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:4 But he who is joined with all the living has hope
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they will die
Ecclesiastes9:6 Their love and their hate and their envy have already perished
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let your garments be always white. Let not oil be lacking on your head.
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with the wife whom you love
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever your hand finds to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 Again I saw that under the sun the race is not to the swift
Ecclesiastes9:12 For man does not know his time. Like fish that are taken in an evil net
Ecclesiastes9:13 I have also seen this example of wisdom under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a little city with few men in it
Ecclesiastes9:15 But there was found in it a poor
Ecclesiastes9:16 But I say that wisdom is better than might
Ecclesiastes9:17 The words of the wise heard in quiet are better than the shouting of a ruler among fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies make the perfumer's ointment give off a stench; so a little folly outweighs wisdom and honor.
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise man's heart inclines him to the right
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even when the fool walks on the road
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the anger of the ruler rises against you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil that I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 folly is set in many high places
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen slaves on horses
Ecclesiastes10:8 He who digs a pit will fall into it
Ecclesiastes10:9 He who quarries stones is hurt by them
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the iron is blunt
Ecclesiastes10:11 If the serpent bites before it is charmed
Ecclesiastes10:12 The words of a wise man's mouth win him favor
Ecclesiastes10:13 The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishness
Ecclesiastes10:14 A fool multiplies words
Ecclesiastes10:15 The toil of a fool wearies him
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to you
Ecclesiastes10:17 Happy are you
Ecclesiastes10:18 Through sloth the roof sinks in
Ecclesiastes10:19 Bread is made for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Even in your thought
Ecclesiastes11:1 Cast your bread upon the waters
Ecclesiastes11:2 Give a portion to seven
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds are full of rain
Ecclesiastes11:4 He who observes the wind will not sow
Ecclesiastes11:5 As you do not know the way the spirit comes to the bones in the womb of a woman with child
Ecclesiastes11:6 In the morning sow your seed
Ecclesiastes11:7 Light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 So if a person lives many years
Ecclesiastes11:9 Rejoice
Ecclesiastes11:10 Remove vexation from your heart
Ecclesiastes12:1 Remember also your Creator in the days of your youth
Ecclesiastes12:2 before the sun and the light and the moon and the stars are darkened and the clouds return after the rain
Ecclesiastes12:3 in the day when the keepers of the house tremble
Ecclesiastes12:4 and the doors on the street are shut- when the sound of the grinding is low
Ecclesiastes12:5 they are afraid also of what is high
Ecclesiastes12:6 before the silver cord is snapped
Ecclesiastes12:7 and the dust returns to the earth as it was
Ecclesiastes12:8 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes12:9 Besides being wise
Ecclesiastes12:10 The Preacher sought to find words of delight
Ecclesiastes12:11 The words of the wise are like goads
Ecclesiastes12:12 My son
Ecclesiastes12:13 The end of the matter; all has been heard. Fear God and keep his commandments
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God will bring every deed into judgment

SongOfSongs1:3 your anointing oils are fragrant; your name is oil poured out; therefore virgins love you.
SongOfSongs1:4 Draw me after you; let us run. The king has brought me into his chambers. We will exult and rejoice in you; we will extol your love more than wine; rightly do they love you.

Isaiah1:1 The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz
Isaiah1:2 Hear
Isaiah1:3 The ox knows its owner
Isaiah1:4 Ah
Isaiah1:5 Why will you still be struck down? Why will you continue to rebel? The whole head is sick
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even to the head
Isaiah1:7 Your country lies desolate; your cities are burned with fire; in your very presence foreigners devour your land; it is desolate
Isaiah1:8 And the daughter of Zion is left like a booth in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 If the LORD of hosts had not left us a few survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the LORD
Isaiah1:11 What to me is the multitude of your sacrifices? says the LORD; I have had enough of burnt offerings of rams and the fat of well-fed beasts; I do not delight in the blood of bulls
Isaiah1:12 When you come to appear before me
Isaiah1:13 Bring no more vain offerings; incense is an abomination to me. New moon and Sabbath and the calling of convocations- I cannot endure iniquity and solemn assembly.
Isaiah1:14 Your new moons and your appointed feasts my soul hates; they have become a burden to me; I am weary of bearing them.
Isaiah1:15 When you spread out your hands
Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves; make yourselves clean; remove the evil of your deeds from before my eyes; cease to do evil
Isaiah1:17 learn to do good; seek justice
Isaiah1:18 Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 but if you refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 How the faithful city has become a whore
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross
Isaiah1:23 Your princes are rebels and companions of thieves. Everyone loves a bribe and runs after gifts. They do not bring justice to the fatherless
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord declares
Isaiah1:25 I will turn my hand against you and will smelt away your dross as with lye and remove all your alloy.
Isaiah1:26 And I will restore your judges as at the first
Isaiah1:27 Zion shall be redeemed by justice
Isaiah1:28 But rebels and sinners shall be broken together
Isaiah1:29 For they shall be ashamed of the oaks that you desired; and you shall blush for the gardens that you have chosen.
Isaiah1:30 For you shall be like an oak whose leaf withers
Isaiah1:31 And the strong shall become tinder
Isaiah2:1 The word that Isaiah the son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem.
Isaiah2:2 It shall come to pass in the latter days that the mountain of the house of the LORD shall be established as the highest of the mountains
Isaiah2:3 and many peoples shall come
Isaiah2:4 He shall judge between the nations
Isaiah2:5 O house of Jacob
Isaiah2:6 For you have rejected your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land is filled with silver and gold
Isaiah2:8 Their land is filled with idols; they bow down to the work of their hands
Isaiah2:9 So man is humbled
Isaiah2:10 Enter into the rock and hide in the dust from before the terror of the LORD
Isaiah2:11 The haughty looks of man shall be brought low
Isaiah2:12 For the LORD of hosts has a day against all that is proud and lofty
Isaiah2:13 against all the cedars of Lebanon
Isaiah2:14 against all the lofty mountains
Isaiah2:15 against every high tower
Isaiah2:16 against all the ships of Tarshish
Isaiah2:17 And the haughtiness of man shall be humbled
Isaiah2:18 And the idols shall utterly pass away.
Isaiah2:19 And people shall enter the caves of the rocks and the holes of the ground
Isaiah2:20 In that day mankind will cast away their idols of silver and their idols of gold
Isaiah2:21 to enter the caverns of the rocks and the clefts of the cliffs
Isaiah2:22 Stop regarding man in whose nostrils is breath
Isaiah3:1 For behold
Isaiah3:2 the mighty man and the soldier
Isaiah3:3 the captain of fifty and the man of rank
Isaiah3:4 And I will make boys their princes
Isaiah3:5 And the people will oppress one another
Isaiah3:6 For a man will take hold of his brother in the house of his father
Isaiah3:7 in that day he will speak out
Isaiah3:8 For Jerusalem has stumbled
Isaiah3:9 For the look on their faces bears witness against them; they proclaim their sin like Sodom; they do not hide it. Woe to them! For they have brought evil on themselves.
Isaiah3:10 Tell the righteous that it shall be well with them
Isaiah3:11 Woe to the wicked! It shall be ill with him
Isaiah3:12 My people- infants are their oppressors
Isaiah3:13 The LORD has taken his place to contend; he stands to judge peoples.
Isaiah3:14 The LORD will enter into judgment with the elders and princes of his people: It is you who have devoured the vineyard
Isaiah3:15 What do you mean by crushing my people
Isaiah3:16 The LORD said: Because the daughters of Zion are haughty and walk with outstretched necks
Isaiah3:17 therefore the Lord will strike with a scab the heads of the daughters of Zion
Isaiah3:18 In that day the Lord will take away the finery of the anklets
Isaiah3:19 the pendants
Isaiah3:20 the headdresses
Isaiah3:21 the signet rings and nose rings;
Isaiah3:22 the festal robes
Isaiah3:23 the mirrors
Isaiah3:24 Instead of perfume there will be rottenness; and instead of a belt
Isaiah3:25 Your men shall fall by the sword and your mighty men in battle.
Isaiah3:26 And her gates shall lament and mourn; empty
Isaiah4:1 And seven women shall take hold of one man in that day
Isaiah4:2 In that day the branch of the LORD shall be beautiful and glorious
Isaiah4:3 And he who is left in Zion and remains in Jerusalem will be called holy
Isaiah4:4 when the Lord shall have washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion and cleansed the bloodstains of Jerusalem from its midst by a spirit of judgment and by a spirit of burning.
Isaiah4:5 Then the LORD will create over the whole site of Mount Zion and over her assemblies a cloud by day
Isaiah4:6 There will be a booth for shade by day from the heat
Isaiah5:1 Let me sing for my beloved my love song concerning his vineyard: My beloved had a vineyard on a very fertile hill.
Isaiah5:2 He dug it and cleared it of stones
Isaiah5:3 And now
Isaiah5:4 What more was there to do for my vineyard
Isaiah5:5 And now I will tell you what I will do to my vineyard. I will remove its hedge
Isaiah5:6 I will make it a waste; it shall not be pruned or hoed
Isaiah5:7 For the vineyard of the LORD of hosts is the house of Israel
Isaiah5:8 Woe to those who join house to house
Isaiah5:9 The LORD of hosts has sworn in my hearing: Surely many houses shall be desolate
Isaiah5:10 For ten acres of vineyard shall yield but one bath
Isaiah5:11 Woe to those who rise early in the morning
Isaiah5:12 They have lyre and harp
Isaiah5:13 Therefore my people go into exile for lack of knowledge; their honored men go hungry
Isaiah5:14 Therefore Sheol has enlarged its appetite and opened its mouth beyond measure
Isaiah5:15 Man is humbled
Isaiah5:16 But the LORD of hosts is exalted in justice
Isaiah5:17 Then shall the lambs graze as in their pasture
Isaiah5:18 Woe to those who draw iniquity with cords of falsehood
Isaiah5:19 who say: Let him be quick
Isaiah5:20 Woe to those who call evil good and good evil
Isaiah5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes
Isaiah5:22 Woe to those who are heroes at drinking wine
Isaiah5:23 who acquit the guilty for a bribe
Isaiah5:24 Therefore
Isaiah5:25 Therefore the anger of the LORD was kindled against his people
Isaiah5:26 He will raise a signal for nations afar off
Isaiah5:27 None is weary
Isaiah5:28 their arrows are sharp
Isaiah5:29 Their roaring is like a lion
Isaiah5:30 They will growl over it on that day
Isaiah6:1 In the year that King Uzziah died I saw the Lord sitting upon a throne
Isaiah6:2 Above him stood the seraphim. Each had six wings: with two he covered his face
Isaiah6:3 And one called to another and said: Holy
Isaiah6:4 And the foundations of the thresholds shook at the voice of him who called
Isaiah6:5 And I said: Woe is me! For I am lost; for I am a man of unclean lips
Isaiah6:6 Then one of the seraphim flew to me
Isaiah6:7 And he touched my mouth and said: Behold
Isaiah6:8 And I heard the voice of the Lord saying
Isaiah6:9 And he said
Isaiah6:10 Make the heart of this people dull
Isaiah6:11 Then I said
Isaiah6:12 and the LORD removes people far away
Isaiah6:13 And though a tenth remain in it
Isaiah7:1 In the days of Ahaz the son of Jotham
Isaiah7:2 When the house of David was told
Isaiah7:3 And the LORD said to Isaiah
Isaiah7:4 And say to him
Isaiah7:5 Because Syria
Isaiah7:6 Let us go up against Judah and terrify it
Isaiah7:7 thus says the Lord GOD: ' It shall not stand
Isaiah7:8 For the head of Syria is Damascus
Isaiah7:9 'And the head of Ephraim is Samaria
Isaiah7:10 Again the LORD spoke to Ahaz
Isaiah7:11 Ask a sign of the LORD your God; let it be deep as Sheol or high as heaven.
Isaiah7:12 But Ahaz said
Isaiah7:13 And he said
Isaiah7:14 Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign. Behold
Isaiah7:15 He shall eat curds and honey when he knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good.
Isaiah7:16 For before the boy knows how to refuse the evil and choose the good
Isaiah7:17 The LORD will bring upon you and upon your people and upon your father's house such days as have not come since the day that Ephraim departed from Judah- the king of Assyria.
Isaiah7:18 In that day the LORD will whistle for the fly that is at the end of the streams of Egypt
Isaiah7:19 And they will all come and settle in the steep ravines
Isaiah7:20 In that day the Lord will shave with a razor that is hired beyond the River- with the king of Assyria- the head and the hair of the feet
Isaiah7:21 In that day a man will keep alive a young cow and two sheep
Isaiah7:22 and because of the abundance of milk that they give
Isaiah7:23 In that day every place where there used to be a thousand vines
Isaiah7:24 With bow and arrows a man will come there
Isaiah7:25 And as for all the hills that used to be hoed with a hoe
Isaiah8:1 Then the LORD said to me
Isaiah8:2 And I will get reliable witnesses
Isaiah8:3 And I went to the prophetess
Isaiah8:4 for before the boy knows how to cry 'My father' or 'My mother
Isaiah8:5 The LORD spoke to me again:
Isaiah8:6 Because this people have refused the waters of Shiloah that flow gently
Isaiah8:7 therefore
Isaiah8:8 and it will sweep on into Judah
Isaiah8:9 Be broken
Isaiah8:10 Take counsel together
Isaiah8:11 For the LORD spoke thus to me with his strong hand upon me
Isaiah8:12 Do not call conspiracy all that this people calls conspiracy
Isaiah8:13 But the LORD of hosts
Isaiah8:14 And he will become a sanctuary and a stone of offense and a rock of stumbling to both houses of Israel
Isaiah8:15 And many shall stumble on it. They shall fall and be broken; they shall be snared and taken.
Isaiah8:16 Bind up the testimony; seal the teaching among my disciples.
Isaiah8:17 I will wait for the LORD
Isaiah8:18 Behold
Isaiah8:19 And when they say to you
Isaiah8:20 To the teaching and to the testimony! If they will not speak according to this word
Isaiah8:21 They will pass through the land
Isaiah8:22 And they will look to the earth
Isaiah9:1 But there will be no gloom for her who was in anguish. In the former time he brought into contempt the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali
Isaiah9:2 The people who walked in darkness have seen a great light; those who dwelt in a land of deep darkness
Isaiah9:3 You have multiplied the nation; you have increased its joy; they rejoice before you as with joy at the harvest
Isaiah9:4 For the yoke of his burden
Isaiah9:5 For every boot of the tramping warrior in battle tumult and every garment rolled in blood will be burned as fuel for the fire.
Isaiah9:6 For to us a child is born
Isaiah9:7 Of the increase of his government and of peace there will be no end
Isaiah9:8 The Lord has sent a word against Jacob
Isaiah9:9 and all the people will know
Isaiah9:10 The bricks have fallen
Isaiah9:11 But the LORD raises the adversaries of Rezin against him
Isaiah9:12 The Syrians on the east and the Philistines on the west devour Israel with open mouth. For all this his anger has not turned away
Isaiah9:13 The people did not turn to him who struck them
Isaiah9:14 So the LORD cut off from Israel head and tail
Isaiah9:15 the elder and honored man is the head
Isaiah9:16 for those who guide this people have been leading them astray
Isaiah9:17 Therefore the Lord does not rejoice over their young men
Isaiah9:18 For wickedness burns like a fire; it consumes briers and thorns; it kindles the thickets of the forest
Isaiah9:19 Through the wrath of the LORD of hosts the land is scorched
Isaiah9:20 They slice meat on the right
Isaiah9:21 Manasseh devours Ephraim
Isaiah10:1 Woe to those who decree iniquitous decrees
Isaiah10:2 to turn aside the needy from justice and to rob the poor of my people of their right
Isaiah10:3 What will you do on the day of punishment
Isaiah10:4 Nothing remains but to crouch among the prisoners or fall among the slain. For all this his anger has not turned away
Isaiah10:5 Ah
Isaiah10:6 Against a godless nation I send him
Isaiah10:7 But he does not so intend
Isaiah10:8 for he says: Are not my commanders all kings?
Isaiah10:9 Is not Calno like Carchemish? Is not Hamath like Arpad? Is not Samaria like Damascus?
Isaiah10:10 As my hand has reached to the kingdoms of the idols
Isaiah10:11 shall I not do to Jerusalem and her idols as I have done to Samaria and her images?
Isaiah10:12 When the Lord has finished all his work on Mount Zion and on Jerusalem
Isaiah10:13 For he says: By the strength of my hand I have done it
Isaiah10:14 My hand has found like a nest the wealth of the peoples; and as one gathers eggs that have been forsaken
Isaiah10:15 Shall the axe boast over him who hews with it
Isaiah10:16 Therefore the Lord GOD of hosts will send wasting sickness among his stout warriors
Isaiah10:17 The light of Israel will become a fire
Isaiah10:18 The glory of his forest and of his fruitful land the LORD will destroy
Isaiah10:19 The remnant of the trees of his forest will be so few that a child can write them down.
Isaiah10:20 In that day the remnant of Israel and the survivors of the house of Jacob will no more lean on him who struck them
Isaiah10:21 A remnant will return
Isaiah10:22 For though your people Israel be as the sand of the sea
Isaiah10:23 For the Lord GOD of hosts will make a full end
Isaiah10:24 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD of hosts: O my people
Isaiah10:25 For in a very little while my fury will come to an end
Isaiah10:26 And the LORD of hosts will wield against them a whip
Isaiah10:27 And in that day his burden will depart from your shoulder
Isaiah10:28 He has come to Aiath; he has passed through Migron; at Michmash he stores his baggage;
Isaiah10:29 they have crossed over the pass; at Geba they lodge for the night; Ramah trembles; Gibeah of Saul has fled.
Isaiah10:30 Cry aloud
Isaiah10:31 Madmenah is in flight; the inhabitants of Gebim flee for safety.
Isaiah10:32 This very day he will halt at Nob; he will shake his fist at the mount of the daughter of Zion
Isaiah10:33 Behold
Isaiah10:34 He will cut down the thickets of the forest with an axe
Isaiah11:1 There shall come forth a shoot from the stump of Jesse
Isaiah11:2 And the Spirit of the LORD shall rest upon him
Isaiah11:3 And his delight shall be in the fear of the LORD. He shall not judge by what his eyes see
Isaiah11:4 but with righteousness he shall judge the poor
Isaiah11:5 Righteousness shall be the belt of his waist
Isaiah11:6 The wolf shall dwell with the lamb
Isaiah11:7 The cow and the bear shall graze; their young shall lie down together; and the lion shall eat straw like the ox.
Isaiah11:8 The nursing child shall play over the hole of the cobra
Isaiah11:9 They shall not hurt or destroy in all my holy mountain; for the earth shall be full of the knowledge of the LORD as the waters cover the sea.
Isaiah11:10 In that day the root of Jesse
Isaiah11:11 In that day the Lord will extend his hand yet a second time to recover the remnant that remains of his people
Isaiah11:12 He will raise a signal for the nations and will assemble the banished of Israel
Isaiah11:13 The jealousy of Ephraim shall depart
Isaiah11:14 But they shall swoop down on the shoulder of the Philistines in the west
Isaiah11:15 And the LORD will utterly destroy the tongue of the Sea of Egypt
Isaiah11:16 And there will be a highway from Assyria for the remnant that remains of his people

Isaiah12:2 Behold
Isaiah12:3 With joy you will draw water from the wells of salvation.
Isaiah12:4 And you will say in that day: Give thanks to the LORD
Isaiah12:5 Sing praises to the LORD
Isaiah12:6 Shout
Isaiah13:1 The oracle concerning Babylon which Isaiah the son of Amoz saw.
Isaiah13:2 On a bare hill raise a signal; cry aloud to them; wave the hand for them to enter the gates of the nobles.
Isaiah13:3 I myself have commanded my consecrated ones
Isaiah13:4 The sound of a tumult is on the mountains as of a great multitude! The sound of an uproar of kingdoms
Isaiah13:5 They come from a distant land
Isaiah13:6 Wail
Isaiah13:7 Therefore all hands will be feeble
Isaiah13:8 They will be dismayed: pangs and agony will seize them; they will be in anguish like a woman in labor. They will look aghast at one another; their faces will be aflame.
Isaiah13:9 Behold
Isaiah13:10 For the stars of the heavens and their constellations will not give their light; the sun will be dark at its rising
Isaiah13:11 I will punish the world for its evil
Isaiah13:12 I will make people more rare than fine gold
Isaiah13:13 Therefore I will make the heavens tremble
Isaiah13:14 And like a hunted gazelle
Isaiah13:15 Whoever is found will be thrust through
Isaiah13:16 Their infants will be dashed in pieces before their eyes; their houses will be plundered and their wives ravished.
Isaiah13:17 Behold
Isaiah13:18 Their bows will slaughter the young men; they will have no mercy on the fruit of the womb; their eyes will not pity children.
Isaiah13:19 And Babylon
Isaiah13:20 It will never be inhabited or lived in for all generations; no Arab will pitch his tent there; no shepherds will make their flocks lie down there.
Isaiah13:21 But wild animals will lie down there
Isaiah13:22 Hyenas will cry in its towers
Isaiah14:1 For the LORD will have compassion on Jacob and will again choose Israel
Isaiah14:2 And the peoples will take them and bring them to their place
Isaiah14:3 When the LORD has given you rest from your pain and turmoil and the hard service with which you were made to serve
Isaiah14:4 you will take up this taunt against the king of Babylon: How the oppressor has ceased
Isaiah14:5 The LORD has broken the staff of the wicked
Isaiah14:6 that struck the peoples in wrath with unceasing blows
Isaiah14:7 The whole earth is at rest and quiet; they break forth into singing.
Isaiah14:8 The cypresses rejoice at you
Isaiah14:9 Sheol beneath is stirred up to meet you when you come; it rouses the shades to greet you
Isaiah14:10 All of them will answer and say to you: 'You too have become as weak as we! You have become like us!'
Isaiah14:11 Your pomp is brought down to Sheol
Isaiah14:12 How you are fallen from heaven
Isaiah14:13 You said in your heart
Isaiah14:14 I will ascend above the heights of the clouds; I will make myself like the Most High.'
Isaiah14:15 But you are brought down to Sheol
Isaiah14:16 Those who see you will stare at you and ponder over you: 'Is this the man who made the earth tremble
Isaiah14:17 who made the world like a desert and overthrew its cities
Isaiah14:18 All the kings of the nations lie in glory
Isaiah14:19 but you are cast out
Isaiah14:20 You will not be joined with them in burial
Isaiah14:21 Prepare slaughter for his sons because of the guilt of their fathers
Isaiah14:22 I will rise up against them
Isaiah14:23 And I will make it a possession of the hedgehog
Isaiah14:24 The LORD of hosts has sworn: As I have planned
Isaiah14:25 that I will break the Assyrian in my land
Isaiah14:26 This is the purpose that is purposed concerning the whole earth
Isaiah14:27 For the LORD of hosts has purposed
