Isaiah14:29 Rejoice not
Isaiah14:30 And the firstborn of the poor will graze
Isaiah14:31 Wail
Isaiah14:32 What will one answer the messengers of the nation? The LORD has founded Zion
Isaiah15:1 An oracle concerning Moab. Because Ar of Moab is laid waste in a night
Isaiah15:2 He has gone up to the temple
Isaiah15:3 in the streets they wear sackcloth; on the housetops and in the squares everyone wails and melts in tears.
Isaiah15:4 Heshbon and Elealeh cry out; their voice is heard as far as Jahaz; therefore the armed men of Moab cry aloud; his soul trembles.
Isaiah15:5 My heart cries out for Moab; her fugitives flee to Zoar
Isaiah15:6 the waters of Nimrim are a desolation; the grass is withered
Isaiah15:7 Therefore the abundance they have gained and what they have laid up they carry away over the Brook of the Willows.
Isaiah15:8 For a cry has gone around the land of Moab; her wailing reaches to Eglaim; her wailing reaches to Beer-elim.
Isaiah15:9 For the waters of Dibon are full of blood; for I will bring upon Dibon even more
Isaiah16:1 Send the lamb to the ruler of the land
Isaiah16:2 Like fleeing birds
Isaiah16:3 Give counsel; grant justice; make your shade like night at the height of noon; shelter the outcasts; do not reveal the fugitive;
Isaiah16:4 let the outcasts of Moab sojourn among you; be a shelter to them from the destroyer. When the oppressor is no more and destruction has ceased
Isaiah16:5 then a throne will be established in steadfast love
Isaiah16:6 We have heard of the pride of Moab- how proud he is!- of his arrogance
Isaiah16:7 Therefore let Moab wail for Moab
Isaiah16:8 For the fields of Heshbon languish
Isaiah16:9 Therefore I weep with the weeping of Jazer for the vine of Sibmah; I drench you with my tears
Isaiah16:10 And joy and gladness are taken away from the fruitful field
Isaiah16:11 Therefore my inner parts moan like a lyre for Moab
Isaiah16:12 And when Moab presents himself
Isaiah16:13 This is the word that the LORD spoke concerning Moab in the past.
Isaiah16:14 But now the LORD has spoken

Isaiah18:2 which sends ambassadors by the sea
Isaiah18:3 All you inhabitants of the world
Isaiah18:4 For thus the LORD said to me: I will quietly look from my dwelling like clear heat in sunshine
Isaiah18:5 For before the harvest

Isaiah19:1 An oracle concerning Egypt. Behold
Isaiah19:2 And I will stir up Egyptians against Egyptians
Isaiah19:3 and the spirit of the Egyptians within them will be emptied out
Isaiah19:4 and I will give over the Egyptians into the hand of a hard master
Isaiah19:5 And the waters of the sea will be dried up
Isaiah19:6 and its canals will become foul
Isaiah19:7 There will be bare places by the Nile
Isaiah19:8 The fishermen will mourn and lament
Isaiah19:9 The workers in combed flax will be in despair
Isaiah19:10 Those who are the pillars of the land will be crushed
Isaiah19:11 The princes of Zoan are utterly foolish; the wisest counselors of Pharaoh give stupid counsel. How can you say to Pharaoh
Isaiah19:12 Where then are your wise men? Let them tell you that they might know what the LORD of hosts has purposed against Egypt.
Isaiah19:13 The princes of Zoan have become fools
Isaiah19:14 The LORD has mingled within her a spirit of confusion
Isaiah19:15 And there will be nothing for Egypt that head or tail
Isaiah19:16 In that day the Egyptians will be like women
Isaiah19:17 And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians. Everyone to whom it is mentioned will fear because of the purpose that the LORD of hosts has purposed against them.
Isaiah19:18 In that day there will be five cities in the land of Egypt that speak the language of Canaan and swear allegiance to the LORD of hosts. One of these will be called the City of Destruction.
Isaiah19:19 In that day there will be an altar to the LORD in the midst of the land of Egypt
Isaiah19:20 It will be a sign and a witness to the LORD of hosts in the land of Egypt. When they cry to the LORD because of oppressors
Isaiah19:21 And the LORD will make himself known to the Egyptians
Isaiah19:22 And the LORD will strike Egypt
Isaiah19:23 In that day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria
Isaiah19:24 In that day Israel will be the third with Egypt and Assyria
Isaiah19:25 whom the LORD of hosts has blessed

Isaiah21:3 Therefore my loins are filled with anguish; pangs have seized me
Isaiah21:4 My heart staggers; horror has appalled me; the twilight I longed for has been turned for me into trembling.

Isaiah21:6 For thus the Lord said to me: Go

Isaiah21:8 Then he who saw cried out: Upon a watchtower I stand

Isaiah21:12 The watchman says: Morning comes

Isaiah22:1 The oracle concerning the valley of vision. What do you mean that you have gone up
Isaiah22:2 you who are full of shoutings
Isaiah22:3 All your leaders have fled together; without the bow they were captured. All of you who were found were captured
Isaiah22:4 Therefore I said: Look away from me; let me weep bitter tears; do not labor to comfort me concerning the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Isaiah22:5 For the Lord GOD of hosts has a day of tumult and trampling and confusion in the valley of vision
Isaiah22:6 And Elam bore the quiver with chariots and horsemen
Isaiah22:7 Your choicest valleys were full of chariots
Isaiah22:8 He has taken away the covering of Judah. In that day you looked to the weapons of the House of the Forest
Isaiah22:9 and you saw that the breaches of the city of David were many. You collected the waters of the lower pool
Isaiah22:10 and you counted the houses of Jerusalem
Isaiah22:11 You made a reservoir between the two walls for the water of the old pool. But you did not look to him who did it
Isaiah22:12 In that day the Lord GOD of hosts called for weeping and mourning
Isaiah22:13 and behold
Isaiah22:14 The LORD of hosts has revealed himself in my ears: Surely this iniquity will not be atoned for you until you die
Isaiah22:15 Thus says the Lord GOD of hosts
Isaiah22:16 What have you to do here
Isaiah22:17 Behold
Isaiah22:18 and whirl you around and around
Isaiah22:19 I will thrust you from your office
Isaiah22:20 In that day I will call my servant Eliakim the son of Hilkiah
Isaiah22:21 and I will clothe him with your robe
Isaiah22:22 And I will place on his shoulder the key of the house of David. He shall open
Isaiah22:23 And I will fasten him like a peg in a secure place
Isaiah22:24 And they will hang on him the whole honor of his father's house
Isaiah22:25 In that day
Isaiah23:1 The oracle concerning Tyre. Wail
Isaiah23:2 Be still
Isaiah23:3 And on many waters your revenue was the grain of Shihor
Isaiah23:4 Be ashamed
Isaiah23:5 When the report comes to Egypt
Isaiah23:6 Cross over to Tarshish; wail
Isaiah23:7 Is this your exultant city whose origin is from days of old
Isaiah23:8 Who has purposed this against Tyre
Isaiah23:9 The LORD of hosts has purposed it
Isaiah23:10 Cross over your land like the Nile
Isaiah23:11 He has stretched out his hand over the sea; he has shaken the kingdoms; the LORD has given command concerning Canaan to destroy its strongholds.
Isaiah23:12 And he said: You will no more exult

Isaiah24:1 Behold

Isaiah24:8 The mirth of the tambourines is stilled

Isaiah24:10 The wasted city is broken down; every house is shut up so that none can enter.
Isaiah24:11 There is an outcry in the streets for lack of wine; all joy has grown dark; the gladness of the earth is banished.
Isaiah24:12 Desolation is left in the city; the gates are battered into ruins.
Isaiah24:13 For thus it shall be in the midst of the earth among the nations

Isaiah44:13 The carpenter stretches a line; he marks it out with a pencil. He shapes it with planes and marks it with a compass. He shapes it into the figure of a man
Isaiah44:14 He cuts down cedars
Isaiah44:15 Then it becomes fuel for a man. He takes a part of it and warms himself; he kindles a fire and bakes bread. Also he makes a god and worships it; he makes it an idol and falls down before it.

Jeremiah2:1 The word of the LORD came to me
Jeremiah2:2 Go and proclaim in the hearing of Jerusalem
Jeremiah2:3 Israel was holy to the LORD
Jeremiah2:4 Hear the word of the LORD
Jeremiah2:5 Thus says the LORD: What wrong did your fathers find in me that they went far from me
Jeremiah2:6 They did not say
Jeremiah2:7 And I brought you into a plentiful land to enjoy its fruits and its good things. But when you came in
Jeremiah2:8 The priests did not say
Jeremiah2:9 Therefore I still contend with you
Jeremiah2:10 For cross to the coasts of Cyprus and see
Jeremiah2:11 Has a nation changed its gods
Jeremiah2:12 Be appalled
Jeremiah2:13 for my people have committed two evils: they have forsaken me
Jeremiah2:14 Is Israel a slave? Is he a homeborn servant? Why then has he become a prey?
Jeremiah2:15 The lions have roared against him; they have roared loudly. They have made his land a waste; his cities are in ruins
Jeremiah2:16 Moreover
Jeremiah2:17 Have you not brought this upon yourself by forsaking the LORD your God
Jeremiah2:18 And now what do you gain by going to Egypt to drink the waters of the Nile? Or what do you gain by going to Assyria to drink the waters of the Euphrates?
Jeremiah2:19 Your evil will chastise you
Jeremiah2:20 For long ago I broke your yoke and burst your bonds; but you said
Jeremiah2:21 Yet I planted you a choice vine
Jeremiah2:22 Though you wash yourself with lye and use much soap
Jeremiah2:23 How can you say
Jeremiah2:24 a wild donkey used to the wilderness
Jeremiah2:25 Keep your feet from going unshod and your throat from thirst. But you said
Jeremiah2:26 As a thief is shamed when caught
Jeremiah2:27 who say to a tree
Jeremiah2:28 But where are your gods that you made for yourself? Let them arise
Jeremiah2:29 Why do you contend with me? You have all transgressed against me
Jeremiah2:30 In vain have I struck your children; they took no correction; your own sword devoured your prophets like a ravening lion.
Jeremiah2:31 And you
Jeremiah2:32 Can a virgin forget her ornaments
Jeremiah2:33 How well you direct your course to seek love! So that even to wicked women you have taught your ways.
Jeremiah2:34 Also on your skirts is found the lifeblood of the guiltless poor; you did not find them breaking in. Yet in spite of all these things
Jeremiah2:35 you say
Jeremiah2:36 How much you go about
Jeremiah2:37 From it too you will come away with your hands on your head
Jeremiah3:1 If a man divorces his wife and she goes from him and becomes another man's wife
Jeremiah3:2 Lift up your eyes to the bare heights
Jeremiah3:3 Therefore the showers have been withheld
Jeremiah3:4 Have you not just now called to me
Jeremiah3:5 will he be angry forever
Jeremiah3:6 The LORD said to me in the days of King Josiah: Have you seen what she did
Jeremiah3:7 And I thought
Jeremiah3:8 She saw that for all the adulteries of that faithless one
Jeremiah3:9 Because she took her whoredom lightly
Jeremiah3:10 Yet for all this her treacherous sister Judah did not return to me with her whole heart
Jeremiah3:11 And the LORD said to me
Jeremiah3:12 Go
Jeremiah3:13 Only acknowledge your guilt
Jeremiah3:14 Return

Jeremiah3:16 And when you have multiplied and increased in the land

Jeremiah3:20 Surely

Jeremiah4:2 and if you swear
Jeremiah4:3 For thus says the LORD to the men of Judah and Jerusalem: Break up your fallow ground

Jeremiah4:7 A lion has gone up from his thicket
Jeremiah4:8 For this put on sackcloth
Jeremiah4:9 In that day
Jeremiah4:10 Then I said
Jeremiah4:11 At that time it will be said to this people and to Jerusalem

Jeremiah4:13 Behold
Jeremiah4:14 O Jerusalem

Jeremiah4:16 Warn the nations that he is coming; announce to Jerusalem
Jeremiah4:17 Like keepers of a field are they against her all around
Jeremiah4:18 Your ways and your deeds have brought this upon you. This is your doom
Jeremiah4:19 My anguish
Jeremiah4:20 Crash follows hard on crash; the whole land is laid waste. Suddenly my tents are laid waste
Jeremiah4:21 How long must I see the standard and hear the sound of the trumpet?
Jeremiah4:22 For my people are foolish; they know me not; they are stupid children; they have no understanding. They are 'wise'- in doing evil! But how to do good they know not.

Jeremiah4:24 I looked on the mountains
Jeremiah4:25 I looked
Jeremiah4:26 I looked

Jeremiah5:2 Though they say
Jeremiah5:3 O LORD
Jeremiah5:4 Then I said
Jeremiah5:5 I will go to the great and will speak to them
Jeremiah5:6 Therefore a lion from the forest shall strike them down; a wolf from the desert shall devastate them. A leopard is watching their cities; everyone who goes out of them shall be torn in pieces
Jeremiah5:7 How can I pardon you? Your children have forsaken me and have sworn by those who are no gods. When I fed them to the full
Jeremiah5:8 They were well-fed
Jeremiah5:9 Shall I not punish them for these things? declares the LORD; and shall I not avenge myself on a nation such as this?
Jeremiah5:10 Go up through her vine rows and destroy
Jeremiah5:11 For the house of Israel and the house of Judah have been utterly treacherous to me
Jeremiah5:12 They have spoken falsely of the LORD and have said
Jeremiah5:13 The prophets will become wind; the word is not in them. Thus shall it be done to them!'
Jeremiah5:14 Therefore thus says the LORD
Jeremiah5:15 Behold
Jeremiah5:16 Their quiver is like an open tomb; they are all mighty warriors.
Jeremiah5:17 They shall eat up your harvest and your food; they shall eat up your sons and your daughters; they shall eat up your flocks and your herds; they shall eat up your vines and your fig trees; your fortified cities in which you trust they shall beat down with the sword.
Jeremiah5:18 But even in those days
Jeremiah5:19 And when your people say

Jeremiah5:21 Hear this
Jeremiah5:22 Do you not fear me? declares the LORD; Do you not tremble before me? I placed the sand as the boundary for the sea
Jeremiah5:23 But this people has a stubborn and rebellious heart; they have turned aside and gone away.
Jeremiah5:24 They do not say in their hearts
Jeremiah5:25 Your iniquities have turned these away
Jeremiah5:26 For wicked men are found among my people; they lurk like fowlers lying in wait. They set a trap; they catch men.
Jeremiah5:27 Like a cage full of birds
Jeremiah5:28 they have grown fat and sleek. They know no bounds in deeds of evil; they judge not with justice the cause of the fatherless
Jeremiah5:29 Shall I not punish them for these things? declares the LORD
Jeremiah5:30 An appalling and horrible thing has happened in the land:
Jeremiah5:31 the prophets prophesy falsely
Jeremiah6:1 Flee for safety
Jeremiah6:2 The lovely and delicately bred I will destroy
Jeremiah6:3 Shepherds with their flocks shall come against her; they shall pitch their tents around her; they shall pasture
Jeremiah6:4 Prepare war against her; arise
Jeremiah6:5 Arise
Jeremiah6:6 For thus says the LORD of hosts: Cut down her trees; cast up a siege mound against Jerusalem. This is the city that must be punished; there is nothing but oppression within her.
Jeremiah6:7 As a well keeps its water fresh
Jeremiah6:8 Be warned
Jeremiah6:9 Thus says the LORD of hosts: They shall glean thoroughly as a vine the remnant of Israel; like a grape-gatherer pass your hand again over its branches.
Jeremiah6:10 To whom shall I speak and give warning
Jeremiah6:11 Therefore I am full of the wrath of the LORD; I am weary of holding it in. Pour it out upon the children in the street
Jeremiah6:12 Their houses shall be turned over to others
Jeremiah6:13 For from the least to the greatest of them
Jeremiah6:14 They have healed the wound of my people lightly
Jeremiah6:15 Were they ashamed when they committed abomination? No
Jeremiah6:16 Thus says the LORD: Stand by the roads
Jeremiah6:17 I set watchmen over you
Jeremiah6:18 Therefore hear
Jeremiah6:19 Hear
Jeremiah6:20 What use to me is frankincense that comes from Sheba
Jeremiah6:21 Therefore thus says the LORD: 'Behold
Jeremiah6:22 Thus says the LORD: Behold
Jeremiah6:23 They lay hold on bow and javelin; they are cruel and have no mercy; the sound of them is like the roaring sea; they ride on horses
Jeremiah6:24 We have heard the report of it; our hands fall helpless; anguish has taken hold of us
Jeremiah6:25 Go not out into the field
Jeremiah6:26 O daughter of my people
Jeremiah6:27 I have made you a tester of metals among my people
Jeremiah6:28 They are all stubbornly rebellious
Jeremiah6:29 The bellows blow fiercely; the lead is consumed by the fire; in vain the refining goes on
Jeremiah6:30 Rejected silver they are called

Jeremiah7:15 And I will cast you out of my sight
Jeremiah7:16 As for you

Jeremiah7:27 So you shall speak all these words to them
Jeremiah7:28 And you shall say to them

Jeremiah7:32 Therefore
Jeremiah7:33 And the dead bodies of this people will be food for the birds of the air
Jeremiah7:34 And I will silence in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem the voice of mirth and the voice of gladness

Jeremiah8:2 And they shall be spread before the sun and the moon and all the host of heaven
Jeremiah8:3 Death shall be preferred to life by all the remnant that remains of this evil family in all the places where I have driven them
Jeremiah8:4 You shall say to them
Jeremiah8:5 Why then has this people turned away in perpetual backsliding? They hold fast to deceit; they refuse to return.

Jeremiah8:7 Even the stork in the heavens knows her times

Jeremiah8:12 Were they ashamed when they committed abomination? No
Jeremiah8:13 When I would gather them
Jeremiah8:14 Why do we sit still? Gather together; let us go into the fortified cities and perish there
Jeremiah8:15 We looked for peace
Jeremiah8:16 The snorting of their horses is heard from Dan; at the sound of the neighing of their stallions the whole land quakes. They come and devour the land and all that fills it
Jeremiah8:17 For behold
Jeremiah8:18 My joy is gone; grief is upon me; my heart is sick within me.
Jeremiah8:19 Behold
Jeremiah8:20 The harvest is past
Jeremiah8:21 For the wound of the daughter of my people is my heart wounded; I mourn
Jeremiah8:22 Is there no balm in Gilead? Is there no physician there? Why then has the health of the daughter of my people not been restored?
Jeremiah9:1 Oh that my head were waters
Jeremiah9:2 Oh that I had in the desert a travelers' lodging place

Jeremiah9:4 Let everyone beware of his neighbor
Jeremiah9:5 Everyone deceives his neighbor
Jeremiah9:6 Heaping oppression upon oppression
Jeremiah9:7 Therefore thus says the LORD of hosts: Behold
Jeremiah9:8 Their tongue is a deadly arrow; it speaks deceitfully; with his mouth each speaks peace to his neighbor

Jeremiah9:10 I will take up weeping and wailing for the mountains
Jeremiah9:11 I will make Jerusalem a heap of ruins

Jeremiah9:14 but have stubbornly followed their own hearts and have gone after the Baals

Jeremiah10:1 Hear the word that the LORD speaks to you
Jeremiah10:2 Thus says the LORD: Learn not the way of the nations
Jeremiah10:3 for the customs of the peoples are vanity. A tree from the forest is cut down and worked with an axe by the hands of a craftsman.
Jeremiah10:4 They decorate it with silver and gold; they fasten it with hammer and nails so that it cannot move.
Jeremiah10:5 Their idols are like scarecrows in a cucumber field
Jeremiah10:6 There is none like you
Jeremiah10:7 Who would not fear you
Jeremiah10:8 They are both stupid and foolish; the instruction of idols is but wood!
Jeremiah10:9 Beaten silver is brought from Tarshish
Jeremiah10:10 But the LORD is the true God; he is the living God and the everlasting King. At his wrath the earth quakes
Jeremiah10:11 Thus shall you say to them: The gods who did not make the heavens and the earth shall perish from the earth and from under the heavens.
Jeremiah10:12 It is he who made the earth by his power
Jeremiah10:13 When he utters his voice
Jeremiah10:14 Every man is stupid and without knowledge; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols
Jeremiah10:15 They are worthless
Jeremiah10:16 Not like these is he who is the portion of Jacob

Jeremiah10:18 For thus says the LORD: Behold
Jeremiah10:19 Woe is me because of my hurt! My wound is grievous. But I said
Jeremiah10:20 My tent is destroyed
Jeremiah10:21 For the shepherds are stupid and do not inquire of the LORD; therefore they have not prospered

Jeremiah10:23 I know
Jeremiah10:24 Correct me

Jeremiah12:2 You plant them
Jeremiah12:3 But you

Jeremiah12:5 If you have raced with men on foot
Jeremiah12:6 For even your brothers and the house of your father
Jeremiah12:7 I have forsaken my house; I have abandoned my heritage; I have given the beloved of my soul into the hands of her enemies.
Jeremiah12:8 My heritage has become to me like a lion in the forest; she has lifted up her voice against me; therefore I hate her.
Jeremiah12:9 Is my heritage to me like a hyena's lair? Are the birds of prey against her all around? Go
Jeremiah12:10 Many shepherds have destroyed my vineyard; they have trampled down my portion; they have made my pleasant portion a desolate wilderness.
Jeremiah12:11 They have made it a desolation; desolate
Jeremiah12:12 Upon all the bare heights in the desert destroyers have come
Jeremiah12:13 They have sown wheat and have reaped thorns; they have tired themselves out but profit nothing. They shall be ashamed of their harvests because of the fierce anger of the LORD.

Jeremiah12:15 And after I have plucked them up

Jeremiah13:1 Thus says the LORD to me
Jeremiah13:2 So I bought a loincloth according to the word of the LORD

Jeremiah13:5 So I went and hid it by the Euphrates
Jeremiah13:6 And after many days the LORD said to me
Jeremiah13:7 Then I went to the Euphrates
Jeremiah13:8 Then the word of the LORD came to me:
Jeremiah13:9 Thus says the LORD: Even so will I spoil the pride of Judah and the great pride of Jerusalem.
Jeremiah13:10 This evil people
Jeremiah13:11 For as the loincloth clings to the waist of a man

Jeremiah13:13 Then you shall say to them
Jeremiah13:14 And I will dash them one against another
Jeremiah13:15 Hear and give ear; be not proud
Jeremiah13:16 Give glory to the LORD your God before he brings darkness
Jeremiah13:17 But if you will not listen
Jeremiah13:18 Say to the king and the queen mother: Take a lowly seat
Jeremiah13:19 The cities of the Negeb are shut up
Jeremiah13:20 Lift up your eyes and see those who come from the north. Where is the flock that was given you
Jeremiah13:21 What will you say when they set as head over you those whom you yourself have taught to be friends to you? Will not pangs take hold of you like those of a woman in labor?
Jeremiah13:22 And if you say in your heart
Jeremiah13:23 Can the Ethiopian change his skin or the leopard his spots? Then also you can do good who are accustomed to do evil.
Jeremiah13:24 I will scatter you like chaff driven by the wind from the desert.
Jeremiah13:25 This is your lot
Jeremiah13:26 I myself will lift up your skirts over your face
Jeremiah13:27 I have seen your abominations
Jeremiah14:1 The word of the LORD that came to Jeremiah concerning the drought:
Jeremiah14:2 Judah mourns and her gates languish; her people lament on the ground
Jeremiah14:3 Her nobles send their servants for water; they come to the cisterns; they find no water; they return with their vessels empty; they are ashamed and confounded and cover their heads.
Jeremiah14:4 Because of the ground that is dismayed
Jeremiah14:5 Even the doe in the field forsakes her newborn fawn because there is no grass.
Jeremiah14:6 The wild donkeys stand on the bare heights; they pant for air like jackals; their eyes fail because there is no vegetation.
Jeremiah14:7 Though our iniquities testify against us
Jeremiah14:8 O you hope of Israel
Jeremiah14:9 Why should you be like a man confused
Jeremiah14:10 Thus says the LORD concerning this people: They have loved to wander thus; they have not restrained their feet; therefore the LORD does not accept them; now he will remember their iniquity and punish their sins.
Jeremiah14:11 The LORD said to me: Do not pray for the welfare of this people.
Jeremiah14:12 Though they fast
Jeremiah14:13 Then I said: Ah
Jeremiah14:14 And the LORD said to me: The prophets are prophesying lies in my name. I did not send them
Jeremiah14:15 Therefore thus says the LORD concerning the prophets who prophesy in my name although I did not send them
Jeremiah14:16 And the people to whom they prophesy shall be cast out in the streets of Jerusalem
Jeremiah14:17 You shall say to them this word: 'Let my eyes run down with tears night and day
Jeremiah14:18 If I go out into the field
Jeremiah14:19 Have you utterly rejected Judah? Does your soul loathe Zion? Why have you struck us down so that there is no healing for us? We looked for peace
Jeremiah14:20 We acknowledge our wickedness
Jeremiah14:21 Do not spurn us
Jeremiah14:22 Are there any among the false gods of the nations that can bring rain? Or can the heavens give showers? Are you not he
Jeremiah15:1 Then the LORD said to me
Jeremiah15:2 And when they ask you
Jeremiah15:3 I will appoint over them four kinds of destroyers
Jeremiah15:4 And I will make them a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth because of what Manasseh the son of Hezekiah
Jeremiah15:5 Who will have pity on you
Jeremiah15:6 You have rejected me
Jeremiah15:7 I have winnowed them with a winnowing fork in the gates of the land; I have bereaved them; I have destroyed my people; they did not turn from their ways.
Jeremiah15:8 I have made their widows more in number than the sand of the seas; I have brought against the mothers of young men a destroyer at noonday; I have made anguish and terror fall upon them suddenly.
Jeremiah15:9 She who bore seven has grown feeble; she has fainted away; her sun went down while it was yet day; she has been shamed and disgraced. And the rest of them I will give to the sword before their enemies
Jeremiah15:10 Woe is me
Jeremiah15:11 The LORD said
Jeremiah15:12 Can one break iron
Jeremiah15:13 Your wealth and your treasures I will give as spoil
Jeremiah15:14 I will make you serve your enemies in a land that you do not know
Jeremiah15:15 O LORD
Jeremiah15:16 Your words were found
Jeremiah15:17 I did not sit in the company of revelers
Jeremiah15:18 Why is my pain unceasing
Jeremiah15:19 Therefore thus says the LORD: If you return
Jeremiah15:20 And I will make you to this people a fortified wall of bronze; they will fight against you
Jeremiah15:21 I will deliver you out of the hand of the wicked
Jeremiah16:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Jeremiah16:2 You shall not take a wife
Jeremiah16:3 For thus says the LORD concerning the sons and daughters who are born in this place
Jeremiah16:4 They shall die of deadly diseases. They shall not be lamented
Jeremiah16:5 For thus says the LORD: Do not enter the house of mourning
Jeremiah16:6 Both great and small shall die in this land. They shall not be buried
Jeremiah16:7 No one shall break bread for the mourner
Jeremiah16:8 You shall not go into the house of feasting to sit with them
Jeremiah16:9 For thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah16:10 And when you tell this people all these words
Jeremiah16:11 then you shall say to them: 'Because your fathers have forsaken me
Jeremiah16:12 and because you have done worse than your fathers
Jeremiah16:13 Therefore I will hurl you out of this land into a land that neither you nor your fathers have known
Jeremiah16:14 Therefore
Jeremiah16:15 but 'As the LORD lives who brought up the people of Israel out of the north country and out of all the countries where he had driven them.' For I will bring them back to their own land that I gave to their fathers.
Jeremiah16:16 Behold
Jeremiah16:17 For my eyes are on all their ways. They are not hidden from me
Jeremiah16:18 But first I will doubly repay their iniquity and their sin
Jeremiah16:19 O LORD
Jeremiah16:20 Can man make for himself gods? Such are not gods!
Jeremiah16:21 Therefore
Jeremiah17:1 The sin of Judah is written with a pen of iron; with a point of diamond it is engraved on the tablet of their heart
Jeremiah17:2 while their children remember their altars and their Asherim
Jeremiah17:3 on the mountains in the open country. Your wealth and all your treasures I will give for spoil as the price of your high places for sin throughout all your territory.
Jeremiah17:4 You shall loosen your hand from your heritage that I gave to you
Jeremiah17:5 Thus says the LORD: Cursed is the man who trusts in man and makes flesh his strength
Jeremiah17:6 He is like a shrub in the desert
Jeremiah17:7 Blessed is the man who trusts in the LORD
Jeremiah17:8 He is like a tree planted by water
Jeremiah17:9 The heart is deceitful above all things
Jeremiah17:10 I the LORD search the heart and test the mind

Jeremiah33:1 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah a second time
Jeremiah33:2 Thus says the LORD who made the earth
Jeremiah33:3 Call to me and I will answer you
Jeremiah33:4 For thus says the LORD
Jeremiah33:5 They are coming in to fight against the Chaldeans and to fill them with the dead bodies of men whom I shall strike down in my anger and my wrath
Jeremiah33:6 Behold
Jeremiah33:7 I will restore the fortunes of Judah and the fortunes of Israel
Jeremiah33:8 I will cleanse them from all the guilt of their sin against me
Jeremiah33:9 And this city shall be to me a name of joy
Jeremiah33:10 Thus says the LORD: In this place of which you say
Jeremiah33:11 the voice of mirth and the voice of gladness
Jeremiah33:12 Thus says the LORD of hosts: In this place that is waste
Jeremiah33:13 In the cities of the hill country
Jeremiah33:14 Behold
Jeremiah33:15 In those days and at that time I will cause a righteous Branch to spring up for David
Jeremiah33:16 In those days Judah will be saved and Jerusalem will dwell securely. And this is the name by which it will be called: 'The LORD is our righteousness.'
Jeremiah33:17 For thus says the LORD: David shall never lack a man to sit on the throne of the house of Israel
Jeremiah33:18 and the Levitical priests shall never lack a man in my presence to offer burnt offerings
Jeremiah33:19 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah:
Jeremiah33:20 Thus says the LORD: If you can break my covenant with the day and my covenant with the night
Jeremiah33:21 then also my covenant with David my servant may be broken
Jeremiah33:22 As the host of heaven cannot be numbered and the sands of the sea cannot be measured
Jeremiah33:23 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah:
Jeremiah33:24 Have you not observed that these people are saying
Jeremiah33:25 Thus says the LORD: If I have not established my covenant with day and night and the fixed order of heaven and earth
Jeremiah33:26 then I will reject the offspring of Jacob and David my servant and will not choose one of his offspring to rule over the offspring of Abraham
Jeremiah34:1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD
Jeremiah34:2 Thus says the LORD
Jeremiah34:3 You shall not escape from his hand but shall surely be captured and delivered into his hand. You shall see the king of Babylon eye to eye and speak with him face to face. And you shall go to Babylon.'
Jeremiah34:4 Yet hear the word of the LORD
Jeremiah34:5 You shall die in peace. And as spices were burned for your fathers
Jeremiah34:6 Then Jeremiah the prophet spoke all these words to Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah34:7 when the army of the king of Babylon was fighting against Jerusalem and against all the cities of Judah that were left
Jeremiah34:8 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD
Jeremiah34:9 that everyone should set free his Hebrew slaves
Jeremiah34:10 And they obeyed
Jeremiah34:11 But afterward they turned around and took back the male and female slaves they had set free
Jeremiah34:12 The word of the LORD came to Jeremiah from the LORD:
Jeremiah34:13 Thus says the LORD
Jeremiah34:14 'At the end of seven years each of you must set free the fellow Hebrew who has been sold to you and has served you six years; you must set him free from your service.' But your fathers did not listen to me or incline their ears to me.
Jeremiah34:15 You recently repented and did what was right in my eyes by proclaiming liberty
Jeremiah34:16 but then you turned around and profaned my name when each of you took back his male and female slaves
Jeremiah34:17 Therefore
Jeremiah34:18 And the men who transgressed my covenant and did not keep the terms of the covenant that they made before me
Jeremiah34:19 the officials of Judah
Jeremiah34:20 And I will give them into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their lives. Their dead bodies shall be food for the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth.
Jeremiah34:21 And Zedekiah king of Judah and his officials I will give into the hand of their enemies and into the hand of those who seek their lives
Jeremiah34:22 Behold
Jeremiah35:1 The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah35:2 Go to the house of the Rechabites and speak with them and bring them to the house of the LORD
Jeremiah35:3 So I took Jaazaniah the son of Jeremiah
Jeremiah35:4 I brought them to the house of the LORD into the chamber of the sons of Hanan the son of Igdaliah
Jeremiah35:5 Then I set before the Rechabites pitchers full of wine
Jeremiah35:6 But they answered
Jeremiah35:7 You shall not build a house; you shall not sow seed; you shall not plant or have a vineyard; but you shall live in tents all your days
Jeremiah35:8 We have obeyed the voice of Jonadab the son of Rechab
Jeremiah35:9 and not to build houses to dwell in. We have no vineyard or field or seed
Jeremiah35:10 but we have lived in tents and have obeyed and done all that Jonadab our father commanded us.
Jeremiah35:11 But when Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up against the land
Jeremiah35:12 Then the word of the LORD came to Jeremiah:
Jeremiah35:13 Thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah35:14 The command that Jonadab the son of Rechab gave to his sons
Jeremiah35:15 I have sent to you all my servants the prophets
Jeremiah35:16 The sons of Jonadab the son of Rechab have kept the command that their father gave them
Jeremiah35:17 Therefore
Jeremiah35:18 But to the house of the Rechabites Jeremiah said
Jeremiah35:19 therefore thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah36:1 In the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah36:2 Take a scroll and write on it all the words that I have spoken to you against Israel and Judah and all the nations
Jeremiah36:3 It may be that the house of Judah will hear all the disaster that I intend to do to them
Jeremiah36:4 Then Jeremiah called Baruch the son of Neriah
Jeremiah36:5 And Jeremiah ordered Baruch
Jeremiah36:6 so you are to go
Jeremiah36:7 It may be that their plea for mercy will come before the LORD
Jeremiah36:8 And Baruch the son of Neriah did all that Jeremiah the prophet ordered him about reading from the scroll the words of the LORD in the LORD's house.
Jeremiah36:9 In the fifth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah
Jeremiah36:10 Then
Jeremiah36:11 When Micaiah the son of Gemariah
Jeremiah36:12 he went down to the king's house
Jeremiah36:13 And Micaiah told them all the words that he had heard
Jeremiah36:14 Then all the officials sent Jehudi the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah36:15 And they said to him
Jeremiah36:16 When they heard all the words
Jeremiah36:17 Then they asked Baruch
Jeremiah36:18 Baruch answered them
Jeremiah36:19 Then the officials said to Baruch
Jeremiah36:20 So they went into the court to the king
Jeremiah36:21 Then the king sent Jehudi to get the scroll
Jeremiah36:22 It was the ninth month
Jeremiah36:23 As Jehudi read three or four columns
Jeremiah36:24 Yet neither the king nor any of his servants who heard all these words was afraid
Jeremiah36:25 Even when Elnathan and Delaiah and Gemariah urged the king not to burn the scroll
Jeremiah36:26 And the king commanded Jerahmeel the king's son and Seraiah the son of Azriel and Shelemiah the son of Abdeel to seize Baruch the secretary and Jeremiah the prophet
Jeremiah36:27 Now after the king had burned the scroll with the words that Baruch wrote at Jeremiah's dictation
Jeremiah36:28 Take another scroll and write on it all the former words that were in the first scroll
Jeremiah36:29 And concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah you shall say
Jeremiah36:30 Therefore thus says the LORD concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah: He shall have none to sit on the throne of David
Jeremiah36:31 And I will punish him and his offspring and his servants for their iniquity. I will bring upon them and upon the inhabitants of Jerusalem and upon the people of Judah all the disaster that I have pronounced against them
Jeremiah36:32 Then Jeremiah took another scroll and gave it to Baruch the scribe

Jeremiah37:2 But neither he nor his servants nor the people of the land listened to the words of the LORD that he spoke through Jeremiah the prophet.
Jeremiah37:3 King Zedekiah sent Jehucal the son of Shelemiah
Jeremiah37:4 Now Jeremiah was still going in and out among the people
Jeremiah37:5 The army of Pharaoh had come out of Egypt. And when the Chaldeans who were besieging Jerusalem heard news about them
Jeremiah37:6 Then the word of the LORD came to Jeremiah the prophet:
Jeremiah37:7 Thus says the LORD
Jeremiah37:8 And the Chaldeans shall come back and fight against this city. They shall capture it and burn it with fire.
Jeremiah37:9 Thus says the LORD
Jeremiah37:10 For even if you should defeat the whole army of Chaldeans who are fighting against you
Jeremiah37:11 Now when the Chaldean army had withdrawn from Jerusalem at the approach of Pharaoh's army
Jeremiah37:12 Jeremiah set out from Jerusalem to go to the land of Benjamin to receive his portion there among the people.
Jeremiah37:13 When he was at the Benjamin Gate
Jeremiah37:14 And Jeremiah said
Jeremiah37:15 And the officials were enraged at Jeremiah
Jeremiah37:16 When Jeremiah had come to the dungeon cells and remained there many days
Jeremiah37:17 King Zedekiah sent for him and received him. The king questioned him secretly in his house and said
Jeremiah37:18 Jeremiah also said to King Zedekiah
Jeremiah37:19 Where are your prophets who prophesied to you
Jeremiah37:20 Now hear
Jeremiah37:21 So King Zedekiah gave orders

Jeremiah38:2 Thus says the LORD: He who stays in this city shall die by the sword
Jeremiah38:3 Thus says the LORD: This city shall surely be given into the hand of the army of the king of Babylon and be taken.
Jeremiah38:4 Then the officials said to the king
Jeremiah38:5 King Zedekiah said
Jeremiah38:6 So they took Jeremiah and cast him into the cistern of Malchiah
Jeremiah38:7 When Ebed-melech the Ethiopian
Jeremiah38:8 Ebed-melech went from the king's house and said to the king
Jeremiah38:9 My lord the king

Jeremiah38:12 Then Ebed-melech the Ethiopian said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:13 Then they drew Jeremiah up with ropes and lifted him out of the cistern. And Jeremiah remained in the court of the guard.
Jeremiah38:14 King Zedekiah sent for Jeremiah the prophet and received him at the third entrance of the temple of the LORD. The king said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:15 Jeremiah said to Zedekiah
Jeremiah38:16 Then King Zedekiah swore secretly to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:17 Then Jeremiah said to Zedekiah
Jeremiah38:18 But if you do not surrender to the officials of the king of Babylon
Jeremiah38:19 King Zedekiah said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:20 Jeremiah said
Jeremiah38:21 But if you refuse to surrender
Jeremiah38:22 Behold
Jeremiah38:23 All your wives and your sons shall be led out to the Chaldeans
Jeremiah38:24 Then Zedekiah said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:25 If the officials hear that I have spoken with you and come to you and say to you
Jeremiah38:26 then you shall say to them
Jeremiah38:27 Then all the officials came to Jeremiah and asked him
Jeremiah38:28 And Jeremiah remained in the court of the guard until the day that Jerusalem was taken.
Jeremiah39:1 In the ninth year of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah39:2 In the eleventh year of Zedekiah

Jeremiah39:4 When Zedekiah king of Judah and all the soldiers saw them
Jeremiah39:5 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued them and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho. And when they had taken him

Jeremiah39:7 He put out the eyes of Zedekiah and bound him in chains to take him to Babylon.

Jeremiah39:10 Nebuzaradan

Jeremiah39:18 For I will surely save you

Jeremiah40:4 Now

Jeremiah40:10 As for me

Jeremiah41:1 In the seventh month
Jeremiah41:2 Ishmael the son of Nethaniah and the ten men with him rose up and struck down Gedaliah the son of Ahikam
Jeremiah41:3 Ishmael also struck down all the Judeans who were with Gedaliah at Mizpah
Jeremiah41:4 On the day after the murder of Gedaliah
Jeremiah41:5 eighty men arrived from Shechem and Shiloh and Samaria
Jeremiah41:6 And Ishmael the son of Nethaniah came out from Mizpah to meet them
Jeremiah41:7 When they came into the city
Jeremiah41:8 But there were ten men among them who said to Ishmael
Jeremiah41:9 Now the cistern into which Ishmael had thrown all the bodies of the men whom he had struck down along with Gedaliah was the large cistern that King Asa had made for defense against Baasha king of Israel; Ishmael the son of Nethaniah filled it with the slain.
Jeremiah41:10 Then Ishmael took captive all the rest of the people who were in Mizpah
Jeremiah41:11 But when Johanan the son of Kareah and all the leaders of the forces with him heard of all the evil that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah had done
Jeremiah41:12 they took all their men and went to fight against Ishmael the son of Nethaniah. They came upon him at the great pool that is in Gibeon.
Jeremiah41:13 And when all the people who were with Ishmael saw Johanan the son of Kareah and all the leaders of the forces with him
Jeremiah41:14 So all the people whom Ishmael had carried away captive from Mizpah turned around and came back
Jeremiah41:15 But Ishmael the son of Nethaniah escaped from Johanan with eight men
Jeremiah41:16 Then Johanan the son of Kareah and all the leaders of the forces with him took from Mizpah all the rest of the people whom he had recovered from Ishmael the son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah41:17 And they went and stayed at Geruth Chimham near Bethlehem
Jeremiah41:18 because of the Chaldeans. For they were afraid of them

Jeremiah42:4 Jeremiah the prophet said to them
Jeremiah42:5 Then they said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah42:6 Whether it is good or bad
Jeremiah42:7 At the end of ten days the word of the LORD came to Jeremiah.
Jeremiah42:8 Then he summoned Johanan the son of Kareah and all the commanders of the forces who were with him
Jeremiah42:9 and said to them

Jeremiah42:11 Do not fear the king of Babylon

Jeremiah42:16 then the sword that you fear shall overtake you there in the land of Egypt

Jeremiah42:18 For thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah42:19 The LORD has said to you

Jeremiah44:1 The word that came to Jeremiah concerning all the Judeans who lived in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah44:2 Thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah44:3 because of the evil that they committed
Jeremiah44:4 Yet I persistently sent to you all my servants the prophets
Jeremiah44:5 But they did not listen or incline their ear
Jeremiah44:6 Therefore my wrath and my anger were poured out and kindled in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem
Jeremiah44:7 And now thus says the Lord GOD of hosts
Jeremiah44:8 Why do you provoke me to anger with the works of your hands
Jeremiah44:9 Have you forgotten the evil of your fathers
Jeremiah44:10 They have not humbled themselves even to this day
Jeremiah44:11 Therefore thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah44:12 I will take the remnant of Judah who have set their faces to come to the land of Egypt to live
Jeremiah44:13 I will punish those who dwell in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah44:14 so that none of the remnant of Judah who have come to live in the land of Egypt shall escape or survive or return to the land of Judah
Jeremiah44:15 Then all the men who knew that their wives had made offerings to other gods
Jeremiah44:16 As for the word that you have spoken to us in the name of the LORD
Jeremiah44:17 But we will do everything that we have vowed
Jeremiah44:18 But since we left off making offerings to the queen of heaven and pouring out drink offerings to her
Jeremiah44:19 And the women said
Jeremiah44:20 Then Jeremiah said to all the people
Jeremiah44:21 As for the offerings that you offered in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem
Jeremiah44:22 The LORD could no longer bear your evil deeds and the abominations that you committed. Therefore your land has become a desolation and a waste and a curse
Jeremiah44:23 It is because you made offerings and because you sinned against the LORD and did not obey the voice of the LORD or walk in his law and in his statutes and in his testimonies that this disaster has happened to you

Jeremiah44:25 Thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah44:26 Therefore hear the word of the LORD
Jeremiah44:27 Behold
Jeremiah44:28 And those who escape the sword shall return from the land of Egypt to the land of Judah
Jeremiah44:29 This shall be the sign to you

Jeremiah46:6 The swift cannot flee away
Jeremiah46:7 Who is this
Jeremiah46:8 Egypt rises like the Nile
Jeremiah46:9 Advance
Jeremiah46:10 That day is the day of the Lord GOD of hosts
Jeremiah46:11 Go up to Gilead
Jeremiah46:12 The nations have heard of your shame

Jeremiah46:14 Declare in Egypt
Jeremiah46:15 Why are your mighty ones face down? They do not stand because the LORD thrust them down.
Jeremiah46:16 He made many stumble
Jeremiah46:17 Call the name of Pharaoh
Jeremiah46:18 As I live
Jeremiah46:19 Prepare yourselves baggage for exile
Jeremiah46:20 A beautiful heifer is Egypt
Jeremiah46:21 Even her hired soldiers in her midst are like fattened calves; yes
Jeremiah46:22 She makes a sound like a serpent gliding away; for her enemies march in force and come against her with axes like those who fell trees.
Jeremiah46:23 They shall cut down her forest
Jeremiah46:24 The daughter of Egypt shall be put to shame; she shall be delivered into the hand of a people from the north.

Jeremiah46:26 I will deliver them into the hand of those who seek their life
Jeremiah46:27 But fear not

Jeremiah47:2 Thus says the LORD: Behold
Jeremiah47:3 At the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his stallions
Jeremiah47:4 because of the day that is coming to destroy all the Philistines
Jeremiah47:5 Baldness has come upon Gaza; Ashkelon has perished. O remnant of their valley
Jeremiah47:6 Ah
Jeremiah47:7 How can it be quiet when the LORD has given it a charge? Against Ashkelon and against the seashore he has appointed it.
Jeremiah48:1 Concerning Moab.Thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah48:2 the renown of Moab is no more. In Heshbon they planned disaster against her: 'Come
Jeremiah48:3 Hark! A cry from Horonaim
Jeremiah48:4 Moab is destroyed; her little ones have made a cry.
Jeremiah48:5 For at the ascent of Luhith they go up weeping; for at the descent of Horonaim they have heard the distressed cry of destruction.
Jeremiah48:6 Flee! Save yourselves! You will be like a juniper in the desert!
Jeremiah48:7 For
Jeremiah48:8 The destroyer shall come upon every city
Jeremiah48:9 Give wings to Moab
Jeremiah48:10 Cursed is he who does the work of the LORD with slackness
Jeremiah48:11 Moab has been at ease from his youth and has settled on his dregs; he has not been emptied from vessel to vessel
Jeremiah48:12 Therefore
Jeremiah48:13 Then Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh
Jeremiah48:14 How do you say
Jeremiah48:15 The destroyer of Moab and his cities has come up
Jeremiah48:16 The calamity of Moab is near at hand
Jeremiah48:17 Grieve for him
Jeremiah48:18 Come down from your glory
Jeremiah48:19 Stand by the way and watch
Jeremiah48:20 Moab is put to shame
Jeremiah48:21 Judgment has come upon the tableland
Jeremiah48:22 and Dibon
Jeremiah48:23 and Kiriathaim
Jeremiah48:24 and Kerioth
Jeremiah48:25 The horn of Moab is cut off
Jeremiah48:26 Make him drunk
Jeremiah48:27 Was not Israel a derision to you? Was he found among thieves
Jeremiah48:28 Leave the cities
Jeremiah48:29 We have heard of the pride of Moab- he is very proud- of his loftiness
Jeremiah48:30 I know his insolence
Jeremiah48:31 Therefore I wail for Moab; I cry out for all Moab; for the men of Kir-hareseth I mourn.
Jeremiah48:32 More than for Jazer I weep for you
Jeremiah48:33 Gladness and joy have been taken away from the fruitful land of Moab; I have made the wine cease from the wine presses; no one treads them with shouts of joy; the shouting is not the shout of joy.
Jeremiah48:34 From the outcry at Heshbon even to Elealeh
Jeremiah48:35 And I will bring to an end in Moab
Jeremiah48:36 Therefore my heart moans for Moab like a flute
Jeremiah48:37 For every head is shaved and every beard cut off. On all the hands are gashes
Jeremiah48:38 On all the housetops of Moab and in the squares there is nothing but lamentation
Jeremiah48:39 How it is broken! How they wail! How Moab has turned his back in shame! So Moab has become a derision and a horror to all that are around him.
Jeremiah48:40 For thus says the LORD: Behold
Jeremiah48:41 the cities shall be taken and the strongholds seized. The heart of the warriors of Moab shall be in that day like the heart of a woman in her birth pains;
Jeremiah48:42 Moab shall be destroyed and be no longer a people
Jeremiah48:43 Terror
Jeremiah48:44 He who flees from the terror shall fall into the pit
Jeremiah48:45 In the shadow of Heshbon fugitives stop without strength
Jeremiah48:46 Woe to you
Jeremiah48:47 Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moab in the latter days
Jeremiah49:1 Concerning the Ammonites.Thus says the LORD: Has Israel no sons? Has he no heir? Why then has Milcom dispossessed Gad
Jeremiah49:2 Therefore
Jeremiah49:3 Wail
Jeremiah49:4 Why do you boast of your valleys
Jeremiah49:5 Behold
Jeremiah49:6 But afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites
Jeremiah49:7 Concerning Edom. Thus says the LORD of hosts: Is wisdom no more in Teman? Has counsel perished from the prudent? Has their wisdom vanished?
Jeremiah49:8 Flee
Jeremiah49:9 If grape-gatherers came to you
Jeremiah49:10 But I have stripped Esau bare; I have uncovered his hiding places
Jeremiah49:11 Leave your fatherless children; I will keep them alive; and let your widows trust in me.
Jeremiah49:12 For thus says the LORD: If those who did not deserve to drink the cup must drink it
Jeremiah49:13 For I have sworn by myself
Jeremiah49:14 I have heard a message from the LORD
Jeremiah49:15 For behold
Jeremiah49:16 The horror you inspire has deceived you
Jeremiah49:17 Edom shall become a horror. Everyone who passes by it will be horrified and will hiss because of all its disasters.
Jeremiah49:18 As when Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring cities were overthrown
Jeremiah49:19 Behold
Jeremiah49:20 Therefore hear the plan that the LORD has made against Edom and the purposes that he has formed against the inhabitants of Teman: Even the little ones of the flock shall be dragged away. Surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate.
Jeremiah49:21 At the sound of their fall the earth shall tremble; the sound of their cry shall be heard at the Red Sea.
Jeremiah49:22 Behold
Jeremiah49:23 Concerning Damascus: Hamath and Arpad are confounded
Jeremiah49:24 Damascus has become feeble
Jeremiah49:25 How is the famous city not forsaken
Jeremiah49:26 Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares
Jeremiah49:27 And I will kindle a fire in the wall of Damascus
Jeremiah49:28 Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor that Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon struck down. Thus says the LORD: Rise up
Jeremiah49:29 Their tents and their flocks shall be taken
Jeremiah49:30 Flee
Jeremiah49:31 Rise up
Jeremiah49:32 Their camels shall become plunder
Jeremiah49:33 Hazor shall become a haunt of jackals
Jeremiah49:34 The word of the LORD that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam
Jeremiah49:35 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Behold
Jeremiah49:36 And I will bring upon Elam the four winds from the four quarters of heaven. And I will scatter them to all those winds
Jeremiah49:37 I will terrify Elam before their enemies and before those who seek their life. I will bring disaster upon them
Jeremiah49:38 and I will set my throne in Elam and destroy their king and officials
Jeremiah49:39 But in the latter days I will restore the fortunes of Elam
Jeremiah50:1 The word that the LORD spoke concerning Babylon
Jeremiah50:2 Declare among the nations and proclaim
Jeremiah50:3 For out of the north a nation has come up against her
Jeremiah50:4 In those days and in that time
Jeremiah50:5 They shall ask the way to Zion
Jeremiah50:6 My people have been lost sheep. Their shepherds have led them astray
Jeremiah50:7 All who found them have devoured them
Jeremiah50:8 Flee from the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah50:9 For behold
Jeremiah50:10 Chaldea shall be plundered; all who plunder her shall be sated
Jeremiah50:11 Though you rejoice
Jeremiah50:12 your mother shall be utterly shamed
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the wrath of the LORD she shall not be inhabited but shall be an utter desolation; everyone who passes by Babylon shall be appalled
Jeremiah50:14 Set yourselves in array against Babylon all around
Jeremiah50:15 Raise a shout against her all around; she has surrendered; her bulwarks have fallen; her walls are thrown down. For this is the vengeance of the LORD: take vengeance on her; do to her as she has done.
Jeremiah50:16 Cut off from Babylon the sower
Jeremiah50:17 Israel is a hunted sheep driven away by lions. First the king of Assyria devoured him
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore
Jeremiah50:19 I will restore Israel to his pasture
Jeremiah50:20 In those days and in that time
Jeremiah50:21 Go up against the land of Merathaim
Jeremiah50:22 The noise of battle is in the land
Jeremiah50:23 How the hammer of the whole earth is cut down and broken! How Babylon has become a horror among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 I set a snare for you and you were taken
Jeremiah50:25 The LORD has opened his armory and brought out the weapons of his wrath
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from every quarter; open her granaries; pile her up like heaps of grain
Jeremiah50:27 Kill all her bulls; let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them
Jeremiah50:28 A voice! They flee and escape from the land of Babylon
Jeremiah50:29 Summon archers against Babylon
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares
Jeremiah50:31 Behold
Jeremiah50:32 The proud one shall stumble and fall
Jeremiah50:33 Thus says the LORD of hosts: The people of Israel are oppressed
Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong; the LORD of hosts is his name. He will surely plead their cause
Jeremiah50:35 A sword against the Chaldeans
Jeremiah50:36 A sword against the diviners
Jeremiah50:37 A sword against her horses and against her chariots
Jeremiah50:38 A drought against her waters
Jeremiah50:39 Therefore wild beasts shall dwell with hyenas in Babylon
Jeremiah50:40 As when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring cities
Jeremiah50:41 Behold
Jeremiah50:42 They lay hold of bow and spear; they are cruel and have no mercy. The sound of them is like the roaring of the sea; they ride on horses
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon heard the report of them
Jeremiah50:44 Behold
Jeremiah50:45 Therefore hear the plan that the LORD has made against Babylon
Jeremiah50:46 At the sound of the capture of Babylon the earth shall tremble
Jeremiah51:1 Thus says the LORD: Behold
Jeremiah51:2 and I will send to Babylon winnowers
Jeremiah51:3 Let not the archer bend his bow
Jeremiah51:4 They shall fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For Israel and Judah have not been forsaken by their God
Jeremiah51:6 Flee from the midst of Babylon; let every one save his life! Be not cut off in her punishment
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon was a golden cup in the LORD's hand
Jeremiah51:8 Suddenly Babylon has fallen and been broken; wail for her! Take balm for her pain; perhaps she may be healed.
Jeremiah51:9 We would have healed Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 The LORD has brought about our vindication; come
Jeremiah51:11 Sharpen the arrows! Take up the shields! The LORD has stirred up the spirit of the kings of the Medes
Jeremiah51:12 Set up a standard against the walls of Babylon; make the watch strong; set up watchmen; prepare the ambushes; for the LORD has both planned and done what he spoke concerning the inhabitants of Babylon.
Jeremiah51:13 O you who dwell by many waters
Jeremiah51:14 The LORD of hosts has sworn by himself: Surely I will fill you with men
Jeremiah51:15 It is he who made the earth by his power
Jeremiah51:16 When he utters his voice there is a tumult of waters in the heavens
Jeremiah51:17 Every man is stupid and without knowledge; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols
Jeremiah51:18 They are worthless
Jeremiah51:19 Not like these is he who is the portion of Jacob
Jeremiah51:20 You are my hammer and weapon of war: with you I break nations in pieces; with you I destroy kingdoms;
Jeremiah51:21 with you I break in pieces the horse and his rider; with you I break in pieces the chariot and the charioteer;
Jeremiah51:22 with you I break in pieces man and woman; with you I break in pieces the old man and the youth; with you I break in pieces the young man and the young woman;
Jeremiah51:23 with you I break in pieces the shepherd and his flock; with you I break in pieces the farmer and his team; with you I break in pieces governors and commanders.
Jeremiah51:24 I will repay Babylon and all the inhabitants of Chaldea before your very eyes for all the evil that they have done in Zion
Jeremiah51:25 Behold
Jeremiah51:26 No stone shall be taken from you for a corner and no stone for a foundation
Jeremiah51:27 Set up a standard on the earth; blow the trumpet among the nations; prepare the nations for war against her; summon against her the kingdoms
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare the nations for war against her
Jeremiah51:29 The land trembles and writhes in pain
Jeremiah51:30 The warriors of Babylon have ceased fighting; they remain in their strongholds; their strength has failed; they have become women; her dwellings are on fire; her bars are broken.
Jeremiah51:31 One runner runs to meet another
Jeremiah51:32 the fords have been seized
Jeremiah51:33 For thus says the LORD of hosts
Jeremiah51:34 Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon has devoured me; he has crushed me; he has made me an empty vessel; he has swallowed me like a monster; he has filled his stomach with my delicacies; he has rinsed me out.
Jeremiah51:35 The violence done to me and to my kinsmen be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus says the LORD: Behold
Jeremiah51:37 and Babylon shall become a heap of ruins
Jeremiah51:38 They shall roar together like lions; they shall growl like lions' cubs.
Jeremiah51:39 While they are inflamed I will prepare them a feast and make them drunk
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How Babylon is taken
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has come up on Babylon; she is covered with its tumultuous waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities have become a horror
Jeremiah51:44 And I will punish Bel in Babylon
Jeremiah51:45 Go out of the midst of her
Jeremiah51:46 Let not your heart faint
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore
Jeremiah51:48 Then the heavens and the earth
Jeremiah51:49 Babylon must fall for the slain of Israel
Jeremiah51:50 You who have escaped from the sword
Jeremiah51:51 'We are put to shame
Jeremiah51:52 Therefore
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon should mount up to heaven
Jeremiah51:54 A voice! A cry from Babylon! The noise of great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans!
Jeremiah51:55 For the LORD is laying Babylon waste and stilling her mighty voice. Their waves roar like many waters; the noise of their voice is raised
Jeremiah51:56 for a destroyer has come upon her
Jeremiah51:57 I will make drunk her officials and her wise men
Jeremiah51:58 Thus says the LORD of hosts: The broad wall of Babylon shall be leveled to the ground
Jeremiah51:59 The word that Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Neriah
Jeremiah51:60 Jeremiah wrote in a book all the disaster that should come upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:61 And Jeremiah said to Seraiah: When you come to Babylon
Jeremiah51:62 and say
Jeremiah51:63 When you finish reading this book
Jeremiah51:64 and say
Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king; and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
Jeremiah52:2 And he did what was evil in the sight of the LORD
Jeremiah52:3 For because of the anger of the LORD things came to the point in Jerusalem and Judah that he cast them out from his presence. And Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
Jeremiah52:4 And in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city was besieged till the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine was so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.
Jeremiah52:7 Then a breach was made in the city
Jeremiah52:8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho. And all his army was scattered from him.
Jeremiah52:9 Then they captured the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath
Jeremiah52:10 The king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
Jeremiah52:11 He put out the eyes of Zedekiah
Jeremiah52:12 In the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 And he burned the house of the LORD
Jeremiah52:14 And all the army of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah52:15 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive some of the poorest of the people and the rest of the people who were left in the city and the deserters who had deserted to the king of Babylon
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and plowmen.
Jeremiah52:17 And the pillars of bronze that were in the house of the LORD
Jeremiah52:18 And they took away the pots and the shovels and the snuffers and the basins and the dishes for incense and all the vessels of bronze used in the temple service;
Jeremiah52:19 also the small bowls and the fire pans and the basins and the pots and the lampstands and the dishes for incense and the bowls for drink offerings. What was of gold the captain of the guard took away as gold
Jeremiah52:20 As for the two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 As for the pillars
Jeremiah52:22 On it was a capital of bronze. The height of the one capital was five cubits. A network and pomegranates
Jeremiah52:23 There were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides; all the pomegranates were a hundred upon the network all around.
Jeremiah52:24 And the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 and from the city he took an officer who had been in command of the men of war
Jeremiah52:26 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:27 And the king of Babylon struck them down
Jeremiah52:28 This is the number of the people whom Nebuchadnezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year
Jeremiah52:29 in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar he carried away captive from Jerusalem 832 persons;
Jeremiah52:30 in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:31 And in the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Jehoiachin king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 And he spoke kindly to him
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin put off his prison garments. And every day of his life he dined regularly at the king's table
Jeremiah52:34 and for his allowance
Lamentations1:1 How lonely sits the citythat was full of people! How like a widow has she become
Lamentations1:2 She weeps bitterly in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into exile because of affliction and hard servitude; she dwells now among the nations
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her foes have become the head; her enemies prosper
Lamentations1:6 From the daughter of Zion all her majesty has departed. Her princes have become like deer that find no pasture; they fled without strength before the pursuer.
Lamentations1:7 Jerusalem remembers in the days of her affliction and wandering all the precious things that were hers from days of old. When her people fell into the hand of the foe
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem sinned grievously; therefore she became filthy; all who honored her despise her
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness was in her skirts; she took no thought of her future; therefore her fall is terrible; she has no comforter. O LORD
Lamentations1:10 The enemy has stretched out his hands over all her precious things; for she has seen the nations enter her sanctuary
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan as they search for bread; they trade their treasures for food to revive their strength. Look
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 From on high he sent fire; into my bones he made it descend; he spread a net for my feet; he turned me back; he has left me stunned
Lamentations1:14 My transgressions were bound into a yoke; by his hand they were fastened together; they were set upon my neck; he caused my strength to fail; the Lord gave me into the hands of those whom I cannot withstand.
Lamentations1:15 The Lord rejected all my mighty men in my midst; he summoned an assembly against me to crush my young men; the Lord has trodden as in a winepress the virgin daughter of Judah.
Lamentations1:16 For these things I weep; my eyes flow with tears; for a comforter is far from me
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches out her hands
Lamentations1:18 The LORD is in the right
Lamentations1:19 I called to my lovers
Lamentations1:20 Look
Lamentations1:21 They heard my groaning
Lamentations1:22 Let all their evildoing come before you
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord in his anger has set the daughter of Zion under a cloud! He has cast down from heaven to earth the splendor of Israel; he has not remembered his footstool in the day of his anger.
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has swallowed up without mercy all the habitations of Jacob; in his wrath he has broken down the strongholds of the daughter of Judah; he has brought down to the ground in dishonor the kingdom and its rulers.
Lamentations2:3 He has cut down in fierce anger all the might of Israel; he has withdrawn from them his right hand in the face of the enemy; he has burned like a flaming fire in Jacob
Lamentations2:4 He has bent his bow like an enemy
Lamentations2:5 The Lord has become like an enemy; he has swallowed up Israel; he has swallowed up all its palaces; he has laid in ruins its strongholds
Lamentations2:6 He has laid waste his booth like a garden
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has scorned his altar
Lamentations2:8 The LORD determined to lay in ruins the wall of the daughter of Zion; he stretched out the measuring line; he did not restrain his hand from destroying; he caused rampart and wall to lament; they languished together.
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have sunk into the ground; he has ruined and broken her bars; her king and princes are among the nations; the law is no more
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the daughter of Zion sit on the ground in silence; they have thrown dust on their heads and put on sackcloth; the young women of Jerusalem have bowed their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes are spent with weeping; my stomach churns; my bile is poured out to the ground because of the destruction of the daughter of my people
Lamentations2:12 They cry to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What can I say for you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets have seen for you false and deceptive visions; they have not exposed your iniquity to restore your fortunes
Lamentations2:15 All who pass along the way clap their hands at you; they hiss and wag their heads at the daughter of Jerusalem; Is this the city that was called the perfection of beauty
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies rail against you; they hiss
Lamentations2:17 The LORD has done what he purposed; he has carried out his word
Lamentations2:18 Their heart cried to the Lord. O wall of the daughter of Zion
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 Look
Lamentations2:21 In the dust of the streets lie the young and the old; my young women and my young men have fallen by the sword; you have killed them in the day of your anger
Lamentations2:22 You summoned as if to a festival day my terrors on every side
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen affliction under the rod of his wrath;
Lamentations3:2 he has driven and brought me into darkness without any light;
Lamentations3:3 surely against me he turns his hand again and again the whole day long.
Lamentations3:4 He has made my flesh and my skin waste away; he has broken my bones;
Lamentations3:5 he has besieged and enveloped me with bitterness and tribulation;
Lamentations3:6 he has made me dwell in darkness like the dead of long ago.
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me about so that I cannot escape; he has made my chains heavy;
Lamentations3:8 though I call and cry for help
Lamentations3:9 he has blocked my ways with blocks of stones; he has made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He is a bear lying in wait for me
Lamentations3:11 he turned aside my steps and tore me to pieces; he has made me desolate;
Lamentations3:12 he bent his bow and set me as a target for his arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He drove into my kidneys the arrows of his quiver;
Lamentations3:14 I have become the laughingstock of all peoples
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness; he has sated me with wormwood.
Lamentations3:16 He has made my teeth grind on gravel
Lamentations3:17 my soul is bereft of peace; I have forgotten what happiness is;
Lamentations3:18 so I say
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and my wanderings
Lamentations3:20 My soul continually remembers it and is bowed down within me.
Lamentations3:21 But this I call to mind
Lamentations3:22 The steadfast love of the LORD never ceases; his mercies never come to an end;
Lamentations3:23 they are new every morning; great is your faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 The LORD is my portion
Lamentations3:25 The LORD is good to those who wait for him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that one should wait quietly for the salvation of the LORD.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone in silence when it is laid on him;
Lamentations3:29 let him put his mouth in the dust- there may yet be hope;
Lamentations3:30 let him give his cheek to the one who strikes
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not cast off forever
Lamentations3:32 but
Lamentations3:33 for he does not willingly afflict or grieve the children of men.
Lamentations3:34 To crush underfoot all the prisoners of the earth
Lamentations3:35 to deny a man justice in the presence of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 to subvert a man in his lawsuit
Lamentations3:37 Who has spoken and it came to pass
Lamentations3:38 Is it not from the mouth of the Most High that good and bad come?
Lamentations3:39 Why should a living man complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us test and examine our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our hearts and hands to God in heaven:
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and rebelled
Lamentations3:43 You have wrapped yourself with anger and pursued us
Lamentations3:44 you have wrapped yourself with a cloud so that no prayer can pass through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us scum and garbage among the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies open their mouths against us;
Lamentations3:47 panic and pitfall have come upon us
Lamentations3:48 my eyes flow with rivers of tears because of the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes will flow without ceasing
Lamentations3:50 until the LORD from heaven looks down and sees;
Lamentations3:51 my eyes cause me grief at the fate of all the daughters of my city.
Lamentations3:52 I have been hunted like a bird by those who were my enemies without cause;
Lamentations3:53 they flung me alive into the pit and cast stones on me;
Lamentations3:54 water closed over my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 I called on your name
Lamentations3:56 you heard my plea
Lamentations3:57 You came near when I called on you; you said
Lamentations3:58 You have taken up my cause
Lamentations3:59 You have seen the wrong done to me
Lamentations3:60 You have seen all their vengeance
Lamentations3:61 You have heard their taunts
Lamentations3:62 The lips and thoughts of my assailants are against me all the day long.
Lamentations3:63 Behold their sitting and their rising; I am the object of their taunts.
Lamentations3:64 You will repay them
Lamentations3:65 You will give them dullness of heart; your curse will be on them.
Lamentations3:66 You will pursue them in anger and destroy them from under your heavens
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has grown dim
Lamentations4:2 The precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even jackals offer the breast; they nurse their young
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the nursing infant sticks to the roof of its mouth for thirst; the children beg for food
Lamentations4:5 Those who once feasted on delicacies perish in the streets; those who were brought up in purple embrace ash heaps.
Lamentations4:6 For the chastisement of the daughter of my people has been greater than the punishment of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her princes were purer than snow
Lamentations4:8 Now their face is blacker than soot; they are not recognized in the streets; their skin has shriveled on their bones; it has become as dry as wood.
Lamentations4:9 Happier were the victims of the sword than the victims of hunger
Lamentations4:10 The hands of compassionate women have boiled their own children; they became their food during the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations4:11 The LORD gave full vent to his wrath; he poured out his hot anger
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth did not believe
Lamentations4:13 This was for the sins of her prophets and the iniquities of her priests
Lamentations4:14 They wandered
Lamentations4:15 Away! Unclean! people cried at them. Away! Away! Do not touch! So they became fugitives and wanderers; people said among the nations
Lamentations4:16 The LORD himself has scattered them; he will regard them no more; no honor was shown to the priests
Lamentations4:17 Our eyes failed
Lamentations4:18 They dogged our steps so that we could not walk in our streets; our end drew near; our days were numbered
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles in the heavens; they chased us on the mountains; they lay in wait for us in the wilderness.
Lamentations4:20 The breath of our nostrils
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of your iniquity
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans
Lamentations5:4 We must pay for the water we drink; the wood we get must be bought.
Lamentations5:5 Our pursuers are at our necks; we are weary; we are given no rest.
Lamentations5:6 We have given the hand to Egypt
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; there is none to deliver us from their hand.
Lamentations5:9 We get our bread at the peril of our lives
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is hot as an oven with the burning heat of famine.
Lamentations5:11 Women are raped in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes are hung up by their hands; no respect is shown to the elders.
Lamentations5:13 Young men are compelled to grind at the mill
Lamentations5:14 The old men have left the city gate
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our hearts has ceased; our dancing has been turned to mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head; woe to us
Lamentations5:17 For this our heart has become sick
Lamentations5:18 for Mount Zion which lies desolate; jackals prowl over it.
Lamentations5:19 But you
Lamentations5:20 Why do you forget us forever
Lamentations5:21 Restore us to yourself
Lamentations5:22 unless you have utterly rejected us
Ezekiel1:1 In the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth day of the month (it was the fifth year of the exile of King Jehoiachin)
Ezekiel1:3 the word of the LORD came to Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 As I looked
Ezekiel1:5 And from the midst of it came the likeness of four living creatures. And this was their appearance: they had a human likeness
Ezekiel1:6 but each had four faces
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight
Ezekiel1:8 Under their wings on their four sides they had human hands. And the four had their faces and their wings thus:
Ezekiel1:9 their wings touched one another. Each one of them went straight forward
Ezekiel1:10 As for the likeness of their faces
Ezekiel1:11 Such were their faces. And their wings were spread out above. Each creature had two wings
Ezekiel1:12 And each went straight forward. Wherever the spirit would go
Ezekiel1:13 As for the likeness of the living creatures
Ezekiel1:14 And the living creatures darted to and fro
Ezekiel1:15 Now as I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 As for the appearance of the wheels and their construction: their appearance was like the gleaming of beryl. And the four had the same likeness
Ezekiel1:17 When they went
Ezekiel1:18 And their rims were tall and awesome
Ezekiel1:19 And when the living creatures went
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the spirit wanted to go
Ezekiel1:21 When those went
Ezekiel1:22 Over the heads of the living creatures there was the likeness of an expanse
Ezekiel1:23 And under the expanse their wings were stretched out straight
Ezekiel1:24 And when they went
Ezekiel1:25 And there came a voice from above the expanse over their heads. When they stood still
Ezekiel1:26 And above the expanse over their heads there was the likeness of a throne
Ezekiel1:27 And upward from what had the appearance of his waist I saw as it were gleaming metal
Ezekiel1:28 Like the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud on the day of rain
Ezekiel2:1 And he said to me
Ezekiel2:2 And as he spoke to me
Ezekiel2:3 And he said to me
Ezekiel2:4 The descendants also are impudent and stubborn: I send you to them
Ezekiel2:5 And whether they hear or refuse to hear (for they are a rebellious house) they will know that a prophet has been among them.
Ezekiel2:6 And you
Ezekiel2:7 And you shall speak my words to them
Ezekiel2:8 But you
Ezekiel2:9 And when I looked
Ezekiel2:10 And he spread it before me. And it had writing on the front and on the back
Ezekiel3:1 And he said to me
Ezekiel3:2 So I opened my mouth
Ezekiel3:3 And he said to me
Ezekiel3:4 And he said to me
Ezekiel3:5 For you are not sent to a people of foreign speech and a hard language
Ezekiel3:6 not to many peoples of foreign speech and a hard language
Ezekiel3:7 But the house of Israel will not be willing to listen to you
Ezekiel3:8 Behold
Ezekiel3:9 Like emery harder than flint have I made your forehead. Fear them not
Ezekiel3:10 Moreover
Ezekiel3:11 And go to the exiles
Ezekiel3:12 Then the Spirit lifted me up
Ezekiel3:13 It was the sound of the wings of the living creatures as they touched one another
Ezekiel3:14 The Spirit lifted me up and took me away
Ezekiel3:15 And I came to the exiles at Tel-abib
Ezekiel3:16 And at the end of seven days
Ezekiel3:17 Son of man
Ezekiel3:18 If I say to the wicked
Ezekiel3:19 But if you warn the wicked
Ezekiel3:20 Again
Ezekiel3:21 But if you warn the righteous person not to sin
Ezekiel3:22 And the hand of the LORD was upon me there. And he said to me
Ezekiel3:23 So I arose and went out into the valley

Ezekiel16:14 And your renown went forth among the nations because of your beauty

Ezekiel16:17 You also took your beautiful jewels of my gold and of my silver
Ezekiel16:18 And you took your embroidered garments to cover them
Ezekiel16:19 Also my bread that I gave you- I fed you with fine flour and oil and honey- you set before them for a pleasing aroma; and so it was
Ezekiel16:20 And you took your sons and your daughters
