Ezekiel16:23 And after all your wickedness (woe
Ezekiel16:24 you built yourself a vaulted chamber and made yourself a lofty place in every square.

Ezekiel16:27 Behold

Ezekiel16:32 Adulterous wife

Ezekiel16:37 therefore

Ezekiel16:44 Behold

Ezekiel16:46 And your elder sister is Samaria

Ezekiel16:49 Behold

Ezekiel16:54 that you may bear your disgrace and be ashamed of all that you have done
Ezekiel16:55 As for your sisters

Ezekiel16:61 Then you will remember your ways and be ashamed when you take your sisters

Ezekiel16:63 that you may remember and be confounded

Ezekiel21:13 For it will not be a testing- what could it do if you despise the rod? declares the Lord GOD.
Ezekiel21:14 As for you
Ezekiel21:15 that their hearts may melt
Ezekiel21:16 Cut sharply to the right; set yourself to the left
Ezekiel21:17 I also will clap my hands
Ezekiel21:18 The word of the LORD came to me again:
Ezekiel21:19 As for you
Ezekiel21:20 Mark a way for the sword to come to Rabbah of the Ammonites and to Judah
Ezekiel21:21 For the king of Babylon stands at the parting of the way
Ezekiel21:22 Into his right hand comes the divination for Jerusalem
Ezekiel21:23 But to them it will seem like a false divination. They have sworn solemn oaths
Ezekiel21:24 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Because you have made your guilt to be remembered
Ezekiel21:25 And you
Ezekiel21:26 thus says the Lord GOD: Remove the turban and take off the crown. Things shall not remain as they are. Exalt that which is low
Ezekiel21:27 A ruin
Ezekiel21:28 And you
Ezekiel21:29 while they see for you false visions
Ezekiel21:30 Return it to its sheath. In the place where you were created
Ezekiel21:31 And I will pour out my indignation upon you; I will blow upon you with the fire of my wrath
Ezekiel21:32 You shall be fuel for the fire. Your blood shall be in the midst of the land. You shall be no more remembered
Ezekiel22:1 And the word of the LORD came to me
Ezekiel22:2 And you
Ezekiel22:3 You shall say
Ezekiel22:4 You have become guilty by the blood that you have shed
Ezekiel22:5 Those who are near and those who are far from you will mock you; your name is defiled; you are full of tumult.
Ezekiel22:6 Behold
Ezekiel22:7 Father and mother are treated with contempt in you; the sojourner suffers extortion in your midst; the fatherless and the widow are wronged in you.
Ezekiel22:8 You have despised my holy things and profaned my Sabbaths.
Ezekiel22:9 There are men in you who slander to shed blood
Ezekiel22:10 In you men uncover their fathers' nakedness; in you they violate women who are unclean in their menstrual impurity.
Ezekiel22:11 One commits abomination with his neighbor's wife; another lewdly defiles his daughter-in-law; another in you violates his sister
Ezekiel22:12 In you they take bribes to shed blood; you take interest and profit and make gain of your neighbors by extortion; but me you have forgotten
Ezekiel22:13 Behold
Ezekiel22:14 Can your courage endure
Ezekiel22:15 I will scatter you among the nations and disperse you through the countries
Ezekiel22:16 And you shall be profaned by your own doing in the sight of the nations
Ezekiel22:17 And the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel22:18 Son of man
Ezekiel22:19 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Because you have all become dross
Ezekiel22:20 As one gathers silver and bronze and iron and lead and tin into a furnace
Ezekiel22:21 I will gather you and blow on you with the fire of my wrath
Ezekiel22:22 As silver is melted in a furnace
Ezekiel22:23 And the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel22:24 Son of man
Ezekiel22:25 The conspiracy of her prophets in her midst is like a roaring lion tearing the prey; they have devoured human lives; they have taken treasure and precious things; they have made many widows in her midst.
Ezekiel22:26 Her priests have done violence to my law and have profaned my holy things. They have made no distinction between the holy and the common
Ezekiel22:27 Her princes in her midst are like wolves tearing the prey
Ezekiel22:28 And her prophets have smeared whitewash for them
Ezekiel22:29 The people of the land have practiced extortion and committed robbery. They have oppressed the poor and needy
Ezekiel22:30 And I sought for a man among them who should build up the wall and stand in the breach before me for the land
Ezekiel22:31 Therefore I have poured out my indignation upon them. I have consumed them with the fire of my wrath. I have returned their way upon their heads
Ezekiel23:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel23:2 Son of man
Ezekiel23:3 They played the whore in Egypt; they played the whore in their youth; there their breasts were pressed and their virgin bosoms handled.
Ezekiel23:4 Oholah was the name of the elder and Oholibah the name of her sister. They became mine
Ezekiel23:5 Oholah played the whore while she was mine
Ezekiel23:6 clothed in purple
Ezekiel23:7 She bestowed her whoring upon them
Ezekiel23:8 She did not give up her whoring that she had begun in Egypt; for in her youth men had lain with her and handled her virgin bosom and poured out their whoring lust upon her.
Ezekiel23:9 Therefore I delivered her into the hands of her lovers
Ezekiel23:10 These uncovered her nakedness; they seized her sons and her daughters; and as for her
Ezekiel23:11 Her sister Oholibah saw this
Ezekiel23:12 She lusted after the Assyrians
Ezekiel23:13 And I saw that she was defiled; they both took the same way.
Ezekiel23:14 But she carried her whoring further. She saw men portrayed on the wall
Ezekiel23:15 wearing belts on their waists
Ezekiel23:16 When she saw them
Ezekiel23:17 And the Babylonians came to her into the bed of love
Ezekiel23:18 When she carried on her whoring so openly and flaunted her nakedness
Ezekiel23:19 Yet she increased her whoring
Ezekiel23:20 and lusted after her paramours there
Ezekiel23:21 Thus you longed for the lewdness of your youth
Ezekiel23:22 Therefore
Ezekiel23:23 the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans
Ezekiel23:24 And they shall come against you from the north with chariots and wagons and a host of peoples. They shall set themselves against you on every side with buckler
Ezekiel23:25 And I will direct my jealousy against you
Ezekiel23:26 They shall also strip you of your clothes and take away your beautiful jewels.
Ezekiel23:27 Thus I will put an end to your lewdness and your whoring begun in the land of Egypt
Ezekiel23:28 For thus says the Lord GOD: Behold
Ezekiel23:29 and they shall deal with you in hatred and take away all the fruit of your labor and leave you naked and bare
Ezekiel23:30 have brought this upon you
Ezekiel23:31 You have gone the way of your sister; therefore I will give her cup into your hand.
Ezekiel23:32 Thus says the Lord GOD: You shall drink your sister's cup that is deep and large; you shall be laughed at and held in derision
Ezekiel23:33 you will be filled with drunkenness and sorrow. A cup of horror and desolation
Ezekiel23:34 you shall drink it and drain it out
Ezekiel23:35 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Because you have forgotten me and cast me behind your back
Ezekiel23:36 The LORD said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed adultery
Ezekiel23:38 Moreover
Ezekiel23:39 For when they had slaughtered their children in sacrifice to their idols
Ezekiel23:40 They even sent for men to come from far
Ezekiel23:41 You sat on a stately couch
Ezekiel23:42 The sound of a carefree multitude was with her; and with men of the common sort drunkards were brought from the wilderness; and they put bracelets on the hands of the women
Ezekiel23:43 Then I said of her who was worn out by adultery
Ezekiel23:44 For they have gone in to her
Ezekiel23:45 But righteous men shall pass judgment on them with the sentence of adulteresses
Ezekiel23:46 For thus says the Lord GOD: Bring up a vast host against them
Ezekiel23:47 And the host shall stone them and cut them down with their swords. They shall kill their sons and their daughters
Ezekiel23:48 Thus will I put an end to lewdness in the land
Ezekiel23:49 And they shall return your lewdness upon you
Ezekiel24:1 In the ninth year
Ezekiel24:2 Son of man
Ezekiel24:3 And utter a parable to the rebellious house and say to them
Ezekiel24:4 put in it the pieces of meat
Ezekiel24:5 Take the choicest one of the flock; pile the logs under it; boil it well; seethe also its bones in it.
Ezekiel24:6 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Woe to the bloody city
Ezekiel24:7 For the blood she has shed is in her midst; she put it on the bare rock; she did not pour it out on the ground to cover it with dust.
Ezekiel24:8 To rouse my wrath
Ezekiel24:9 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Woe to the bloody city! I also will make the pile great.
Ezekiel24:10 Heap on the logs
Ezekiel24:11 Then set it empty upon the coals
Ezekiel24:12 She has wearied herself with toil; its abundant corrosion does not go out of it. Into the fire with its corrosion!
Ezekiel24:13 On account of your unclean lewdness
Ezekiel24:14 I am the LORD. I have spoken; it shall come to pass; I will do it. I will not go back; I will not spare; I will not relent; according to your ways and your deeds you will be judged
Ezekiel24:15 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel24:16 Son of man
Ezekiel24:17 Sigh
Ezekiel24:18 So I spoke to the people in the morning
Ezekiel24:19 And the people said to me
Ezekiel24:20 Then I said to them
Ezekiel24:21 'Say to the house of Israel
Ezekiel24:22 And you shall do as I have done; you shall not cover your lips
Ezekiel24:23 Your turbans shall be on your heads and your shoes on your feet; you shall not mourn or weep
Ezekiel24:24 Thus shall Ezekiel be to you a sign; according to all that he has done you shall do. When this comes

Ezekiel24:26 on that day a fugitive will come to you to report to you the news.
Ezekiel24:27 On that day your mouth will be opened to the fugitive

Ezekiel25:2 Son of man
Ezekiel25:3 Say to the Ammonites
Ezekiel25:4 therefore behold

Ezekiel25:6 For thus says the Lord GOD: Because you have clapped your hands and stamped your feet and rejoiced with all the malice within your soul against the land of Israel
Ezekiel25:7 therefore
Ezekiel25:8 Thus says the Lord GOD: Because Moab and Seir said

Ezekiel25:11 and I will execute judgments upon Moab. Then they will know that I am the LORD.

Ezekiel26:1 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel26:2 Son of man
Ezekiel26:3 therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Behold
Ezekiel26:4 They shall destroy the walls of Tyre and break down her towers
Ezekiel26:5 She shall be in the midst of the sea a place for the spreading of nets
Ezekiel26:6 and her daughters on the mainland shall be killed by the sword. Then they will know that I am the LORD.
Ezekiel26:7 For thus says the Lord GOD: Behold
Ezekiel26:8 He will kill with the sword your daughters on the mainland. He will set up a siege wall against you and throw up a mound against you
Ezekiel26:9 He will direct the shock of his battering rams against your walls
Ezekiel26:10 His horses will be so many that their dust will cover you. Your walls will shake at the noise of the horsemen and wagons and chariots
Ezekiel26:11 With the hoofs of his horses he will trample all your streets. He will kill your people with the sword
Ezekiel26:12 They will plunder your riches and loot your merchandise. They will break down your walls and destroy your pleasant houses. Your stones and timber and soil they will cast into the midst of the waters.
Ezekiel26:13 And I will stop the music of your songs
Ezekiel26:14 I will make you a bare rock. You shall be a place for the spreading of nets. You shall never be rebuilt
Ezekiel26:15 Thus says the Lord GOD to Tyre: Will not the coastlands shake at the sound of your fall
Ezekiel26:16 Then all the princes of the sea will step down from their thrones and remove their robes and strip off their embroidered garments. They will clothe themselves with trembling; they will sit on the ground and tremble every moment and be appalled at you.
Ezekiel26:17 And they will raise a lamentation over you and say to you
Ezekiel26:18 Now the coastlands tremble on the day of your fall
Ezekiel26:19 For thus says the Lord GOD: When I make you a city laid waste
Ezekiel26:20 then I will make you go down with those who go down to the pit
Ezekiel26:21 I will bring you to a dreadful end
Ezekiel27:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel27:2 Now you
Ezekiel27:3 and say to Tyre
Ezekiel27:4 Your borders are in the heart of the seas; your builders made perfect your beauty.
Ezekiel27:5 They made all your planks of fir trees from Senir; they took a cedar from Lebanon to make a mast for you.
Ezekiel27:6 Of oaks of Bashan they made your oars; they made your deck of pines from the coasts of Cyprus
Ezekiel27:7 Of fine embroidered linen from Egypt was your sail
Ezekiel27:8 The inhabitants of Sidon and Arvad were your rowers; your skilled men
Ezekiel27:9 The elders of Gebal and her skilled men were in you
Ezekiel27:10 Persia and Lud and Put were in your army as your men of war. They hung the shield and helmet in you; they gave you splendor.
Ezekiel27:11 Men of Arvad and Helech were on your walls all around
Ezekiel27:12 Tarshish did business with you because of your great wealth of every kind; silver
Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 From Beth-togarmah they exchanged horses
Ezekiel27:15 The men of Dedan traded with you. Many coastlands were your own special markets; they brought you in payment ivory tusks and ebony.
Ezekiel27:16 Syria did business with you because of your abundant goods; they exchanged for your wares emeralds
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel traded with you; they exchanged for your merchandise wheat of Minnith
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus did business with you for your abundant goods
Ezekiel27:19 and casks of wine from Uzal they exchanged for your wares; wrought iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan traded with you in saddlecloths for riding.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar were your favored dealers in lambs
Ezekiel27:22 The traders of Sheba and Raamah traded with you; they exchanged for your wares the best of all kinds of spices and all precious stones and gold.
Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 In your market these traded with you in choice garments
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tarshish traveled for you with your merchandise. So you were filled and heavily laden in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:26 Your rowers have brought you out into the high seas. The east wind has wrecked you in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:27 Your riches
Ezekiel27:28 At the sound of the cry of your pilots the countryside shakes
Ezekiel27:29 and down from their ships come all who handle the oar. The mariners and all the pilots of the sea stand on the land
Ezekiel27:30 and shout aloud over you and cry out bitterly. They cast dust on their heads and wallow in ashes;
Ezekiel27:31 they make themselves bald for you and put sackcloth on their waist
Ezekiel27:32 In their wailing they raise a lamentation for you and lament over you: 'Who is like Tyre
Ezekiel27:33 When your wares came from the seas
Ezekiel27:34 Now you are wrecked by the seas
Ezekiel27:35 All the inhabitants of the coastlands are appalled at you
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants among the peoples hiss at you; you have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more forever.'

Ezekiel28:7 therefore
Ezekiel28:8 They shall thrust you down into the pit
Ezekiel28:9 Will you still say

Ezekiel28:11 Moreover
Ezekiel28:12 Son of man
Ezekiel28:13 You were in Eden
Ezekiel28:14 You were an anointed guardian cherub. I placed you; you were on the holy mountain of God; in the midst of the stones of fire you walked.
Ezekiel28:15 You were blameless in your ways from the day you were created
Ezekiel28:16 In the abundance of your trade you were filled with violence in your midst
Ezekiel28:17 Your heart was proud because of your beauty; you corrupted your wisdom for the sake of your splendor. I cast you to the ground; I exposed you before kings
Ezekiel28:18 By the multitude of your iniquities
Ezekiel28:19 All who know you among the peoples are appalled at you; you have come to a dreadful end and shall be no more forever.

Ezekiel28:25 Thus says the Lord GOD: When I gather the house of Israel from the peoples among whom they are scattered
Ezekiel28:26 And they shall dwell securely in it

Ezekiel31:1 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel31:2 Son of man
Ezekiel31:3 Behold
Ezekiel31:4 The waters nourished it; the deep made it grow tall
Ezekiel31:5 So it towered high above all the trees of the field; its boughs grew large and its branches long from abundant water in its shoots.
Ezekiel31:6 All the birds of the heavens made their nests in its boughs; under its branches all the beasts of the field gave birth to their young
Ezekiel31:7 It was beautiful in its greatness
Ezekiel31:8 The cedars in the garden of God could not rival it
Ezekiel31:9 I made it beautiful in the mass of its branches
Ezekiel31:10 Therefore thus says the Lord GOD: Because it towered high and set its top among the clouds
Ezekiel31:11 I will give it into the hand of a mighty one of the nations. He shall surely deal with it as its wickedness deserves. I have cast it out.
Ezekiel31:12 Foreigners
Ezekiel31:13 On its fallen trunk dwell all the birds of the heavens
Ezekiel31:14 All this is in order that no trees by the waters may grow to towering height or set their tops among the clouds
Ezekiel31:15 Thus says the Lord GOD: On the day the cedar went down to Sheol I caused mourning; I closed the deep over it
Ezekiel31:16 I made the nations quake at the sound of its fall
Ezekiel31:17 They also went down to Sheol with it
Ezekiel31:18 Whom are you thus like in glory and in greatness among the trees of Eden? You shall be brought down with the trees of Eden to the world below. You shall lie among the uncircumcised

Ezekiel32:30 The princes of the north are there

Ezekiel33:14 Again

Ezekiel33:16 None of the sins that he has committed shall be remembered against him. He has done what is just and right; he shall surely live.

Ezekiel33:18 When the righteous turns from his righteousness and does injustice

Ezekiel33:30 As for you
Ezekiel33:31 And they come to you as people come
Ezekiel33:32 And behold
Ezekiel33:33 When this comes- and come it will!- then they will know that a prophet has been among them.
Ezekiel34:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel34:2 Son of man
Ezekiel34:3 You eat the fat
Ezekiel34:4 The weak you have not strengthened
Ezekiel34:5 So they were scattered
Ezekiel34:6 My sheep were scattered; they wandered over all the mountains and on every high hill. My sheep were scattered over all the face of the earth
Ezekiel34:7 Therefore
Ezekiel34:8 As I live
Ezekiel34:9 therefore
Ezekiel34:10 Thus says the Lord GOD
Ezekiel34:11 For thus says the Lord GOD: Behold
Ezekiel34:12 As a shepherd seeks out his flock when he is among his sheep that have been scattered
Ezekiel34:13 And I will bring them out from the peoples and gather them from the countries
Ezekiel34:14 I will feed them with good pasture
Ezekiel34:15 I myself will be the shepherd of my sheep
Ezekiel34:16 I will seek the lost
Ezekiel34:17 As for you
Ezekiel34:18 Is it not enough for you to feed on the good pasture
Ezekiel34:19 And must my sheep eat what you have trodden with your feet
Ezekiel34:20 Therefore
Ezekiel34:21 Because you push with side and shoulder
Ezekiel34:22 I will rescue my flock; they shall no longer be a prey. And I will judge between sheep and sheep.
Ezekiel34:23 And I will set up over them one shepherd
Ezekiel34:24 And I

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Daniel1:2 And the Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 Then the king commanded Ashpenaz
Daniel1:4 youths without blemish
Daniel1:5 The king assigned them a daily portion of the food that the king ate
Daniel1:6 Among these were Daniel
Daniel1:7 And the chief of the eunuchs gave them names: Daniel he called Belteshazzar
Daniel1:8 But Daniel resolved that he would not defile himself with the king's food
Daniel1:9 And God gave Daniel favor and compassion in the sight of the chief of the eunuchs
Daniel1:10 and the chief of the eunuchs said to Daniel

Daniel11:8 He shall also carry off to Egypt their gods with their metal images and their precious vessels of silver and gold

Daniel11:11 Then the king of the south

Daniel11:15 Then the king of the north shall come and throw up siegeworks and take a well-fortified city. And the forces of the south shall not stand
Daniel11:16 But he who comes against him shall do as he wills
Daniel11:17 He shall set his face to come with the strength of his whole kingdom
Daniel11:18 Afterward he shall turn his face to the coastlands and shall capture many of them
Daniel11:19 Then he shall turn his face back toward the fortresses of his own land
Daniel11:20 Then shall arise in his place one who shall send an exactor of tribute for the glory of the kingdom. But within a few days he shall be broken
Daniel11:21 In his place shall arise a contemptible person to whom royal majesty has not been given. He shall come in without warning and obtain the kingdom by flatteries.
Daniel11:22 Armies shall be utterly swept away before him and broken
Daniel11:23 And from the time that an alliance is made with him he shall act deceitfully
Daniel11:24 Without warning he shall come into the richest parts of the province
Daniel11:25 And he shall stir up his power and his heart against the king of the south with a great army. And the king of the south shall wage war with an exceedingly great and mighty army
Daniel11:26 Even those who eat his food shall break him. His army shall be swept away
Daniel11:27 And as for the two kings
Daniel11:28 And he shall return to his land with great wealth
Daniel11:29 At the time appointed he shall return and come into the south
Daniel11:30 For ships of Kittim shall come against him
Daniel11:31 Forces from him shall appear and profane the temple and fortress
Daniel11:32 He shall seduce with flattery those who violate the covenant
Daniel11:33 And the wise among the people shall make many understand
Daniel11:34 When they stumble
Daniel11:35 and some of the wise shall stumble
Daniel11:36 And the king shall do as he wills. He shall exalt himself and magnify himself above every god
Daniel11:37 He shall pay no attention to the gods of his fathers
Daniel11:38 He shall honor the god of fortresses instead of these. A god whom his fathers did not know he shall honor with gold and silver
Daniel11:39 He shall deal with the strongest fortresses with the help of a foreign god. Those who acknowledge him he shall load with honor. He shall make them rulers over many and shall divide the land for a price.
Daniel11:40 At the time of the end
Daniel11:41 He shall come into the glorious land. And tens of thousands shall fall
Daniel11:42 He shall stretch out his hand against the countries
Daniel11:43 He shall become ruler of the treasures of gold and of silver
Daniel11:44 But news from the east and the north shall alarm him
Daniel11:45 And he shall pitch his palatial tents between the sea and the glorious holy mountain. Yet he shall come to his end

Hosea7:6 For with hearts like an oven they approach their intrigue; all night their anger smolders; in the morning it blazes like a flaming fire.

Hosea8:13 As for my sacrificial offerings
Hosea8:14 For Israel has forgotten his Maker and built palaces

Hosea9:2 Threshing floor and wine vat shall not feed them
Hosea9:3 They shall not remain in the land of the LORD
Hosea9:4 They shall not pour drink offerings of wine to the LORD
Hosea9:5 What will you do on the day of the appointed festival
Hosea9:6 For behold
Hosea9:7 The days of punishment have come; the days of recompense have come; Israel shall know it. The prophet is a fool; the man of the spirit is mad
Hosea9:8 The prophet is the watchman of Ephraim with my God; yet a fowler's snare is on all his ways
Hosea9:9 They have deeply corrupted themselves as in the days of Gibeah: he will remember their iniquity; he will punish their sins.
Hosea9:10 Like grapes in the wilderness
Hosea9:11 Ephraim's glory shall fly away like a bird- no birth
Hosea9:12 Even if they bring up children
Hosea9:13 Ephraim
Hosea9:14 Give them
Hosea9:15 Every evil of theirs is in Gilgal; there I began to hate them. Because of the wickedness of their deeds I will drive them out of my house. I will love them no more; all their princes are rebels.
Hosea9:16 Ephraim is stricken; their root is dried up; they shall bear no fruit. Even though they give birth
Hosea9:17 My God will reject them because they have not listened to him; they shall be wanderers among the nations.
Hosea10:1 Israel is a luxuriant vine that yields its fruit. The more his fruit increased
Hosea10:2 Their heart is false; now they must bear their guilt. The LORD will break down their altars and destroy their pillars.
Hosea10:3 For now they will say: We have no king
Hosea10:4 They utter mere words; with empty oaths they make covenants; so judgment springs up like poisonous weeds in the furrows of the field.
Hosea10:5 The inhabitants of Samaria tremble for the calf of Beth-aven. Its people mourn for it
Hosea10:6 The thing itself shall be carried to Assyria as tribute to the great king. Ephraim shall be put to shame
Hosea10:7 Samaria's king shall perish like a twig on the face of the waters.
Hosea10:8 The high places of Aven
Hosea10:9 From the days of Gibeah
Hosea10:10 When I please
Hosea10:11 Ephraim was a trained calf that loved to thresh
Hosea10:12 Sow for yourselves righteousness; reap steadfast love; break up your fallow ground
Hosea10:13 You have plowed iniquity; you have reaped injustice; you have eaten the fruit of lies. Because you have trusted in your own way and in the multitude of your warriors
Hosea10:14 therefore the tumult of war shall arise among your people
Hosea10:15 Thus it shall be done to you
Hosea11:1 When Israel was a child
Hosea11:2 The more they were called

Hosea11:11 they shall come trembling like birds from Egypt
Hosea11:12 Ephraim has surrounded me with lies

Hosea12:6 So you

Hosea12:13 By a prophet the LORD brought Israel up from Egypt

Hosea13:2 And now they sin more and more

Joel1:1 The word of the LORD that came to Joel
Joel1:2 Hear this
Joel1:3 Tell your children of it
Joel1:4 What the cutting locust left
Joel1:5 Awake
Joel1:6 For a nation has come up against my land
Joel1:7 It has laid waste my vine and splintered my fig tree; it has stripped off their bark and thrown it down; their branches are made white.
Joel1:8 Lament like a virgin wearing sackcloth for the bridegroom of her youth.
Joel1:9 The grain offering and the drink offering are cut off from the house of the LORD. The priests mourn
Joel1:10 The fields are destroyed
Joel1:11 Be ashamed
Joel1:12 The vine dries up; the fig tree languishes. Pomegranate
Joel1:13 Put on sackcloth and lament
Joel1:14 Consecrate a fast; call a solemn assembly. Gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land to the house of the LORD your God
Joel1:15 Alas for the day! For the day of the LORD is near
Joel1:16 Is not the food cut off before our eyes
Joel1:17 The seed shrivels under the clods; the storehouses are desolate; the granaries are torn down because the grain has dried up.
Joel1:18 How the beasts groan! The herds of cattle are perplexed because there is no pasture for them; even the flocks of sheep suffer.
Joel1:19 To you
Joel1:20 Even the beasts of the field pant for you because the water brooks are dried up
Joel2:1 Blow a trumpet in Zion;sound an alarm on my holy mountain! Let all the inhabitants of the land tremble
Joel2:2 a day of darkness and gloom
Joel2:3 Fire devours before them
Joel2:4 Their appearance is like the appearance of horses
Joel2:5 As with the rumbling of chariots
Joel2:6 Before them peoples are in anguish; all faces grow pale.
Joel2:7 Like warriors they charge; like soldiers they scale the wall. They march each on his way; they do not swerve from their paths.
Joel2:8 They do not jostle one another; each marches in his path; they burst through the weapons and are not halted.
Joel2:9 They leap upon the city
Joel2:10 The earth quakes before them; the heavens tremble. The sun and the moon are darkened
Joel2:11 The LORD utters his voice before his army
Joel2:12 Yet even now
Joel2:13 and rend your hearts and not your garments. Return to the LORD
Joel2:14 Who knows whether he will not turn and relent

Joel2:18 Then the LORD became jealous for his land and had pity on his people.
Joel2:19 The LORD answered and said to his people
Joel2:20 I will remove the northerner far from you
Joel2:21 Fear not
Joel2:22 Fear not
Joel2:23 Be glad
Joel2:24 The threshing floors shall be full of grain; the vats shall overflow with wine and oil.
Joel2:25 I will restore to you the years that the swarming locust has eaten
Joel2:26 You shall eat in plenty and be satisfied
Joel2:27 You shall know that I am in the midst of Israel
Joel2:28 And it shall come to pass afterward
Joel2:29 Even on the male and female servants in those days I will pour out my Spirit.
Joel2:30 And I will show wonders in the heavens and on the earth
Joel2:31 The sun shall be turned to darkness
Joel2:32 And it shall come to pass that everyone who calls on the name of the LORD shall be saved. For in Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there shall be those who escape

Amos1:5 I will break the gate-bar of Damascus

Amos1:7 So I will send a fire upon the wall of Gaza
Amos1:8 I will cut off the inhabitants from Ashdod

Amos1:10 So I will send a fire upon the wall of Tyre

Amos2:2 So I will send a fire upon Moab
Amos2:3 I will cut off the ruler from its midst
Amos2:4 Thus says the LORD: For three transgressions of Judah
Amos2:5 So I will send a fire upon Judah
Amos2:6 Thus says the LORD: For three transgressions of Israel
Amos2:7 those who trample the head of the poor into the dust of the earth and turn aside the way of the afflicted; a man and his father go in to the same girl
Amos2:8 they lay themselves down beside every altar on garments taken in pledge
Amos2:9 Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorite before them

Amos2:12 But you made the Nazirites drink wine

Amos3:5 Does a bird fall in a snare on the earth
Amos3:6 Is a trumpet blown in a city

Obadiah1:1 The vision of Obadiah. Thus says the Lord GOD concerning Edom: We have heard a report from the LORD
Obadiah1:2 Behold
Obadiah1:3 The pride of your heart has deceived you
Obadiah1:4 Though you soar aloft like the eagle
Obadiah1:5 If thieves came to you
Obadiah1:6 How Esau has been pillaged
Obadiah1:7 All your allies have driven you to your border; those at peace with you have deceived you; they have prevailed against you; those who eat your bread have set a trap beneath you- you have no understanding.
Obadiah1:8 Will I not on that day
Obadiah1:9 And your mighty men shall be dismayed
Obadiah1:10 Because of the violence done to your brother Jacob
Obadiah1:11 On the day that you stood aloof
Obadiah1:12 But do not gloat over the day of your brother in the day of his misfortune; do not rejoice over the people of Judah in the day of their ruin; do not boast in the day of distress.
Obadiah1:13 Do not enter the gate of my people in the day of their calamity; do not gloat over his disaster in the day of his calamity; do not loot his wealth in the day of his calamity.
Obadiah1:14 Do not stand at the crossroads to cut off his fugitives; do not hand over his survivors in the day of distress.
Obadiah1:15 For the day of the LORD is near upon all the nations. As you have done
Obadiah1:16 For as you have drunk on my holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But in Mount Zion there shall be those who escape
Obadiah1:18 The house of Jacob shall be a fire
Obadiah1:19 Those of the Negeb shall possess Mount Esau
Obadiah1:20 The exiles of this host of the people of Israel shall possess the land of the Canaanites as far as Zarephath
Obadiah1:21 Saviors shall go up to Mount Zion to rule Mount Esau
Jonah1:1 Now the word of the LORD came to Jonah the son of Amittai
Jonah1:2 Arise

Jonah1:4 But the LORD hurled a great wind upon the sea
Jonah1:5 Then the mariners were afraid
Jonah1:6 So the captain came and said to him
Jonah1:7 And they said to one another
Jonah1:8 Then they said to him
Jonah1:9 And he said to them

Jonah1:11 Then they said to him
Jonah1:12 He said to them
Jonah1:13 Nevertheless
Jonah1:14 Therefore they called out to the LORD
Jonah1:15 So they picked up Jonah and hurled him into the sea
Jonah1:16 Then the men feared the LORD exceedingly
Jonah1:17 And the LORD appointed a great fish to swallow up Jonah. And Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.

Jonah2:7 When my life was fainting away
Jonah2:8 Those who pay regard to vain idols forsake their hope of steadfast love.
Jonah2:9 But I with the voice of thanksgiving will sacrifice to you; what I have vowed I will pay. Salvation belongs to the LORD!

Micah1:1 The word of the LORD that came to Micah of Moresheth in the days of Jotham
Micah1:2 Hear
Micah1:3 For behold
Micah1:4 And the mountains will melt under him
Micah1:5 All this is for the transgression of Jacob and for the sins of the house of Israel. What is the transgression of Jacob? Is it not Samaria? And what is the high place of Judah? Is it not Jerusalem?
Micah1:6 Therefore I will make Samaria a heap in the open country
Micah1:7 All her carved images shall be beaten to pieces
Micah1:8 For this I will lament and wail; I will go stripped and naked; I will make lamentation like the jackals
Micah1:9 For her wound is incurable
Micah1:10 Tell it not in Gath; weep not at all; in Beth-le-aphrah roll yourselves in the dust.
Micah1:11 Pass on your way
Micah1:12 For the inhabitants of Maroth wait anxiously for good
Micah1:13 Harness the steeds to the chariots
Micah1:14 Therefore you shall give parting gifts to Moresheth-gath; the houses of Achzib shall be a deceitful thing to the kings of Israel.
Micah1:15 I will again bring a conqueror to you
Micah1:16 Make yourselves bald and cut off your hair
Micah2:1 Woe to those who devise wickedness and work evil on their beds! When the morning dawns
Micah2:2 They covet fields and seize them
Micah2:3 Therefore thus says the LORD: behold
Micah2:4 In that day they shall take up a taunt song against you and moan bitterly
Micah2:5 Therefore you will have none to cast the line by lot in the assembly of the LORD.
Micah2:6 Do not preach- thus they preach- one should not preach of such things; disgrace will not overtake us.
Micah2:7 Should this be said
Micah2:8 But lately my people have risen up as an enemy; you strip the rich robe from those who pass by trustingly with no thought of war.
Micah2:9 The women of my people you drive out from their delightful houses; from their young children you take away my splendor forever.

Micah2:12 I will surely assemble all of you
Micah2:13 He who opens the breach goes up before them; they break through and pass the gate

Micah4:5 For all the peoples walk each in the name of its god
Micah4:6 In that day
Micah4:7 and the lame I will make the remnant
Micah4:8 And you
Micah4:9 Now why do you cry aloud? Is there no king in you? Has your counselor perished
Micah4:10 Writhe and groan
Micah4:11 Now many nations are assembled against you
Micah4:12 But they do not know the thoughts of the LORD; they do not understand his plan
Micah4:13 Arise and thresh
Micah5:1 Now muster your troops
Micah5:2 But you
Micah5:3 Therefore he shall give them up until the time when she who is in labor has given birth; then the rest of his brothers shall return to the people of Israel.
Micah5:4 And he shall stand and shepherd his flock in the strength of the LORD
Micah5:5 And he shall be their peace. When the Assyrian comes into our land and treads in our palaces
Micah5:6 they shall shepherd the land of Assyria with the sword
Micah5:7 Then the remnant of Jacob shall be in the midst of many peoples like dew from the LORD
Micah5:8 And the remnant of Jacob shall be among the nations
Micah5:9 Your hand shall be lifted up over your adversaries
Micah5:10 And in that day
Micah5:11 and I will cut off the cities of your land and throw down all your strongholds;
Micah5:12 and I will cut off sorceries from your hand
Micah5:13 and I will cut off your carved images and your pillars from among you
Micah5:14 and I will root out your Asherah images from among you and destroy your cities.
Micah5:15 And in anger and wrath I will execute vengeance on the nations that did not obey.
Micah6:1 Hear what the LORD says:Arise
Micah6:2 Hear
Micah6:3 O my people
Micah6:4 For I brought you up from the land of Egypt and redeemed you from the house of slavery
Micah6:5 O my people
Micah6:6 With what shall I come before the LORD
Micah6:7 Will the LORD be pleased with thousands of rams
Micah6:8 He has told you
Micah6:9 The voice of the LORD cries to the city- and it is sound wisdom to fear your name: Hear of the rod and of him who appointed it!
Micah6:10 Can I forget any longer the treasures of wickedness in the house of the wicked
Micah6:11 Shall I acquit the man with wicked scales and with a bag of deceitful weights?
Micah6:12 Your rich men are full of violence; your inhabitants speak lies
Micah6:13 Therefore I strike you with a grievous blow
Micah6:14 You shall eat
Micah6:15 You shall sow
Micah6:16 For you have kept the statutes of Omri
Micah7:1 Woe is me! For I have become as when the summer fruit has been gathered
Micah7:2 The godly has perished from the earth
Micah7:3 Their hands are on what is evil
Micah7:4 The best of them is like a brier
Micah7:5 Put no trust in a neighbor; have no confidence in a friend; guard the doors of your mouth from her who lies in your arms;
Micah7:6 for the son treats the father with contempt
Micah7:7 But as for me
Micah7:8 Rejoice not over me
Micah7:9 I will bear the indignation of the LORD because I have sinned against him
Micah7:10 Then my enemy will see
Micah7:11 A day for the building of your walls! In that day the boundary shall be far extended.
Micah7:12 In that day they will come to you
Micah7:13 But the earth will be desolate because of its inhabitants
Micah7:14 Shepherd your people with your staff
Micah7:15 As in the days when you came out of the land of Egypt
Micah7:16 The nations shall see and be ashamed of all their might; they shall lay their hands on their mouths; their ears shall be deaf;
Micah7:17 they shall lick the dust like a serpent
Micah7:18 Who is a God like you
Micah7:19 He will again have compassion on us; he will tread our iniquities under foot. You will cast all our sins into the depths of the sea.
Micah7:20 You will show faithfulness to Jacob and steadfast love to Abraham
Nahum1:1 An oracle concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum of Elkosh.
Nahum1:2 The LORD is a jealous and avenging God; the LORD is avenging and wrathful; the LORD takes vengeance on his adversaries and keeps wrath for his enemies.
Nahum1:3 The LORD is slow to anger and great in power
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and makes it dry; he dries up all the rivers; Bashan and Carmel wither; the bloom of Lebanon withers.
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before him; the hills melt; the earth heaves before him
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before his indignation? Who can endure the heat of his anger? His wrath is poured out like fire
Nahum1:7 The LORD is good
Nahum1:8 But with an overflowing flood he will make a complete end of the adversaries
Nahum1:9 What do you plot against the LORD? He will make a complete end; trouble will not rise up a second time.
Nahum1:10 For they are like entangled thorns
Nahum1:11 From you came one who plotted evil against the LORD
Nahum1:12 Thus says the LORD
Nahum1:13 And now I will break his yoke from off you and will burst your bonds apart.
Nahum1:14 The LORD has given commandment about you: No more shall your name be perpetuated; from the house of your gods I will cut off the carved image and the metal image. I will make your grave
Nahum1:15 Behold
Nahum2:1 The scatterer has come up against you. Man the ramparts; watch the road; dress for battle; collect all your strength.
Nahum2:2 For the LORD is restoring the majesty of Jacob as the majesty of Israel
Nahum2:3 The shield of his mighty men is red; his soldiers are clothed in scarlet. The chariots come with flashing metal on the day he musters them; the cypress spears are brandished.
Nahum2:4 The chariots race madly through the streets; they rush to and fro through the squares; they gleam like torches; they dart like lightning.
Nahum2:5 He remembers his officers; they stumble as they go
Nahum2:6 The river gates are opened; the palace melts away;
Nahum2:7 its mistress is stripped; she is carried off
Nahum2:8 Nineveh is like a pool whose waters run away. Halt! Halt! they cry
Nahum2:9 Plunder the silver
Nahum2:10 Desolate! Desolation and ruin! Hearts melt and knees tremble; anguish is in all loins; all faces grow pale!
Nahum2:11 Where is the lions' den
Nahum2:12 The lion tore enough for his cubs and strangled prey for his lionesses; he filled his caves with prey and his dens with torn flesh.
Nahum2:13 Behold
Nahum3:1 Woe to the bloody city
Nahum3:2 The crack of the whip
Nahum3:3 Horsemen charging
Nahum3:4 And all for the countless whorings of the prostitute
Nahum3:5 Behold
Nahum3:6 I will throw filth at you and treat you with contempt and make you a spectacle.
Nahum3:7 And all who look at you will shrink from you and say
Nahum3:8 Are you better than Thebes that sat by the Nile
Nahum3:9 Cush was her strength; Egypt too
Nahum3:10 Yet she became an exile; she went into captivity; her infants were dashed in pieces at the head of every street; for her honored men lots were cast
Nahum3:11 You also will be drunken; you will go into hiding; you will seek a refuge from the enemy.
Nahum3:12 All your fortresses are like fig trees with first-ripe figs- if shaken they fall into the mouth of the eater.
Nahum3:13 Behold
Nahum3:14 Draw water for the siege; strengthen your forts; go into the clay; tread the mortar; take hold of the brick mold!
Nahum3:15 There will the fire devour you; the sword will cut you off. It will devour you like the locust. Multiply yourselves like the locust; multiply like the grasshopper!
Nahum3:16 You increased your merchants more than the stars of the heavens. The locust spreads its wings and flies away.
Nahum3:17 Your princes are like grasshoppers
Nahum3:18 Your shepherds are asleep
Nahum3:19 There is no easing your hurt; your wound is grievous. All who hear the news about you clap their hands over you. For upon whom has not come your unceasing evil?
Habakkuk1:1 The oracle that Habakkuk the prophet saw.
Habakkuk1:2 O LORD
Habakkuk1:3 Why do you make me see iniquity
Habakkuk1:4 So the law is paralyzed
Habakkuk1:5 Look among the nations
Habakkuk1:6 For behold
Habakkuk1:7 They are dreaded and fearsome; their justice and dignity go forth from themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards
Habakkuk1:9 They all come for violence
Habakkuk1:10 At kings they scoff
Habakkuk1:11 Then they sweep by like the wind and go on
Habakkuk1:12 Are you not from everlasting
Habakkuk1:13 You who are of purer eyes than to see evil and cannot look at wrong
Habakkuk1:14 You make mankind like the fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 He brings all of them up with a hook; he drags them out with his net; he gathers them in his dragnet; so he rejoices and is glad.
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore he sacrifices to his net and makes offerings to his dragnet; for by them he lives in luxury
Habakkuk1:17 Is he then to keep on emptying his net and mercilessly killing nations forever?
Habakkuk2:1 I will take my stand at my watchpost and station myself on the tower
Habakkuk2:2 And the LORD answered me: Write the vision; make it plain on tablets
Habakkuk2:3 For still the vision awaits its appointed time; it hastens to the end- it will not lie. If it seems slow
Habakkuk2:4 Behold
Habakkuk2:5 Moreover
Habakkuk2:6 Shall not all these take up their taunt against him
Habakkuk2:7 Will not your debtors suddenly arise
Habakkuk2:8 Because you have plundered many nations
Habakkuk2:9 Woe to him who gets evil gain for his house
Habakkuk2:10 You have devised shame for your house by cutting off many peoples; you have forfeited your life.
Habakkuk2:11 For the stone will cry out from the wall
Habakkuk2:12 Woe to him who builds a town with blood and founds a city on iniquity!
Habakkuk2:13 Behold
Habakkuk2:14 For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD as the waters cover the sea.
Habakkuk2:15 Woe to him who makes his neighbors drink- you pour out your wrath and make them drunk
Habakkuk2:16 You will have your fill of shame instead of glory. Drink
Habakkuk2:17 The violence done to Lebanon will overwhelm you
Habakkuk2:18 What profit is an idol when its maker has shaped it
Habakkuk2:19 Woe to him who says to a wooden thing
Habakkuk2:20 But the LORD is in his holy temple; let all the earth keep silence before him.
Habakkuk3:1 A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet
Habakkuk3:2 O LORD
Habakkuk3:3 God came from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 His brightness was like the light; rays flashed from his hand; and there he veiled his power.
Habakkuk3:5 Before him went pestilence
Habakkuk3:6 He stood and measured the earth; he looked and shook the nations; then the eternal mountains were scattered; the everlasting hills sank low. His were the everlasting ways.
Habakkuk3:7 I saw the tents of Cushan in affliction; the curtains of the land of Midian did tremble.
Habakkuk3:8 Was your wrath against the rivers
Habakkuk3:9 You stripped the sheath from your bow
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw you and writhed; the raging waters swept on; the deep gave forth its voice; it lifted its hands on high.
Habakkuk3:11 The sun and moon stood still in their place at the light of your arrows as they sped
Habakkuk3:12 You marched through the earth in fury; you threshed the nations in anger.
Habakkuk3:13 You went out for the salvation of your people
Habakkuk3:14 You pierced with his own arrows the heads of his warriors
Habakkuk3:15 You trampled the sea with your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I hear
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree should not blossom
Habakkuk3:18 yet I will rejoice in the LORD; I will take joy in the God of my salvation.
Habakkuk3:19 GOD

Zephaniah1:16 a day of trumpet blast and battle cry against the fortified cities and against the lofty battlements.
Zephaniah1:17 I will bring distress on mankind
Zephaniah1:18 Neither their silver nor their gold shall be able to deliver them on the day of the wrath of the LORD. In the fire of his jealousy

Zephaniah3:2 She listens to no voice; she accepts no correction. She does not trust in the LORD; she does not draw near to her God.
Zephaniah3:3 Her officials within her are roaring lions; her judges are evening wolves that leave nothing till the morning.
Zephaniah3:4 Her prophets are fickle
Zephaniah3:5 The LORD within her is righteous; he does no injustice; every morning he shows forth his justice; each dawn he does not fail; but the unjust knows no shame.
Zephaniah3:6 I have cut off nations; their battlements are in ruins; I have laid waste their streets so that no one walks in them; their cities have been made desolate
Zephaniah3:7 I said
Zephaniah3:8 Therefore wait for me

Zephaniah3:11 On that day you shall not be put to shame because of the deeds by which you have rebelled against me; for then I will remove from your midst your proudly exultant ones
Zephaniah3:12 But I will leave in your midst a people humble and lowly. They shall seek refuge in the name of the LORD
Zephaniah3:13 those who are left in Israel; they shall do no injustice and speak no lies

Zephaniah3:15 The LORD has taken away the judgments against you; he has cleared away your enemies. The King of Israel

Zephaniah3:19 Behold
Zephaniah3:20 At that time I will bring you in
Haggai1:1 In the second year of Darius the king

Haggai1:4 Is it a time for you yourselves to dwell in your paneled houses
Haggai1:5 Now
Haggai1:6 You have sown much
Haggai1:7 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Consider your ways.
Haggai1:8 Go up to the hills and bring wood and build the house
Haggai1:9 You looked for much
Haggai1:10 Therefore the heavens above you have withheld the dew
Haggai1:11 And I have called for a drought on the land and the hills
Haggai1:12 Then Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel
Haggai1:13 Then Haggai
Haggai1:14 And the LORD stirred up the spirit of Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel

Haggai2:2 Speak now to Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel
Haggai2:3 'Who is left among you who saw this house in its former glory? How do you see it now? Is it not as nothing in your eyes?
Haggai2:4 Yet now be strong
Haggai2:5 according to the covenant that I made with you when you came out of Egypt. My Spirit remains in your midst. Fear not.
Haggai2:6 For thus says the LORD of hosts: Yet once more
Haggai2:7 And I will shake all nations
Haggai2:8 The silver is mine
Haggai2:9 The latter glory of this house shall be greater than the former

Haggai2:11 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Ask the priests about the law:
Haggai2:12 'If someone carries holy meat in the fold of his garment and touches with his fold bread or stew or wine or oil or any kind of food
Haggai2:13 Then Haggai said
Haggai2:14 Then Haggai answered and said
Haggai2:15 Now then
Haggai2:16 how did you fare? When one came to a heap of twenty measures
Haggai2:17 I struck you and all the products of your toil with blight and with mildew and with hail
Haggai2:18 Consider from this day onward
Haggai2:19 Is the seed yet in the barn? Indeed
Haggai2:20 The word of the LORD came a second time to Haggai on the twenty-fourth day of the month
Haggai2:21 Speak to Zerubbabel
Haggai2:22 and to overthrow the throne of kingdoms. I am about to destroy the strength of the kingdoms of the nations
Haggai2:23 On that day

Zechariah7:1 In the fourth year of King Darius
Zechariah7:2 Now the people of Bethel had sent Sharezer and Regem-melech and their men to entreat the favor of the LORD
Zechariah7:3 saying to the priests of the house of the LORD of hosts and the prophets
Zechariah7:4 Then the word of the LORD of hosts came to me:
Zechariah7:5 Say to all the people of the land and the priests
Zechariah7:6 And when you eat and when you drink
Zechariah7:7 Were not these the words that the LORD proclaimed by the former prophets
Zechariah7:8 And the word of the LORD came to Zechariah
Zechariah7:9 Thus says the LORD of hosts
Zechariah7:10 do not oppress the widow
Zechariah7:11 But they refused to pay attention and turned a stubborn shoulder and stopped their ears that they might not hear.
Zechariah7:12 They made their hearts diamond-hard lest they should hear the law and the words that the LORD of hosts had sent by his Spirit through the former prophets. Therefore great anger came from the LORD of hosts.
Zechariah7:13 As I called
Zechariah7:14 and I scattered them with a whirlwind among all the nations that they had not known. Thus the land they left was desolate
Zechariah8:1 And the word of the LORD of hosts came
Zechariah8:2 Thus says the LORD of hosts: I am jealous for Zion with great jealousy
Zechariah8:3 Thus says the LORD: I have returned to Zion and will dwell in the midst of Jerusalem
Zechariah8:4 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Old men and old women shall again sit in the streets of Jerusalem
Zechariah8:5 And the streets of the city shall be full of boys and girls playing in its streets.
Zechariah8:6 Thus says the LORD of hosts: If it is marvelous in the sight of the remnant of this people in those days
Zechariah8:7 Thus says the LORD of hosts: behold
Zechariah8:8 and I will bring them to dwell in the midst of Jerusalem. And they shall be my people
Zechariah8:9 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Let your hands be strong
Zechariah8:10 For before those days there was no wage for man or any wage for beast
Zechariah8:11 But now I will not deal with the remnant of this people as in the former days
Zechariah8:12 For there shall be a sowing of peace. The vine shall give its fruit
Zechariah8:13 And as you have been a byword of cursing among the nations
Zechariah8:14 For thus says the LORD of hosts: As I purposed to bring disaster to you when your fathers provoked me to wrath
Zechariah8:15 so again have I purposed in these days to bring good to Jerusalem and to the house of Judah; fear not.
Zechariah8:16 These are the things that you shall do: Speak the truth to one another; render in your gates judgments that are true and make for peace;
Zechariah8:17 do not devise evil in your hearts against one another
Zechariah8:18 And the word of the LORD of hosts came to me
Zechariah8:19 Thus says the LORD of hosts: The fast of the fourth month and the fast of the fifth and the fast of the seventh and the fast of the tenth shall be to the house of Judah seasons of joy and gladness and cheerful feasts. Therefore love truth and peace.
Zechariah8:20 Thus says the LORD of hosts: Peoples shall yet come
Zechariah8:21 The inhabitants of one city shall go to another
Zechariah8:22 Many peoples and strong nations shall come to seek the LORD of hosts in Jerusalem and to entreat the favor of the LORD.
Zechariah8:23 Thus says the LORD of hosts: In those days ten men from the nations of every tongue shall take hold of the robe of a Jew

Matthew26:3 Then the chief priests and the elders of the people gathered in the palace of the high priest
Matthew26:4 and plotted together in order to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill him.
Matthew26:5 But they said
Matthew26:6 Now when Jesus was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper
Matthew26:7 a woman came up to him with an alabaster flask of very expensive ointment
Matthew26:8 And when the disciples saw it
Matthew26:9 For this could have been sold for a large sum and given to the poor.
Matthew26:10 But Jesus
Matthew26:11 For you always have the poor with you
Matthew26:12 In pouring this ointment on my body
Matthew26:13 Truly
Matthew26:14 Then one of the twelve
Matthew26:15 and said
Matthew26:16 And from that moment he sought an opportunity to betray him.
Matthew26:17 Now on the first day of Unleavened Bread the disciples came to Jesus
Matthew26:18 He said
Matthew26:19 And the disciples did as Jesus had directed them
Matthew26:20 When it was evening
Matthew26:21 And as they were eating
Matthew26:22 And they were very sorrowful and began to say to him one after another
Matthew26:23 He answered
Matthew26:24 The Son of Man goes as it is written of him
Matthew26:25 Judas
Matthew26:26 Now as they were eating
Matthew26:27 And he took a cup
Matthew26:28 for this is my blood of the covenant
Matthew26:29 I tell you I will not drink again of this fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father's kingdom.
Matthew26:30 And when they had sung a hymn
Matthew26:31 Then Jesus said to them
Matthew26:32 But after I am raised up
Matthew26:33 Peter answered him
Matthew26:34 Jesus said to him
Matthew26:35 Peter said to him
Matthew26:36 Then Jesus went with them to a place called Gethsemane
Matthew26:37 And taking with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee
Matthew26:38 Then he said to them
Matthew26:39 And going a little farther he fell on his face and prayed
Matthew26:40 And he came to the disciples and found them sleeping. And he said to Peter
Matthew26:41 Watch and pray that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing
Matthew26:42 Again
Matthew26:43 And again he came and found them sleeping
Matthew26:44 So
Matthew26:45 Then he came to the disciples and said to them
Matthew26:46 Rise
Matthew26:47 While he was still speaking
Matthew26:48 Now the betrayer had given them a sign
Matthew26:49 And he came up to Jesus at once and said
Matthew26:50 Jesus said to him
Matthew26:51 And behold
Matthew26:52 Then Jesus said to him
Matthew26:53 Do you think that I cannot appeal to my Father
Matthew26:54 But how then should the Scriptures be fulfilled
Matthew26:55 At that hour Jesus said to the crowds
Matthew26:56 But all this has taken place that the Scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left him and fled.
Matthew26:57 Then those who had seized Jesus led him to Caiaphas the high priest
Matthew26:58 And Peter was following him at a distance
Matthew26:59 Now the chief priests and the whole Council were seeking false testimony against Jesus that they might put him to death
Matthew26:60 but they found none
Matthew26:61 and said
Matthew26:62 And the high priest stood up and said
Matthew26:63 But Jesus remained silent. And the high priest said to him
Matthew26:64 Jesus said to him
Matthew26:65 Then the high priest tore his robes and said
Matthew26:66 What is your judgment? They answered
Matthew26:67 Then they spit in his face and struck him. And some slapped him
Matthew26:68 saying
Matthew26:69 Now Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard. And a servant girl came up to him and said
Matthew26:70 But he denied it before them all
Matthew26:71 And when he went out to the entrance
Matthew26:72 And again he denied it with an oath: I do not know the man.
Matthew26:73 After a little while the bystanders came up and said to Peter
Matthew26:74 Then he began to invoke a curse on himself and to swear
Matthew26:75 And Peter remembered the saying of Jesus
Matthew27:1 When morning came
Matthew27:2 And they bound him and led him away and delivered him over to Pilate the governor.
Matthew27:3 Then when Judas
Matthew27:4 saying
Matthew27:5 And throwing down the pieces of silver into the temple
Matthew27:6 But the chief priests
Matthew27:7 So they took counsel and bought with them the potter's field as a burial place for strangers.
Matthew27:8 Therefore that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
Matthew27:9 Then was fulfilled what had been spoken by the prophet Jeremiah
Matthew27:10 and they gave them for the potter's field
Matthew27:11 Now Jesus stood before the governor
Matthew27:12 But when he was accused by the chief priests and elders
Matthew27:13 Then Pilate said to him
Matthew27:14 But he gave him no answer
Matthew27:15 Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to release for the crowd any one prisoner whom they wanted.
Matthew27:16 And they had then a notorious prisoner called Barabbas.
Matthew27:17 So when they had gathered
Matthew27:18 For he knew that it was out of envy that they had delivered him up.
Matthew27:19 Besides
Matthew27:20 Now the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowd to ask for Barabbas and destroy Jesus.

Matthew27:22 Pilate said to them
Matthew27:23 And he said
Matthew27:24 So when Pilate saw that he was gaining nothing
Matthew27:25 And all the people answered
Matthew27:26 Then he released for them Barabbas
Matthew27:27 Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the governor's headquarters
Matthew27:28 And they stripped him and put a scarlet robe on him
Matthew27:29 and twisting together a crown of thorns
Matthew27:30 And they spit on him and took the reed and struck him on the head.
Matthew27:31 And when they had mocked him
Matthew27:32 As they went out
Matthew27:33 And when they came to a place called Golgotha (which means Place of a Skull)
Matthew27:34 they offered him wine to drink
Matthew27:35 And when they had crucified him
Matthew27:36 Then they sat down and kept watch over him there.
Matthew27:37 And over his head they put the charge against him
Matthew27:38 Then two robbers were crucified with him
Matthew27:39 And those who passed by derided him
Matthew27:40 and saying
Matthew27:41 So also the chief priests
Matthew27:42 He saved others; he cannot save himself. He is the King of Israel; let him come down now from the cross
Matthew27:43 He trusts in God; let God deliver him now
Matthew27:44 And the robbers who were crucified with him also reviled him in the same way.
Matthew27:45 Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land until the ninth hour.
Matthew27:46 And about the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice
Matthew27:47 And some of the bystanders
Matthew27:48 And one of them at once ran and took a sponge
Matthew27:49 But the others said
Matthew27:50 And Jesus cried out again with a loud voice and yielded up his spirit.
Matthew27:51 And behold
Matthew27:52 The tombs also were opened. And many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised
Matthew27:53 and coming out of the tombs after his resurrection they went into the holy city and appeared to many.
Matthew27:54 When the centurion and those who were with him
Matthew27:55 There were also many women there
Matthew27:56 among whom were Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James and Joseph and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
Matthew27:57 When it was evening
Matthew27:58 He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate ordered it to be given to him.
Matthew27:59 And Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen shroud
Matthew27:60 and laid it in his own new tomb
Matthew27:61 Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there
Matthew27:62 Next day
Matthew27:63 and said
Matthew27:64 Therefore order the tomb to be made secure until the third day
Matthew27:65 Pilate said to them
Matthew27:66 So they went and made the tomb secure by sealing the stone and setting a guard.

Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 As it is written in Isaiah the prophet
Mark1:3 the voice of one crying in the wilderness: Prepare the way of the Lord
Mark1:4 John appeared
Mark1:5 And all the country of Judea and all Jerusalem were going out to him and were being baptized by him in the river Jordan
Mark1:6 Now John was clothed with camel's hair and wore a leather belt around his waist and ate locusts and wild honey.
Mark1:7 And he preached
Mark1:8 I have baptized you with water
Mark1:9 In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
Mark1:10 And when he came up out of the water
Mark1:11 And a voice came from heaven
Mark1:12 The Spirit immediately drove him out into the wilderness.
Mark1:13 And he was in the wilderness forty days
Mark1:14 Now after John was arrested
Mark1:15 and saying
Mark1:16 Passing alongside the Sea of Galilee
Mark1:17 And Jesus said to them
Mark1:18 And immediately they left their nets and followed him.
Mark1:19 And going on a little farther
Mark1:20 And immediately he called them
Mark1:21 And they went into Capernaum
Mark1:22 And they were astonished at his teaching
Mark1:23 And immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit. And he cried out
Mark1:24 What have you to do with us
Mark1:25 But Jesus rebuked him
Mark1:26 And the unclean spirit
Mark1:27 And they were all amazed
Mark1:28 And at once his fame spread everywhere throughout all the surrounding region of Galilee.
Mark1:29 And immediately he left the synagogue and entered the house of Simon and Andrew
Mark1:30 Now Simon's mother-in-law lay ill with a fever
Mark1:31 And he came and took her by the hand and lifted her up
Mark1:32 That evening at sundown they brought to him all who were sick or oppressed by demons.
Mark1:33 And the whole city was gathered together at the door.
Mark1:34 And he healed many who were sick with various diseases
Mark1:35 And rising very early in the morning
Mark1:36 And Simon and those who were with him searched for him
Mark1:37 and they found him and said to him
Mark1:38 And he said to them
Mark1:39 And he went throughout all Galilee
Mark1:40 And a leper came to him
Mark1:41 Moved with pity
Mark1:42 And immediately the leprosy left him
Mark1:43 And Jesus sternly charged him and sent him away at once
Mark1:44 and said to him
Mark1:45 But he went out and began to talk freely about it

Mark2:22 And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. If he does

Mark2:26 how he entered the house of God

Mark2:28 So the Son of Man is lord even of the Sabbath.

Mark4:1 Again he began to teach beside the sea. And a very large crowd gathered about him
Mark4:2 And he was teaching them many things in parables
Mark4:3 Listen! A sower went out to sow.
Mark4:4 And as he sowed
Mark4:5 Other seed fell on rocky ground
Mark4:6 And when the sun rose it was scorched
Mark4:7 Other seed fell among thorns
Mark4:8 And other seeds fell into good soil and produced grain
Mark4:9 And he said
Mark4:10 And when he was alone
Mark4:11 And he said to them
Mark4:12 so that they may indeed see but not perceive
Mark4:13 And he said to them
Mark4:14 The sower sows the word.
Mark4:15 And these are the ones along the path
Mark4:16 And these are the ones sown on rocky ground: the ones who
Mark4:17 And they have no root in themselves
Mark4:18 And others are the ones sown among thorns. They are those who hear the word
Mark4:19 but the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of riches and the desires for other things enter in and choke the word
Mark4:20 But those that were sown on the good soil are the ones who hear the word and accept it and bear fruit
Mark4:21 And he said to them
Mark4:22 For nothing is hidden except to be made manifest; nor is anything secret except to come to light.
Mark4:23 If anyone has ears to hear
Mark4:24 And he said to them
Mark4:25 For to the one who has
Mark4:26 And he said
Mark4:27 He sleeps and rises night and day
Mark4:28 The earth produces by itself
Mark4:29 But when the grain is ripe
Mark4:30 And he said
Mark4:31 It is like a grain of mustard seed
Mark4:32 yet when it is sown it grows up and becomes larger than all the garden plants and puts out large branches

Mark4:34 He did not speak to them without a parable

John4:6 Jacob's well was there; so Jesus
John4:7 There came a woman of Samaria to draw water. Jesus said to her
John4:8 (For his disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.)
John4:9 The Samaritan woman said to him
John4:10 Jesus answered her
John4:11 The woman said to him
John4:12 Are you greater than our father Jacob? He gave us the well and drank from it himself
John4:13 Jesus said to her

John4:16 Jesus said to her

John5:4 ***

John5:29 and come out

John5:33 You sent to John

John5:35 He was a burning and shining lamp
John5:36 But the testimony that I have is greater than that of John. For the works that the Father has given me to accomplish
John5:37 And the Father who sent me has himself borne witness about me. His voice you have never heard
John5:38 and you do not have his word abiding in you
John5:39 You search the Scriptures because you think that in them you have eternal life; and it is they that bear witness about me
John5:40 yet you refuse to come to me that you may have life.

Acts20:7 On the first day of the week
Acts20:8 There were many lamps in the upper room where we were gathered.
Acts20:9 And a young man named Eutychus
Acts20:10 But Paul went down and bent over him
Acts20:11 And when Paul had gone up and had broken bread and eaten
Acts20:12 And they took the youth away alive
Acts20:13 But going ahead to the ship
Acts20:14 And when he met us at Assos
Acts20:15 And sailing from there we came the following day opposite Chios; the next day we touched at Samos; and the day after that we went to Miletus.
Acts20:16 For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus
Acts20:17 Now from Miletus he sent to Ephesus and called the elders of the church to come to him.
Acts20:18 And when they came to him
Acts20:19 serving the Lord with all humility and with tears and with trials that happened to me through the plots of the Jews;
Acts20:20 how I did not shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable
Acts20:21 testifying both to Jews and to Greeks of repentance toward God and of faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.
Acts20:22 And now
Acts20:23 except that the Holy Spirit testifies to me in every city that imprisonment and afflictions await me.
Acts20:24 But I do not account my life of any value nor as precious to myself
Acts20:25 And now
Acts20:26 Therefore I testify to you this day that I am innocent of the blood of all of you
Acts20:27 for I did not shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God.
Acts20:28 Pay careful attention to yourselves and to all the flock
Acts20:29 I know that after my departure fierce wolves will come in among you
Acts20:30 and from among your own selves will arise men speaking twisted things
Acts20:31 Therefore be alert
Acts20:32 And now I commend you to God and to the word of his grace
Acts20:33 I coveted no one's silver or gold or apparel.
Acts20:34 You yourselves know that these hands ministered to my necessities and to those who were with me.
Acts20:35 In all things I have shown you that by working hard in this way we must help the weak and remember the words of the Lord Jesus
Acts20:36 And when he had said these things
Acts20:37 And there was much weeping on the part of all; they embraced Paul and kissed him
Acts20:38 being sorrowful most of all because of the word he had spoken
Acts21:1 And when we had parted from them and set sail

Acts21:3 When we had come in sight of Cyprus
Acts21:4 And having sought out the disciples
Acts21:5 When our days there were ended
Acts21:6 and said farewell to one another. Then we went on board the ship

Acts21:9 He had four unmarried daughters

Acts24:1 And after five days the high priest Ananias came down with some elders and a spokesman
Acts24:2 And when he had been summoned

Acts24:5 For we have found this man a plague
Acts24:6 He even tried to profane the temple
Acts24:7 ***
Acts24:8 By examining him yourself you will be able to find out from him about everything of which we accuse him.
Acts24:9 The Jews also joined in the charge
Acts24:10 And when the governor had nodded to him to speak
Acts24:11 You can verify that it is not more than twelve days since I went up to worship in Jerusalem
Acts24:12 and they did not find me disputing with anyone or stirring up a crowd
Acts24:13 Neither can they prove to you what they now bring up against me.
Acts24:14 But this I confess to you
Acts24:15 having a hope in God
Acts24:16 So I always take pains to have a clear conscience toward both God and man.
Acts24:17 Now after several years I came to bring alms to my nation and to present offerings.
Acts24:18 While I was doing this
Acts24:19 they ought to be here before you and to make an accusation
Acts24:20 Or else let these men themselves say what wrongdoing they found when I stood before the council
Acts24:21 other than this one thing that I cried out while standing among them: 'It is with respect to the resurrection of the dead that I am on trial before you this day.'
Acts24:22 But Felix

Acts24:25 And as he reasoned about righteousness and self-control and the coming judgment
Acts24:26 At the same time he hoped that money would be given him by Paul. So he sent for him often and conversed with him.
Acts24:27 When two years had elapsed
Acts25:1 Now three days after Festus had arrived in the province
Acts25:2 And the chief priests and the principal men of the Jews laid out their case against Paul
Acts25:3 asking as a favor against Paul that he summon him to Jerusalem- because they were planning an ambush to kill him on the way.
Acts25:4 Festus replied that Paul was being kept at Caesarea and that he himself intended to go there shortly.
Acts25:5 So
Acts25:6 After he stayed among them not more than eight or ten days

Acts25:9 But Festus
Acts25:10 But Paul said
Acts25:11 If then I am a wrongdoer and have committed anything for which I deserve to die
Acts25:12 Then Festus
Acts25:13 Now when some days had passed
Acts25:14 And as they stayed there many days
Acts25:15 and when I was at Jerusalem

Acts25:17 So when they came together here
Acts25:18 When the accusers stood up
Acts25:19 Rather they had certain points of dispute with him about their own religion and about a certain Jesus

Acts25:25 But I found that he had done nothing deserving death. And as he himself appealed to the emperor
Acts25:26 But I have nothing definite to write to my lord about him. Therefore I have brought him before you all
Acts25:27 For it seems to me unreasonable
Acts26:1 So Agrippa said to Paul
Acts26:2 I consider myself fortunate that it is before you
Acts26:3 especially because you are familiar with all the customs and controversies of the Jews. Therefore I beg you to listen to me patiently.
Acts26:4 My manner of life from my youth
Acts26:5 They have known for a long time
Acts26:6 And now I stand here on trial because of my hope in the promise made by God to our fathers
Acts26:7 to which our twelve tribes hope to attain
Acts26:8 Why is it thought incredible by any of you that God raises the dead?
Acts26:9 I myself was convinced that I ought to do many things in opposing the name of Jesus of Nazareth.
Acts26:10 And I did so in Jerusalem. I not only locked up many of the saints in prison after receiving authority from the chief priests
Acts26:11 And I punished them often in all the synagogues and tried to make them blaspheme
Acts26:12 In this connection I journeyed to Damascus with the authority and commission of the chief priests.
Acts26:13 At midday
Acts26:14 And when we had all fallen to the ground
Acts26:15 And I said
Acts26:16 But rise and stand upon your feet
Acts26:17 delivering you from your people and from the Gentiles- to whom I am sending you
Acts26:18 to open their eyes
Acts26:19 Therefore
Acts26:20 but declared first to those in Damascus
Acts26:21 For this reason the Jews seized me in the temple and tried to kill me.
Acts26:22 To this day I have had the help that comes from God
Acts26:23 that the Christ must suffer and that
