Acts26:28 And Agrippa said to Paul
Acts26:29 And Paul said
Acts26:30 Then the king rose
Acts26:31 And when they had withdrawn
Acts26:32 And Agrippa said to Festus
Acts27:1 And when it was decided that we should sail for Italy
Acts27:2 And embarking in a ship of Adramyttium
Acts27:3 The next day we put in at Sidon. And Julius treated Paul kindly and gave him leave to go to his friends and be cared for.
Acts27:4 And putting out to sea from there we sailed under the lee of Cyprus
Acts27:5 And when we had sailed across the open sea along the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia
Acts27:6 There the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing for Italy and put us on board.
Acts27:7 We sailed slowly for a number of days and arrived with difficulty off Cnidus
Acts27:8 Coasting along it with difficulty
Acts27:9 Since much time had passed
Acts27:10 saying
Acts27:11 But the centurion paid more attention to the pilot and to the owner of the ship than to what Paul said.
Acts27:12 And because the harbor was not suitable to spend the winter in
Acts27:13 Now when the south wind blew gently
Acts27:14 But soon a tempestuous wind
Acts27:15 And when the ship was caught and could not face the wind
Acts27:16 Running under the lee of a small island called Cauda
Acts27:17 After hoisting it up
Acts27:18 Since we were violently storm-tossed
Acts27:19 And on the third day they threw the ship's tackle overboard with their own hands.
Acts27:20 When neither sun nor stars appeared for many days
Acts27:21 Since they had been without food for a long time
Acts27:22 Yet now I urge you to take heart
Acts27:23 For this very night there stood before me an angel of the God to whom I belong and whom I worship
Acts27:24 and he said
Acts27:25 So take heart
Acts27:26 But we must run aground on some island.
Acts27:27 When the fourteenth night had come
Acts27:28 So they took a sounding and found twenty fathoms. A little farther on they took a sounding again and found fifteen fathoms.
Acts27:29 And fearing that we might run on the rocks
Acts27:30 And as the sailors were seeking to escape from the ship
Acts27:31 Paul said to the centurion and the soldiers
Acts27:32 Then the soldiers cut away the ropes of the ship's boat and let it go.
Acts27:33 As day was about to dawn
Acts27:34 Therefore I urge you to take some food. It will give you strength
Acts27:35 And when he had said these things
Acts27:36 Then they all were encouraged and ate some food themselves.
Acts27:37 (We were in all 276 persons in the ship.)
Acts27:38 And when they had eaten enough
Acts27:39 Now when it was day
Acts27:40 So they cast off the anchors and left them in the sea
Acts27:41 But striking a reef
Acts27:42 The soldiers' plan was to kill the prisoners
Acts27:43 But the centurion
Acts27:44 and the rest on planks or on pieces of the ship. And so it was that all were brought safely to land.
Acts28:1 After we were brought safely through
Acts28:2 The native people showed us unusual kindness
Acts28:3 When Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks and put them on the fire
Acts28:4 When the native people saw the creature hanging from his hand
Acts28:5 He
Acts28:6 They were waiting for him to swell up or suddenly fall down dead. But when they had waited a long time and saw no misfortune come to him
Acts28:7 Now in the neighborhood of that place were lands belonging to the chief man of the island
Acts28:8 It happened that the father of Publius lay sick with fever and dysentery. And Paul visited him and prayed
Acts28:9 And when this had taken place
Acts28:10 They also honored us greatly
Acts28:11 After three months we set sail in a ship that had wintered in the island
Acts28:12 Putting in at Syracuse
Acts28:13 And from there we made a circuit and arrived at Rhegium. And after one day a south wind sprang up
Acts28:14 There we found brothers and were invited to stay with them for seven days. And so we came to Rome.
Acts28:15 And the brothers there
Acts28:16 And when we came into Rome
Acts28:17 After three days he called together the local leaders of the Jews
Acts28:18 When they had examined me
Acts28:19 But because the Jews objected
Acts28:20 For this reason
Acts28:21 And they said to him
Acts28:22 But we desire to hear from you what your views are
Acts28:23 When they had appointed a day for him
Acts28:24 And some were convinced by what he said
Acts28:25 And disagreeing among themselves
Acts28:26 'Go to this people
Acts28:27 For this people's heart has grown dull
Acts28:28 Therefore let it be known to you that this salvation of God has been sent to the Gentiles; they will listen.
Acts28:29 ***
Acts28:30 He lived there two whole years at his own expense
Acts28:31 proclaiming the kingdom of God and teaching about the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness and without hindrance.
Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 which he promised beforehand through his prophets in the holy Scriptures
Romans1:3 concerning his Son
Romans1:4 and was declared to be the Son of God in power according to the Spirit of holiness by his resurrection from the dead
Romans1:5 through whom we have received grace and apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith for the sake of his name among all the nations
Romans1:6 including you who are called to belong to Jesus Christ
Romans1:7 To all those in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 For God is my witness
Romans1:10 always in my prayers
Romans1:11 For I long to see you
Romans1:12 that is
Romans1:13 I want you to know
Romans1:14 I am under obligation both to Greeks and to barbarians
Romans1:15 So I am eager to preach the gospel to you also who are in Rome.
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel
Romans1:17 For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith for faith
Romans1:18 For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men
Romans1:19 For what can be known about God is plain to them
Romans1:20 For his invisible attributes
Romans1:21 For although they knew God
Romans1:22 Claiming to be wise
Romans1:23 and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling mortal man and birds and animals and reptiles.
Romans1:24 Therefore God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to impurity
Romans1:25 because they exchanged the truth about God for a lie and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator
Romans1:26 For this reason God gave them up to dishonorable passions. For their women exchanged natural relations for those that are contrary to nature;
Romans1:27 and the men likewise gave up natural relations with women and were consumed with passion for one another
Romans1:28 And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God
Romans1:29 They were filled with all manner of unrighteousness
Romans1:30 slanderers
Romans1:31 foolish
Romans1:32 Though they know God's decree that those who practice such things deserve to die
Romans2:1 Therefore you have no excuse
Romans2:2 We know that the judgment of God rightly falls on those who do such things.
Romans2:3 Do you suppose
Romans2:4 Or do you presume on the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience
Romans2:5 But because of your hard and impenitent heart you are storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath when God's righteous judgment will be revealed.
Romans2:6 He will render to each one according to his works:
Romans2:7 to those who by patience in well-doing seek for glory and honor and immortality
Romans2:8 but for those who are self-seeking and do not obey the truth
Romans2:9 There will be tribulation and distress for every human being who does evil
Romans2:10 but glory and honor and peace for everyone who does good
Romans2:11 For God shows no partiality.
Romans2:12 For all who have sinned without the law will also perish without the law
Romans2:13 For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous before God
Romans2:14 For when Gentiles
Romans2:15 They show that the work of the law is written on their hearts
Romans2:16 on that day when
Romans2:17 But if you call yourself a Jew and rely on the law and boast in God
Romans2:18 and know his will and approve what is excellent
Romans2:19 and if you are sure that you yourself are a guide to the blind
Romans2:20 an instructor of the foolish
Romans2:21 you then who teach others
Romans2:22 You who say that one must not commit adultery
Romans2:23 You who boast in the law dishonor God by breaking the law.
Romans2:24 For
Romans2:25 For circumcision indeed is of value if you obey the law
Romans2:26 So
Romans2:27 Then he who is physically uncircumcised but keeps the law will condemn you who have the written code and circumcision but break the law.
Romans2:28 For no one is a Jew who is merely one outwardly
Romans2:29 But a Jew is one inwardly
Romans3:1 Then what advantage has the Jew? Or what is the value of circumcision?
Romans3:2 Much in every way. To begin with
Romans3:3 What if some were unfaithful? Does their faithlessness nullify the faithfulness of God?
Romans3:4 By no means! Let God be true though every one were a liar
Romans3:5 But if our unrighteousness serves to show the righteousness of God
Romans3:6 By no means! For then how could God judge the world?
Romans3:7 But if through my lie God's truth abounds to his glory
Romans3:8 And why not do evil that good may come?- as some people slanderously charge us with saying. Their condemnation is just.
Romans3:9 What then? Are we Jews any better off? No
Romans3:10 as it is written: None is righteous
Romans3:11 no one understands; no one seeks for God.
Romans3:12 All have turned aside; together they have become worthless; no one does good
Romans3:13 Their throat is an open grave; they use their tongues to deceive. The venom of asps is under their lips.
Romans3:14 Their mouth is full of curses and bitterness.
Romans3:15 Their feet are swift to shed blood;
Romans3:16 in their paths are ruin and misery
Romans3:17 and the way of peace they have not known.
Romans3:18 There is no fear of God before their eyes.
Romans3:19 Now we know that whatever the law says it speaks to those who are under the law
Romans3:20 For by works of the law no human being will be justified in his sight
Romans3:21 But now the righteousness of God has been manifested apart from the law
Romans3:22 the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe. For there is no distinction:
Romans3:23 for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God
Romans3:24 and are justified by his grace as a gift
Romans3:25 whom God put forward as a propitiation by his blood
Romans3:26 It was to show his righteousness at the present time
Romans3:27 Then what becomes of our boasting? It is excluded. By what kind of law? By a law of works? No
Romans3:28 For we hold that one is justified by faith apart from works of the law.
Romans3:29 Or is God the God of Jews only? Is he not the God of Gentiles also? Yes
Romans3:30 since God is one. He will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith.
Romans3:31 Do we then overthrow the law by this faith? By no means! On the contrary
Romans4:1 What then shall we say was gained by Abraham
Romans4:2 For if Abraham was justified by works
Romans4:3 For what does the Scripture say? Abraham believed God
Romans4:4 Now to the one who works
Romans4:5 And to the one who does not work but trusts him who justifies the ungodly
Romans4:6 just as David also speaks of the blessing of the one to whom God counts righteousness apart from works:
Romans4:7 Blessed are those whose lawless deeds are forgiven
Romans4:8 blessed is the man against whom the Lord will not count his sin.
Romans4:9 Is this blessing then only for the circumcised
Romans4:10 How then was it counted to him? Was it before or after he had been circumcised? It was not after
Romans4:11 He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. The purpose was to make him the father of all who believe without being circumcised
Romans4:12 and to make him the father of the circumcised who are not merely circumcised but who also walk in the footsteps of the faith that our father Abraham had before he was circumcised.
Romans4:13 For the promise to Abraham and his offspring that he would be heir of the world did not come through the law but through the righteousness of faith.
Romans4:14 For if it is the adherents of the law who are to be the heirs
Romans4:15 For the law brings wrath
Romans4:16 That is why it depends on faith
Romans4:17 as it is written
Romans4:18 In hope he believed against hope
Romans4:19 He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body
Romans4:20 No distrust made him waver concerning the promise of God
Romans4:21 fully convinced that God was able to do what he had promised.
Romans4:22 That is why his faith was counted to him as righteousness.
Romans4:23 But the words it was counted to him were not written for his sake alone
Romans4:24 but for ours also. It will be counted to us who believe in him who raised from the dead Jesus our Lord
Romans4:25 who was delivered up for our trespasses and raised for our justification.
Romans5:1 Therefore
Romans5:2 Through him we have also obtained access by faith into this grace in which we stand
Romans5:3 More than that
Romans5:4 and endurance produces character
Romans5:5 and hope does not put us to shame
Romans5:6 For while we were still weak
Romans5:7 For one will scarcely die for a righteous person- though perhaps for a good person one would dare even to die-
Romans5:8 but God shows his love for us in that while we were still sinners
Romans5:9 Since
Romans5:10 For if while we were enemies we were reconciled to God by the death of his Son
Romans5:11 More than that
Romans5:12 Therefore
Romans5:13 for sin indeed was in the world before the law was given
Romans5:14 Yet death reigned from Adam to Moses
Romans5:15 But the free gift is not like the trespass. For if many died through one man's trespass
Romans5:16 And the free gift is not like the result of that one man's sin. For the judgment following one trespass brought condemnation
Romans5:17 If
Romans5:18 Therefore
Romans5:19 For as by the one man's disobedience the many were made sinners
Romans5:20 Now the law came in to increase the trespass
Romans5:21 so that
Romans6:1 What shall we say then? Are we to continue in sin that grace may abound?
Romans6:2 By no means! How can we who died to sin still live in it?
Romans6:3 Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
Romans6:4 We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death
Romans6:5 For if we have been united with him in a death like his
Romans6:6 We know that our old self was crucified with him in order that the body of sin might be brought to nothing
Romans6:7 For one who has died has been set free from sin.
Romans6:8 Now if we have died with Christ
Romans6:9 We know that Christ being raised from the dead will never die again; death no longer has dominion over him.
Romans6:10 For the death he died he died to sin
Romans6:11 So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God in Christ Jesus.
Romans6:12 Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal bodies
Romans6:13 Do not present your members to sin as instruments for unrighteousness
Romans6:14 For sin will have no dominion over you
Romans6:15 What then? Are we to sin because we are not under law but under grace? By no means!
Romans6:16 Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves
Romans6:17 But thanks be to God
Romans6:18 and

Romans6:22 But now that you have been set free from sin and have become slaves of God
Romans6:23 For the wages of sin is death

1Corinthians7:12 To the rest I say (I
1Corinthians7:13 If any woman has a husband who is an unbeliever
1Corinthians7:14 For the unbelieving husband is made holy because of his wife
1Corinthians7:15 But if the unbelieving partner separates
1Corinthians7:16 Wife
1Corinthians7:17 Only let each person lead the life that the Lord has assigned to him
1Corinthians7:18 Was anyone at the time of his call already circumcised? Let him not seek to remove the marks of circumcision. Was anyone at the time of his call uncircumcised? Let him not seek circumcision.
1Corinthians7:19 For neither circumcision counts for anything nor uncircumcision
1Corinthians7:20 Each one should remain in the condition in which he was called.
1Corinthians7:21 Were you a slave when called? Do not be concerned about it. But if you can gain your freedom
1Corinthians7:22 For he who was called in the Lord as a slave is a freedman of the Lord. Likewise he who was free when called is a slave of Christ.
1Corinthians7:23 You were bought with a price; do not become slaves of men.
1Corinthians7:24 So
1Corinthians7:25 Now concerning the betrothed
1Corinthians7:26 I think that in view of the present distress it is good for a person to remain as he is.
1Corinthians7:27 Are you bound to a wife? Do not seek to be free. Are you free from a wife? Do not seek a wife.
1Corinthians7:28 But if you do marry
1Corinthians7:29 This is what I mean
1Corinthians7:30 and those who mourn as though they were not mourning
1Corinthians7:31 and those who deal with the world as though they had no dealings with it. For the present form of this world is passing away.
1Corinthians7:32 I want you to be free from anxieties. The unmarried man is anxious about the things of the Lord
1Corinthians7:33 But the married man is anxious about worldly things
1Corinthians7:34 and his interests are divided. And the unmarried or betrothed woman is anxious about the things of the Lord
1Corinthians7:35 I say this for your own benefit
1Corinthians7:36 If anyone thinks that he is not behaving properly toward his betrothed
1Corinthians7:37 But whoever is firmly established in his heart
1Corinthians7:38 So then he who marries his betrothed does well
1Corinthians7:39 A wife is bound to her husband as long as he lives. But if her husband dies
1Corinthians7:40 Yet in my judgment she is happier if she remains as she is. And I think that I too have the Spirit of God.
1Corinthians8:1 Now concerning food offered to idols: we know that all of us possess knowledge. This knowledge puffs up
1Corinthians8:2 If anyone imagines that he knows something
1Corinthians8:3 But if anyone loves God
1Corinthians8:4 Therefore
1Corinthians8:5 For although there may be so-called gods in heaven or on earth- as indeed there are many gods and many lords-
1Corinthians8:6 yet for us there is one God
1Corinthians8:7 However
1Corinthians8:8 Food will not commend us to God. We are no worse off if we do not eat
1Corinthians8:9 But take care that this right of yours does not somehow become a stumbling block to the weak.
1Corinthians8:10 For if anyone sees you who have knowledge eating in an idol's temple
1Corinthians8:11 And so by your knowledge this weak person is destroyed
1Corinthians8:12 Thus
1Corinthians8:13 Therefore
1Corinthians9:1 Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are not you my workmanship in the Lord?
1Corinthians9:2 If to others I am not an apostle
1Corinthians9:3 This is my defense to those who would examine me.
1Corinthians9:4 Do we not have the right to eat and drink?
1Corinthians9:5 Do we not have the right to take along a believing wife
1Corinthians9:6 Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working for a living?
1Corinthians9:7 Who serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard without eating any of its fruit? Or who tends a flock without getting some of the milk?
1Corinthians9:8 Do I say these things on human authority? Does not the Law say the same?
1Corinthians9:9 For it is written in the Law of Moses
1Corinthians9:10 Does he not speak entirely for our sake? It was written for our sake
1Corinthians9:11 If we have sown spiritual things among you
1Corinthians9:12 If others share this rightful claim on you
1Corinthians9:13 Do you not know that those who are employed in the temple service get their food from the temple
1Corinthians9:14 In the same way
1Corinthians9:15 But I have made no use of any of these rights
1Corinthians9:16 For if I preach the gospel
1Corinthians9:17 For if I do this of my own will
1Corinthians9:18 What then is my reward? That in my preaching I may present the gospel free of charge
1Corinthians9:19 For though I am free from all
1Corinthians9:20 To the Jews I became as a Jew
1Corinthians9:21 To those outside the law I became as one outside the law (not being outside the law of God but under the law of Christ) that I might win those outside the law.
1Corinthians9:22 To the weak I became weak
1Corinthians9:23 I do it all for the sake of the gospel
1Corinthians9:24 Do you not know that in a race all the runners compete
1Corinthians9:25 Every athlete exercises self-control in all things. They do it to receive a perishable wreath
1Corinthians9:26 So I do not run aimlessly; I do not box as one beating the air.
1Corinthians9:27 But I discipline my body and keep it under control

1Corinthians12:1 Now concerning spiritual gifts
1Corinthians12:2 You know that when you were pagans you were led astray to mute idols
1Corinthians12:3 Therefore I want you to understand that no one speaking in the Spirit of God ever says Jesus is accursed! and no one can say Jesus is Lord except in the Holy Spirit.
1Corinthians12:4 Now there are varieties of gifts
1Corinthians12:5 and there are varieties of service
1Corinthians12:6 and there are varieties of activities
1Corinthians12:7 To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
1Corinthians12:8 To one is given through the Spirit the utterance of wisdom
1Corinthians12:9 to another faith by the same Spirit
1Corinthians12:10 to another the working of miracles
1Corinthians12:11 All these are empowered by one and the same Spirit
1Corinthians12:12 For just as the body is one and has many members
1Corinthians12:13 For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body- Jews or Greeks
1Corinthians12:14 For the body does not consist of one member but of many.

1Corinthians12:16 And if the ear should say
1Corinthians12:17 If the whole body were an eye
1Corinthians12:18 But as it is
1Corinthians12:19 If all were a single member
1Corinthians12:20 As it is
1Corinthians12:21 The eye cannot say to the hand
1Corinthians12:22 On the contrary
1Corinthians12:23 and on those parts of the body that we think less honorable we bestow the greater honor
1Corinthians12:24 which our more presentable parts do not require. But God has so composed the body
1Corinthians12:25 that there may be no division in the body
1Corinthians12:26 If one member suffers
1Corinthians12:27 Now you are the body of Christ and individually members of it.

1Corinthians12:29 Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers? Do all work miracles?
1Corinthians12:30 Do all possess gifts of healing? Do all speak with tongues? Do all interpret?
1Corinthians12:31 But earnestly desire the higher gifts. And I will show you a still more excellent way.

1Corinthians15:37 And what you sow is not the body that is to be
1Corinthians15:38 But God gives it a body as he has chosen
1Corinthians15:39 For not all flesh is the same
1Corinthians15:40 There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies
1Corinthians15:41 There is one glory of the sun
1Corinthians15:42 So is it with the resurrection of the dead. What is sown is perishable; what is raised is imperishable.
1Corinthians15:43 It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.

1Corinthians15:45 Thus it is written
1Corinthians15:46 But it is not the spiritual that is first but the natural

2Corinthians7:10 For godly grief produces a repentance that leads to salvation without regret
2Corinthians7:11 For see what earnestness this godly grief has produced in you

Galatians3:1 O foolish Galatians! Who has bewitched you? It was before your eyes that Jesus Christ was publicly portrayed as crucified.

Galatians3:4 Did you suffer so many things in vain- if indeed it was in vain?

Galatians3:8 And the Scripture
Galatians3:9 So then
Galatians3:10 For all who rely on works of the law are under a curse; for it is written
Galatians3:11 Now it is evident that no one is justified before God by the law
Galatians3:12 But the law is not of faith

Galatians3:14 so that in Christ Jesus the blessing of Abraham might come to the Gentiles
Galatians3:15 To give a human example

Galatians3:17 This is what I mean: the law

Galatians3:24 So then

Ephesians5:2 And walk in love
Ephesians5:3 But sexual immorality and all impurity or covetousness must not even be named among you

Ephesians5:14 for anything that becomes visible is light. Therefore it says

Ephesians5:25 Husbands
Ephesians5:26 that he might sanctify her
Ephesians5:27 so that he might present the church to himself in splendor
Ephesians5:28 In the same way husbands should love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself.
Ephesians5:29 For no one ever hated his own flesh
Ephesians5:30 because we are members of his body.
Ephesians5:31 Therefore a man shall leave his father and mother and hold fast to his wife
Ephesians5:32 This mystery is profound
Ephesians5:33 However
Ephesians6:1 Children
Ephesians6:2 Honor your father and mother (this is the first commandment with a promise)
Ephesians6:3 that it may go well with you and that you may live long in the land.
Ephesians6:4 Fathers
Ephesians6:5 Slaves
Ephesians6:6 not by the way of eye-service
Ephesians6:7 rendering service with a good will as to the Lord and not to man
Ephesians6:8 knowing that whatever good anyone does
Ephesians6:9 Masters
Ephesians6:10 Finally
Ephesians6:11 Put on the whole armor of God
Ephesians6:12 For we do not wrestle against flesh and blood
Ephesians6:13 Therefore take up the whole armor of God
Ephesians6:14 Stand therefore
Ephesians6:15 and
Ephesians6:16 In all circumstances take up the shield of faith
Ephesians6:17 and take the helmet of salvation
Ephesians6:18 praying at all times in the Spirit

Ephesians6:20 for which I am an ambassador in chains
Ephesians6:21 So that you also may know how I am and what I am doing

Ephesians6:23 Peace be to the brothers

Philippians1:1 Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:3 I thank my God in all my remembrance of you
Philippians1:4 always in every prayer of mine for you all making my prayer with joy
Philippians1:5 because of your partnership in the gospel from the first day until now.
Philippians1:6 And I am sure of this
Philippians1:7 It is right for me to feel this way about you all
Philippians1:8 For God is my witness
Philippians1:9 And it is my prayer that your love may abound more and more
Philippians1:10 so that you may approve what is excellent
Philippians1:11 filled with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ
Philippians1:12 I want you to know
Philippians1:13 so that it has become known throughout the whole imperial guard and to all the rest that my imprisonment is for Christ.
Philippians1:14 And most of the brothers
Philippians1:15 Some indeed preach Christ from envy and rivalry
Philippians1:16 The latter do it out of love
Philippians1:17 The former proclaim Christ out of rivalry
Philippians1:18 What then? Only that in every way
Philippians1:19 for I know that through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ this will turn out for my deliverance
Philippians1:20 as it is my eager expectation and hope that I will not be at all ashamed
Philippians1:21 For to me to live is Christ
Philippians1:22 If I am to live in the flesh
Philippians1:23 I am hard pressed between the two. My desire is to depart and be with Christ
Philippians1:24 But to remain in the flesh is more necessary on your account.
Philippians1:25 Convinced of this
Philippians1:26 so that in me you may have ample cause to glory in Christ Jesus
Philippians1:27 Only let your manner of life be worthy of the gospel of Christ
Philippians1:28 and not frightened in anything by your opponents. This is a clear sign to them of their destruction
Philippians1:29 For it has been granted to you that for the sake of Christ you should not only believe in him but also suffer for his sake
Philippians1:30 engaged in the same conflict that you saw I had and now hear that I still have.
Philippians2:1 So if there is any encouragement in Christ
Philippians2:2 complete my joy by being of the same mind
Philippians2:3 Do nothing from rivalry or conceit
Philippians2:4 Let each of you look not only to his own interests
Philippians2:5 Have this mind among yourselves
Philippians2:6 who
Philippians2:7 but made himself nothing
Philippians2:8 he humbled himself by becoming obedient to the point of death
Philippians2:9 Therefore God has highly exalted him and bestowed on him the name that is above every name
Philippians2:10 so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow
Philippians2:11 and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord
Philippians2:12 Therefore
Philippians2:13 for it is God who works in you
Philippians2:14 Do all things without grumbling or questioning
Philippians2:15 that you may be blameless and innocent
Philippians2:16 holding fast to the word of life
Philippians2:17 Even if I am to be poured out as a drink offering upon the sacrificial offering of your faith
Philippians2:18 Likewise you also should be glad and rejoice with me.
Philippians2:19 I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon
Philippians2:20 For I have no one like him
Philippians2:21 They all seek their own interests
Philippians2:22 But you know Timothy's proven worth
Philippians2:23 I hope therefore to send him just as soon as I see how it will go with me
Philippians2:24 and I trust in the Lord that shortly I myself will come also.
Philippians2:25 I have thought it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus my brother and fellow worker and fellow soldier
Philippians2:26 for he has been longing for you all and has been distressed because you heard that he was ill.
Philippians2:27 Indeed he was ill
Philippians2:28 I am the more eager to send him
Philippians2:29 So receive him in the Lord with all joy
Philippians2:30 for he nearly died for the work of Christ
Philippians3:1 Finally
Philippians3:2 Look out for the dogs
Philippians3:3 For we are the real circumcision
Philippians3:4 though I myself have reason for confidence in the flesh also. If anyone else thinks he has reason for confidence in the flesh
Philippians3:5 circumcised on the eighth day
Philippians3:6 as to zeal
Philippians3:7 But whatever gain I had
Philippians3:8 Indeed
Philippians3:9 and be found in him
Philippians3:10 that I may know him and the power of his resurrection
Philippians3:11 that by any means possible I may attain the resurrection from the dead.
Philippians3:12 Not that I have already obtained this or am already perfect
Philippians3:13 Brothers
Philippians3:14 I press on toward the goal for the prize of the upward call of God in Christ Jesus.
Philippians3:15 Let those of us who are mature think this way
Philippians3:16 Only let us hold true to what we have attained.
Philippians3:17 Brothers
Philippians3:18 For many
Philippians3:19 Their end is destruction
Philippians3:20 But our citizenship is in heaven
Philippians3:21 who will transform our lowly body to be like his glorious body
Philippians4:1 Therefore
Philippians4:2 I entreat Euodia and I entreat Syntyche to agree in the Lord.
Philippians4:3 Yes
Philippians4:4 Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say
Philippians4:5 Let your reasonableness be known to everyone. The Lord is at hand;
Philippians4:6 do not be anxious about anything
Philippians4:7 And the peace of God
Philippians4:8 Finally
Philippians4:9 What you have learned and received and heard and seen in me- practice these things
Philippians4:10 I rejoiced in the Lord greatly that now at length you have revived your concern for me. You were indeed concerned for me
Philippians4:11 Not that I am speaking of being in need
Philippians4:12 I know how to be brought low
Philippians4:13 I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
Philippians4:14 Yet it was kind of you to share my trouble.
Philippians4:15 And you Philippians yourselves know that in the beginning of the gospel
Philippians4:16 Even in Thessalonica you sent me help for my needs once and again.
Philippians4:17 Not that I seek the gift
Philippians4:18 I have received full payment
Philippians4:19 And my God will supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
Philippians4:20 To our God and Father be glory forever and ever. Amen.
Philippians4:21 Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The brothers who are with me greet you.
Philippians4:22 All the saints greet you
Philippians4:23 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
Colossians1:1 Paul
Colossians1:2 To the saints and faithful brothers in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.
Colossians1:3 We always thank God
Colossians1:4 since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love that you have for all the saints
Colossians1:5 because of the hope laid up for you in heaven. Of this you have heard before in the word of the truth
Colossians1:6 which has come to you
Colossians1:7 just as you learned it from Epaphras our beloved fellow servant. He is a faithful minister of Christ on your behalf
Colossians1:8 and has made known to us your love in the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 And so
Colossians1:10 so as to walk in a manner worthy of the Lord
Colossians1:11 May you be strengthened with all power
Colossians1:12 giving thanks to the Father
Colossians1:13 He has delivered us from the domain of darkness and transferred us to the kingdom of his beloved Son
Colossians1:14 in whom we have redemption
Colossians1:15 He is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 For by him all things were created
Colossians1:17 And he is before all things
Colossians1:18 And he is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 For in him all the fullness of God was pleased to dwell
Colossians1:20 and through him to reconcile to himself all things
Colossians1:21 And you
Colossians1:22 he has now reconciled in his body of flesh by his death
Colossians1:23 if indeed you continue in the faith
Colossians1:24 Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake
Colossians1:25 of which I became a minister according to the stewardship from God that was given to me for you
Colossians1:26 the mystery hidden for ages and generations but now revealed to his saints.
Colossians1:27 To them God chose to make known how great among the Gentiles are the riches of the glory of this mystery
Colossians1:28 Him we proclaim
Colossians1:29 For this I toil
Colossians2:1 For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you and for those at Laodicea and for all who have not seen me face to face
Colossians2:2 that their hearts may be encouraged
Colossians2:3 in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
Colossians2:4 I say this in order that no one may delude you with plausible arguments.
Colossians2:5 For though I am absent in body
Colossians2:6 Therefore
Colossians2:7 rooted and built up in him and established in the faith
Colossians2:8 See to it that no one takes you captive by philosophy and empty deceit
Colossians2:9 For in him the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily
Colossians2:10 and you have been filled in him
Colossians2:11 In him also you were circumcised with a circumcision made without hands
Colossians2:12 having been buried with him in baptism
Colossians2:13 And you
Colossians2:14 by canceling the record of debt that stood against us with its legal demands. This he set aside
Colossians2:15 He disarmed the rulers and authorities and put them to open shame
Colossians2:16 Therefore let no one pass judgment on you in questions of food and drink
Colossians2:17 These are a shadow of the things to come
Colossians2:18 Let no one disqualify you
Colossians2:19 and not holding fast to the Head
Colossians2:20 If with Christ you died to the elemental spirits of the world
Colossians2:21 Do not handle
Colossians2:22 (referring to things that all perish as they are used)- according to human precepts and teachings?
Colossians2:23 These have indeed an appearance of wisdom in promoting self-made religion and asceticism and severity to the body

Colossians3:2 Set your minds on things that are above
Colossians3:3 For you have died

Colossians3:5 Put to death therefore what is earthly in you: sexual immorality
Colossians3:6 On account of these the wrath of God is coming.
Colossians3:7 In these you too once walked
Colossians3:8 But now you must put them all away: anger
Colossians3:9 Do not lie to one another
Colossians3:10 and have put on the new self
Colossians3:11 Here there is not Greek and Jew
Colossians3:12 Put on then
Colossians3:13 bearing with one another and
Colossians3:14 And above all these put on love
Colossians3:15 And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts
Colossians3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly

Colossians3:19 Husbands
Colossians3:20 Children
Colossians3:21 Fathers
Colossians3:22 Slaves
Colossians3:23 Whatever you do
Colossians3:24 knowing that from the Lord you will receive the inheritance as your reward. You are serving the Lord Christ.
Colossians3:25 For the wrongdoer will be paid back for the wrong he has done

Colossians4:4 that I may make it clear
Colossians4:5 Conduct yourselves wisely toward outsiders
Colossians4:6 Let your speech always be gracious

1Thessalonians1:1 Paul
1Thessalonians1:2 We give thanks to God always for all of you
1Thessalonians1:3 remembering before our God and Father your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Thessalonians1:4 For we know
1Thessalonians1:5 because our gospel came to you not only in word
1Thessalonians1:6 And you became imitators of us and of the Lord
1Thessalonians1:7 so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and in Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 For not only has the word of the Lord sounded forth from you in Macedonia and Achaia
1Thessalonians1:9 For they themselves report concerning us the kind of reception we had among you
1Thessalonians1:10 and to wait for his Son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 For you yourselves know
1Thessalonians2:2 But though we had already suffered and been shamefully treated at Philippi
1Thessalonians2:3 For our appeal does not spring from error or impurity or any attempt to deceive
1Thessalonians2:4 but just as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel
1Thessalonians2:5 For we never came with words of flattery
1Thessalonians2:6 Nor did we seek glory from people
1Thessalonians2:7 But we were gentle among you
1Thessalonians2:8 So
1Thessalonians2:9 For you remember
1Thessalonians2:10 You are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 For you know how
1Thessalonians2:12 we exhorted each one of you and encouraged you and charged you to walk in a manner worthy of God
1Thessalonians2:13 And we also thank God constantly for this
1Thessalonians2:14 For you
1Thessalonians2:15 who killed both the Lord Jesus and the prophets
1Thessalonians2:16 by hindering us from speaking to the Gentiles that they might be saved- so as always to fill up the measure of their sins. But God's wrath has come upon them at last!
1Thessalonians2:17 But since we were torn away from you
1Thessalonians2:18 because we wanted to come to you- I
1Thessalonians2:19 For what is our hope or joy or crown of boasting before our Lord Jesus at his coming? Is it not you?
1Thessalonians2:20 For you are our glory and joy.

1Thessalonians3:2 and we sent Timothy
1Thessalonians3:3 that no one be moved by these afflictions. For you yourselves know that we are destined for this.
1Thessalonians3:4 For when we were with you
1Thessalonians3:5 For this reason
1Thessalonians3:6 But now that Timothy has come to us from you
1Thessalonians3:7 for this reason
1Thessalonians3:8 For now we live
1Thessalonians3:9 For what thanksgiving can we return to God for you
1Thessalonians3:10 as we pray most earnestly night and day that we may see you face to face and supply what is lacking in your faith?

1Thessalonians3:12 and may the Lord make you increase and abound in love for one another and for all
1Thessalonians3:13 so that he may establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father
1Thessalonians4:1 Finally
1Thessalonians4:2 For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 For this is the will of God
1Thessalonians4:4 that each one of you know how to control his own body in holiness and honor
1Thessalonians4:5 not in the passion of lust like the Gentiles who do not know God;
1Thessalonians4:6 that no one transgress and wrong his brother in this matter
1Thessalonians4:7 For God has not called us for impurity
1Thessalonians4:8 Therefore whoever disregards this
1Thessalonians4:9 Now concerning brotherly love you have no need for anyone to write to you
1Thessalonians4:10 for that indeed is what you are doing to all the brothers throughout Macedonia. But we urge you
1Thessalonians4:11 and to aspire to live quietly
1Thessalonians4:12 so that you may live properly before outsiders and be dependent on no one.
1Thessalonians4:13 But we do not want you to be uninformed
1Thessalonians4:14 For since we believe that Jesus died and rose again
1Thessalonians4:15 For this we declare to you by a word from the Lord
1Thessalonians4:16 For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command
1Thessalonians4:17 Then we who are alive
1Thessalonians4:18 Therefore encourage one another with these words.
1Thessalonians5:1 Now concerning the times and the seasons
1Thessalonians5:2 For you yourselves are fully aware that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
1Thessalonians5:3 While people are saying
1Thessalonians5:4 But you are not in darkness
1Thessalonians5:5 For you are all children of light
1Thessalonians5:6 So then let us not sleep
1Thessalonians5:7 For those who sleep
1Thessalonians5:8 But since we belong to the day
1Thessalonians5:9 For God has not destined us for wrath
1Thessalonians5:10 who died for us so that whether we are awake or asleep we might live with him.
1Thessalonians5:11 Therefore encourage one another and build one another up
1Thessalonians5:12 We ask you
1Thessalonians5:13 and to esteem them very highly in love because of their work. Be at peace among yourselves.
1Thessalonians5:14 And we urge you
1Thessalonians5:15 See that no one repays anyone evil for evil
1Thessalonians5:16 Rejoice always
1Thessalonians5:17 pray without ceasing
1Thessalonians5:18 give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
1Thessalonians5:19 Do not quench the Spirit.
1Thessalonians5:20 Do not despise prophecies
1Thessalonians5:21 but test everything; hold fast what is good.
1Thessalonians5:22 Abstain from every form of evil.
1Thessalonians5:23 Now may the God of peace himself sanctify you completely
