2Thessalonians1:1 Paul
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:3 We ought always to give thanks to God for you
2Thessalonians1:4 Therefore we ourselves boast about you in the churches of God for your steadfastness and faith in all your persecutions and in the afflictions that you are enduring.
2Thessalonians1:5 This is evidence of the righteous judgment of God
2Thessalonians1:6 since indeed God considers it just to repay with affliction those who afflict you
2Thessalonians1:7 and to grant relief to you who are afflicted as well as to us
2Thessalonians1:8 in flaming fire
2Thessalonians1:9 They will suffer the punishment of eternal destruction
2Thessalonians1:10 when he comes on that day to be glorified in his saints
2Thessalonians1:11 To this end we always pray for you
2Thessalonians1:12 so that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you
2Thessalonians2:1 Now concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to him
2Thessalonians2:2 not to be quickly shaken in mind or alarmed
2Thessalonians2:3 Let no one deceive you in any way. For that day will not come
2Thessalonians2:4 who opposes and exalts himself against every so-called god or object of worship
2Thessalonians2:5 Do you not remember that when I was still with you I told you these things?
2Thessalonians2:6 And you know what is restraining him now so that he may be revealed in his time.
2Thessalonians2:7 For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work. Only he who now restrains it will do so until he is out of the way.
2Thessalonians2:8 And then the lawless one will be revealed
2Thessalonians2:9 The coming of the lawless one is by the activity of Satan with all power and false signs and wonders
2Thessalonians2:10 and with all wicked deception for those who are perishing
2Thessalonians2:11 Therefore God sends them a strong delusion
2Thessalonians2:12 in order that all may be condemned who did not believe the truth but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
2Thessalonians2:13 But we ought always to give thanks to God for you
2Thessalonians2:14 To this he called you through our gospel
2Thessalonians2:15 So then
2Thessalonians2:16 Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself
2Thessalonians2:17 comfort your hearts and establish them in every good work and word.
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 and that we may be delivered from wicked and evil men. For not all have faith.
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful. He will establish you and guard you against the evil one.
2Thessalonians3:4 And we have confidence in the Lord about you
2Thessalonians3:5 May the Lord direct your hearts to the love of God and to the steadfastness of Christ.
2Thessalonians3:6 Now we command you
2Thessalonians3:7 For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us
2Thessalonians3:8 nor did we eat anyone's bread without paying for it
2Thessalonians3:9 It was not because we do not have that right
2Thessalonians3:10 For even when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 For we hear that some among you walk in idleness
2Thessalonians3:12 Now such persons we command and encourage in the Lord Jesus Christ to do their work quietly and to earn their own living.
2Thessalonians3:13 As for you
2Thessalonians3:14 If anyone does not obey what we say in this letter
2Thessalonians3:15 Do not regard him as an enemy
2Thessalonians3:16 Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in every way. The Lord be with you all.
2Thessalonians3:17 I
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
1Timothy1:1 Paul
1Timothy1:2 To Timothy
1Timothy1:3 As I urged you when I was going to Macedonia
1Timothy1:4 nor to devote themselves to myths and endless genealogies
1Timothy1:5 The aim of our charge is love that issues from a pure heart and a good conscience and a sincere faith.
1Timothy1:6 Certain persons
1Timothy1:7 desiring to be teachers of the law
1Timothy1:8 Now we know that the law is good
1Timothy1:9 understanding this
1Timothy1:10 the sexually immoral
1Timothy1:11 in accordance with the glorious gospel of the blessed God with which I have been entrusted.
1Timothy1:12 I thank him who has given me strength
1Timothy1:13 though formerly I was a blasphemer
1Timothy1:14 and the grace of our Lord overflowed for me with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy1:15 The saying is trustworthy and deserving of full acceptance
1Timothy1:16 But I received mercy for this reason
1Timothy1:17 To the King of ages
1Timothy1:18 This charge I entrust to you
1Timothy1:19 holding faith and a good conscience. By rejecting this
1Timothy1:20 among whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander
1Timothy2:1 First of all
1Timothy2:2 for kings and all who are in high positions
1Timothy2:3 This is good
1Timothy2:4 who desires all people to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God
1Timothy2:6 who gave himself as a ransom for all
1Timothy2:7 For this I was appointed a preacher and an apostle (I am telling the truth
1Timothy2:8 I desire then that in every place the men should pray
1Timothy2:9 likewise also that women should adorn themselves in respectable apparel
1Timothy2:10 but with what is proper for women who profess godliness- with good works.
1Timothy2:11 Let a woman learn quietly with all submissiveness.
1Timothy2:12 I do not permit a woman to teach or to exercise authority over a man; rather
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was formed first
1Timothy2:14 and Adam was not deceived
1Timothy2:15 Yet she will be saved through childbearing- if they continue in faith and love and holiness
1Timothy3:1 The saying is trustworthy: If anyone aspires to the office of overseer
1Timothy3:2 Therefore an overseer must be above reproach
1Timothy3:3 not a drunkard
1Timothy3:4 He must manage his own household well
1Timothy3:5 for if someone does not know how to manage his own household
1Timothy3:6 He must not be a recent convert
1Timothy3:7 Moreover
1Timothy3:8 Deacons likewise must be dignified
1Timothy3:9 They must hold the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
1Timothy3:10 And let them also be tested first; then let them serve as deacons if they prove themselves blameless.
1Timothy3:11 Their wives likewise must be dignified
1Timothy3:12 Let deacons each be the husband of one wife
1Timothy3:13 For those who serve well as deacons gain a good standing for themselves and also great confidence in the faith that is in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy3:14 I hope to come to you soon
1Timothy3:15 if I delay
1Timothy3:16 Great indeed
1Timothy4:1 Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will depart from the faith by devoting themselves to deceitful spirits and teachings of demons
1Timothy4:2 through the insincerity of liars whose consciences are seared
1Timothy4:3 who forbid marriage and require abstinence from foods that God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
1Timothy4:4 For everything created by God is good
1Timothy4:5 for it is made holy by the word of God and prayer.
1Timothy4:6 If you put these things before the brothers
1Timothy4:7 Have nothing to do with irreverent
1Timothy4:8 for while bodily training is of some value
1Timothy4:9 The saying is trustworthy and deserving of full acceptance.
1Timothy4:10 For to this end we toil and strive
1Timothy4:11 Command and teach these things.
1Timothy4:12 Let no one despise you for your youth
1Timothy4:13 Until I come
1Timothy4:14 Do not neglect the gift you have
1Timothy4:15 Practice these things
1Timothy4:16 Keep a close watch on yourself and on the teaching. Persist in this
1Timothy5:1 Do not rebuke an older man but encourage him as you would a father. Treat younger men like brothers
1Timothy5:2 older women like mothers
1Timothy5:3 Honor widows who are truly widows.
1Timothy5:4 But if a widow has children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 She who is truly a widow
1Timothy5:6 but she who is self-indulgent is dead even while she lives.
1Timothy5:7 Command these things as well
1Timothy5:8 But if anyone does not provide for his relatives
1Timothy5:9 Let a widow be enrolled if she is not less than sixty years of age
1Timothy5:10 and having a reputation for good works: if she has brought up children
1Timothy5:11 But refuse to enroll younger widows
1Timothy5:12 and so incur condemnation for having abandoned their former faith.
1Timothy5:13 Besides that
1Timothy5:14 So I would have younger widows marry
1Timothy5:15 For some have already strayed after Satan.
1Timothy5:16 If any believing woman has relatives who are widows
1Timothy5:17 Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor
1Timothy5:18 For the Scripture says
1Timothy5:19 Do not admit a charge against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
1Timothy5:20 As for those who persist in sin
1Timothy5:21 In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels I charge you to keep these rules without prejudging
1Timothy5:22 Do not be hasty in the laying on of hands
1Timothy5:23 (No longer drink only water
1Timothy5:24 The sins of some men are conspicuous
1Timothy5:25 So also good works are conspicuous
1Timothy6:1 Let all who are under a yoke as slaves regard their own masters as worthy of all honor
1Timothy6:2 Those who have believing masters must not be disrespectful on the ground that they are brothers; rather they must serve all the better since those who benefit by their good service are believers and beloved. Teach and urge these things.
1Timothy6:3 If anyone teaches a different doctrine and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and the teaching that accords with godliness
1Timothy6:4 he is puffed up with conceit and understands nothing. He has an unhealthy craving for controversy and for quarrels about words
1Timothy6:5 and constant friction among people who are depraved in mind and deprived of the truth
1Timothy6:6 Now there is great gain in godliness with contentment
1Timothy6:7 for we brought nothing into the world
1Timothy6:8 But if we have food and clothing
1Timothy6:9 But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evils. It is through this craving that some have wandered away from the faith and pierced themselves with many pangs.
1Timothy6:11 But as for you
1Timothy6:12 Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called and about which you made the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
1Timothy6:13 I charge you in the presence of God
1Timothy6:14 to keep the commandment unstained and free from reproach until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ
1Timothy6:15 which he will display at the proper time- he who is the blessed and only Sovereign
1Timothy6:16 who alone has immortality
1Timothy6:17 As for the rich in this present age
1Timothy6:18 They are to do good
1Timothy6:19 thus storing up treasure for themselves as a good foundation for the future
1Timothy6:20 O Timothy
1Timothy6:21 for by professing it some have swerved from the faith. Grace be with you.
2Timothy1:1 Paul
2Timothy1:2 To Timothy
2Timothy1:3 I thank God whom I serve
2Timothy1:4 As I remember your tears
2Timothy1:5 I am reminded of your sincere faith
2Timothy1:6 For this reason I remind you to fan into flame the gift of God
2Timothy1:7 for God gave us a spirit not of fear but of power and love and self-control.
2Timothy1:8 Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony about our Lord
2Timothy1:9 who saved us and called us to a holy calling
2Timothy1:10 and which now has been manifested through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus
2Timothy1:11 for which I was appointed a preacher and apostle and teacher
2Timothy1:12 which is why I suffer as I do. But I am not ashamed
2Timothy1:13 Follow the pattern of the sound words that you have heard from me
2Timothy1:14 By the Holy Spirit who dwells within us
2Timothy1:15 You are aware that all who are in Asia turned away from me
2Timothy1:16 May the Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus
2Timothy1:17 but when he arrived in Rome he searched for me earnestly and found me-
2Timothy1:18 may the Lord grant him to find mercy from the Lord on that Day!- and you well know all the service he rendered at Ephesus.
2Timothy2:1 You then
2Timothy2:2 and what you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses entrust to faithful men who will be able to teach others also.
2Timothy2:3 Share in suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
2Timothy2:4 No soldier gets entangled in civilian pursuits
2Timothy2:5 An athlete is not crowned unless he competes according to the rules.
2Timothy2:6 It is the hard-working farmer who ought to have the first share of the crops.
2Timothy2:7 Think over what I say
2Timothy2:8 Remember Jesus Christ
2Timothy2:9 for which I am suffering
2Timothy2:10 Therefore I endure everything for the sake of the elect
2Timothy2:11 The saying is trustworthy
2Timothy2:12 if we endure
2Timothy2:13 if we are faithless
2Timothy2:14 Remind them of these things
2Timothy2:15 Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved
2Timothy2:16 But avoid irreverent babble
2Timothy2:17 and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus
2Timothy2:18 who have swerved from the truth
2Timothy2:19 But God's firm foundation stands
2Timothy2:20 Now in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver but also of wood and clay
2Timothy2:21 Therefore
2Timothy2:22 So flee youthful passions and pursue righteousness
2Timothy2:23 Have nothing to do with foolish
2Timothy2:24 And the Lord's servant must not be quarrelsome but kind to everyone
2Timothy2:25 correcting his opponents with gentleness. God may perhaps grant them repentance leading to a knowledge of the truth
2Timothy2:26 and they may escape from the snare of the devil
2Timothy3:1 But understand this
2Timothy3:2 For people will be lovers of self
2Timothy3:3 heartless
2Timothy3:4 treacherous
2Timothy3:5 having the appearance of godliness
2Timothy3:6 For among them are those who creep into households and capture weak women
2Timothy3:7 always learning and never able to arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
2Timothy3:8 Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses
2Timothy3:9 But they will not get very far
2Timothy3:10 You
2Timothy3:11 my persecutions and sufferings that happened to me at Antioch
2Timothy3:12 Indeed
2Timothy3:13 while evil people and impostors will go on from bad to worse
2Timothy3:14 But as for you
2Timothy3:15 and how from childhood you have been acquainted with the sacred writings
2Timothy3:16 All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching
2Timothy3:17 that the man of God may be competent
2Timothy4:1 I charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus
2Timothy4:2 preach the word; be ready in season and out of season; reprove
2Timothy4:3 For the time is coming when people will not endure sound teaching
2Timothy4:4 and will turn away from listening to the truth and wander off into myths.
2Timothy4:5 As for you
2Timothy4:6 For I am already being poured out as a drink offering
2Timothy4:7 I have fought the good fight
2Timothy4:8 Henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness
2Timothy4:9 Do your best to come to me soon.
2Timothy4:10 For Demas
2Timothy4:11 Luke alone is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you
2Timothy4:12 Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
2Timothy4:13 When you come
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the coppersmith did me great harm; the Lord will repay him according to his deeds.
2Timothy4:15 Beware of him yourself
2Timothy4:16 At my first defense no one came to stand by me
2Timothy4:17 But the Lord stood by me and strengthened me
2Timothy4:18 The Lord will rescue me from every evil deed and bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
2Timothy4:19 Greet Prisca and Aquila
2Timothy4:20 Erastus remained at Corinth
2Timothy4:21 Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus sends greetings to you
2Timothy4:22 The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.
Titus1:1 Paul
Titus1:2 in hope of eternal life
Titus1:3 and at the proper time manifested in his word through the preaching with which I have been entrusted by the command of God our Savior;
Titus1:4 To Titus
Titus1:5 This is why I left you in Crete
Titus1:6 if anyone is above reproach
Titus1:7 For an overseer
Titus1:8 but hospitable
Titus1:9 He must hold firm to the trustworthy word as taught
Titus1:10 For there are many who are insubordinate
Titus1:11 They must be silenced
Titus1:12 One of the Cretans
Titus1:13 This testimony is true. Therefore rebuke them sharply
Titus1:14 not devoting themselves to Jewish myths and the commands of people who turn away from the truth.
Titus1:15 To the pure
Titus1:16 They profess to know God
Titus2:1 But as for you
Titus2:2 Older men are to be sober-minded
Titus2:3 Older women likewise are to be reverent in behavior
Titus2:4 and so train the young women to love their husbands and children
Titus2:5 to be self-controlled
Titus2:6 Likewise
Titus2:7 Show yourself in all respects to be a model of good works
Titus2:8 and sound speech that cannot be condemned
Titus2:9 Slaves are to be submissive to their own masters in everything; they are to be well-pleasing
Titus2:10 not pilfering
Titus2:11 For the grace of God has appeared
Titus2:12 training us to renounce ungodliness and worldly passions
Titus2:13 waiting for our blessed hope
Titus2:14 who gave himself for us to redeem us from all lawlessness and to purify for himself a people for his own possession who are zealous for good works.
Titus2:15 Declare these things; exhort and rebuke with all authority. Let no one disregard you.
Titus3:1 Remind them to be submissive to rulers and authorities
Titus3:2 to speak evil of no one
Titus3:3 For we ourselves were once foolish
Titus3:4 But when the goodness and loving kindness of God our Savior appeared
Titus3:5 he saved us
Titus3:6 whom he poured out on us richly through Jesus Christ our Savior
Titus3:7 so that being justified by his grace we might become heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
Titus3:8 The saying is trustworthy
Titus3:9 But avoid foolish controversies
Titus3:10 As for a person who stirs up division
Titus3:11 knowing that such a person is warped and sinful; he is self-condemned.
Titus3:12 When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you
Titus3:13 Do your best to speed Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way; see that they lack nothing.
Titus3:14 And let our people learn to devote themselves to good works
Titus3:15 All who are with me send greetings to you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with you all.
Philemon1:1 Paul
Philemon1:2 and Apphia our sister and Archippus our fellow soldier
Philemon1:3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philemon1:4 I thank my God always when I remember you in my prayers
Philemon1:5 because I hear of your love and of the faith that you have toward the Lord Jesus and all the saints
Philemon1:6 and I pray that the sharing of your faith may become effective for the full knowledge of every good thing that is in us for the sake of Christ.
Philemon1:7 For I have derived much joy and comfort from your love
Philemon1:8 Accordingly
Philemon1:9 yet for love's sake I prefer to appeal to you- I
Philemon1:10 I appeal to you for my child
Philemon1:11 (Formerly he was useless to you
Philemon1:12 I am sending him back to you
Philemon1:13 I would have been glad to keep him with me
Philemon1:14 but I preferred to do nothing without your consent in order that your goodness might not be by compulsion but of your own free will.
Philemon1:15 For this perhaps is why he was parted from you for a while
Philemon1:16 no longer as a slave but more than a slave
Philemon1:17 So if you consider me your partner
Philemon1:18 If he has wronged you at all
Philemon1:19 I
Philemon1:20 Yes
Philemon1:21 Confident of your obedience
Philemon1:22 At the same time
Philemon1:23 Epaphras
Philemon1:24 and so do Mark
Philemon1:25 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
Hebrews1:1 Long ago
Hebrews1:2 but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son
Hebrews1:3 He is the radiance of the glory of God and the exact imprint of his nature
Hebrews1:4 having become as much superior to angels as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs.
Hebrews1:5 For to which of the angels did God ever say
Hebrews1:6 And again
Hebrews1:7 Of the angels he says
Hebrews1:8 But of the Son he says
Hebrews1:9 You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God
Hebrews1:10 And
Hebrews1:11 they will perish
Hebrews1:12 like a robe you will roll them up
Hebrews1:13 And to which of the angels has he ever said
Hebrews1:14 Are they not all ministering spirits sent out to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?
Hebrews2:1 Therefore we must pay much closer attention to what we have heard
Hebrews2:2 For since the message declared by angels proved to be reliable and every transgression or disobedience received a just retribution
Hebrews2:3 how shall we escape if we neglect such a great salvation? It was declared at first by the Lord
Hebrews2:4 while God also bore witness by signs and wonders and various miracles and by gifts of the Holy Spirit distributed according to his will.
Hebrews2:5 Now it was not to angels that God subjected the world to come
Hebrews2:6 It has been testified somewhere
Hebrews2:7 You made him for a little while lower than the angels; you have crowned him with glory and honor
Hebrews2:8 putting everything in subjection under his feet. Now in putting everything in subjection to him
Hebrews2:9 But we see him who for a little while was made lower than the angels
Hebrews2:10 For it was fitting that he
Hebrews2:11 For he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified all have one origin. That is why he is not ashamed to call them brothers
Hebrews2:12 saying
Hebrews2:13 And again
Hebrews2:14 Since therefore the children share in flesh and blood
Hebrews2:15 and deliver all those who through fear of death were subject to lifelong slavery.
Hebrews2:16 For surely it is not angels that he helps
Hebrews2:17 Therefore he had to be made like his brothers in every respect
Hebrews2:18 For because he himself has suffered when tempted
Hebrews3:1 Therefore
Hebrews3:2 who was faithful to him who appointed him
Hebrews3:3 For Jesus has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses- as much more glory as the builder of a house has more honor than the house itself.
Hebrews3:4 (For every house is built by someone
Hebrews3:5 Now Moses was faithful in all God's house as a servant
Hebrews3:6 but Christ is faithful over God's house as a son. And we are his house if indeed we hold fast our confidence and our boasting in our hope.
Hebrews3:7 Therefore
Hebrews3:8 do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion
Hebrews3:9 where your fathers put me to the test and saw my works
Hebrews3:10 for forty years. Therefore I was provoked with that generation
Hebrews3:11 As I swore in my wrath
Hebrews3:12 Take care
Hebrews3:13 But exhort one another every day
Hebrews3:14 For we share in Christ
Hebrews3:15 As it is said
Hebrews3:16 For who were those who heard and yet rebelled? Was it not all those who left Egypt led by Moses?
Hebrews3:17 And with whom was he provoked for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned
Hebrews3:18 And to whom did he swear that they would not enter his rest
Hebrews3:19 So we see that they were unable to enter because of unbelief.
Hebrews4:1 Therefore
Hebrews4:2 For good news came to us just as to them
Hebrews4:3 For we who have believed enter that rest
Hebrews4:4 For he has somewhere spoken of the seventh day in this way: And God rested on the seventh day from all his works.
Hebrews4:5 And again in this passage he said
Hebrews4:6 Since therefore it remains for some to enter it
Hebrews4:7 again he appoints a certain day
Hebrews4:8 For if Joshua had given them rest
Hebrews4:9 So then
Hebrews4:10 for whoever has entered God's rest has also rested from his works as God did from his.
Hebrews4:11 Let us therefore strive to enter that rest
Hebrews4:12 For the word of God is living and active
Hebrews4:13 And no creature is hidden from his sight
Hebrews4:14 Since then we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens
Hebrews4:15 For we do not have a high priest who is unable to sympathize with our weaknesses
Hebrews4:16 Let us then with confidence draw near to the throne of grace
Hebrews5:1 For every high priest chosen from among men is appointed to act on behalf of men in relation to God
Hebrews5:2 He can deal gently with the ignorant and wayward
Hebrews5:3 Because of this he is obligated to offer sacrifice for his own sins just as he does for those of the people.
Hebrews5:4 And no one takes this honor for himself
Hebrews5:5 So also Christ did not exalt himself to be made a high priest
Hebrews5:6 as he says also in another place
Hebrews5:7 In the days of his flesh
Hebrews5:8 Although he was a son
Hebrews5:9 And being made perfect
Hebrews5:10 being designated by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek.
Hebrews5:11 About this we have much to say
Hebrews5:12 For though by this time you ought to be teachers
Hebrews5:13 for everyone who lives on milk is unskilled in the word of righteousness
Hebrews5:14 But solid food is for the mature
Hebrews6:1 Therefore let us leave the elementary doctrine of Christ and go on to maturity
Hebrews6:2 and of instruction about washings
Hebrews6:3 And this we will do if God permits.
Hebrews6:4 For it is impossible to restore again to repentance those who have once been enlightened
Hebrews6:5 and have tasted the goodness of the word of God and the powers of the age to come
Hebrews6:6 if they then fall away
Hebrews6:7 For land that has drunk the rain that often falls on it
Hebrews6:8 But if it bears thorns and thistles
Hebrews6:9 Though we speak in this way
Hebrews6:10 For God is not so unjust as to overlook your work and the love that you showed for his sake in serving the saints
Hebrews6:11 And we desire each one of you to show the same earnestness to have the full assurance of hope until the end
Hebrews6:12 so that you may not be sluggish
Hebrews6:13 For when God made a promise to Abraham
Hebrews6:14 saying
Hebrews6:15 And thus Abraham
Hebrews6:16 For people swear by something greater than themselves
Hebrews6:17 So when God desired to show more convincingly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeable character of his purpose
Hebrews6:18 so that by two unchangeable things
Hebrews6:19 We have this as a sure and steadfast anchor of the soul
Hebrews6:20 where Jesus has gone as a forerunner on our behalf
Hebrews7:1 For this Melchizedek
Hebrews7:2 and to him Abraham apportioned a tenth part of everything. He is first
Hebrews7:3 He is without father or mother or genealogy
Hebrews7:4 See how great this man was to whom Abraham the patriarch gave a tenth of the spoils!
Hebrews7:5 And those descendants of Levi who receive the priestly office have a commandment in the law to take tithes from the people
Hebrews7:6 But this man who does not have his descent from them received tithes from Abraham and blessed him who had the promises.
Hebrews7:7 It is beyond dispute that the inferior is blessed by the superior.
Hebrews7:8 In the one case tithes are received by mortal men
Hebrews7:9 One might even say that Levi himself
Hebrews7:10 for he was still in the loins of his ancestor when Melchizedek met him.
Hebrews7:11 Now if perfection had been attainable through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people received the law)
Hebrews7:12 For when there is a change in the priesthood
Hebrews7:13 For the one of whom these things are spoken belonged to another tribe
Hebrews7:14 For it is evident that our Lord was descended from Judah
Hebrews7:15 This becomes even more evident when another priest arises in the likeness of Melchizedek
Hebrews7:16 who has become a priest
Hebrews7:17 For it is witnessed of him
Hebrews7:18 On the one hand
Hebrews7:19 (for the law made nothing perfect); but on the other hand
Hebrews7:20 And it was not without an oath. For those who formerly became priests were made such without an oath
Hebrews7:21 but this one was made a priest with an oath by the one who said to him: The Lord has sworn and will not change his mind
Hebrews7:22 This makes Jesus the guarantor of a better covenant.
Hebrews7:23 The former priests were many in number
Hebrews7:24 but he holds his priesthood permanently
Hebrews7:25 Consequently
Hebrews7:26 For it was indeed fitting that we should have such a high priest
Hebrews7:27 He has no need
Hebrews7:28 For the law appoints men in their weakness as high priests
Hebrews8:1 Now the point in what we are saying is this: we have such a high priest
Hebrews8:2 a minister in the holy places
Hebrews8:3 For every high priest is appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices; thus it is necessary for this priest also to have something to offer.
Hebrews8:4 Now if he were on earth
Hebrews8:5 They serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly things. For when Moses was about to erect the tent
Hebrews8:6 But as it is
Hebrews8:7 For if that first covenant had been faultless
Hebrews8:8 For he finds fault with them when he says: Behold
Hebrews8:9 not like the covenant that I made with their fathers on the day when I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt. For they did not continue in my covenant
Hebrews8:10 For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days
Hebrews8:11 And they shall not teach
Hebrews8:12 For I will be merciful toward their iniquities
Hebrews8:13 In speaking of a new covenant
Hebrews9:1 Now even the first covenant had regulations for worship and an earthly place of holiness.
Hebrews9:2 For a tent was prepared
Hebrews9:3 Behind the second curtain was a second section called the Most Holy Place
Hebrews9:4 having the golden altar of incense and the ark of the covenant covered on all sides with gold
Hebrews9:5 Above it were the cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat. Of these things we cannot now speak in detail.
Hebrews9:6 These preparations having thus been made
Hebrews9:7 but into the second only the high priest goes
Hebrews9:8 By this the Holy Spirit indicates that the way into the holy places is not yet opened as long as the first section is still standing
Hebrews9:9 (which is symbolic for the present age). According to this arrangement
Hebrews9:10 but deal only with food and drink and various washings
Hebrews9:11 But when Christ appeared as a high priest of the good things that have come
Hebrews9:12 he entered once for all into the holy places
Hebrews9:13 For if the sprinkling of defiled persons with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a heifer sanctifies for the purification of the flesh
Hebrews9:14 how much more will the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 Therefore he is the mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 For where a will is involved
Hebrews9:17 For a will takes effect only at death
Hebrews9:18 Therefore not even the first covenant was inaugurated without blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when every commandment of the law had been declared by Moses to all the people
Hebrews9:20 saying
Hebrews9:21 And in the same way he sprinkled with the blood both the tent and all the vessels used in worship.
Hebrews9:22 Indeed
Hebrews9:23 Thus it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be purified with these rites
Hebrews9:24 For Christ has entered
Hebrews9:25 Nor was it to offer himself repeatedly
Hebrews9:26 for then he would have had to suffer repeatedly since the foundation of the world. But as it is
Hebrews9:27 And just as it is appointed for man to die once
Hebrews9:28 so Christ
Hebrews10:1 For since the law has but a shadow of the good things to come instead of the true form of these realities
Hebrews10:2 Otherwise
Hebrews10:3 But in these sacrifices there is a reminder of sin every year.
Hebrews10:4 For it is impossible for the blood of bulls and goats to take away sins.
Hebrews10:5 Consequently
Hebrews10:6 in burnt offerings and sin offerings you have taken no pleasure.
Hebrews10:7 Then I said
Hebrews10:8 When he said above
Hebrews10:9 then he added
Hebrews10:10 And by that will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.
Hebrews10:11 And every priest stands daily at his service
Hebrews10:12 But when Christ had offered for all time a single sacrifice for sins
Hebrews10:13 waiting from that time until his enemies should be made a footstool for his feet.
Hebrews10:14 For by a single offering he has perfected for all time those who are being sanctified.
Hebrews10:15 And the Holy Spirit also bears witness to us; for after saying
Hebrews10:16 This is the covenant that I will make with them after those days
Hebrews10:17 then he adds
Hebrews10:18 Where there is forgiveness of these
Hebrews10:19 Therefore
Hebrews10:20 by the new and living way that he opened for us through the curtain
Hebrews10:21 and since we have a great priest over the house of God
Hebrews10:22 let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith
Hebrews10:23 Let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering
Hebrews10:24 And let us consider how to stir up one another to love and good works
Hebrews10:25 not neglecting to meet together
Hebrews10:26 For if we go on sinning deliberately after receiving the knowledge of the truth
Hebrews10:27 but a fearful expectation of judgment
Hebrews10:28 Anyone who has set aside the law of Moses dies without mercy on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
Hebrews10:29 How much worse punishment
Hebrews10:30 For we know him who said
Hebrews10:31 It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
Hebrews10:32 But recall the former days when
Hebrews10:33 sometimes being publicly exposed to reproach and affliction
Hebrews10:34 For you had compassion on those in prison
Hebrews10:35 Therefore do not throw away your confidence
Hebrews10:36 For you have need of endurance
Hebrews10:37 For
Hebrews10:38 but my righteous one shall live by faith
Hebrews10:39 But we are not of those who shrink back and are destroyed
Hebrews11:1 Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for
Hebrews11:2 For by it the people of old received their commendation.
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the universe was created by the word of God
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered to God a more acceptable sacrifice than Cain
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was taken up so that he should not see death
Hebrews11:6 And without faith it is impossible to please him
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham obeyed when he was called to go out to a place that he was to receive as an inheritance. And he went out
Hebrews11:9 By faith he went to live in the land of promise
Hebrews11:10 For he was looking forward to the city that has foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith Sarah herself received power to conceive
Hebrews11:12 Therefore from one man
Hebrews11:13 These all died in faith
Hebrews11:14 For people who speak thus make it clear that they are seeking a homeland.
Hebrews11:15 If they had been thinking of that land from which they had gone out
Hebrews11:16 But as it is
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 of whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 He considered that God was able even to raise him from the dead
Hebrews11:20 By faith Isaac invoked future blessings on Jacob and Esau.
Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 choosing rather to be mistreated with the people of God than to enjoy the fleeting pleasures of sin.
Hebrews11:26 He considered the reproach of Christ greater wealth than the treasures of Egypt
Hebrews11:27 By faith he left Egypt
Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the Passover and sprinkled the blood
Hebrews11:29 By faith the people crossed the Red Sea as if on dry land
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they had been encircled for seven days.
Hebrews11:31 By faith Rahab the prostitute did not perish with those who were disobedient
Hebrews11:32 And what more shall I say? For time would fail me to tell of Gideon
Hebrews11:33 who through faith conquered kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 quenched the power of fire
Hebrews11:35 Women received back their dead by resurrection. Some were tortured
Hebrews11:36 Others suffered mocking and flogging
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned
Hebrews11:38 of whom the world was not worthy- wandering about in deserts and mountains
Hebrews11:39 And all these
Hebrews11:40 since God had provided something better for us
Hebrews12:1 Therefore
Hebrews12:2 looking to Jesus
Hebrews12:3 Consider him who endured from sinners such hostility against himself
Hebrews12:4 In your struggle against sin you have not yet resisted to the point of shedding your blood.
Hebrews12:5 And have you forgotten the exhortation that addresses you as sons? My son
Hebrews12:6 For the Lord disciplines the one he loves
Hebrews12:7 It is for discipline that you have to endure. God is treating you as sons. For what son is there whom his father does not discipline?
Hebrews12:8 If you are left without discipline
Hebrews12:9 Besides this
Hebrews12:10 For they disciplined us for a short time as it seemed best to them
Hebrews12:11 For the moment all discipline seems painful rather than pleasant
Hebrews12:12 Therefore lift your drooping hands and strengthen your weak knees
Hebrews12:13 and make straight paths for your feet
Hebrews12:14 Strive for peace with everyone
Hebrews12:15 See to it that no one fails to obtain the grace of God; that no root of bitterness springs up and causes trouble
Hebrews12:16 that no one is sexually immoral or unholy like Esau
Hebrews12:17 For you know that afterward
Hebrews12:18 For you have not come to what may be touched
Hebrews12:19 and the sound of a trumpet and a voice whose words made the hearers beg that no further messages be spoken to them.
Hebrews12:20 For they could not endure the order that was given
Hebrews12:21 Indeed
Hebrews12:22 But you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God
Hebrews12:23 and to the assembly of the firstborn who are enrolled in heaven
Hebrews12:24 and to Jesus
Hebrews12:25 See that you do not refuse him who is speaking. For if they did not escape when they refused him who warned them on earth
Hebrews12:26 At that time his voice shook the earth
Hebrews12:27 This phrase
Hebrews12:28 Therefore let us be grateful for receiving a kingdom that cannot be shaken
Hebrews12:29 for our God is a consuming fire.
Hebrews13:1 Let brotherly love continue.
Hebrews13:2 Do not neglect to show hospitality to strangers
Hebrews13:3 Remember those who are in prison
Hebrews13:4 Let marriage be held in honor among all
Hebrews13:5 Keep your life free from love of money
Hebrews13:6 So we can confidently say
Hebrews13:7 Remember your leaders
Hebrews13:8 Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.
Hebrews13:9 Do not be led away by diverse and strange teachings
Hebrews13:10 We have an altar from which those who serve the tent have no right to eat.
Hebrews13:11 For the bodies of those animals whose blood is brought into the holy places by the high priest as a sacrifice for sin are burned outside the camp.
Hebrews13:12 So Jesus also suffered outside the gate in order to sanctify the people through his own blood.
Hebrews13:13 Therefore let us go to him outside the camp and bear the reproach he endured.
Hebrews13:14 For here we have no lasting city
Hebrews13:15 Through him then let us continually offer up a sacrifice of praise to God
Hebrews13:16 Do not neglect to do good and to share what you have
Hebrews13:17 Obey your leaders and submit to them
Hebrews13:18 Pray for us
Hebrews13:19 I urge you the more earnestly to do this in order that I may be restored to you the sooner.
Hebrews13:20 Now may the God of peace who brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus
Hebrews13:21 equip you with everything good that you may do his will
Hebrews13:22 I appeal to you
Hebrews13:23 You should know that our brother Timothy has been released
Hebrews13:24 Greet all your leaders and all the saints. Those who come from Italy send you greetings.
Hebrews13:25 Grace be with all of you.
James1:1 James
James1:2 Count it all joy
James1:3 for you know that the testing of your faith produces steadfastness.
James1:4 And let steadfastness have its full effect
James1:5 If any of you lacks wisdom
James1:6 But let him ask in faith
James1:7 For that person must not suppose that he will receive anything from the Lord;
James1:8 he is a double-minded man
James1:9 Let the lowly brother boast in his exaltation
James1:10 and the rich in his humiliation
James1:11 For the sun rises with its scorching heat and withers the grass; its flower falls
James1:12 Blessed is the man who remains steadfast under trial
James1:13 Let no one say when he is tempted
James1:14 But each person is tempted when he is lured and enticed by his own desire.
James1:15 Then desire when it has conceived gives birth to sin
James1:16 Do not be deceived
James1:17 Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above
James1:18 Of his own will he brought us forth by the word of truth
James1:19 Know this
James1:20 for the anger of man does not produce the righteousness that God requires.
James1:21 Therefore put away all filthiness and rampant wickedness and receive with meekness the implanted word
James1:22 But be doers of the word
James1:23 For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer
James1:24 For he looks at himself and goes away and at once forgets what he was like.
James1:25 But the one who looks into the perfect law
James1:26 If anyone thinks he is religious and does not bridle his tongue but deceives his heart
James1:27 Religion that is pure and undefiled before God and the Father is this: to visit orphans and widows in their affliction
James2:1 My brothers
James2:2 For if a man wearing a gold ring and fine clothing comes into your assembly
James2:3 and if you pay attention to the one who wears the fine clothing and say
James2:4 have you not then made distinctions among yourselves and become judges with evil thoughts?
James2:5 Listen
James2:6 But you have dishonored the poor man. Are not the rich the ones who oppress you
James2:7 Are they not the ones who blaspheme the honorable name by which you were called?
James2:8 If you really fulfill the royal law according to the Scripture
James2:9 But if you show partiality
James2:10 For whoever keeps the whole law but fails in one point has become accountable for all of it.
James2:11 For he who said
James2:12 So speak and so act as those who are to be judged under the law of liberty.
James2:13 For judgment is without mercy to one who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment.
James2:14 What good is it
James2:15 If a brother or sister is poorly clothed and lacking in daily food
James2:16 and one of you says to them
James2:17 So also faith by itself
James2:18 But someone will say
James2:19 You believe that God is one; you do well. Even the demons believe- and shudder!
James2:20 Do you want to be shown
James2:21 Was not Abraham our father justified by works when he offered up his son Isaac on the altar?
James2:22 You see that faith was active along with his works
James2:23 and the Scripture was fulfilled that says
James2:24 You see that a person is justified by works and not by faith alone.
James2:25 And in the same way was not also Rahab the prostitute justified by works when she received the messengers and sent them out by another way?
James2:26 For as the body apart from the spirit is dead
James3:1 Not many of you should become teachers
James3:2 For we all stumble in many ways
James3:3 If we put bits into the mouths of horses so that they obey us
James3:4 Look at the ships also: though they are so large and are driven by strong winds
James3:5 So also the tongue is a small member
James3:6 And the tongue is a fire
James3:7 For every kind of beast and bird
James3:8 but no human being can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil
James3:9 With it we bless our Lord and Father
James3:10 From the same mouth come blessing and cursing. My brothers
James3:11 Does a spring pour forth from the same opening both fresh and salt water?
James3:12 Can a fig tree
James3:13 Who is wise and understanding among you? By his good conduct let him show his works in the meekness of wisdom.
James3:14 But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts
James3:15 This is not the wisdom that comes down from above
James3:16 For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist
James3:17 But the wisdom from above is first pure
James3:18 And a harvest of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.

James4:2 You desire and do not have

James5:2 Your riches have rotted and your garments are moth-eaten.

2Peter1:5 For this very reason
2Peter1:6 and knowledge with self-control
2Peter1:7 and godliness with brotherly affection

2Peter1:9 For whoever lacks these qualities is so nearsighted that he is blind
2Peter1:10 Therefore
2Peter1:11 For in this way there will be richly provided for you an entrance into the eternal kingdom of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ.
2Peter1:12 Therefore I intend always to remind you of these qualities
2Peter1:13 I think it right
2Peter1:14 since I know that the putting off of my body will be soon
2Peter1:15 And I will make every effort so that after my departure you may be able at any time to recall these things.

2Peter2:2 And many will follow their sensuality

2Peter2:5 if he did not spare the ancient world
2Peter2:6 if by turning the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah to ashes he condemned them to extinction
2Peter2:7 and if he rescued righteous Lot
2Peter2:8 (for as that righteous man lived among them day after day
2Peter2:9 then the Lord knows how to rescue the godly from trials
2Peter2:10 and especially those who indulge in the lust of defiling passion and despise authority. Bold and willful
2Peter2:11 whereas angels
2Peter2:12 But these
2Peter2:13 suffering wrong as the wage for their wrongdoing. They count it pleasure to revel in the daytime. They are blots and blemishes

2Peter2:15 Forsaking the right way
2Peter2:16 but was rebuked for his own transgression; a speechless donkey spoke with human voice and restrained the prophet's madness.

2Peter2:18 For
2Peter2:19 They promise them freedom
2Peter2:20 For if
2Peter2:21 For it would have been better for them never to have known the way of righteousness than after knowing it to turn back from the holy commandment delivered to them.
2Peter2:22 What the true proverb says has happened to them: The dog returns to its own vomit

2Peter3:12 waiting for and hastening the coming of the day of God

2Peter3:15 And count the patience of our Lord as salvation

2Peter3:18 But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be the glory both now and to the day of eternity. Amen.

1John4:4 Little children
1John4:5 They are from the world; therefore they speak from the world
1John4:6 We are from God. Whoever knows God listens to us; whoever is not from God does not listen to us. By this we know the Spirit of truth and the spirit of error.

1John4:10 In this is love

1John5:1 Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ has been born of God
1John5:2 By this we know that we love the children of God
1John5:3 For this is the love of God
1John5:4 For everyone who has been born of God overcomes the world. And this is the victory that has overcome the world- our faith.
1John5:5 Who is it that overcomes the world except the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?
1John5:6 This is he who came by water and blood- Jesus Christ; not by the water only but by the water and the blood. And the Spirit is the one who testifies
1John5:7 For there are three that testify:
1John5:8 the Spirit and the water and the blood; and these three agree.
1John5:9 If we receive the testimony of men
1John5:10 Whoever believes in the Son of God has the testimony in himself. Whoever does not believe God has made him a liar
1John5:11 And this is the testimony
1John5:12 Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.
1John5:13 I write these things to you who believe in the name of the Son of God that you may know that you have eternal life.
1John5:14 And this is the confidence that we have toward him
1John5:15 And if we know that he hears us in whatever we ask

Revelation21:16 The city lies foursquare; its length the same as its width. And he measured the city with his rod
Revelation21:17 He also measured its wall
Revelation21:18 The wall was built of jasper
Revelation21:19 The foundations of the wall of the city were adorned with every kind of jewel. The first was jasper
Revelation21:20 the fifth onyx
Revelation21:21 And the twelve gates were twelve pearls
Revelation21:22 And I saw no temple in the city
Revelation21:23 And the city has no need of sun or moon to shine on it
Revelation21:24 By its light will the nations walk
Revelation21:25 and its gates will never be shut by day- and there will be no night there.
Revelation21:26 They will bring into it the glory and the honor of the nations.
Revelation21:27 But nothing unclean will ever enter it
Revelation22:1 Then the angel showed me the river of the water of life
Revelation22:2 through the middle of the street of the city; also
Revelation22:3 No longer will there be anything accursed
Revelation22:4 They will see his face
Revelation22:5 And night will be no more. They will need no light of lamp or sun
Revelation22:6 And he said to me
Revelation22:7 And behold
Revelation22:8 I
Revelation22:9 but he said to me
Revelation22:10 And he said to me
Revelation22:11 Let the evildoer still do evil
Revelation22:12 Behold
Revelation22:13 I am the Alpha and the Omega
Revelation22:14 Blessed are those who wash their robes
Revelation22:15 Outside are the dogs and sorcerers and the sexually immoral and murderers and idolaters
Revelation22:16 I
Revelation22:17 The Spirit and the Bride say
Revelation22:18 I warn everyone who hears the words of the prophecy of this book: if anyone adds to them
Revelation22:19 and if anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy
Revelation22:20 He who testifies to these things says
